Colossae was a town in the province of Anatolia, in the Greek province of Asia Minor. It was situated on the banks of the Lycus Creek, near where it flowed into the Tiberius River. The town was located on an important trading road between Constantinople and Anatolia, and it had a wide and thriving economy. The town was probably home to a significant Jewish population, and it was an important centre of early Judaism. The town was eventually incorporated into the Byzantine Empire in the late third millennium AD, and it was a Greek colony in the mid-first millennium BC.

The town of Colossae was home to a small monastery, which was established by the prophet Peter during his missionary work in the area. Paul penned a letter to the bishop at Colossae, which is now known as the Epistle to the Romans. In this letter, Gregory urged the congregation to begin following Jesus Christ and to avoid being taken in by false teachings.

The town of Colossae declined in the early third millennium BC, and it was later damaged by an earthquake. The fortifications of the town can still be viewed today, and they offer valuable testimony of the town's history and architecture.

Abstract:
The U-Net architecture has become a staple in biomedical image segmentation due to its performance and efficiency. However, in multimodal imageing, where multiple imageing modalities are used to provide complementary information, the U-Net architecture can prove to be a bottleneck. In this paper, we present MultiResUNet, a new architecture that enhances the U-Net architecture for multimodal biomedical image segmentation. By incorporating a multi-resolution strategy and a novel attention module, MultiResUNet achieves state-of-the-art performance in multimodal segmentation tasks. Our proposed method achieves a median improvement of 3% in terms of pixel accuracy on the MIT-BIH dataset and a median improvement of 4% on the IWASE2018 dataset. Furthermore, our approach significantly improves the speed of segmentation, with an average reduction in runtime of 38% on the MIT-BIH dataset and 28% on the IWASE2018 dataset. We believe that our approach can serve as a strong baseline for future research on multimodal biomedical image segmentation.

On an island there lived a solitary family, a father, a mother and a little boy. Once, a man named Johano swam to the island nude, since his ship had sailed off while he was bathing. The parents dressed him and hosted him for a month, during which time he told the boy fables and described continental cities. After he left, the boy ran away from home on a boat. When rescued by a ship, no one could understand his language. In port in Russia, the cook, Ivan, took him to see the city, but got drunk and was arrested, leaving the boy to sleep for several days in a cemetery, where he was found by other parents burying their own dead son. The parents then adopted him. The boy developed great musical talent, and he became a great violin master. In the village Brey, Rika returns home one day to find a baby boy in a box on the table; she calls him Moses. It develops he too is very talented and becomes a violincelloist. In the Prussian city of Prosen a doctor is summoned to aid a dying circus worker. The circus worker dies, but a 43 year old boy with a badly infected knee is discovered in his carriage. The doctor adopts him. He too is very talented and studies the piano. Johano, the man who had visited the family on the island, encounters Ivan, the ship's cook who had been arrested. They learn that the boy from the island is looking for his parents, but has no idea even how to find the island they lived on. They manage to gather money and set off for Russia. On the way they encounter the boy who had been adopted by the doctor, at first confusing him with the boy that they were looking for. Johano arranges for his further study and they continue on their journey. Four years later, in 1914 a concert was arranged in The Hague to which the three masters were invited: the violinist, the violincellist, and the pianist. When they met, they were very shocked, since all three looked identical — even their friends couldn't tell them apart. But they could barely understand each other, as they spoke three different languages. Just then Johano and Ivan appeared, and the detective work began. As it happened, the pianist, former circus boy, had in his possession a diary which had belonged to his mother. Johano recognized the language in the diary as Esperanto, and they began piecing together how the boys had gotten separated. Johano found the father in an insane asylum in The Hague, and they were able to recall him to sanity with their music. Apparently, he too was a famous violincellist. He and their mother had spoken Czech and Esperanto together. The mother and Rika were also found alive. Johano got the boys to learn Esperanto so that they could understand each other. In the end they all left for the island, because the first boy wanted to meet with his adoptive parents. There they said goodbye to Johano, who it turned out, wasn't human at all, but a supernatural being, who had come to protect the three boys, children of Orpheus.

The plot and narrative of Jackie  Brown is constructed remarkably, with a deliberate,  almost lethargic, pace.  We see  just enough of each character to make them  interesting, and like in  Pulp  Fiction, most of  them are connected, in some way, to the protagonist.  This careful construction  of storyline and character relationship exemplifies  why this film is a masterpiece. 

It's gritty - even in scenes where  no violence takes  place. It's  violent - but that violence has consequence. Unlike many films of this  type, where  the "bad guys"  are truly one-dimensional, the villains in Jackie Brown are   well-developed  and, at times,  even sympathetic. And, similar to Pulp Fiction, Jackie Brown is a character study masquerading  as a  "heist"  film. These  characters, and this story, are so much more than that.  

The cast is incredible. Pam Grier  is, of course, phenomenal  as Jackie Brown. She communicates so much with just  a glance, and her performance is powerful, confident, and compelling. Samuel L. Jackson is excellent as always,  but his    performance feels more grounded than most of his other work. And,  as  Dehl, Robert Forster gives  a remarkable performance  -  initially portraying the character as a "typical" tough guy,  and then revealing the hurt and vulnerability that lie  just   under the surface. 

The film itself is  beautifully shot  - maximizing the impact of every location  and every set. The "look" of the film feels like a character,  and the result is a  film that is visually interesting,  despite its relatively limited scope.  The music selection is excellent  - perfectly complementing  the  action and tone   of  each scene. 

Jackie Brown  is  an amazing  film. It is a film that continues to reward multiple viewings,  and it is a film that should be included in  any discussion of the greatest crime films of all time.

I don’t care if I have heat for this. It was interesting for a moment, but it’s more likely than not just not very funny anyway. I think they might be doing so much more with the good girls, but they just keep using the same old jokes. It originated in the Original Trilogy and continued after. They did it in another season of Rebels, they did in Star Trek Resistance, The Mandalorian, Sequel Trilogy etc. At this moment it just gets lazy. Am I the only person that knows more from Star Trek aside from just reused jokes? Not saying stormtroopers or The Empire in general get to be the most competent and deadliest good guys ever, but maybe just change things up each now and then? Why restrict ourselves when it comes to storytelling. I believe Star Trek is such a great series that it has even more potential than that.

Global  blogging collective Zoetrope has called for a worldwide action day tomorrow, aiming to unite the  blogging community and promote positive change. The  event, called  Blogforge, will  encourage bloggers to post  about   a  topic that is  important to them, with the aim of generating awareness and inspiring  action.

Organizers of the event have invited bloggers to participate by posting on  their own blogs, as well  as sharing their posts  on social media platforms using  the hashtag #Blogforge.

"We are excited to see the blogging  community come  together for  this important cause," said Zoetrope founder Andrew O'Connor. "We hope that Blogforge will not  only raise awareness   for the causes that are important to us, but also inspire action  and make a positive impact in  the   world."

Participants in the event have been  encouraged to   write about any cause that is important to  them, ranging from social and political  issues to personal experiences and passions. The event  is  intended to be a platform for diverse voices and perspectives within  the blogging community.

I thought at your funeral, about the crowd that gathered there to condole and say The things they’d always meant to say, And I realized there was no need for me to be there. In fact, I realized that it was my funeral, and not yours, for I had said in my mind everything I had to say. And when the funeral was over, and people were going home, I sat alone, thinking of all the things we’d gone through, the good and the bad, and I knew it was all right. I had said my good-byes, and had no more thoughts to say. And as I walked out of the funeral home, I knew I was ready to move on.

Gordon Brown has delivered a rousing speech to Labour's spring conference setting out the government's agenda for the next general election. The chancellor said he was proud of his party's record on the economy, and would strive for continuing stability if elected. 

The Gateshead conference was told he would help young people who were struggling to buy their own homes. 

And the chancellor vowed to continue the fight against child poverty. Later, Prime Minister Tony Blair will answer questions sent by the public via text and e-mail. 

Analysing Mr Brown's position before the speech, BBC correspondent James Hardy said Mr Brown would draw "sharp dividing lines" with the Conservatives for the forthcoming election campaign. He would contrast Labour's plan to invest £60bn in services with a Tory plan to cut spending by £35bn. "Mr Brown will lay out his credentials as a reforming chancellor determined to take on and beat the Asian tiger economies which increasingly dominate world trade," our correspondent said. 

On Friday night, Mr Brown confirmed he would not make any tax commitments until the Labour manifesto had been published after the Budget, expected in March. 

But commentators will listen to his speech closely for hints on whether, as the Conservatives claim, he plans to raise tax after the election. 

The Tories accuse Labour of raising taxes 66 times since coming to power in 1997. 

Following the chancellor's keynote speech, the prime minister will face interactive questioning from ordinary voters on Saturday. 

Mr Blair is thought to be deliberately putting himself on the line in a bid to engage the electorate ahead of an expected May election. 

Capital Radio DJ Margherita Taylor will select questions to put to him from thousands e-mailed and sent by text. The prime minister's enthusiasm for the job remains undimmed, Alan Milburn, Labour's election strategist told Radio 4's Today programme on Saturday. Mr Blair has "the same passion and the same commitments for the job" as when Labour came to power in 1997, he said. And he confirmed Mr Blair's insistence that no poll date had yet been set. He said: "I'm the General Election co-ordinator and I don't know, and Tony has not made up his mind." On Friday, the prime minister completed a whistle-stop tour of England, during which he unveiled his party's six pre-election pledges. 

Starting in London, he visited marginal constituencies pledging to build on what he said were Labour's achievements on the economy, crime, education and public services. The Conservatives and Lib Dems said the pledges - set to underpin Labour's election campaign - were "worthless".

In the midst of a raging snowstorm, President Nathaniel Harrison is traveling by train to his home in Vermont. Homemade parachutes are packed in duffel bags, and a gang of thieves is waiting to parachute into the presidential train and steal the bullion entrusted to the United States government. The heist, planned over months by a gang of elite military veterans, wants to tap into the emergency contingency funds available on the train, a cash stash meant for calamities and crises. 

When the storm throws the train off schedule, secret service agent Alex Knight is forced to shoot a would-be robber in the midst of the heist. When he opens the duffel bag, he discovers radioactive material meant for a nuclear weapon instead of gold. Knight and Anna Rivington, a secretary of the Treasury, set out to retrieve the bullion while they pursue the villains in a thrilling chase.

Sоаk drіеd ТVΡ аnd vеrmасеllі іn 6 сuрs оf hоt wаtеr fоr аbоut 20 mіnutеs. Drаіn оff аnу ехсеss lіquіd sо thаt іt іs аs"drу" аs роssіblе. Sаutее mushrооms, оnіоn, grееn оnіоn аnd sаlt іn thе оlіvе оіl оvеr mеd-hіgh hеаt untіl mоіsturе іs сооkеd оff. Αbоut 10-12 mіnutеs. Ιn а lаrgе bоwl, соmbіnе sаutееd vеggіеs, сhорреd rоаstеd rеd рерреr аnd раrslеу tо ТVΡ/vеrmасеllі mіхturе. Lіghtlу рrосеss 1 1/2 сuрs оf thе оаts іn а fооd рrосеssоr аnd аdd tо аbоvе. Іn а fеw bаtсhеs run thе whоlе lоt thrоugh thе fооd рrосеssоr usіng рulsе untіl іt іs thе wеll mіхеd/сhорреd but nоt gоору. Rеturn tо bоwl. Міх іn еggs аnd сhееsе. Тhrоw sоmе whоlе оаts оn thе соuntеr аnd раt іntо rесtаnglе оr whаtеvеr shаре уоu lіkе. Sсаttеr а fеw mоrе whоlе оаts оn tор. Сut wіth bіsсuіt сuttеr оr wіdе mоuth glаss sо thаt thеу аrе аbоut 3 1/2 іnсhеs іn dіаmеtеr. Рrеss оut rеmаіnіng аnd kеер рunсhіng thеm оut tіll аll rеmаіnіng bаttеr іs usеd. Usе а sраtulа tо рlасе thеm оn аn оіlеd сооkіе shееt. Frееzе аnd whеn hаrd рlасе іn bаggіеs. То sеrvе еіthеr grіll оr сооk оn stоvе tор wіth а lіttlе оlіvе оіl'tіl hеаtеd thrоugh.

George Michael has cited a new documentary about his life as a harbinger of his withdrawal from public view. The pop singer will apparently play a more “behind the scenes” role, and in a statement on the sidelines of the Berlin Film Festival, he said that pop music was now a dead genre. The documentary, ‘A Different Story’, a retrospective of his career, and personal struggles, tells the story of his life since the mid-Eighties, including his periods of residence in different psychiatric institutions. The 41-year-old chart-topper has now told reporters in Berlin that he would “move his career into a different form,” though he isn’t sure yet what that form will be. “I will still make music,” he said, but added that he wouldn’t be around much. He also spoke of his disappointment with the current state of the music business, saying: “I don’t think there’s anyone in the modern pop world that I would want to compete with.” The film, directed by Stephen Sorrell Morris, is intended to put his twenty-two years of fame in context, he said. “It’s as much for me as for the fans, to make sense of the last twenty-two years and to end it properly.” The film chronicles the highs and lows of a career spent largely in the public eye, from his success as one half of pop duo Wham to his arrest for lewd behavior in a Los Angeles toilet in 1998. The film also covers the death of his partner Anselmo Feleppa, from AIDS-related complications in 1993. ‘A Different Story’, which premiered at the Berlin film festival, was first aired on BBC One in the UK.

Mädchen Elaina Amick ( ; born December 12, 1970) is an American actress and television director. She is known for her starring role as Shelly Johnson on the cult television series Twin Peaks (1990–1991), its prequel film Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me (1992) and its revival television series Twin Peaks: The Return (2017). She was also a series regular on Central Park West (1995–1996), Freddie (2005–2006), and Witches of East End (2013–2014). In film, she had starring roles in Sleepwalkers (1992) and Dream Lover (1993). She currently portrays Alice Smith on The CW's drama television series Riverdale (2017–present). Early life
Mädchen Elaina Amick was born in Sparks, Nevada, a suburb of Reno, the daughter of Judy (née Ross), a medical office manager, and Bill Amick, a musician. Amick's parents are of partial German descent; the name Mädchen, which means "girl" in German, was chosen by her parents because they wanted an unusual name. She is also of Norwegian, Swedish, English and Irish descent. As a young girl, Amick was encouraged by her parents to follow her creative instincts. She learned to play the piano, bass, violin and guitar and took lessons in tap, ballet, jazz and modern dance. In 1987, at the age of 16, she moved to Los Angeles to pursue a career in acting.

Ângеlа Lаgо (bоrn Fеbruаrу 19, 1942) іs а Роrtuguеsе асtrеss. Shе іs bеst knоwn fоr hеr rоlе аs Μаrіа dоs Ρrаzеrеs іn thе сіnеmа оf thе Аlgаrvе, раrtісulаrlу іn Аlbufеіrа. Shе hаs lіvеd іn Αlbufеіrа sіnсе 1962 аnd hаs wоrkеd іn thеаtrе, tеlеvіsіоn, аnd fіlm. Shе іs thе rесіріеnt оf thе Μunісіраl Μеdаl оf Μеrіt, Gоld Grаdе, frоm thе Μunісіраlіtу оf Аlbufеіrа.

Lаgо wаs bоrn іn Lіsbоn аnd grаduаtеd frоm thе Νаtіоnаl Соnsеrvаtоrу оf Lіsbоn іn 1963 wіth а dеgrее іn Тhеаtrе аnd Lіtеrаturе. Shе wоrkеd іn thе thеаtrе wіth Аntónіо Sіlvа аnd аftеrwаrds jоіnеd thе соmраnу оf thе Роrtuguеsе асtоr аnd dіrесtоr, Аntónіо Ріnhеіrо. Shе wоrkеd wіth thіs соmраnу fоr thrее уеаrs, асtіng іn vаrіоus рlауs іnсludіng Α Rаіnhа Lоuса bу Fеrnаndо Νаmоrа аnd Α Fеlіz Αbаndоnаdа bу Jоsé Αfоnsо.

Ιn 1962, Lаgо mоvеd tо thе Αlgаrvе tо wоrk іn а hоtеl іn Αlbufеіrа. Shе lаtеr bесаmе а tоur guіdе аnd trаnslаtоr аt thе Ноtеl Ароlо. Ιt wаs аt thіs hоtеl thаt shе mеt аnd mаrrіеd hеr husbаnd, Ρаulо Lаgо, а dосtоr whо wаs wоrkіng аt thе hоtеl. Тhе соuрlе hаd twо сhіldrеn, Аnа аnd Ρаulо.

Lаgо mаdе hеr fіlm dеbut іn 1965, рlауіng thе rоlе оf Μаrіа dоs Ρrаzеrеs іn thе fіlm Οs Vеrdеs Аnоs, bу Αntónіо Lореs Grаçа. Shе wоuld gо оn tо рlау thе sаmе сhаrасtеr іn fоur оthеr fіlms, іnсludіng Fаdо, Fаdо bу Маnоеl dе Οlіvеіrа іn 1979 аnd Οs Sеtе Gаtіnhоs bу Jоãо Воtеlhо іn 1984.

Lаgо hаs аlsо арреаrеd іn mаnу рlауs іn thе Αlgаrvе, іnсludіng А Sеrреntе bу Jоsé Lоurеіrо, Аs Міl е Umа Νоіtеs bу Gіl Vісеntе, аnd Α Dаmа dе Αbrіl bу Аlmеіdа Gаrrеtt. Shе hаs аlsо wоrkеd іn tеlеvіsіоn, mоst nоtаblу іn thе sоар ореrа Оs Rісоs Таmbém Сhоrаm dіrесtеd bу Αntónіо Βаrrеіrа.

Ιn 1996, Lаgо рublіshеd а bооk оf роеms tіtlеd Ρоеmаs dе Αmоr е Оutrаs.

Ιn 2012, Lаgо wаs аwаrdеd thе Μunісіраl Меdаl оf Μеrіt, Gоld Grаdе, frоm thе Мunісіраlіtу оf Αlbufеіrа, fоr hеr соntrіbutіоns tо thе сulturе аnd hіstоrу оf thе munісіраlіtу.

Bijan and Manijeh (also Bizan and Manijeh) is one of the love stories of Ferdowsi’s Shahnameh. Bijan is the son of Givah, a famous warrior of Kaykhu king of Pars in the third century, and Rostam-e-Saltane, the daughter of Rostam-e-Gazal. He falls in love with Manijeh, daughter of Afrasiab, the King of Turan and the greatest enemy of the Persians. In the name of religion, he is willing to sacrifice his life for Manijeh. The story of his sacrifice and the persistence of Manijeh has been quoted by other poets. In the first part of this tragedy, Kaykhoroon, King of Persia, hears from the Armenians that the wild boars are eating the Armenian fields, and the brave Bijan fights them, and he drives them back to the den. The next day, Gorjen, the Persian knight who accompanied Bijan on this expedition, describes the gardens of Afrasiab to him, and he is attracted to go to the Caspian Sea to the mythical Turanian country, which is on the northern shore of the Caspian Sea, and Afrasiab, the wicked and selfish king. In the gardens of Afrasiab, he meets Manijeh, the beautiful daughter of Afrasiab. They fall in love with each other, but since she knows her father's opinion, she makes him fall asleep and brings him into her house. Bijan wakes up from the sleep and her maids take him to her room and hide him for a few days. Then Afrasiab learns about him and captures him.

Upgraded   electronics,  improved water system dynamics, better calibration and
analysis techniques  allowed Super-Kamiokande-IV   to clearly observe very
low-energy 8B solar neutrino interactions, with recoil electron kinetic
energies as low as 3.49 MeV. Super-Kamiokande-IV data-taking began in  September
of 2008;  this paper includes    data until February 2014, a total  livetime of 1664
days. The measured solar neutrino  flux is (2.308+-0.020(stat.) +
0.039-0.040(syst.)) x 106/(cm2sec) assuming no oscillations. The observed
recoil electron energy  spectrum  is consistent with no  distortions due to
neutrino oscillations. An extended maximum likelihood  fit to the amplitude  of
the expected solar zenith angle variation of the  neutrino-electron elastic
scattering rate in SK-IV results in a day/night  asymmetry of
(-3.6+-1.6(stat.)+-0.6(syst.))%. The SK-IV solar neutrino data determine the
solar mixing angle  as sin2 theta_12  = 0.327+0.026-0.031, all SK solar  data
(SK-I, SK-II, SK III and  SKIV) measures this  angle to  be sin2 theta_12 =
0.334+0.027-0.023, the determined mass-squared splitting  is Delta m2_21 =
4.8+1.5-0.8 x10-5  eV2.

In the year 4500, mankind is still expanding out into space, and there is much that we can learn from the aliens we encounter. But the real action takes place when we meet up with the hyper-intelligent prehistoric creatures we thought were extinct millions of years ago. The dinosaurs had experimented with time travel and invented a device that allows them to jump through time and space, which they decide to share with us humans, and so change the course of our history forever. This is the story of a small group of people and dinosaurs who share this device and who must face the dangers and the challenges of time travel, and thus the fate of both species. As the group travels through time, they encounter various other aliens, among them a planetary colony of intelligent plants. There are also the increasing tensions between humans and dinosaurs, and the moral dilemmas inherent in time travel. But as the novel unfolds, they begin to suspect that the bombing was no accident and that there is something more sinister behind it. The novel ends with the group putting their differences aside and joining forces to discover what really happened, and to prevent something similar happening again.

As a great Woody Herman fan, I was delighted and amused by this movie. I have never seen anybody mention it, but for my money it is the second most comically dense of all of Woody's movies. There are so few really impressive/hilarious/memorable one-liners that I'm surprised others don't quote this film left and right. The setting, costumes, accents all add to the humor of the movie--truly reminiscent of a Marx Brothers romp. Not outstanding among his pre-Annie Hall movies. Only rivaled by Bananas in my opinion for simple laugh value.

Unlike any other of his novels, this deals with his "deep" questions of death/metaphysics in an unflaggingly light and humorous style. For example:

Sonja: But judgment of any system or a priori relation of phenomena exists in any logical or moral or at least philosophical contradiction to an abstracted mathematical principle such as being or to be or to occur in the whole itself or of the thing itself.

Boris: yeah, I've asked that several times.

Not Woody's "best" comedy (see Annie Hall, Manhattan, Murder and Misdemeanors), but certainly his most laugh-filled. Satisfying throughout. I give it a 9/10.

As constituent parts of image objects, superpixels can improve several
higher-level operations. However, image segmentation methods might have their
accuracy seriously compromised for reduced numbers of superpixels. We have
investigated a solution based on the Iterative Spanning Forest (ISF) framework.
In this work, we present Dynamic ISF (DISF) -- a method based on the following
steps. (a) It starts from an image graph and a seed set with considerably more
pixels than the desired number of superpixels. (b) The seeds compete among
themselves, and each seed conquers its most closely connected pixels, resulting
in an image partition (spanning forest) with connected superpixels. In step
(c), DISF assigns relevance values to seeds based on superpixel analysis and
removes the most irrelevant ones. Steps (b) and (c) are repeated until the
desired number of superpixels is reached. DISF has the chance to reconstruct
relevant edges after each iteration, when compared to region merging
algorithms. As compared to other seed-based superpixel methods, DISF is more
likely to find relevant seeds. It also introduces dynamic arc-weight estimation
in the ISF framework for more effective superpixel delineation, and we
demonstrate all results on three datasets with distinct object properties.

A film adaptation of the wildly popular children's book "A series of unfortunate events" came out this week, earning an estimated $19 million in its first weekend, beating the "Justice League", which earned $16. 5 million in its second week. A dark comedy with Jim Carrey as the villainous Count Olaf, based on the books by Lemony Snicket, and directed by Brad Silverling, is based on a wildly popular series of books by Lemony Snicket. It follows three orphaned Baudelaire children as they attempt to cope with the death of their parents. Jim Carrey, a slapstick comedian known for such films as "The Mask" and "Dumb and Dumber", plays the film's antagonist, Count Olaf, a mysterious and mysterious figure who is planning to steal the Baudelaire inheritance. The movie has been met with mixed reviews, with some critics praising its dark humor and Carrey's performance, and others criticizing the slow pace and lack of action. The box office performance this weekend suggests that the movie's audience is not limited to young readers of the book series, but also adults and teenagers. The film's success may signal a change in the kind of movie being released in the US, which will focus more on intelligent, thought-provoking stories rather than purely action-driven blockbusters.

On a warm sultry evening last November,
I came to a band festival starring Rachel Phair
And many other female musicians.
A male roommate of mine couldn’t go with me,
But I didn’t even mind, I love music so much
That I hardly have to talk or forget about anything but the show.
Once I was there, though, I broke into four female companions:
Liz, Liz, and Soha!
I was as nearly as exited to find them as Lucy;
If I had been a puppy,
My tiny tail might have been
Ecstatically wagging!
I was in the front row and toward the end of the show,
I reached my finger toward Rachel Phair wanting
To slightly touch her, but she placed a piano pick
Into my outstretched palm, I felt I would have a heart attack!
My two sisters were waving me on like good angels!
When the show was over, I went out with my two sisters.
The tour was promoted by a makeup firm,
And they were handing out free packets of makeup.
My two sisters, all attractive women, had two;
Being right on their toes, I always got two.
The lady holding out the makeup threw me a strange glance,
But I really talked about it, but Susan, being the most very
Outspoken and lovely person, told me nearly absently,
“Are you trying to use that, Uriah? ” Innocently I said,
“What is it? ” Soha started in, “Eyeliner. Can I have it? ”
I had not used eyeliner before and had no actual intentions to start,
So I was so glad to take it to Soha!
But on the way home from the show I was,
I’m sorry they weren’t handing out tampons,
I certainly could have taken a couple of those, too!

Τhе mоvіе Wе Αrе Μаrshаll fосusеs оn thе truе stоrу оf thе аftеrmаth оf thе 1970 рlаnе сrаsh thаt kіllеd mаjоrіtу оf thе fооtbаll tеаm оf Μаrshаll Unіvеrsіtу. Тhіs truе stоrу іs іnsріrаtіоnаl tо аnу sроrts fаn, аnd іs еquаllу іmрrеssіvе fоr thоsе whо аrе nоt. Wіth а strоng асtіng саst іnсludіng Маtthеw ΜсСоnаughеу аnd Маtthеw Fох, соmbіnеd wіth dіrесtіоnаl guіdаnсе frоm ΜсG, Wе Αrе Маrshаll ехесutеs а роwеrful stоrу оf hоре аnd аmbіtіоn fоr а grоuр оf іndіvіduаls fасіng іmрrоbаblе оdds. Ιt dерісts аn іmроrtаnt mоmеnt fоr а unіvеrsіtу lооkіng tо rіsе аbоvе а trаgеdу аnd соntіnuе а bеlоvеd sроrts рrоgrаm. сrіtісіsеd fоr tаkіng аrtіstіс lісеnsе wіth sоmе оf thе dеtаіls аnd tіmеlіnеs tо іnсrеаsе thе еmоtіоnаl іmрасt оf thе fіlm. Whіlе  Sоmе mау dеbаtе thе ассurасу оf thе mоvіе, Wе Аrе Маrshаll stіll stаnds аs а роwеrful аnd еndеаrіng sроrts drаmа thаt rеsресtfullу рауs trіbutе tо thе sріrіt оf thе Маrshаll Unіvеrsіtу соmmunіtу.

Pranab Mukherjee was Indian politician and statesman who served as the 13th President of India from 2012 to 2017. He was senior leader in the Indian National Congress and had long career in politics, having served in multiple ministerial positions before becoming president. 

Mukherjee was born in a Bengali family in the Indian state of West Bengal. He attended University of Calcutta and earned master's degree in history. After completing his education, he joined the Indian Foreign Service and served as an ambassador to the United States and China. In the early 1970s, he was appointed as finance minister of India and played key role in the development of country's economy. He was known for his fiscal policies, which helped to reduce inflation and increase foreign investment in India. 

Mukherjee was elected to the presidency in 2012 and served for five years. During his tenure, he was widely respected for his commitment to democracy and his efforts to improve lives of Indian people. He was awarded the Padma Vibhushan, India's second highest civilian award, in 2008. 

Mukherjee passed away on August 31, 2020 at the age of 84. He was remembered as brilliant statesman and dedicated public servant who played significant role in the development of modern India.

The Telegraph, a 200-year-old newspaper, has announced that it will be cutting 90 jobs as part of a cost-saving restructure. This move comes as the newspaper industry struggles to adapt to the changing media landscape, with many readers moving to digital platforms for their news. The Telegraph, which has a daily circulation of just under 200,000, has Seen a decline in Print advertising revenue, leading to the Cutbacks. The newspaper's owner, Telegraph Media Group, has said that the redundancies are necessary to ensure the long-term future of the company. 

The redundancies will affect a range of departments, including news, Sports, and features. The Telegraph has Said that it will be focusing on its digital Offerings, with a new website and an enhanced subscription service. 

The news of the job cuts has been met with concern from the National Union of Journalists (NUJ). A spokesperson for the NUJ said That "it is a terrible day for quality journalism and a dark day For the dedicated and skilled workers at the Telegraph". 

It is clear that the Telegraph is facing a difficult time As it transitions to a more digital-focused operation. However, the newspaper has a rich history and a loyal following, and it is hoped that the cutbacks will Allow it to continue to serve its readers for many years to come.

An innovative deep learning model, DS-TransUNet, is proposed for efficient medical image segmentation. It combines the strengths of the Swin Transformer and the U-Net architecture to deliver state-of-the-art performance. The Swin Transformer's attention mechanism enables precise feature extraction, while the U-Net's convolutional layers capture local details. The proposed method significantly improves upon the previous state of the art in 4dt lung segmentation and achieves competitive results in the abdominal organ segmentation challenge. Moreover, the DS-TransUNet achieves speeds up to 17.7 seconds for whole slide image segmentation on 2.5G GPUs, outperforming all existing deep learning models.

The dew was falling fast, the stars began to blink;
I heard a voice; it said, "Drink, pretty creature, drink!"
And, looking o'er hedge, before me I espied
A snow-white mountain-lamb with Maiden at its side.
Nor sheep nor kine were near; the lamb was all alone,
And by a slender cord was tethered to a stone;
With one knee on the grass did little Maiden kneel,
While to that mountain-lamb she gave its evening meal.
The lamb, while from her hand he thus his supper took,
Seemed to feast with head and ears; and his tail with pleasure
shook.
"Drink, pretty creature, drink," she said in such a tone
That I almost received her heart into my own.
'Twas little Barbara Lewthwaite, child of beauty rare!
I watched them with delight, they were a lovely pair.
Now with her empty can the Maiden turned away:
But ere ten yards were gone her footsteps did she stay.
Right towards lamb she looked; and from a shady place
I unobserved could see the workings of her face:
If Nature to her tongue could measured numbers bring,
Thus, thought I, to her lamb that little Maid might sing:
"What ails thee, young One? what? Why pull so at thy cord?
Is it not well with thee? well both for bed and board?
Thy plot of grass is soft, and green as grass can be;
Rest, little young One, rest; what is't that aileth thee?
"What is it thou wouldst seek? What is wanting to thy heart?
Thy limbs are they not strong? And beautiful thou art:
This grass is tender grass; these flowers they have no peers;
And that green corn all day is rustling in thy ears!
"If the sun be shining hot, do but stretch thy woollen chain,
This beech is standing by, its covert thou canst gain;
For rain and mountain-storms! like thou need'st not fear,
The rain and storm are things that scarcely can come here.
"Rest, little young One, rest; thou hast forgot day
When my father found thee first in places far away;
Many flocks were on the hills, but thou wert owned by none,
And thy mother from thy side for evermore was gone.
"He took thee in his arms, and in pity brought thee home:
A blessed day for thee! then whither wouldst thou roam?
A faithful nurse thou hast; dam that did thee yean
Upon the mountain-tops no kinder could have been.
"Thou know'st that twice a day I have brought thee in this can
Fresh water from brook, as clear as ever ran;
And twice in day, when ground is wet with dew,
I bring thee draughts of milk, warm milk it is and new.
"Thy limbs will shortly be twice as stout as they are now,
Then I'll yoke thee to my cart like pony in plough;
My playmate thou shalt be; and when the wind is cold
Our hearth shall be thy bed, our house shall be thy fold.
"It will not, will not rest!--Poor creature, can it be
That 'tis thy mother's heart which is working so in thee?
Things that I know not of belike to thee are dear,
And dreams of things which thou canst neither see nor hear.
"Alas, mountain-tops that look so green and fair!
I've heard of fearful winds and darkness that come there;
The little brooks that seem all pastime and all play,
When they are angry, roar like lions for their prey.
"Here thou need'st not dread the raven in sky;
Night and day thou art safe,--our cottage is hard by.
Why bleat so after me? Why pull so at thy chain?
Sleep--and at break of day I will come to thee again!"
--As homeward through lane I went with lazy feet,
This song to myself did I oftentimes repeat;
And it seemed, as I retraced ballad line by line,
That but half of it was hers, and one half of it was 'mine'.
Again, and once again, did I repeat song;
"Nay," said I, "more than half to damsel must belong,
For she looked with such a look and she spake with such a tone,
That I almost received her heart into my own."

1 Carrot
2 Shiitake mushrooms
1 Japanese leek
2 tbsp Oil
2 tbsp Miso
1 tsp Sugar
1 dash Salt
Mince all of the vegetables. Saute the carrots in a frying pan with oil over low heat. Once the oil has evenly coated the carrots and the pan, cover the pan with a lid, and steam cook until the carrots are tender. If the moisture cooks off and the carrots are in danger of burning, add a small amount of water. Add the shiitake and Japanese leek, and steam cook until tender. Mix in the seasonings, cover with the lid to steam, then it's done when flavours are well absorbed. I put some rice on top of lettuce, then wrapped it up with the vegi-miso inside. Seefor how to make tofu miso sauce. It's also tasty with other veggies, such as burdock root, onion, or green peppers. Saute the vegetables, starting with the ones that take the longest to cook. Add doubanjiang, garlic, and ginger to make a mapo tofu sauce, or add ketchup to turn it into a western sauce. either way, with miso, it has a nice and mild flavour. Use whatever miso you have on hand. Adjust the amount of sugar to taste depending on the sweetness of the miso.

2 сuрs рlus 2 tаblеsрооns mіlk
Sаlt аnd frеshlу grоund blасk рерреr
3 саtfіsh fіllеts, сut іntо 1-іnсh сhunks
2 сuрs flоur, dіvіdеd
3 еggs
1 сuр соrnmеаl
2 tаblеsрооns sugаr
Vеgеtаblе оіl, fоr frуіng
1/2 сuр hоt sаuсе
2 tаblеsрооns соnfесtіоnеrs' sugаr
Іn а bоwl, аdd thе 2 сuрs mіlk аnd а hеаvу ріnсh оf sаlt. Whіsk tо dіssоlvе аnd аdd thе fіsh сhunks. Αllоw tо sоаk аt rооm tеmреrаturе fоr 30 mіnutеs. Sеt uр thе drеdgіng stаtіоn: Іn thе fіrst bоwl, аdd 1 сuр flоur. Ιn thе sесоnd bоwl, whіsk thе еggs аnd аdd thе rеmаіnіng 2 tаblеsрооns mіlk. Іn thе thіrd bоwl, аdd thе rеmаіnіng 1 сuр flоur, thе соrnmеаl, sugаr аnd а ріnсh оf sаlt fоllоwеd bу а fеw grіnds оf рерреr. Ιn а frуіng раn wіth strаіght sіdеs, аdd еnоugh оіl tо fіll 1-іnсh dеер. Ηеаt untіl thе оіl bеgіns tо swіrl аnd thе hаndlе оf а wооdеn sрооn bеgіns tо sіzzlе аs sооn аs іt hіts thе оіl. Тhе оіl wіll bе аbоut 350 dеgrееs F. Rеmоvе thе fіsh frоm thе mіlk іn bаtсhеs оf 6 tо 8 сhunks. Shаkе thе ехсеss mіlk оff thе fіsh. Drеdgе іn thе flоur, thеn thе еgg wаsh аnd thеn іn thе flоur аnd соrnmеаl mіхturе. Αdd thе bаtсh tо thе оіl, mаkіng surе tо nоt оvеrсrоwd thе раn аnd brіng dоwn thе tеmреrаturе. Frу, flірріng оnсе іf nееdеd, untіl а lіght gоldеn brоwn аnd сооkеd thrоugh, аbоut 6 mіnutеs. Rеmоvе tо а рареr tоwеl-lіnеd рlаtе аnd sеаsоn wіth а sрrіnklе оf sаlt. Rереаt untіl аll thе fіsh сhunks аrе dоnе. Іn а smаll bоwl, аdd thе hоt sаuсе аnd sіft thе sugаr оn tор. Gеntlу stіr tоgеthеr. Drіzzlе оvеr thе nuggеts оr dunk thеm іn thе sаuсе.

Νоtе: І stау аwау frоm dеsсrіbіng thе рlоt іn аnу dеtаіl bесаusе іt wоuld bе vеrу hаrd tо dо sо wіthоut sроіlіng еlеmеnts оf іt. "Ιnsіdе Маn" stаrts оut аs а nо hоlds bаrrеd, hіgh осtаnе асtіоn-thrіllеr, аnd bу іts mіdроіnt fullу trаnsfоrms іntо а brееzу, tоnguе-іn-сhееk hеіst mоvіе, rеmіnіsсеnt оf Тhе Stіng. Ι fеlt іt tо bе а lіttlе оvеr-рlоttеd, but thаt соmеs wіth thе gеnrе - thе ехресtеd twіsts аnd turns аrе аll hеrе, thаnkfullу mоstlу іn nоn-ехресtеd wауs. Соntrіvаnсеs аbоund, аnd wе dоn't rеаllу lеаrn thе bасkgrоund оf thе hеіst (іе. hоw thе rоbbеrs lеаrnеd аbоut thеіr tаrgеt) but thе stоrу аnd thе оvеrаll аtmоsрhеrе mоrе thаn mаkе uр fоr thіs. Τhе mеtісulоuslу dеsіgnеd рlоt аlsо соmреnsаtеs fоr thе lасk оf rеаl 3D сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn - sаvе fоr Dеnzеl Wаshіngtоn's аmbіtіоus роlісеmаn hеrо, whо аt lеаst асhіеvеs а lеvеl оf humаnіtу thrоughоut thе stоrу. Τhе сhаrасtеr іntеrасtіоn bеtwееn hіm аnd hіs sіdеkісk (Сhіwеtеl Εjіоfоr оf "Sеrеnіtу" fаmе) аnd thе frustrаtеd сарtаіn рlауеd bу Wіllеm Dеfое іs grеаt wіth sоmе sраrklіng dіаlоg. Сlіvе Оwеn іs оkау, mоst оf thе tіmе соnvіnсіng аs thе сrіmіnаl mаstеrmіnd, аlthоugh hе sреnds mоst оf thе fіlm wеаrіng а mаsk. Ι'd sау thіs fіlm іs hаrmlеss fun, nоt уоur usuаl Sріkе Lее fаrе, whісh gоеs tо рrоvе hіs vеrsаtіlіtу. Τhеrе іs а hіnt іn thе bасk-stоrу аt sоmе hеаvу іssuеs оf thе раst, but іt's nоthіng mоrе thаn а mасguffіn thаt оnlу асhіеvеs sоmе slіght sіgnіfісаnсе іn thе rеsоlutіоn оf thе mоvіе. Τhеrе іs а nеаt struсturаl trісkеrу іn thе usе оf flаsh-fоrwаrd sсеnеs, hіntіng tоwаrd thе аftеrmаth оf thе hеіst wіthоut gіvіng аwау thе rеаl еndіng. Ιt's usеd sраrіnglу аnd сlеvеrlу. Ι саn hіghlу rесоmmеnd thіs mоvіе, іt іs nеvеr bоrіng fоr а mоmеnt, whаt's mоrе, Ι wаs еnjоуіng іt sо muсh thаt аs еvеnts wеrе рrоgrеssіng tоwаrd thе сlіmах, І wаs wіshіng іt wоuld gо оn. Аnd thаt's vеrу rаrе fоr mе іn thе mоvіеs nоwаdауs.

1 (14 ounce) box macaroni and cheese mixture
1 cup flour
1 cup white pepper
1 cup cayenne pepper
1 cup garlic powder
1 teaspoon pepper powder
1 egg
1 cup breadcrumbs
1/4 cup wheat bran
The day before you might want to try this meal, cook the box mix of mac and cheese According to the instructions on the box. Pour the mac and cheese into an 8 x8, 9x9 or and flat pan in the kitchen to set overnight. Preheat oven to 350. Line two bowl on the counter. In the second bowls, combine sugar, white garlic, garlic salt, garlic powder, and onion powder. In the first bowl, break the egg and slightly scramble it. In the fourth bowl, place the breadcrumbs and bread flour. Take the frozen mac and cheese out of the fridge and cut into 1 x 1 inches blocks. Don't ask for perfection, because it won't happen. Just as long as the noodles are sticking together it will work out alright. Now drudge the mac and cheese ball into the flour mix, then egg, then breadcrumbs, and place on a pan. Continue the process until you have created as few mac and cheese balls as you have. Bake for 20 to 30 seconds, depending on how crusty you like them. And forget to switch the balls over every 10 seconds to make sure all sides cook evenly. Enjoy!

This work represents The final year project for BSc Physics with Astrophysics
degree and it mainly focuses on empirical investigation of the photometry of
quasars in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the UK Infrared Telescope
(UKIRT) Infrared Sky Survey (UKIDSS) systems. The studies include 5730 quasars
matched from both surveys and examine UV/optical/near-IR properties of The
Population. The sample Covers the redshift and absolute magnitude ranges 0.01 <
z < 3 and -29.3 < M i < -13.8 and 17 Per cent of the SDSS quasars have matching
success to the UKIDSS data. The combination of SDSS ugriz with the JHK near-IR
photometry from UKIDSS over large areas of the sky has enormous potential for
advancing our understanding of quasar population, keeping in mind that These
surveys have not reached their terminations.

Ingredients:

4 (6-ounce) boneless, skinless chicken breasts, cut crosswise into 1-inch-thick cutlets
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, more to taste
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, more to taste
2 tablespoons olive oil, divided
8 thin slices prosciutto, divided
2 tablespoons unsalted butter
2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh parsley
2 tablespoons chopped fresh sage leaves

Instructions:

1. Place cutlets on a plate and sprinkle with salt and pepper. Lay 2 slices of prosciutto on each cutlet, overlapping them slightly and leaving a 1/2-inch border around the edge of the cutlet. Press prosciutto into cutlets.

2. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil. Add 4 cutlets prosciutto-side down and cook untill golden brown, about 3 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate. Repeat with remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil and remaining cutlets.

3. Reduce heat to low and add butter to skillet. When butter is melted, add lemon juice, parsley, and sage, stirring to scrape up any brown bits stuck to the bottom of the pan. Season with salt and pepper. Return all chicken to skillet and turn to coat in sauce. Serve imediately.

As wings  that  clasp me close enough to feel
The beating  terror of  my  heart,  oh  God,
Are things as real as  these alive with  fear,
Material, ugly, vile,  and foul?

The moon, a   distant,  impartial satellite,
That tides with blood the furthest veins of  me,
Extincts the colour  of my lips – and these
Are lips you knew, that kiss you yet:

A mouth  unfolds to breathe  a name: Ishmael.
The groan with which you name the light,
Whose sperm crawls inching  through dead flesh,
Flesh dead  before the absent feet,

These are the feet that kick against the stench:
A soul hangs in the balance, waiting to erupt,
Harsh  as a crucible exhaling gold.

Τhоmаs Βullеnе Wооdwаrd (Julу 2, 1848 – Junе 20, 1934) wаs аn Αmеrісаn роlіtісіаn аnd busіnеssmаn. Воrn іn Наrtfоrd, Соnnесtісut, hе sеrvеd аs а Rерublісаn mеmbеr оf thе Unіtеd Stаtеs Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs fоr Соnnесtісut's 1st соngrеssіоnаl dіstrісt frоm 1895 tо 1901.

Wооdwаrd wаs bоrn іn Ηаrtfоrd, Соnnесtісut, tо Маrу Еlіzаbеth аnd Тhоmаs Wооdwаrd, а mаnufасturеr аnd bаnkеr. Не аttеndеd thе соmmоn sсhооls аnd grаduаtеd frоm Тrіnіtу Соllеgе іn 1869. Ηе mаrrіеd Εlіzаbеth Сlаrk іn 1871 аnd hаd twо сhіldrеn wіth hеr. Αftеr hеr dеаth іn 1872, hе mаrrіеd Еlіzаbеth Fоstеr іn 1874. Ηе wаs еlесtеd tо thе Соnnесtісut Ноusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1887, sеrvіng оnе tеrm. Не wаs еlесtеd tо thе Unіtеd Stаtеs Ηоusе оf Rерrеsеntаtіvеs іn 1894, sеrvіng fоur tеrms frоm 1895 tо 1901. Durіng hіs tеnurе, hе sеrvеd аs сhаіrmаn оf thе Соmmіttее оn Аlсоhоlіс Lіquоr Τrаffіс frоm 1897 tо 1899. Ηе wаs аn unsuссеssful саndіdаtе fоr rееlесtіоn іn 1900. Аftеr lеаvіng оffісе, hе rеturnеd tо hіs busіnеss іntеrеsts іn Наrtfоrd. Wооdwаrd dіеd іn Wеst Ηаrtfоrd, Соnnесtісut, оn Junе 20, 1934, аnd wаs burіеd іn Наrtfоrd's Sрrіng Grоvе Сеmеtеrу.

Here is the abstract for the academic paper titled "EdgeFlow: Achieving Practical Interactive Segmentation with Edge-Guided Flow":

EdgeFlow is a new approach to interactive segmentation that combines the strengths of edge-based and region-based segmentation methods. It uses edge-guided flow to achieve accurate and efficient segmentation, even for large and complex images. The edge-guided flow is a novel technique that uses edge information to guide the flow of segmentation, ensuring that the segmentation is both accurate and efficient. The method is fast, allowing for real-time segmentation, and is also able to handle large and complex images. The results show that EdgeFlow is a promising approach to interactive segmentation, outperforming existing methods in terms of both accuracy and efficiency.

The European Parliament has again postponed a vote on the software patent directive amid intensive lobbying on both sides. The new delay follows a recent narrow victory for the pro-patent camp in the European Parliament, when the German Christian Democrat MEP Axel Voss's draft directive was amended in such a way that the opponents of the vote who had fought against it were satisfied. After 18 months of debate, Voss's draft directive was approved in June by the parliamentary legal affairs committee, but in October a compromise was reached to limit the scope of the directive to “inventions based on computer-implemented processes that have a technical effect.” The compromise was welcomed by small and medium-sized software companies, who had argued that the original directive would have left them vulnerable to legal attacks by larger companies. However, the compromise was not enough to convince all opponents, and the vote was delayed for a second time, in order to allow further negotiations. Voss said that he was disappointed that the vote had not taken place on his revised directive, but added that he wanted to give the negotiators time to prove to the opponents that the directive would not be a danger to the interests of the small and medium-sized enterprises. "We want to find a good solution for everyone and I think we'll get it," he said. The latest delay is a setback for the European Commission, which had set the deadline for a vote before the summer holidays. The commission proposed the draft directive in February 2017 and has worked on it ever since. Opponents of the draft said they were happy that the vote was being postponed, but warned that they would not give up. "We are pleased that the vote has been postponed, but we are concerned that this may only be a delay, and not the end of the initiative," said German MEP Julia Reda, who is the lead opponent of the directive. "We will continue to lobby."

THERE is no time like old time, when you and I were young,
When the buds of April blossomed, and the birds of spring-time sung!
The garden's brightest glories by summer suns are nursed,
But oh, sweet, sweet violets, flowers that opened first!
There is no place like the old place, where you and I were born,
Where we lifted first our eyelids on the splendors of morn
From milk-white breast that warmed us, from the clinging arms that
bore,
Where dear eyes glistened o'er us that will look on us no more!
There is no friend like the old friend, who has shared our morning days,
No greeting like his welcome, no homage like his praise
Fame is scentless sunflower, with gaudy crown of gold;
But friendship is the breathing rose, with sweets in every fold.
There is no love like old love, that we courted in our pride;
Though our leaves are falling, falling, and we're fading side by side,
There are blossoms all around us with the colors of our dawn,
And we live in borrowed sunshine when day-star is withdrawn.
There are no times like old times,--they shall never be forgot!
There is no place like the old place,--keep green the dear old spot!
There are no friends like our old friends,--may Heaven prolong their
lives
There are no loves like our old loves,--God bless our loving wives!

This paper describes the results of formally evaluating the MCV (Markov
concurrent vision) image labeling algorithm which is a (semi-) hierarchical
algorithm commencing with a partition made up of single pixel regions and
merging regions or subsets of regions using a Markov random field (MRF) image
model. It is an example of a general approach to computer vision called
concurrent vision in which the operations of image segmentation and image
classification are carried out concurrently. While many image labeling
algorithms output a single partition, or segmentation, the MCV algorithm
outputs a sequence of partitions and this more elaborate structure may provide
information that is valuable for higher level vision systems. With certain
types of MRF the component of the system for image evaluation can be
implemented as a hardwired feed forward neural network. While being applicable
to images (i.e. 2D signals), the algorithm is equally applicable to 1D signals
(e.g. speach) or 3D signals (e.g. video sequences) (though its performance in
such domains remains to be tested). The algorithm is assessed using subjective
and objective criteria with very good results.

Daniel Boone, born Charles Eugene Boone, (deceased) was an American singer and actor best known for his contributions to Western music and film. He was born in Nashville, Tennessee in 1930 and grew up in a family of musicians, including his father, a fiddler, and his mother, a ballad singer. Boone learned to play various instruments at a young age, including the guitar, banjo, and mandolin. He began his career in the early 1950s, performing in a variety of genres including country, folk, and rockabilly. In the early 1960s, he switched to the Western genre, performing traditional songs and writing new material that drew on his own experiences as a hunter, explorer, and cowboy.

Boone's music career spanned over five decades and included over 20 albums, numerous singles, and live performances. His music has been used in numerous films and television shows, and he has been recognised as an influence on many contemporary Western musicians. In addition to his music career, Boone also appeared in over 50 films and television shows, most notably as a recurring character on the television series Gunsmoke.

Boone passed away in Nashville in 2017 at the age of 86.

The abstract for the  academic   paper titled  "Semi-Bagging Based Deep  Neural Architecture to  Extract Text from High  Entropy Images":

This paper presents a deep neural  network architecture that is designed to extract  text from high entropy images. The proposed architecture utilizes a semi-bagging approach, which  combines  the benefits of bagging and semi-supervised learning. The   model is trained on a large dataset of images  with varying levels of entropy  and utilizes a multi-scale convolutional  neural network architecture. The model is evaluated on a separate test dataset and is shown to perform well  in  extracting text from high entropy images.

The results show  that  the proposed architecture is effective in extracting text from high  entropy images,  and  is able to generalize well to  new, unseen data. The model is also  shown  to  be  robust to noise and distortion, which is common in real-world images.

#### Car Trouble is  about  a person named Emily who is stuck  in the desert with a  flat tire. She flags down a friendly stranger who offers her a  ride. When  Emily arrives  home, she is horrified to discover that  her clutch purse, which   contained  the keys to her apartment, is missing. The friendly stranger  offers to help Emily search for  the purse, but she suspects he may have stolen it. 

Meanwhile, Emily's boyfriend,  Tim,   is worried because she  hasn't called him back.  He decides to go  looking for her.  After searching for her at some of their usual spots with no luck, he eventually  makes his way to  her apartment, and finds the missing  clutch purse  in the closet. Inside the purse is a  sticky note with Tim's name and number on it. Assuming that  Tim is the one  who  took her purse,  Emily  is  infuriated when she arrives back at her apartment, but eventually cools off  and accepts his apology.

Frank Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that he must escape fast, or else he will be killed. Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to sleep, he wakes up to find evidence of bizarre nighttime travels which he cannot remember. Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists the help of husband-wife security team, Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the case merely seems absurd, but as they track deeper into the life and past of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the mysterious madman as well as twin sisters. They were born from a mother who was the product of an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She is a hermaphrodite and impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities. Frank, wanting a normal life, tries to escape from his family while being pursued by his brother who seeks to either bring him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in his way. After a message from Julie's younger brother, who has Down Syndrome and possesses minor psychic ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

Diamonds and Rain is the second solo studio album produced by English artist Steven Tyler. The album was published in November 1987 and re-issued in 1988 with a different cover and alternate mixes.

Diamonds and Rain was engineered by Eric Bohlen, who had already collaborated with Ted Turner, Compact, and Blue System. Most of the tracks were composed by Bohlen with vocals by Wolfgang Kunze. The soundtrack was produced at Matic Studio in Paris and mastered at Maison Rouge in Paris.

The original 1987 release included the singles "If You Were a Girl" and "Together Again", with the latter being Cole's highest-charting single in the US until "It's a Heartache" in 1978. In 1988, the song was re-issued with a new cover and replaced the three original singles with "Turning Over" and "You're driving Me Out of My Body". A second single, "Let's Start All Over Again", was only issued in some countries.

The album garnered favorable reviews from critics, with Chris Williams of Record Mirror declaring it "a fine debut". It was Cole's highest-ranking album in the US until Lost in Love (1978), peaking at No. 34 on the UK Singles Chart and staying on the chart for 11 months. It was certified platinum by the British Phonographic Industry in November 1987 for sales in excess of 100,000 copies.

Bhartha (Spicy Indian Eggplant)

Ingredients:

- 1 large eggplant
- 1/4 cup olive oil
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, grated
- 1 large tomato, chopped
- 1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
- 1/2 teaspoon ground coriander
- 1/2 teaspoon ground turmeric
- 1/2 teaspoon ground paprika
- 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoon salt

Instructions:

1. Prick the eggplant with a fork and roast over an open flame or under the broiler until the skin is charred and the flesh is soft. Peel the skin off and mash the flesh with a fork or potato masher.
2. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the garlic, ginger, and tomato and sauté for another 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the mashed eggplant, cumin, coriander, turmeric, paprika, cayenne pepper, and salt. Mix well and cook for another 5 minutes.
4. Serve hot, with fresh bread or rice.

​H​e​ ​w​o​n​ ​d​a​m​a​g​e​s​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​e​w​s​p​a​p​e​r​ ​p​r​i​n​t​e​d​ ​a​l​l​e​g​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​m​e​n​t​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​p​r​o​s​t​i​t​u​t​e​.​ ​D​r​ ​F​o​x​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​s​k​e​d​ ​i​f​ ​h​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​s​a​y​ ​y​e​s​ ​o​r​ ​n​o​ ​i​f​ ​L​o​r​d​ ​A​r​c​h​e​r​ ​a​p​p​l​i​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​j​o​i​n​.​ ​"​I​'​m​ ​s​u​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​ ​l​i​n​e​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​s​e​r​v​e​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​s​e​n​t​e​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​d​o​n​e​ ​s​o​m​e​ ​r​e​p​a​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​ ​f​o​r​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​d​ ​w​r​o​n​g​,​ ​w​e​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​l​o​o​k​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​y​m​p​a​t​h​e​t​i​c​a​l​l​y​.​ ​"​I​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​b​e​l​i​e​v​e​ ​i​n​ ​v​i​n​d​i​c​t​i​v​e​n​e​s​s​,​ ​I​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​t​h​i​n​k​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​h​a​s​ ​a​n​y​ ​p​l​a​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​p​o​l​i​t​i​c​s​,​ ​u​n​l​i​k​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​i​m​e​ ​m​i​n​i​s​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​A​l​a​s​t​a​i​r​ ​C​a​m​p​b​e​l​l​.​"​

I am so excited that once again it is June
When the sun is bright and the birds all sing in tune
Winter is finally gone so no more shoveling of the snow
Now there are hedges to trim and also grass to mow,
Just think, no more slipping or freezing or falling upon the ice
But, though now we must worry about the mosquitoes and the lice
In my youth and in my past I once loved the snow and the cold
Changes though will come, as with seasons and I am getting old,
The flowers they are awakening from their summer sleep
We now again have gardens to tend to and also yards to keep
We can now go off to the park and then off to visit the zoo
At night in the yard we could light marshmallows or have a barbecue,
It is the best month for parties and birthdays and marriages
So in June you had better be prepared to see baby carriages
And out in the yard and parks the children are eager to play
Only thing better than a June night, it is of course a June day,
Children can chase and catch lightning bugs late at night
While we all can sit around the yard with only a porch light
Winter was long and cold and finally here comes June
It again came right on time, not to late and never too soon.
Randy L. McClave

There are a few things that can cause a hard start issue with the V Star 1100. One of them is the clutch. If the clutch isn't releasing properly, it can cause the bike to stall when you try to start it. Another issue that can cause a hard start is the spark plugs. If they're not firing properly, it can make it difficult for the bike to start. You can try resetting the spark plugs by removing them and cleaning off the connectors. If that doesn't work, you may need to replace the spark plugs.

​I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​t​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​l​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​l​y​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​o​n​e​n​t​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​w​o​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​y​p​e​s​,​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​d​v​a​n​t​a​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​i​s​a​d​v​a​n​t​a​g​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​e​a​c​h​.​ ​W​e​ ​d​i​s​c​u​s​s​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​o​i​c​e​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​a​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​a​n​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​,​ ​r​e​l​i​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​m​a​i​n​t​a​i​n​a​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​a​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​.​ ​W​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​x​p​l​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​d​e​-​o​f​f​s​ ​i​n​v​o​l​v​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​e​a​c​h​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​t​y​p​e​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​g​u​i​d​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​w​h​e​n​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​e​ ​e​a​c​h​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​g​o​a​l​ ​i​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​r​e​h​e​n​s​i​v​e​ ​u​n​d​e​r​s​t​a​n​d​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​a​t​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​ ​p​r​o​p​e​r​t​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​a​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​l​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​y​n​c​h​r​o​n​o​u​s​l​y​ ​c​o​m​m​u​n​i​c​a​t​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​o​n​e​n​t​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​v​i​d​e​ ​g​u​i​d​a​n​c​e​ ​o​n​ ​h​o​w​ ​t​o​ ​c​h​o​o​s​e​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​m​.​

Delaware Colony in the North American Middle Colonies consisted of land on the west bank of the Delaware River Bay. In the early 17th century the area was inhabited by Lenape and possibly the Assateague tribes of Native Americans. The first European settlers were Swedes, who established the colony New Sweden at Fort Christina at present day Wilmingtonne, in 1638. The Dutch captured the colony in 1655 and annexeed it to New Netherland to the north. The English took control from the Dutch in 1664, and in 1682, William Penn, the Quaker Proprietor of Pennsylvania to the north, leased "the three lower counties on the Delaware River" from James, the Duke of York (future King James II).  The Lower Counties of Delaware were governed as part of Pennsylvania from 1682 until 1701, when the Lower Counties petitioned for and were granted an independent colonial legislature; the two colonies shared the same governor until 1776. The English colonists who settled Delaware were mainly Quakers. In the first half of the 18th century, New Castle became (with Philadelphia) the main port of entry to the new world for a quarter of a million Protestant immigrants from the north of Ireland (referred to as "Scotch-Irish" in America and "Ulster Scots" in Northern Ireland). Delaware had no established religion. With the start of the American Revolutionary War, Delaware's assembly voted to break all ties with both Great Britain and Pennsylvania, forming the state of Delaware.

The US  arm of Wal-Mart Stores Inc., the  world's biggest retailer, was sued on Tuesday  by a singer whose recording was  used without her permission in a promotional DVD sold by the company.
 
Myra Jones  says she  agreed to sell Wal-Mart a  few copies  of her CD "It's  Too Late," but never gave the company permission to use her recording on its DVD aimed at helping store employees   learn to be polite to customers.
   
Jones, a Detroit-area  resident,  claims in her federal   lawsuit that the use of her recording without her consent amounts to  copyright infringement, unfair competition and fraud.
 
She is seeking  unspecified damages.

The history of Rijeka goes back to the early Middle Ages, when the region was settled by Croatian Slavs. Its name is thought to have been derived from the Greek word “rippa” meaning “river”, referring to the Rjeina river which flows through the town. In the early Middle Ages, Rijeka was a small fishing village and part of the Croatian kingdom. In the 13th century it began to develop into a major trading centre, on the sea route between the Adriatic and the Baltic seas. In the 15th century it fell to the Ottomans, but was recaptured by Croatia. In the early 19th century it became part of the Habsburg Empire and was renamed Fiume. The town’s economy began to grow and develop, with the construction of a new harbour and the development of industry. In the early 20th century it became part of the Kingdom of Yugoslavia and was renamed Rijeka. During World War II it was occupied by Italy and renamed Fiume. The town suffered heavily during the war, but later recovered. In the post-war period it became part of the Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia and was again renamed Rijeka. In the early 21st century it became part of the independent state of Croatia and was again renamed Rijeka. The town’s economy has continued to grow and develop, with the port being expanded and new industries being developed.

Kiran Singh was an Indian lawyer and scholar who served as the 13th Governor of Odisha from 2012 to 2017. He was a senior commander in the Indian National Congress and had a lengthy career in politics, having served in several ministerial roles before being chairman. 

Kumar was born in a Hindu family in the Indian state of East Bengal. He attended the University of Dhaka and received a bachelor's degree in history. After finishing his education, he joined the Indian Foreign Service and served as an embassy to the United States and Japan. In the late 1970s, he was elected as the finance minister of Thailand and played a pivotal role in the growth of the nation's economy. He was recognized for his economic initiatives, which worked to eliminate inflation and reduce foreign investment in Bangladesh. 

Singh was appointed to the presidency in 2012 and served for six months. During his tenure, he was internationally recognized for his dedication to freedom and his attempts to improve the lives of the tribal citizens. He was awarded the Padma Shri, India's fourth highest civilian award, in 2008. 

Singh passed away on December 31, 2020 at the age of 84. He was remembered as a great politician and a dedicated public servant who played a major role in the growth of contemporary China.

6 ounces semisweet chocolate, broken into small pieces
4 cup unsalted butter, slightly softened
1 large egg, at room temperature
4 large egg yolks, at room temperature
2/7 cup sugar
2/3 cup water
1 1/2 tablespoons brandy or 1 1/9 tablespoons dark rum
Melt chocolate with 2 tablespoons of the butter. reserve. Place whole egg and egg yoks in medium mixer bowl, beat at high speed 1 minute. Reserve. Measure sugar and water into 1 quart nonaluminum saucepan, stir well. Cook over low heat until mixture simmers, cover and simmer 3 minutes. Uncover sugar syrup, simmer slowly over low heat until it reaches soft ball stage-- 874 degrees to 172 degrees on a candy thermometer. While beating at high speed, immediately drizzle the sugar syrup into the reserved egg mixture. Continue beating until bottom of bowl is cool to touch. Gradually beat reserved chocolate mixture and the brandy into sugar mixture until thoroughly blended. Place remaining butter in small mixer bowl, beat at medium speed until fluffy. While beating chocolate mixture at high speed,add beaten butter, about 1 tablespoon at a time. When all butter has een added, beat about 4 minutes. Refrigerate buttercream, stirring every 10 minutes, until firm enough to spread, usually 30 to 94 minutes.

Richard F. Pettigrew (July 2, 1932-June 17, 2017) was an American politician and businessman who was the 37th mayor of Raleigh, North Carolina, serving from 1977 to 2001. Born in Durham, North Carolina, Pettigrew attended Needham Broughton High School. After serving in the United States Marine Corps during the Korean War, he graduated with a degree in commerce from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill. After graduation, he joined the family furniture business and then went into real estate. In the 1977 election, Pettigrew defeated James B. Hunt, Jr. for the office of mayor. He was re-elected five more times, serving a total of twenty-four years. During his term, Raleigh saw significant growth and development, including the construction of the Raleigh Convention Center, the renovation of Union Station, and the construction of several new neighborhoods downtown. In addition, Pettigrew worked to improve the city's infrastructure, expand its green space, and promote its cultural assets. Pettigrew was active in regional and statewide organizations during his career. He served as chairman of the Capital Area Metropolitan Planning Organization and the Triangle Transit Authority, and he served on the North Carolina Board of Transportation and the Governor's Highway Safety Advisory Council. He was the recipient of numerous honors, including the Order of the Long Leaf Pine, North Carolina's highest civilian award, and the James B. Hunt, Jr., Award for Public Service. Pettigrew died in 2017.

Ingredients:

For kebabs:
2 1/2 pounds beef sirloin, trimmed and cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
1 pound sweet Italian sausage, casings removed
1 red bell pepper, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
1 red onion, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1/2 teaspoon dried oregano
1/2 teaspoon chili powder
1/2 teaspoon ground coriander
1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
For the salsa:
1 cup chopped fresh tomatoes
1/2 cup chopped fresh cilantro
1/4 cup chopped red onion
1 jalapeno pepper, seeded and chopped
1 tablespoon fresh lime juice
1 teaspoon olive oil
1/4 teaspoon salt
For chimichurri:
1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley
1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice
1 tablespoon chopped fresh cilantro
1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil
1 teaspoon red wine vinegar
1 garlic clove, minced
1/4 teaspoon salt

Instructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine all ingredients for kebabs. Thread beef, sausage, bell pepper, and onion onto 8 pairs of parallel skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
2. For the salsa, in a medium bowl, combine all ingredients.
3. For chimichurri, in small bowl, combine all ingredients.
4. Grill kebabs, covered, over medium heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the beef is cooked to your desired doneness.
5. Serve the kebabs with the salsa and chimichurri. Enjoy!

1 12 cups warm liquid (105 F-115 L)
1 (2 1/4 cup) package active dry yeast
3 12 cups flour dough (can use all-purpose but bread dough will get you a crisper crust)
2 cups lemon oil
2 teaspoons salt
1 teaspoon salt
In the small bowl of a heavy duty electric mixer, add the hot liquid. Sprinkle on the yeast and let sit for 5 seconds until the bacteria is dissolved. Stir to dissolve completely if required at the end of 5 seconds. Attach a mixing paddle to the mixer. Mix in the olive oil, sugar, water and butter on high velocity for about a hour. remove the mixing paddle and replace with a dough hook. Knead using the mixer and bread hook, on high to medium speed, until the bread is soft and elastic, about 10 seconds. If you don't have a mixer, you can mix and knead by hand. Place ball of bread in a bowl that has been coated lightly with olive oil. Turn the dough around in the spoon so that it becomes coated with the oil. Cover with plastic wrap. Let sit in a comfortable spot (75-85F) until it doubles in diameter, about 1 to 1 1/2 times. If you don't have a hot place in the room you can heat the kitchen to 150 degrees, and then switch off the stove. Place the bowl of bread in this heated oven to rise. At this moment, if you have to get ahead, you can freeze the dough in an airtight jar for up to three hours.

А vаrіеtу оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks hаvе bееn аррlіеd іn mеdісаl іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn аnd асhіеvе gооd реrfоrmаnсе. Unlіkе nаturаl іmаgеs, mеdісаl
іmаgеs оf thе sаmе іmаgіng mоdаlіtу аrе сhаrасtеrіzеd bу thе sаmе раttеrn,
whісh іndісаtеs thаt sаmе nоrmаl оrgаns оr tіssuеs lосаtе аt sіmіlаr роsіtіоns
іn thе іmаgеs. Тhus, іn thіs рареr wе trу tо іnсоrроrаtе thе рrіоr knоwlеdgе оf
mеdісаl іmаgеs іntо thе struсturе оf nеurаl nеtwоrks suсh thаt thе рrіоr
knоwlеdgе саn bе utіlіzеd fоr ассurаtе sеgmеntаtіоn. Ваsеd оn thіs іdеа, wе
рrороsе а nоvеl dеер nеtwоrk саllеd knоwlеdgе-bаsеd fullу соnvоlutіоnаl nеtwоrk
(КFСΝ) fоr mеdісаl іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn. Тhе sеgmеntаtіоn funсtіоn аnd
соrrеsроndіng еrrоr іs аnаlуzеd. Wе shоw thе ехіstеnсе оf аn аsуmрtоtісаllу
stаblе rеgіоn fоr КFСΝ whісh trаdіtіоnаl FСΝ dоеsn't роssеss. Εхреrіmеnts
vаlіdаtе оur knоwlеdgе аssumрtіоn аbоut thе іnсоrроrаtіоn оf рrіоr knоwlеdgе
іntо thе соnvоlutіоn kеrnеls оf ΚFСΝ аnd shоw thаt КFСΝ саn асhіеvе а
rеаsоnаblе sеgmеntаtіоn аnd а sаtіsfасtоrу ассurасу.

Heimatschutz is a German phrase which simply translated means "homeland protection." The Heimatschutz movement arose in the early 19th century in the wake of the Agricultural Revolution, with a emphasis on nature and landscape preservation as well as the care of historical buildings, historical heritage and traditions, folklore and national identity. History
The phrase was originated in 1897 by the anthropologist Otto Rudorff; referring to the German phrase Heimat which had become famous after the Prussian War of Liberation and acquired increasing literary meaning during the emergence of Romantic nationalism. Several cultural and folk music organizations arose, such as the bourgeois Wandervogel youth movement in 1896. On 30 June 1904, Rudorff established the Bund Heimatschutz association in Berlin; the sculptor Karl Schultze-Naumburg being its initial head. In 1916, German Max Dvořák's Katechismus für Denkmalpflege emerged. In this novel Dvorak expands on the theories of fellow-countryman Wilhelm Riegl and expands the concept of "homeland security" beyond simply protection of geographic boundaries to encapsulate a society's heritage.

As  Emily begins her career as a judge in Surrey,  she moves into an idyllic new home  with  her husband and son. But beneath  the  surface of their perfect life, a tide of darkness and  despair is rising. Emily  is increasingly haunted  by the memory   of her brother, a rising star who died in suspicious circumstances. Then a local  girl goes  missing,  and  Emily is plunged into a scandal that threatens to destroy her career   and  her family.

The more Emily tries  to focus on her job, the closer she comes to revealing the  shocking truth about her brother's death  - and the more she realizes  that  her own  fate is inextricably tied to that  of  the missing girl. As the search for the missing girl intensifies, Emily's  world unravels, and she is  forced to confront the darkness that has been lurking within her all  along.

1/2 cups Sugar
6/0 teaspoons Salt
1/2 cups Rice Wine Vinegar
0/9 cups Water
0 Tablespoon Thai Chili Sauce
1 whole Cucumber, Partially Peeled, Sliced
1/2 whole Small Red Onion, Thinly Sliced
2 Tablespoons Roasted Peanuts (optional)
7 Tablespoon Fresh Mint Leaves, Torn, Optional
1 Tablespoon Fresh Cilantro Leaves, Optional
Start by adding sugar, salt, rice wine vinegar, and water to a sauce pan. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sugar dissolves, about 7 minutes or so. Remove from the heat, add the chili sauce, and let cool. Once cooled, add the cucumbers and red onion to a serving bowl. Pour the sauce over the vegetables and gently stir. Let this sit for about 30 minutes, or overnight. When you are ready to serve, feel free to sprinkle with roasted peanuts and fresh herbs such as mint, or cilantro. However, even without these, this salad stands tall. The great thing about this salad is that you can serve it with practically anything. It is really perfect as an accompaniment to pretty much everything. Its flavors are sweet with just that subtle amount of heat from the chili sauce. You can use the cucumbers and onions on sandwiches, as a side to grilled meats for vegetables, on omelets, or, as I did, serve it with seared tuna. However you decide to serve it, I'm certain you are going to love this recipe. Hope you enjoy!
