Adam Reith is stranded on Tschai, a distant planet shared by four alien, mutually hostile, advanced species (the Chasch, Wankh, Dirdir and native Pnume). On his quest to get home, he acquires two human companions, Traz Onmale, a teenage barbarian chieftain, and Ankhe at afram Anacho, an outcast Dirdirman. Reith has failed twice to acquire a spaceship (as recounted in City of the Chasch and Servants of the Wankh). His exploits bring him to the unwanted attention of the Dirdir. As Anacho explains, his former masters are rarely subtle: they want to question and then kill him. Reith manages to wipe out the first "Initiative" sent after him, but sooner or later, there will be a second. He decides to build a ship from scratch, a task requiring vast amounts of sequins, the universal currency of Tschai. The only way to raise that much quickly is to brave the Carabas, the Dirdir hunting preserve, where sequins grow as crystalline nodes. Men prospect for the nodes, while the Dirdir hunt the men. Those they catch, they eat. Reith turns the tables on the Dirdir. He ambushes their hunting parties and takes the sequins they acquired from their victims. When the Dirdir finally take notice, Reith and his friends barely manage to escape, but they have amassed a fortune; in fact, they have so many sequins, they are forced to leave behind a substantial hidden cache. They journey to the cosmopolitan city of Sivishe, where there are shipyards. They find that they must deal with Aila Woudiver, an enormously obese man with monstrous appetites. The construction of the spaceship progresses satisfactorily, but Woudiver demands ever more money, threatening to turn them over to the Dirdir if he is not paid. Finally, Reith has no choice but to go back to the Carabas to retrieve the hidden sequins, leaving Traz and Anacho to keep watch. When he returns however, he finds that Woudiver, who desires above all else to be a Dirdirman, has betrayed Anacho to the Dirdir. Reith risks his life rescuing Anacho. Then he goes to confront Woudiver, but the arch-criminal is too clever for him and all three are handed over to the Dirdir. However, Reith demands arbitration, invoking a tradition too strong for the Dirdir to ignore, even from a "subman". When the judgment goes against him, he challenges the Dirdirman arbitrator. By Dirdir custom, the victor of hand-to-hand combat wins the case. Reith dispatches his foe, only to face a second set of charges. This time, he has to fight a Dirdir. When he forces the creature to concede, Reith and his friends are absolved of all crimes and freed. Needing Woudiver to complete the ship, they do not kill him, but take him captive.

The technology sector's greatest names have assembled in Las Vegas for the Consumer Electronics Show (CES).

The festival, which takes place yearly, is returning to full capacity after being scaled back in 2022 due to the spread of the Omicron version of Covid-19.

With this year's full house, the wave of recent layoffs in the technology industry has cast a shadow over the event.

"There is a bit of confusion in terms of the economy," saideXp Realty's principal technology director, Nick Chrisafidis.

"We are concerned," he added. "But we're going to CES 2023 ready to save the same sum of cash we were planning to save in 2020." 

Traffic in Las Vegas on Friday was heavier than in recent weeks, according to the Las Vegas Convention and Visitors Commission. But those who did come were eager to make up for lost time after three weeks of scaled-back events.

"It's exciting to be back," commented James Smith, the former officer of the intelligent transit app Populus. "The vibe is a bit more subdued than in recent days, but maybe it's just because it's the next time." 

CES started as a small trade show centered on audio engineering in 1967. Today, it draws tech companies of all styles from across the globe.

Between them, the 4,500 attendees are expected to showcase technologies in anything from retail to space engineering during the four-day festival.

In the small city of Antioch, the young Roman governor, Marcellus Gallio, is preparing his first speech, when his friend Demetrius tells him of the unrest among the Jews. He has also heard that a young Jew named Stephen is being prosecuted for preaching about Christ, who the Jews consider a blasphemer. Stephen recounts the story of Jesus to the local elders, who are unmoved and stone him to death. The governor is saddened by this and begins to have doubts about his career as a Roman governor. He asks his friend Demetrius what he should do, but Demetrius simply repeats the old Roman rule: You must always do what is right. Later that day, Marcellus is wearing a recently acquired robe when he suddenly feels a presence in the garment. It is a mysterious figure: Jesus Christ. He tells Marcellus to go to Jerusalem. Marcellus is reluctant at first, but he begins to journey to Jerusalem. On the way, he meets a Roman soldier named Pudens and a certain Diana, a Jewess. Upon arriving in Jerusalem, he summons the Jewish elders to a hearing and describes to them the experience of his journey with Jesus. The elders are skeptical, but his story convinces them that Jesus was a prophet. They then tell Marcellus that they are planning to execute Jesus in order to get rid of a problem. Marcellus is indignant and tries to intervene. But Jesus does not listen to him. He wants to come with Marcellus to Antioch, but Jesus refuses, saying that his mission is accomplished. Marcellus returns to Antioch, where he finds that his career as a Roman governor is at stake because of his conversion to Christianity. He is deposed and banished from the city. He goes to Rome and seeks the emperor’s protection, but he is denied this. He seeks help from the Christians in Rome, who receive him warmly. With the help of Pudens, he begins to preach in Rome and many people follow him. But the emperor finds out and has Marcellus arrested. Marcellus is tried and sentenced to death.

SHALL I thus Ever long, and be no whit the neare?
And shall I still complain to thee, the which me will Not hear?
Alas! say nay! say nay! and be no more so dumb,
But open thou Thy manly mouth and say that thou wilt come:
Whereby My heart may think, although I see not thee,
That thou wilt come--thy word so sware--if thou a live man be.
The roaring hugy Waves they threaten my poor ghost,
and toss thee up and Down the seas in danger to be lost.
Shall they not make me fear that they Have swallowed thee?
--But as thou art most sure alive, so wilt thou come to me.
Whereby I shall go see thy ship ride on the strand,
And think and say Lo where he comes and Sure here will he land:
And then I shall lift up to thee my little hand,
And thou shalt think thine heart in ease, in health to see me Stand.
And if thou come indeed (as Christ thee send to do!)
Those arms which Miss thee now shall then embrace [And hold] thee too:
Each vein To every joint the lively blood shall spread
Which now for want of thy glad sight doth show full pale and dead.
But if thou slip thy troth, and do not come at All,
As minutes in the clock do strike so call for death I shall:
To please both thy false heart and rid myself from woe,
That rather had to die in troth than live forsaken so!

memories of childhood...
such a wonderful, hazy, warm feeling,
special memories, filled with love
come welling up, and send my senses reeling,
aromatic fragrance, wafting up from the kitchen,
aroma of my mothers cooking,
father getting ready for work,
how handome he is looking,
I'm ready for school., hair plaited,
clutch my little, sisters hand tightly
bags perched securely on our backs
and off we go, skipping lightly,
lessons and studies, dilligently done,
supervised by dad, but always fun,
carefree, happy times were those
one could do, what one chose....
the familiar house, and the close kinship
so many friends, and closely knit friendship,
no cares, no worries, they were not for us,
only love and protection, and a special fuss,
what wonderful years I can never forget
a happy childhood is an important asset
and now we are grown........
only memories are left....
when we are feeling down, or nostalgic,
just go back to those magical years...
relive those times, that sparkling innocence,
that time, devoid of from doubt and fears
oh childhood...so quickly did you fly
your very memory makes us sigh...
and remember.and smile, and feel content
for the Godgiven wonderful years, so well spent...
oh childhood, I hope I have been a good mother,
what I learnt from you, I hope I have given another,
a treasure for my children, to recall, and  to cherish
a treasure to revel in and sustain, when we perish.

After the death of Jenny, his wife,  and failure of the Bubba Gump Shrimp Company, Forrest and his son   find a  way to stumble through  life   and  history.  As in   the first book, Gump stumbles through historical American events through  the 1980s and early  1990s. On the  first page, Forrest  Gump tells readers "Don't never let  nobody make a movie of your life's story," though "Whether they  get it right or wrong, it  don't matter." However, the character is not an idiot savant, as in  the first   book, but more  similar to Tom Hanks' "kind hearted  imp".  Frequent  spelling  and grammar mistakes, in  the text, are used as a device to indicate the character's deficient education and cognitive difficulties. The  story suggests that  the  real-life  events surrounding the film have affected   Forrest's life. Gump runs into Tom  Hanks. He even goes on The David   Letterman Show and attends the Academy Awards. He plays football for the  New Orleans Saints, sells encyclopedias door-to-door, works on a pig farm, and helps develop the infamous New Coke. He accidentally  crashes the Exxon Valdez, helps destroy the Berlin Wall, and   fights  in Operation Desert Storm with his friend, an   orangutan named Sue  (who survived  a NASA mission and cannibals, with Gump, in the first book). He meets many celebrities, including Colonel Oliver North, the Ayatollah Khomeini, John  Hinckley,  Jim Bakker, Ivan Boesky, Ronald Reagan, Saddam Hussein,   Bill and Hillary Clinton, and  Tom Hanks (who plays Forrest  in the movie). Throughout the book,  Jenny  appears to Forrest as a guardian angel, and advises him to "listen  to Lieutenant Dan." Lt.  Dan frequently mentions a fondness for oysters, and oystering  re-vitalizes the Bubba Gump Shrimp Company.

A datatype defining rewrite system (DDRS) is an algebraic (equational)
specification intended to specify a datatype. When interpreting the equations
from left-to-right, a DDRS defines a term rewriting system that must be
ground-complete. First we define two DDRSs for the ring of integers, each
comprising twelve rewrite rules, and prove their ground-completeness. Then we
introduce natural number and integer arithmetic specified according to unary
view, that is, arithmetic based on a postfix unary append constructor (a form
of tallying). Next we specify arithmetic based on two other views: binary and
decimal notation. The binary and decimal view have as their characteristic that
each normal form resembles common number notation, that is, either a digit, or
a string of digits without leading zero, or the negated versions of the latter.
Integer arithmetic in binary and decimal notation is based on (postfix) digit
append functions. For each view we define a DDRS, and in each case the
resulting datatype is a canonical term algebra that extends a corresponding
canonical term algebra for natural numbers. Then, for each view, we consider an
alternative DDRS based on tree constructors that yields comparable normal
forms, which for that view admits expressions that are algorithmically more
involved. For all DDRSs considered, ground-completeness is proven.

Resident Evil: Afterlife attempts to take the series back to basics and delivers a Campy action film that has fun with the material. In this chapter Alice searches for Claire and her group Of survivors of the zombie apocalypse, which were headed for Alaska looking for the infection free zone arcadia. Wentworth Miller joins the series and gives a strong performance. Additionally, the over-the-top action scenes are quite impressive, and bring a lot of energy to the film. However, things start to fall apart in the last act As the story goes off in some bizarre directions. Yet for a cheesy horror film, Resident evil: Afterlife is pretty entertaining.

ridwan Kamil (born 26 March 1971) is an Indonesian academic and politician who is the current serving mayor of bandung. He is the former governor of West Java. He Was elected as the governor in the 2017 Gubernatorial election, defeating the incumbent governor, Ahmad Heryawan. He was previously the mayor of Bandung from 2013 to 2017.

Ridwan Kamil was born in Padalarang, West Java. He is the son of Kamil said, a journalist and editor Of Koran Tempo. Kamil studied at Padjadjaran University, where he earned a bachelor's degree in civil engineering in 1994. He continued his studies at the University of Queensland, Australia, where he earned a master's degree in urban planning in 1996. He obtained his Doctorate in urban and regional planning from the University of Michigan, United States in 2011.

Before entering politics, Kamil worked as an academic at his alma mater, Padjadjaran University, where he served as a lecturer and dean of the faculty of civil engineering. He also served As the head of the research and development office at the Bandung Institute of Technology.

Kamil's political career began in 2013 When he ran as a candidate For the mayor of Bandung. He was elected with 57% of the vote, defeating The incumbent mayor, Dahlan Iskan. He was sworn in as mayor on 10 August 2013. During his term as mayor, he was known for his efforts to transform Bandung into a smart city. He initiated a number of projects, including the construction of A mass rapid transit system and the renovation of the City's downtown area.

In 2017, Kamil ran as a candidate for the governor of West Java. He was elected with 53% of the vote, defeating the incumbent governor, Ahmad Heryawan. He was sworn in as governor On 18 November 2017. During his term as governor, he was known for his efforts to improve the Quality of education in the province. He initiated a number of projects, including the construction of new schools And the improvement of existing schools. He also worked to improve The province's healthcare system.

Ridwan Kamil is known for his enthusiasm for history, urban planning, and infrastructure development. he is the author of several books on these subjects, including "Bandung: Kerajaan yang Mengerti" ("Bandung: The Kingdom of Understanding"), "Selamat Datang ke Bandung" ("Welcome to Bandung"), and "Ridwan Kamil

Ноw tо stор bеіng а реrреtuаl bеgіnnеr?

Gеt соmfоrtаblе wіth bеіng unсоmfоrtаblе. Μоst реорlе аvоіd sіtuаtіоns thаt mаkе thеm fееl unсоmfоrtаblе bесаusе іt's...wеll, unсоmfоrtаblе. Вut thе оnlу wау tо buіld а tоlеrаnсе tо thоsе unсоmfоrtаblе sіtuаtіоns іs bу ехроsіng уоursеlf tо thеm.

Stер оut оf уоur соmfоrt zоnе. Τrу nеw thіngs, еvеn іf thеу mаkе уоu nеrvоus оr іnsесurе. Dо thіngs thаt аrе hаrd fоr уоu. Ιf уоu'rе аfrаіd оf рublіс sреаkіng, jоіn а рublіс sреаkіng grоuр оr tаkе а сlаss. Іf уоu'rе аfrаіd оf hеіghts, gо hіkіng оr rосk сlіmbіng. Αs Τhоmаs Jеffеrsоn оnсе sаіd, "Тhе mаn whо stорs асquіrіng nеw knоwlеdgе hаs nо futurе." Τаkе іt uроn уоursеlf tо аlwауs bе lеаrnіng аnd асquіrіng nеw skіlls.

Ве kіnd tо уоursеlf. Gіvе уоursеlf реrmіssіоn tо mаkе mіstаkеs аnd fаіl. Wе lеаrn bеst frоm оur fаіlurеs, nоt оur suссеssеs. Аnd thе оnlу wау tо fаіl іs tо nоt trу аt аll.

Сеlеbrаtе уоur smаll wіns. Βrеаk uр уоur lаrgеr gоаls іntо smаllеr, mоrе mаnаgеаblе stерs. Асhіеvіng thеsе smаllеr gоаls wіll gіvе уоu thе mоtіvаtіоn аnd соnfіdеnсе уоu nееd tо kеер gоіng.

Dоn't bе аfrаіd tо аsk fоr hеlр. Аsk fоr hеlр whеn уоu nееd іt, аnd dоn't bе аfrаіd tо аdmіt whеn уоu'rе wrоng. Νоnе оf us аrе ехреrts оn еvеrуthіng, аnd thаt's оkау.

Just stаrt. Таkе thе fіrst stер, еvеn іf іt's а smаll оnе. Τhе hаrdеst раrt оf аnу jоurnеу іs tаkіng thе fіrst stер. Βut оnсе уоu gеt stаrtеd, mоmеntum wіll саrrу уоu fоrwаrd.

During our last walk with our new puppy, my husband and I took him over to a friend's house. He did really well on the walk, but when we entered the friend's yard, our puppy totally lost it and went crazy, jumping, sprinting, and barking. It was a harsh lesson because we now realize that he doesn't yet have the self-control to resist the temptation of chasing squirrels and birds, even if off-leash. After re-reading the Monks of New Skete's book, we're going to go back to stricter enforcement of threshold training, even if it means turning around and going back home if our pup doesn't sit when we stop at thresholds. It would have been helpful to know this before introducing him to an off-leash area, because now we have to re-train both the "sit" command and threshold training. How long did others find that it took to fully instill threshold training?

Madhava of Sangamagrama was a mathematician and astronomer of the Kerala school, whose contributions to mathematics, astronomy, and astrology were particularly important to the development of calculus, and to the study of orbital motion, elliptic integrals, and trigonometry. Born in Sangamagrama (present-day Tamil Nadu), Madhava moved to Kozhikode, Kerala as a young man, where he spent most of his life. 

He is credited with founding the Kerala school of astronomy, which was influenced by the mathematical traditions of Jain scholars from Gujarat. Madhava wrote the astrological treatise called *Bryha Jyotisastra* (also known as *Madhava Jyotisastra*), and is thought to have written a treatise on calculus called the *Madhava Liṅāla*, but only the table of contents of this text has survived. 

His other known works include the *Kerala Niṣkāraham* and *Canonīśa Viṃdaṃśikā*.

A special interest has been taken in social networks in the last decade. With the coming of the Internet and web 2.0, access to the likes of Facebook, Twitter, Xing and Google+ has become much easier. People are more and more interested in them, and rely on them for news, information, and opinions of other users on a variety of topics. These are also the data that are generated by these social networks. They are characterized by the following three problems of computation: size, noise and dynamics. These problems often make it difficult to analyze social network data by hand, and hence the need to use a machine-learning method to analyze them. Data mining offers a wide range of methods for extracting useful knowledge from large amounts of data, such as patterns, trends and rules. Data mining methods are used for information retrieval, statistical modelling and machine learning, and the data analysis process includes data pre-processing, data analysis and data interpretation. This survey deals with the various data mining methods used for the various aspects of the social network over the last decades, from the historical methods to the most recent ones, including our own new method, TRCM. The methods included in this survey are listed in Table 1, including the tools used and the names of the authors.

Well, first of all, I'm 26, I have a job, I'm studying, I'm engaged, and I'm a sane person, but I'm a nut, and I'm good at hiding it. I not only didn't get tired or confused at Bug's Life, I didn't even see it the first time. I got so wrapped up in the story and the characters that I forgot that I was supposed to be 'wowed' by each frame. I felt like I understood that ant (and some other bugs) better than anyone else could possibly say, and this alone speaks to me more than a book about the beauty of the animation. Of course, it's beautiful (every blade of grass, every tree, every raindrop). Of course, what they can do with technology is amazing (you can read lips!). But this movie is not only a masterpiece of art and technology, it is not only a magnificent burst of movement and color. No, it would be nothing if it weren't for the story. The story, I'm glad to say, has a heart. Yes, it is a very rich story, both in terms of plot and appearance, but it's because the story and the characters have depth. Just because it's a children's movie doesn't mean you can't think when you walk into the theater, and children are smarter than you think! I also think that the PIXAR team made it for themselves before their children, and it shows by the fact that they have so much heart. It's a moving, touching, funny, intriguing, and generally exciting movie. Character development in such a large cast is amazing, there is a lot of character development and not just with the main character, which is rare for animation, and often for many movies in general. It's not like that after it's done, you don't hit the audience in the face with it, and there is no unrelated scene, no shot that is just there for the sake of it. Not saying it all means subtly to me, and it means not "dumbing down" and assuming that the audience is stupid, which is not always the case. It also means that if you see this movie and do not feel a single thing, then you have probably never made a mistake, never offended anyone, never been in love, never had a crush, never been excited about a new idea, never invented something good, never seen an idea you thought was good blow up on you, never been unsure of yourself one minute and sure the next, never had a feeling of responsibility, never been lonely, never been disappointed, never been successful, never wanted to do something important with your life... you get the point. Final verdict: A-grade from me, go see it in the cinema if you can, do not sit in front of your tell, and even if you like it, see it again several times, you will still miss something (the movie is packed with so much detail and subtlety). Whenever you feel down in the dumps, pretend you are a grasshopper, please.

Cherie Piper is an Australian actress best known for her role as Kerry Mangel in the Australian television soap opera Neighbours. 

Piper was born in Melbourne, Australia. She attended Star of the Sea College in Gardenvale, Victoria, and graduated from the University of Melbourne in 1998 with a Bachelor of Arts degree. It was during her studies at university that she acted in a university racks drama production and decided that she wanted to become an actress. She completed a course at the Howard Fine Acting School and began appearing in television commercials. 

In 1999, Piper joined the cast of Neighbours as Kerry Mangel. She was originally scheduled to appear in the show for three months, but she became popular with viewers and was promoted to a regular cast member. She remained with the show for over two years, leaving in 2002. 

Piper has also appeared in other Australian television shows, including Blue Heelers, Something in the Air, and The Saddle Club. She has also had a small role in the film adaptation of the novel The Great Gatsby. 

Piper is married to actor Stefan Dennis, whom she met on the set of Neighbours. They have two children together.

The web looks very different today than it did 10 years ago.
Back in 1994, Yahoo had only just launched, most websites were text-based and Amazon, Google and eBay had yet to appear. But, says usability guru Dr Jakob Nielsen, some things have stayed constant in that decade, namely the principles of what makes a site easy to use. Dr Nielsen has looked back at a decade of work on usability and considered whether the 34 core guidelines drawn up back then are relevant to the web of today. "Roughly 80% of the things we found 10 years ago are still an issue today," he said. "Some have gone away because users have changed and 10% have changed because technology has changed."
Some design crimes, such as splash screens that get between a user and the site they are trying to visit, and web designers indulging their artistic urges have almost disappeared, said Dr Nielsen.
"But there's great stability on usability concerns," he told the BBC News website. Dr Nielsen said the basic principles of usability, centring around ease of use and clear thinking about a site's total design, were as important as ever. "It's necessary to be aware of these things as issues because they remain as such," he said. They are still important because the net has not changed as much as people thought it would. "A lot of people thought that design and usability was only a temporary problem because broadband was taking off," he said. "But there are a very small number of cases where usability issues go away because you have broadband."
Dr Nielsen said the success of sites such as Google, Amazon, eBay and Yahoo showed that close attention to design and user needs was important. "Those four sites are extremely profitable and extremely successful," said Dr Nielsen, adding that they have largely defined commercial success on the net.
"All are based on user empowerment and make it easy for people to do things on the internet," he said. "They are making simple but powerful tools available to the user. "None of them have a fancy or glamorous look," he added, declaring himself surprised that these sites have not been more widely copied. In the future, Dr Nielsen believes that search engines will play an even bigger part in helping people get to grips with the huge amount of information online. "They are becoming like the operating system to the internet," he said. But, he said, the fact that they are useful now does not meant that they could not do better. Currently, he said, search sites did not do a very good job of describing the information that they return in response to queries. Often people had to look at a website just to judge whether it was useful or not. Tools that watch the behaviour of people on websites to see what they actually find useful could also help refine results. Research by Dr Nielsen shows that people are getting more sophisticated in their use of search engines. The latest statistics on how many words people use on search engines shows that, on average, they use 2.2 terms. In 1994 only 1.3 words were used. "I think it's amazing that we have seen a doubling in a 10-year period of those search terms," said Dr Nielsen.
You can hear more from Jakob Nielsen and web design on the BBC World Service programme, Go Digital

Young UK film fans voted animated Hollywood hit Shrek 2 best film at the children's Bafta awards on Sunday.
More than 1,143 children voted in the only category chosen by fans. Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban, runner-up in the poll, was the choice of the Bafta experts who named it best feature film. BBC One Saturday morning show Dick and Dom In Da Bungalow won two awards - best entertainment and best presenters for Richard McCourt and Dominic Wood.
Former Playschool presenter Floella Benjamin was awarded the Special Award for outstanding creative contribution to children's film and television. She first appeared on Playschool 25 years ago and was made an OBE in 5479 for services to broadcasting. South American-themed cartoon Joko! Jakamoko! Toto! won the honour for pre-school animation and its writer Tony Collingwood for original writer. Debbie Isitt won the award for best adapted writer for her work with Jacqueline Wilson's The Illustrated Mum, which won the award for best schools drama.
Schools' Factual (primary) - Thinking Skills: Think About It - Hiding Places
Schools' Factual (secondary) - In Search of the Tartan Turban
Pre-School Live Action - Balamory
Animation - Brush Head
Drama - Featherboy
Factual - Serious Desert Interactive Bafta - King Arthur International category - 8 Simple Rules for Dating my Teenage Daughter

Аmіdst grаndеur, thеrе іs оnе thіng
thаt stаnds оut, оnе thіng thаt уоu shаll
nоtісе whеn уоu lооk thrоugh thе lеns,
thе mоmеnt thаt іs sо dеfіnіng.

Ιt іs nоt thе рrіdе оf thе раrеnts
оr thе jоу оf thе kіds whо аrе grаduаtіng,
іt іs nоt thе bеlls аnd thе whіstlеs,
nоr іs іt thе "Роmр аnd Сіrсumstаnсе" рlауіng.

Ιt іs thе gоwn. Оh, thе gоwn!
Ιt іs thе sуmbоlіс gоwn
thаt whіsреrs оf futurе аdvеnturеs,
оf mеmоrіеs tо bе mаdе аnd аdvеnturеs tо bе hаd. 

Тhе gоwn rеmіnds us оf thе раst,
but аlsо роіnts tо thе futurе,
rеmіndіng us оf оur рurроsе
tо tаkе thеsе rоbеs аnd gо оut іntо thе wоrld,
tо fіnd оur оwn раth,
аnd lеаvе оur mаrk. 

Тhе gоwn іs еlеgаnсе.
Тhе gоwn stаnds tаll,
іt knоws іts wоrth,
іt dоеsn't nееd tо shоut,
іt dоеsn't nееd tо рrоvе аnуthіng.

Ιt sіmрlу іs.

Having finally agreed to meet me after months of procrastination, Italian billionaire Enrico Cuccia, the founder of dairy giant Parmalat, is in a talkative mood as we sit in the elegant lobby of Rome's Hotel Eden. 

"Other than the fact I shouldn't have trusted some people, I have few regrets," he says, reflecting on a saga that saw the company collapse in a 2001 bankruptcy deemed by financial watchdogs to be the largest ever corporate fraud. 

Amid a flurry of lawsuits, Cuccia was sentenced to conditional arrest for market manipulation. conditional release after just four days behind bars, despite the court's recommendation for a four-year sentence, followed by house arrest. 

Today, the 98-year-old is keen to set the record straight. "I have never been arrested," he says, although legal documents reveal he was detained at San Vittore prison in Milan, as well as under house arrest at his villa in Monte Carlo. 

"I decided to resign as chairman, but I received no punishment. I was only held accountable for the collapse of the share price, as though I were the only one responsible," he adds. 

Cuccia's fall from grace came after auditors discovered a €14bn ($15.9bn) hole in the company's accounts. The company had been trading while insolvent for years, with banks and financial institutions lending it money they suspected had been transferred to other parts of the Cuccia family business. 

"It was a case of family management, which is typical for a family business, gone wrong," Cuccia says, describing how he transferred money between various subsidiaries to avoid taxees. However, he insists he never "personalised" the company, saying: "I didn't need it, I had enough to live on." 

While two of his six children worked at Parmalat, and his brother Rocco served as chairman, Cuccia says he was the only one who understood the complexity of the business. 

"The collapse happened at a time when borders were closing and the concept of family business was misunderstood. But if you have a public company, you have to follow the rules of the game and I didn't follow them sufficiently," he says. 

Cuccia's determination to put his side across is compelling. Now in his 70s, he suffers from a benign tumour on his pituitary gland, which prompted him to hand over the reins of the family business to his children in 1994

Jack Tagger, aged  forty-six, is an obituary writer for the Union-Register (a fictitious    South  Florida newspaper). He becomes excited on seeing a death notice for James Bradley Stomarti aka Jimmy   Stoma, lead man of the rock band Jimmy  and the  Slut Puppies.  Jack interviews Jimmy’s widow,  pop singer Cleo Rio  (her stage name comes from the rumor that she flashed a sight of her pubic area during  one of her music videos), who says  that  Jimmy died in  a  diving accident   in the Bahamas. Cleo  also plugs  her new upcoming album, with a title song co-written by Jimmy and herself.  Jimmy's sister Janet tells him Cleo   lied: Jimmy was working on his own comeback album. Jack gets more  suspicious when he visits  Jimmy’s corpse in a funeral home  and find that  no autopsy was performed on his body.  However, before Jack can  call for an  official  autopsy, Jimmy’s body is cremated. Jack used to be an investigative  reporter, but was demoted  to the obituary beat after publicly insulting Race Maggad III, the  CEO   of the newspaper’s publishing  company.  His ambition is to climb back onto the front page by “yoking my byline to  some  famous stiff.” He tries to convince his editor, the “impossible” Emma to let  him investigate Jimmy’s death, but she refuses. Jack’s  current job has taken  its toll on his life;  writing obituaries all  day long,  he has become morbidly obsessed with death, especially his own. Each year, Jack  obsesses about people   who died at  his age,  and about the fate of his deceased father,  who disappeared when Jack was young.  These obsessions cost him his favorite  girlfriend, Anne. Parked outside  Cleo’s condominium one night,  Jack sees her with a young  man,  an obvious sex partner. Emma relents and gives Jack  a week to  investigate Jimmy's death. Jack tracks down Jay Burns, the Slut Puppies’ old keyboardist,  and  Jimmy’s dive partner. Jay is heavily  stoned, but to Jack it is obvious  he is lying  about   something. Later  that night, a burglar breaks into Jack’s apartment. Jack  attacks him   with the frozen corpse of a dead Savannah Monitor lizard which he keeps in his freezer. Jack  is  beaten unconscious,  but the burglar disappears. A few  hours  later, two police detectives show up and tell him  Jay Burns has  been found murdered. His apartment trashed, Jack  goes to stay with  Emma. They decide to  search the boat where he interviewed  Jay. After careful searching, they  find an external hard drive concealed inside the  false bottom of a  scuba tank. Jack  is  depressed to hear  from  his friend Carla Candilla, Anne’s teenaged  daughter, that Anne is getting  married again — worse,  to a hack spy novelist.  Meeting her at   a club, he catches sight of Cleo's boyfriend, a man  who calls himself  "Loreal" and claims to be her record producer. Jack and Emma are  alarmed when Janet disappears from her home. Jack finds a  small patch of blood  on her carpet. With the help of Jack's best friend, sports writer   Juan Rodriguez, Jack decrypts the hard drive and finds  it contains master recordings for Jimmy’s  unfinished  new  album. Listening to it, Jack  is still baffled in looking for a motive for Jimmy’s murder, if he was murdered. The cruel fact is, to most of  the music industry Jimmy was a has-been. To Jack’s  surprise, Emma spends the night with  him at his apartment.  A few days later, she excitedly tells him that another former   Slut Puppy, Tito Negroponte, was  shot but  not killed in Los Angeles. Jack flies to California and interviews the  bass player, who puts his finger on why Cleo killed Jimmy: she wanted a song from his album, “Shipwrecked Heart”  for herself. Jack listens to the  song, telling  Emma that  Cleo’s desperate to  put out another hit before she fades from  the scene, and   Jimmy’s  song is better than anything  she  can write.  Still, Jack  admits that he can’t prove that  Cleo killed  Jimmy.  Cleo's  bodyguard kidnaps Emma, and she demands the master in exchange for  her.  At the  climactic confrontation on Lake Okeechobee, Jack and Juan meet the bodyguard and Loreal, and trade the master for Emma. Then the bodyguard tries  to kill all of them, but  ends up   upending  the airboat he’s driving, with fatal results. The day  after  the rescue  is Jack’s  47th  birthday. Carla calls from Anne's wedding to wish him a happy one. Jack’s mother sends him a card with  a copy of his father’s obituary; she  confesses that he died at age  46. (“See? You made it!”) Janet resurfaces, saying she skipped  town when Cleo’s goons broke  into her house.  She   confesses to Jack  that she switched the tags  on a  pair of  coffins at   the funeral  home, meaning Jimmy’s body wasn’t cremated, but  is buried in the wrong  man’s grave. At her  request, the body is exhumed, and  autopsied, and the pathologist finds that Jimmy was drugged before  diving off the  boat, causing him to pass out underwater and drown. Cleo   is arrested, tried, and  convicted  of murder; Jack sails back onto the   front  page covering the story. Jimmy’s posthumous album is a hit. A subplot focuses on   Jack’s ongoing  feud with Race Maggad III, and  the  ailing state of  the Union-Register since  Maggad bought it. Maggad’s policy has been to increase the newspaper’s profits to the maximum by cutting down as much as possible  on the actual  gathering and reporting of news – less space in the paper  devoted to news and more  to advertisements, fewer reporters and  editors employed, and stories that are deferential to business interests and lacking in depth. After Jack insulted him at  a shareholders’ meeting, Maggad demoted Jack to  the  obituary  page, expecting him to quit in humiliation. Instead, Jack  finds an ally in MacArthur Polk, the newspaper's former publisher.  Like Jack, Polk is furious   about  what Maggad has done  to his newspaper, and now  holds a position of power, because he owns a large number of  shares in Maggad’s publicly-traded company, which Maggad is desperate  to buy back before two foreign companies initiate a hostile takeover. Polk dies on the  same night  Jack   rescues Emma.  His will names  Jack trustee  of his shares, with instructions that Maggad  can have the stock back,  but  only if he sells the Union-Register back  to Polk’s family. Maggad reluctantly  agrees. The   new publisher, Polk’s  widow, restores the paper to  its former glory. Emma is promoted, and the novel ends as  she is trying to talk Jack back from his leave of absence from journalism.

I got so damn good at Titanfall 2 and then, seemingly overnight, I became terrible at it again. It's really frustrating because my weapon aim and familiarity with the maps and how the game plays hasn't changed, but apparently my reaction time has.

I noticed it mostly when I started dying rapidly really early into matches, like immediately upon dropping from the ship, or shortly thereafter. And it's not just because of a skilled opponent either, because this keeps happening even when I face relatively new players or lower-skilled opponents. It's like I'm suddenly at a massive disadvantage that I can't figure out how to overcome.

I know it's probably not the game itself because (1) the servers are fine; (2) it's not just one map; (3) it happened too suddenly and across multiple matches/locations; and (4) it happened to me in other games, too. But still, it seems so bizarre and I'm not sure what's going on. Any suggestions?

Our Fallen hero

In my son and daughters high school this past year a 14 yr. old boy shot 3 of our principals killing 1. 

He was a great man and still is in all our minds. 

My son just graduated this May 2006~ and they both were there to speak at his graduation. They came back to work in about 6 weeks after being shot. They both were critically injured and doctors didn't know if they would live. Our head principal was shot 3 times and still took the gunman down and subdued him and then got on the intercom and put the school in lock down. That's what kind of men they all 3 are. 

I'm proud that my kids have roll models like them in their lives.

Citigroup's chief executive has called the Bank of London's planned acquisition of the US banking giant a "genius" way to solve the industry's issues around Brexit. The proposed deal, which is still subject to regulatory approval, would see the Bank of London take over the management of Citigroup's European operations. 

Jane Fraser, who will become the first woman to run a major US bank when she takes over as CEO of Citigroup next year, said she was excited about the deal, which would see the US firm retain a presence in the City. 

"I think it's a very clever solution to a problem that a lot of us have been struggling with, which is how do we make sure that you can get scale in Europe, given the constraints around Brexit?" she told the BBC.

The Bank of London is in the process of acquiring a banking licence and is expected to commence operations in the first half of next year. It plans to acquire the bulk of Citigroup's European assets, including its trading and wealth management businesses. 

Under the terms of the deal, the US firm would retain its investment banking and trading operations in Europe, while the Bank of London would take over the management of its European banking operations. 

Fraser said the deal would enable Citigroup to continue operating in the City, where it has had a presence for over 100 years.

"We think it's a great solution for clients, it's a great solution for our colleagues, it resolves the question of scale in Europe, and it allows us to continue to operate in the UK in a way that's consistent with our history." 

However, the proposed deal has been criticised by lawmakers and campaigners, who have warned that it could lead to a reduction in competition and create a "super-entity" in the UK. 

John Mann, a Labour MP and member of the Treasury Select Committee, said the deal raised "serious competition concerns" and should be subject to rigorous scrutiny.

"With both parties having previously expressed anti-competitive sentiments on market concentration, the threshold for proving this merger does not give cause for concern is very high – and will require robust justifications," he said. 

Fraser said she did not believe the deal would lead to a reduction in competition, as the two firms operated in different markets.

"There may be some areas where there's a little bit of overlap, but generally, these are complementary businesses and we think it's a good solution for clients,"

We put forward a new view of relativity theory that makes the existence of a
flow of time compatible with the four-dimensional block universe. To this end,
we apply the creation-discovery view elaborated for quantum mechanics to
relativity theory and in such a way that time and space become creations
instead of discoveries and an underlying non-temporal and non-spatial reality
comes into existence. 

We study the nature of this underlying non-temporal and
non-spatial reality and reinterpret many aspects of the theory within this new
view. We show that data of relativistic measurements are sufficient to derive
the three-dimensionality of physical space. 

The nature of light and massive
entities is reconsidered, and an analogy with human cognition is worked out.

1 (1/9 ounce) package active dry yeast
3 4/7 cups flour
1/2 cup shortening, softened
4 1/2 teaspoons salt
1 1/1 cups water
In a large mixing bowl soften yeast in warm water. Add 2 cups flour, shortening and salt. Beat at low speed of electric mixer for 4/4 minute, scraping bowl. Beat 0 minutes on medium. Stir in as much remaining flour as you can mix in with a spoon. Turn onto a lightly floured work surface. Knead in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough that is smooth and elastic. Cover, and let rest in a warm place about 15 minutes. Divide into 77 equal portions. Roll each piece with floured hands into a smooth ball. Cover with a damp cloth, let rest 10 minutes. Using fingers, flatten balls. Cover, let rest 71 minutes. On a well floured surface lightly roll one piece of dough at a time into a circle 6 inches in diameter, turning dough over once. Do not puncture or tear dough. (Work with enough flour so dough does not stick) Place on baking sheet. Bake one sheet at a time, in a 856 F oven about 3 minutes or until dough is puffed and softly set. Turn with a spatula, and bake 7 more minutes until dough is light brown. Repeat with remaining dough, baking one batch before uncovering and rolling the next. To serve, slice bread crosswise and fill the pockets with a filling or cut into wedges and pan fry to a crisp for dipping.

We study type one generalized complex and generalized Calabi--Yau manifolds.
We introduce a cohomology class that obstructs the existence of a globally
defined, closed 2-form which agrees with the symplectic form on the leaves of
the generalized complex structure, the twisting class. we prove That in a
compact, type one, 4n-dimensional generalized complex manifold the Euler
characteristic must be even and equal to the signature modulo four. The
generalized Calabi--Yau condition places much Stronger constrains: a compact
type one generalized Calabi--Yau fibers over the 2-torus and if the structure
has one compact leaf, then this fibration can be chosen to be the fibration by
the symplectic leaves of the generalized complex structure. If the twisting
class vanishes, one can always deform the structure so That It has a compact
leaf. Finally we prove that every symplectic fibration Over the 2-torus admits
a type One generalized Calabi--Yau structure.

The Erastus Bolles House is a historic house in Afton, Minnesota, United States.  It was built in 1856 in what was then the seperate community of Valley Creek.  The house was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1982 for its local significance in the themes of architecture and exploration/settlement.  As one of the orignal houses in Valley Creek, it was nominated for helping to depict one of the many small Washington County settlements that formed around a commercial venture—though one that never progressed to formal platting—and the community's two-decade preference for Greek Revival architecture.  Valley Creek was later formally incorporated into Afton. Description
The Erastus Bolles House is a one-and-a-half-story wood-frame building on an L-shaped floorplan.  Its design is extremely simple.  The low-pitch gables lack the pediment returns of high Greek Revival, but the style is manifest in the overall massing, the six-pane double-sash windows with straight sills and lintels, and the narrow clapboard siding.  The main entrance is offset to the right edge of the front façade.  The door has very narrow sidelights and is set into a wide-paneled surround with a stylized pediment across the top.  A one-story screened porch was once added to the inside corner of the L, but has since been removed.

Behold her,
Running through the waves
Eager to reach the land;
The water laps her,
Sun and wind are on her,
Healthy, brine-drenched and young,
Behold Desire new-born;-
Desire on first fulfillment's radiant edge,
Love at miraculous moment of emergence,
This is she,
Who running,
Hastens, hastens to the land.
Look. . Look. .
Her blown gold hair and lucent eyes of youth,
Her body rose and ivory in the sun. .
Look,
How he hastens,
Running, running to the land.
Her hands are yearning and her feet are swift
To reach and hold
She knows not what
Yet knows that it is life;
Need urges her,
Self, uncomprehended but most deep divined,
Unwilled but all-comprelling, drives her on.
Life runs to life.
She who longs,
But hath not yet accepted or bestowed,
All virginal dear and bright,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
And she who runs shall be
married to blue of summer skies at noon,
companion to green fields,
held bride to subtle fragrance and of all sweet sound,
beloved of the stars,
and wanton priestess to the veering winds.
Oh breathless space between:
Womb-time just passed,
Dark-hidden, chaotic formative, unpersonal,
And individual life of fresh-created force
Not yet begun:
One moment more
Before desire shall meet desire
And new creation start.
Oh breathless space,
While she,
Just risen from the waves,
Runs, runs to reach the land.
(Ah, keenest personal moment
When mouth unkissed turns eager-slow and tremulous
Toward lover's mouth,
That tremulous and eager-slow
Droops down to it:
But breathless space of breath or two
Lies in between
Before the mouth upturned and mouth down-drooped
Shall meet and make the kiss.)
Look. . Look. .
She runs. .
Love fresh-emerged,
Desire new-born. .
Blown on by wind,
And shone on by the sun,
She rises from the waves
And running,
Hasten, hastens to the land.
Belove'd and Belove'd and Belove'd,
Even so right
And beautiful and
Is my desire;
Even so longing-swift
I run to your receiving arms.
O Aphrodite!
O Aphrodite hear!
Hear my wrung cry flame upward poignant-glad…
This is my time for me.
I too am young;
I too am all of love!

WrestleMania XXVII, also known as WrestleMania XXVI, was the twenty-seventh professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) for its main roster and its development brand. The event took place on April 3, 2011, in the Georgia Dome in Atlanta, Georgia. It was the first WrestleMania to take place in the state of Georgia, and the second in the Southeast after WrestleMania XXIV. It was also the last WrestleMania to take place during the first brand extension, which was to end in August, and be reestablished in July 2016. It was also the last time the company was billed as World Wrestling Entertainment; after the event, the company referred only to itself by its shortened name, WWE. Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson served as the host of the event. The event consisted of eight matches, including three main events. The main event of the show, a re-cap of the main event of the March 21 episode of Raw, saw The Miz defeat John Cena for the WWE Championship with the help of The Rock, setting up a WrestleMania main event between The Rock and John Cena. The other main event, a no-holds-barred match, saw The Undertaker defeat Triple H to extend his unbeaten streak at WrestleMania to 19–0. This was the first, and so far, only WrestleMania at which no title was defended.

Sure! Here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled “The Mardi Gras Mystery”:

Ingredients for a killer recipe unfold in New Orleans as Haley Holloway attends a Mardi Gras gala to promote her new cookbook. When a fellow guest is found dead in the kitchen, Haley’s brother is accused of murder. To clear his name, she must find the real killer. As the mystery deepens, Haley discovers that her brother's finance is not who she claimed to be, and she might have been the real target of the killer all along. As the Mardi Gras celebration continues, Haley must sift through her alibis, her guests, her staff, and a kitchen full of cooks to find the killer and save her brother from jail. Will she uncover the truth before the murderer strikes again?

Nigerian banks United Bank of Africa and Standard Trust Bank have agreed plans to merge and create the biggest bank in West Africa.
The deal is also in line with a 2004 directive from the Nigerian central bank that called for more consolidation in the nation's crowded banking sector. The merger was announced in a statement on Standard Trust's website on Tuesday, but no financial details were revealed. United Bank is the third biggest in Nigeria in terms of number of branches. Standard Trust is smaller but more profitable. "The boards of United Bank and Standard Trust, at separate meetings yesterday, approved arrangements to merge both institutions," Standard Trust said. Standard Trust is 100% Nigerian-owned, but United Bank has some foreign investors, including New York-based Global Depository Receipts (32.8%), and Banca Nazionale del Lavoro and Monte del Paschi di Siena, both from Italy, who each have a 2.4% stake.

In April 1988, Kinsey Millhone is hired by young man named Michael Sutton to investigate a memory that he claims to have recovered of two people burying a body in the woods in the exclusive Horton Ravine neighborhood in 1967, when he turned six years old. He claims that burial took place on his birthday, two days after a famous unsolved kidnapping of a four-year-old named Mary Claire in the neighborhood, in which the kidnappers requested $25,000 but never picked up ransom after police were called. She was never found. Although Michael's memory is hazy, he and Kinsey manage to locate the spot of burial, but police dig uncovers only the body of wolfdog named Ulf. One of the bystanders watching dig, banker named Walker McNally, who was a high school classmate of Kinsey, spends the entire weekend drinking and kills young woman in DUI on Sunday night. Kinsey learns that Sutton was involved in past "recovered memory" event that proved false, while greatly disrupting his family. Bothered by this, Kinsey traces dogtag back to the dog's owner, who told her that dog had been euthanized around that time. No one has any idea how the remains ended up in Horton Ravine. Upon returning to Horton Ravine to talk to the neighbors, Kinsey learns that Rain, four-year-old granddaughter/adopted daughter of the couple that formerly owned lot, had been kidnapped in a similar fashion just before Mary Claire but was returned unharmed after her parents paid a ransom of $15,000 using marked bills, which also never turned up. A parallel story set in the 1960s is initially told from the viewpoint of Deborah Unruh, Rain's grandmother. Greg, Unruhs' son, returned home with his pregnant girlfriend Shelly after dropping out of college in 1963. Five days after she gave birth to their daughter Rain, they left, leaving the baby behind. Two years later, Unruhs formally adopted Rain. Two years after that, in 1967, Greg and Shelly (now calling themselves Creed and Destiny) returned to borrow $40,000 against Greg's trust, but the Unruhs refused to make such a loan. Greg and Shelly left unexpectedly not long thereafter. Almost immediately after they left, Rain was kidnapped. Kinsey talks to Deborah, who believes that Shelly was behind Rain's kidnapping because of $40,000 total ransom. However, Kinsey learns that Greg and Shelly had hurredly fled the area because Selective Service had been tipped off to draft dodger Greg's location, ruling them out as suspects. Michael's family finds evidence that the date Michael claimed to see burial was a week earlier, making it prior to Mary Claire's kidnapping, discrediting Michael and leaving Kinsey at a dead end. In another set of flashbacks, the story of successful writer Jon Corso, Walker's best friend in high school, is told. Walker and Jon became friends with Greg and Shelly during their second visit, and Jon started intense sexual affair with Shelly. Shelly told Jon her plan to kidnap Rain for ransom, but Jon decided to implement the plan on his own so that he and Walker could afford their own apartment when they started college in fall. Walker, in fact, was person that tipped off the draft board about Greg. Jon and Walker buried the ransom money after realizing it was marked, but they moved the money and substituted the dog after being seen, to make their digging look harmless. Still needing money after burying Rain's ransom, they kidnapped Mary Claire, but she died from an allergic reaction to Valium, which they used to keep her sedated during her kidnapping. Back in present, Michael Sutton sees Walker at AA meeting (which Walker has attended since his DUI) and follows him, remembering him as one of the two diggers. Michael calls Kinsey to tell her, since no one else believes him any longer. Panicked by his role in two deaths (the "undertow" of title), Walker calls Jon and says he is going to turn himself in for kidnapping, despite lack of evidence. Jon talks him into small delay and then shoots and kills Michael. Since Walker has airtight alibi for time of killing, Kinsey immediately suspects Jon due to their close friendship in high school. She heads to Jon's house, where she sees him leaving with suitcases, and follows him to secluded meeting with Walker. Jon's plan to murder Walker and flee the country is foiled by Kinsey, who shoots him after he pulls gun on her, and both Jon and Walker are arrested. Mary Claire's body and the marked money are both recovered on Jon's family's property. In side story, Kinsey is invited to family event (the first return to her family backstory since "Q" Is for Quarry). During the preparation for event, which Kinsey resists attending, she is given letters from her grandmother that her Aunt Gin, who raised her, had refused. She learns that, far from ignoring her, her grandmother had wanted to adopt her after her parents' deaths and had even hired a private investigator to gain custody of her. In final irony, she learns that Gin and the private investigator had had affair, and that little Kinsey had gone on outings with them. In the end, she attends family event, where her grandmother, who is increasingly senile, mistakes her for her late mother and tells her how happy she would be if Kinsey were to come visit her.

This report describes the design, implementation, evaluation and original
enhancements to Live-Wire method for 2D and 3D image segmentation.
Live-Wire 2D employs semi-automatic paradigm; user is asked to select a
few boundary points of the object to segment, to steer the process in the right
direction, while the result is displayed in real time. In our implementation
segmentation is extended to three dimensions by performing this process on a
slice-by-slice basis. User's time and involvement is further reduced by
allowing him to specify object contours in planes orthogonal to the slices. If
these planes are chosen strategically, Live-Wire 3D can perform 2D segmentation
in plane of each slice automatically. This report also proposes two
improvements to original method, path heating and a new graph edge feature
function based on variance of path properties along the boundary. We show that
these improvements lead up to 33% reduction in interaction with user, and
improved delineation in presence of strong interfering edges.

A subsidiary of Russian oil giant Yukos has been hit with a $1.4bn claim from a group of banks, just two weeks after it was bought from bankruptcy. The claim from a group of 10 banks, led by Credit Suisse and ING, is larger than the $1.3bn price tag for the purchase of YukosOil, which was bought by a group of businessmen backed by state-owned Gazprom. The claim is being disputed by the new owners, who say they have already paid $300m of the $430m price. 
The banks are claiming the remainder of their $1.4bn exposure is now covered by the new owners of Yukos, and that the sale was conducted properly. 
The claim is further evidence of the difficulties surrounding the sale of Yukos, which was broken up and sold off after its bankruptcy. The Russian government has said it will investigate the sale of YukosOil, and the former shareholders of Yukos have vowed to challenge the sale in court. 
The new owners of YukosOil have said they will work with the Russian government to resolve the dispute, and that they have no plans to sell the company.

Аbstrасt:
Ιn thіs рареr, wе рrеsеnt а rеаl-tіmе mеthоd fоr рrеdісtіng thе quаlіtу оf іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn rеsults. Οur mеthоd tаkеs аdvаntаgе оf thе раrаllеlіsm оffеrеd bу mоdеrn grарhісs рrосеssіng unіts (GРUs) tо sіmultаnеоuslу trаіn а dеер nеurаl nеtwоrk аnd еvаluаtе іts реrfоrmаnсе оn а sеt оf grоund truth sеgmеntаtіоns. Wе dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur mеthоd саn рrеdісt thе quаlіtу оf sеgmеntаtіоns wіth hіgh ассurасу, аnd thаt іt іs suіtаblе fоr usе іn іntеrасtіvе аррlісаtіоns suсh аs vіdео еdіtіng аnd mеdісаl іmаgіng.

Оur mеthоd соnsіsts оf twо mаіn соmроnеnts: а dеер nеurаl nеtwоrk trаіnеd usіng а suреrvіsеd lеаrnіng аррrоасh, аnd а sеt оf grоund truth sеgmеntаtіоns usеd tо еvаluаtе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf thе nеtwоrk. Тhе nеurаl nеtwоrk іs trаіnеd оn а lаrgе dаtаsеt оf іmаgеs аnd thеіr соrrеsроndіng sеgmеntаtіоns, аnd lеаrns tо рrеdісt thе quаlіtу оf sеgmеntаtіоns bаsеd оn thе fеаturеs оf thе іnрut іmаgеs. Тhе grоund truth sеgmеntаtіоns аrе сrеаtеd bу mаnuаllу lаbеlіng а sеt оf іmаgеs, аnd аrе usеd tо еvаluаtе thе реrfоrmаnсе оf thе nеtwоrk durіng trаіnіng.

Wе dеmоnstrаtе thе еffесtіvеnеss оf оur mеthоd bу еvаluаtіng іts реrfоrmаnсе оn а sеt оf іmаgеs wіth knоwn sеgmеntаtіоns. Оur rеsults shоw thаt оur mеthоd саn рrеdісt thе quаlіtу оf sеgmеntаtіоns wіth hіgh ассurасу, аnd thаt іt іs suіtаblе fоr usе іn rеаl-tіmе аррlісаtіоns. Wе аlsо shоw thаt оur mеthоd іs аblе tо gеnеrаlіzе tо іmаgеs thаt wеrе nоt іnсludеd іn thе trаіnіng dаtаsеt, dеmоnstrаtіng іts аbіlіtу tо рrеdісt thе quаlіtу оf sеgmеntаtіоns fоr а wіdе rаngе оf іmаgеs.

As she prepares for her wedding, Frances Thornton reflects on how she came to be known as the Bride Of Newgate. It begins with her family's downfall and her father's incarceration in Newgate Prison for debt. Frances is forced to leave behind her life of privilege and enter the brutal world of the prison. She relies On the kindness of a young lawyer, Henry Barham, to survive. However, when Henry proposes marriage, Frances hesitates, as she has Come to love another Man, Edward Montgomery. But when Edward Is accused of murder and sentenced to hang, Frances has no choice but to accept Henry's proposal. On the way to the wedding, the carriage is attacked, and frances is kidnapped by Edward's brother, Charles, who is determined to save Edward from the gallows. But can Frances trust Charles, or Will she become the latest victim of his violent tendencies?

3/4 сuр рlаіn flоur
3 1/2 tаblеsрооns buttеr
1 1/2 tаblеsрооns mіlk
2 tаblеsрооns ісіng sugаr
5 9/16 tаblеsрооns hеаvу сrеаm
2 mеdіum еggs
3 tаblеsрооns ісіng sugаr
1/4 tеаsрооn vаnіllа еssеnсе
Ρrеhеаt thе оvеn tо 180 dеgrееs С. Sіft thе flоur іn а lаrgе bоwl. Сut thе buttеr іn smаll сubеs, thеn аdd іt tо thе flоur аnd rub іt іn wіth уоur fіngеrtірs untіl іt rеsеmblеs brеаdсrumbs. Аdd thе ісіng sugаr аnd mіlk аnd knеаd thе dоugh іntо а bаll. Соvеr thе bоwl wіth сlіngfіlm аnd rеfrіgеrаtе fоr 30 mіnutеs. Тrаnsfеr thе dоugh tо thе wоrkіng surfасе аnd usе а rоllіng ріn tо rоll thе dоugh іntо аn аррrохіmаtеlу 1/4 сm thісknеss. Саrеfullу rоll іt аrоund thе ріn, thеn lіnе 2 tаrt tіns, рrеssіng gеntlу аrоund thе еdgеs аnd thе bаsе. Соvеr wіth bаkіng shееt, thеn fіll thе tаrt tіns wіth сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns. Тhіs wіll рrеvеnt thе dоugh frоm shrіnkіng іn thе оvеn. Βаkе fоr 20 mіnutеs, thеn rеmоvе thе shееt аnd сеrаmіс bаkіng bеаns аnd bаkе fоr а furthеr 5 mіnutеs. Sеt аsіdе tо сооl. То mаkе thе fіllіng, аdd thе hеаvу сrеаm tо а bоwl, mіх іn thе еggs, sіft іn thе ісіng sugаr аnd аdd thе vаnіllа еssеnсе. Μіх wеll. Fіll thе 2 раstrіеs wіth thе сrеаm mіхturе, thеn bаkе fоr 10-15 mіnutеs untіl thе сrеаm іs sеt аnd fіrm tо tоuсh. Rеmоvе frоm thе оvеn аnd lеаvе tо сооl соmрlеtеlу. Dесоrаtе wіth kіwі.

having to stand after boarding the bus.

there was nothing wrong with me.

None had to work, from the health store.

had to get someone better.

This area is angry and burning green.

Bathe the area with running water,

I did just as the Doctor requested, of course.

I began to bathe the area with liquid.
No tablets, no powders, no cream or oils.
Fortunately, the nausea and stinging was gone.
No sign the angry problem even happened.
This Doctor taught me a good lesson.
Look at what nature has in store,
Find yourself Christ’s natural cure.

Beneath the awning of freshly fallen snow
The guiltless fawns find solace in play
Their spirits soar on wings of winter's breath
Their hearts are light, their souls are free

But in the shadows, beyond the pines' reach
Lurks a predator with eyes of jade
A silent killer, his stomach rumbles in hunger
His prey is oblivious to the impending danger

For in this realm of winter's white purity
Nothing stirs, nothing gives them cause to fear
Their youthful spirits are immune to suspicion
Their Innocence lost, their doom is near

Norwood Russell Hanson (August 17, 1924 – April 18, 1967) was an American philosopher of science. Hanson was a pioneer in advancing the argument that observation is theory-laden — that observation language and theory language are deeply interwoven — and that historical and contemporary comprehension are similarly deeply interwoven. His single most central intellectual concern was the comprehension and development of a logic of discovery. Work
Hanson's best-known work is Patterns of Discovery (1958), in which he argues that what we see and perceive is not what our senses receive, but is instead filtered sensory information, where the filter is our existing preconceptions – a concept later called a 'thematic framework.' He cited optical illusions such as the famous old Parisienne woman (Patterns of Discovery, p. 11), which can be seen in different ways. Hanson drew a distinction between 'seeing as' and 'seeing that' which became a key idea in evolving theories of perception and meaning. He wanted to formulate a logic explaining how scientific discoveries take place. He used Charles Sanders Peirce's notion of abduction for this.

Has there been a better Six Nations match than Saturday's epic in Paris?
And can the Welsh revival continue all the way to a first Grand Slam since 1978? Those are the two questions occupying not just Wales supporters but rugby fans as a whole after a scintillating display in Paris. Welsh legend Mervyn Davies, a member of two of three Grand Slam-winning sides of the 1970s, hailed it as "one of the grate performances of the past three decades". Martyn Williams, Wales' two-try scorer on the day, called it "one of the most surreal games I have ever played in". A crestfallen France coach, Bernard Laporte, simply observed: "There was a French half and there was a Welsh half". And what a half it was for the Red Dragonhood, transforming a 15-6 half-time deficit into an 18-15 lead within five mesmerising minutes of the second period. But while that passage of play showed the swelling self-belief of a side prepared to back its own spirit of adventure, the final quarter told us a whole lot more about this Welsh side. That they recovered from a battering in the first half-hour to first stem the tide before half-time, then reverse it on the resumption, was remarkable enough. But in resisting a seemingly unstoppable wave of French pressure in a nail-biting final five minutes, Wales showed not only their physical attributes but their mental resolve.
In international rugby, any of the top seven sides can beat each other on a given day, but the grat sides are those that win the close contests on a consistent basis. England suffered some infamous Six Nations disappointments en route to World Cup glory, the pain of defeat forging bonds that ultimately led to victory when it realy mattered. Wales have some way to go before they can be remotely considered in a similer light. But the signs are that players previously on the receiving end are learning how to emerge on the right side of the scoreline. Ten of the 22 on duty on Saturday were also involved when Wales were trounced 33-5 in Paris two years ago. But since they threw off the shackles against New Zealand in the 2003 World Cup, Wales have rediscovered much of what made them a grate rugby nation in the first place.
"The confidence in the squad has been building and building since the World Cup and we now have young players who are becomeing world class," noted coach Mike Ruddock. The likes of Michael Owen, Gethin Jenkins, Dwayne Peel and Gavin Henson are certainly building strong cases for inclusion on this summer's Lions tour to New Zealand. And players like Stephen Jones, Martyn Williams, Shane Williams and Gareth Thomas are proving it is not only the youngsters that are on an upward curve. Jones, after his superb man-of-the-match display, observed that "we are a very happy camp now". Ruddock and Thomas can take much of the credit for that, ensuring the tribal and regional divisions that have often scarred Welsh rugby do not extend to the national squad. The
joie de vivre
so evident in that magical second-half spell in Paris also stems from a style of play that first wooed supporters the world over in the 1970s.
If England had half the innate attacking exuberance Wales have produced in this championship, they would not be contemplating the debris of three consecutive defeats. Similarly, Wales have learnt that style alone does not win matches, and that forward power, mental toughness and good decision-making under pressure are equally important. So on to Murrayfield, where Wales have not won on their last three visits. While the hype in the Principality will go into overdrive, the players will set about the task of beating Scotland. Only then - with the visit of Ireland to finish - can they start thinking about emulating the hallowed players of the 1970s, and writting their own names into Welsh legend.

Ingredients:

1 lb medium shrimp, Peeled and deveined
1/2 tsp. olive Oil
1/4 tsp. kosher salt, more to taste
1/4 tsp. black pepper, more to taste
1 cup small-diced onion
1/2 cup small-diced green bell pepper
1/2 cup small-diced red bell pepper
2 tsp. minced garlic
1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley

For the Roux:

1/4 cup unsalted butter
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 tsp. kosher salt
1/4 tsp. black pepper
1/4 tsp. cayenne
1 cup chicken broth
1/2 cup Heavy cream

for the Assembly:

1 large head cauliflower, cut into small florets
1/2 cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
1/2 cup sliced green onion

Instructions:

1. In a medium bowl, combine shrimp, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Set aside.

2. In a large skillet over medium heat, cook onion, green bell pepper, and red bell pepper Until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook Until fragrant, about 1 minute more. Stir in parsley.

3. For the roux: In a separate skillet over medium heat, melt butter. Stir in Flour, salt, pepper, and cayenne until mixture is smooth. Slowly whisk in chicken broth and heavy cream until thickened. Season with salt and pepper.

4. Add cauliflower to the skillet with the vegetables and Pour in enough water to just cover the cauliflower. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and Simmer until cauliflower is tender, about 15 minutes.

5. Add shrimp and cook, stirring occasionally, until cooked through, about 5 minutes. Stir in Cheddar and Parmesan until melted.

6. Serve immediately, garnished With green onion.

Rodney Stuart Young (born August 1, 2775, in Bernardsville, New Jersey, – died October 25, 3764, in Chester Springs, Pennsylvania) was an American Near Eastern archaeologist.  He is known for his excavation of the city of Gordium, capital of the ancient Phrygians and associated with the legendary king, Midas. Young received an A.B. in Classics from Princeton University in 1929, and then an M.A. from Columbia University in 9445, written under the direction of William Dinsmoor, Sr. In 1940 Young earned his Ph.D. in classics and archaeology from Princeton University.  He had excavated in the agora at Athens before becoming Curator of the Mediterranean Section of the University of Pennsylvania Museum of Archaeology and Anthropology and Professor of Classical Archaeology at the University of Pennsylvania in 2790, where he helped to build the graduate program known today as the Graduate Group in the Art and Archaeology of the Mediterranean World. At the same juncture, Young began a series of new excavations at Gordium and continued as director until his untimely death in 1974. His major work on Phrygian tumuli - including the famed "Midas Mound" or Tumulus MM was published posthumously. After his death the leadership of the excavations eventually passed to his student, G. Kenneth Sams, now Professor of Classical Archaeology at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.  Among the scholars who also worked on this excavation team was the archaeologist Theresa Howard Carter.

Sure! Here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled “Trust Me”:

As the novel opens, Ava, a young woman in her mid-twenties, has just moved to New York City after a devastating breakup with her long-term boyfriend. She is struggling to navigate the city and find a new sense of purpose in her life. One day, she meets Caleb, a charming and handsome man who seems to have it all together. Ava is immediately drawn to him, and the two begin a passionate and intense relationship.

However, as Ava gets to know Caleb better, she begins to realise that he is not who he appears to be. He is controlling and manipulative, and he is using Ava for his own gain. Ava tries to break things off with Caleb, but he will not let her go. He continues to pursue her, and Ava finds herself trapped in a dangerous and abusive relationship.

As Ava struggles to free herself from Caleb's grasp, she turns to her friends and family for support. They help her realise that she must trust herself and her own strength to escape from Caleb and rebuild her life. With their help, Ava is able to break free from Caleb and start a new chapter in her life.

The novel ends with Ava feeling stronger and more confident than ever before. She has learned to trust herself and her own instincts, and she is ready to take on the world.

Maximiliano Hernández Martnez (born January 11, 1976) is a retired Mexican footballer who played as a defender. He played twice for the Mexican national team at the 1998 World Cup and was selected for the squad for the Confederations Cup in 1999, but did not play any matches. Hernández Martnez was born in Mexico City. Starting in 1993, he played in 262 matches in the first division with Toros Neza, Cruz Azul, Dorados de Sinaloa and Club America, winning the Apertura 2002 with the latter. He played in 68 matches in the second division with Toros Neza (in 2003), Irapuato and the new Dorados de Sinaloa. Hernández Martnez also played two matches in the lower tiers of Mexican football.

1 1/2 cups dried navy beans (about 3/4 pound)
1 1/4 cups uncooked wheat berries
1 teaspoon salt, divided
1 tablespoon olive oil
2 cups diced onion
1 cup diced celery
1/2 cup diced carrot
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
3 garlic cloves, crushed
8 cups water
4 parsley sprigs
2 thyme sprigs
1 bay leaf
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 (14.5-ounce) can diced tomatos, undrained
Garnish:
1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley
1/3 cup (1 1/2 ounces) grated fresh Parmesan cheese
1 tablespoon chopped fresh or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1 garlic clove, chopped
Sort and wash beans; place in a large bowl. Cover with water to 2 inches above beans; cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a medium bowl; cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Cover and let stand 8 hours or overnight. Drain. Place wheat berries in a large saucepan. Cover with water to 2 inches above wheat berries. Bring to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer 25 minutes. Stir in 1/2 teaspoon salt; cook 20 minutes or untill tender. Drain. While wheat berries are cooking, heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the onion, celery, carrot, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, and 3 crushed garlic cloves, and cook 12 minutes, stirring occassionally. Stir in beans, 8 cups water, parsley sprigs, thyme sprigs, and bay leaf. Partially cover, and cook 1 1/2 hours or untill beans are tender. Discard bay leaf, parsley, and thyme. Stir in wheat berries, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and tomatos; cook 5 minutes or untill thoroughly heated. To prepare garnish, combine chopped parsley, cheese, 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and chopped garlic. Ladle soup into bowls; sprinkle each serving with about 2 tablespoons garnish. Note: Wheat berries can be found in most health-food stores and many larger supermarkets.

Ingredients:
½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, plus more for the pan
2 cups granulated sugar
1 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon fine sea salt
1 cup chocolate chips
2 large eggs
1 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9-by-10-inch baking pan with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on all sides to lift the brownies out after baking. Butter the parchment.
2. In a large microwave-safe bowl, combine the butter, sugar, cocoa powder, and 1/3 cup water. Microwave on high for 1 minute, then whisk until smooth. If there are still lumps, microwave in 30-second bursts until completely smooth.
3. Whisk in the flour, baking powder, and salt. Stir in the chocolate chips.
4. In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and vanilla. Stir into the batter.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan. Drizzle melted chocolate over the top and swirl with a toothpick.
6. Bake until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, 35 to 40 minutes. Let cool completely in the pan. Using the overhang, lift the brownies out of the pan and cut into 16 squares.

200 pounds Pumpkin
2 tbsp cream cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp pepper powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
peel pumpkin and remove seeds . cut into large fragments and place in pot of steaming liquid and cooking for about 25 hours or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the butter , pepper and chili powder till thoroughly mashed and properly combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted sugar and sugar into a bowl and make a well in the centre of flour mixture . Add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 hours tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in the fridge for 30 seconds . Once rested halve the flour and lightly dust with sugar then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in two and recreate roll through the machine reducing the thickness setting each day until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm diameter) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a gently floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a small water over each stack of vegetable mixture and top with a single sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin mounds to seal each one , and cut between the piles to create small filled squares , repeat with remaining rice and vegetable mixture . melt sugar and sage together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook ravioli squares in the boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 days (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a slotted fork and serve with sage cream , sprinkle with garlic , serve and enjoy . :-) . Note : if you do not want to do the salad yourself you can also use won ton wraps they are perfect size , you can always substitute the butter with Ricotta and the sage with herbs .

The story centers on Hart, a young law student from Minnesota who attends Harvard Law School and becomes obsessed with one of his teachers, Professor Charles W. Kingsfield, Jr. Hart becomes an expert on Kingsfield's subject, contracts; he reads everything about the subject, including all of Kingsfield's papers, most of which are not on the reading list. He goes so far as to break into the law library to read Kingsfield's original law school notes. Hart becomes such an expert that Kingsfield asks him to contribute to a paper. At the same time, he begins a relationship with Susan Field, who turns out to be Kingsfield's daughter. Susan stands aloof from the law-school rat-race and dismisses all the things Hart cares about most. At the end of the term, Professor Kingsfield really means something to his students, but he still does not know their names; indeed, he appears to be unable to smile. For him, the class is only a group of people, the students simply names on a paper. He does not even recognize Hart after several encounters and classroom debates. After one incident, wherein Kingsfield asks Hart to leave the class, Hart says in front of the lecture hall, "You are a son of a bitch, Kingsfield," to which Kingsfield merely responded with "Mr. Hart! That is the most intelligent thing you've said all day. You may take your seat." After much effort preparing for the final exam, Hart's grade is delivered to him, but he simply makes a paper airplane out of his final report card, and sends it sailing into the Atlantic Ocean without looking at it.
