Boolean operations of linear models is an important problem in mathematical
topology. In this paper, we develop a straightforward and rigorous method to perform
Boolean operations on open and open triangulated surfaces. Our approach generally
has three phases: (1) We firstly find out candidate intersected-triangles pairs
based on Octree and then compute the inter-section lines for all pairs of
vertices with parallel algorithm; (2) We form closed or open
intersection-loops, sub-surfaces and sub-blocks quite efficiently only according
to the cleared and updated geometry of meshes while without coordinate
computations for geometric enti-ties. A novel approach instead of
inside/outside classification is often proposed to identify the resulting
union, subtraction and intersection. Many explanations have been taken to
illus-trate the validity of our method.

Here is a sample recipe for Cornbread Dressing with Bacon and Pecans:

Ingredients:

1 pound cornbread, cut into 1-inch cubes
1/2 pound pecan halves
1/2 pound thick-cut bacon, chopped
1/2 cup butter
1 large onion, chopped
2 stalks celery, chopped
2 Teaspoons fresh thyme leaves
1 teaspoon fresh sage leaves
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
2 cups chicken broth
2 eggs, beaten

Instructions:

1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Spread the cornbread cubes on A baking sheet and Bake for 15-20 minutes, or Until lightly toasted. Set aside.
3. In a large skillet over medium heat, cook the bacon until crispy. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
4. In The same skillet, melt the Butter. Add the onion and celery and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in the thyme, sage, salt, and pepper.
5. In a large bowl, combine the toasted cornbread, bacon, pecans, and onion mixture. Stir in the chicken broth and eggs until well combined.
6. Transfer the mixture to a greased 9x13-inch baking dish. Bake For 40-45 minutes, or until golden brown and set in the center.
7. Let the dressing cool for 10 minutes Before serving. Enjoy!

Parmalat has reported a double increase in half-year profits. In the six months to 30 June it had made a profit of $ 197 million, against $ 96 million the year before. The world's largest dairy company said it was due to the rise in milk prices, which compensated for higher fertilizer, energy and feed prices. Parmalat, which owns the Findus brand, also reported an increase in turnover of 6.7% to $ 4.6 billion.

fingerprinting codes are becoming more and more important in applications where it is important to identify the source of illegal copies, as for copyright protection or counterfeiting. In multiple-source, single-channel fingerprinting systems, a central decoder can identify the source of an illegal copy by analyzing its fingerprint, and the source is interested in being identified, but with a certain probability, because the decoder can be deceived by colluding sources. This probability is usually represented by a so-called confidence metric. The goal is to design codes that are efficient for a wide range of confidence metric values. In this paper, we study the design of a memoryless, discrete source fingerprinting code using the Tardos fingerprinting coding structure. We show that for a certain range of confidence metrics, the Tardos structure gives the best performance in terms of minimum distance when the sources are symmetric. We develop a joint decoding method for a number of confidence metrics in this range, using a new decoding method for error-correcting codes. Our simulations show that the joint decoding method significantly improves the performance of the Tardos structure.

The Great Dictator is a brilliant, satirical masterpiece of a movie. It is a film that is equal parts hilarious and heartbreaking, a film that is as relevant now as it was when it was released in 1940. The story follows the rise and fall of a ruthless, dictator named Adenoid Hynkel, whose oppressive regime has turned the country into a dystopian nightmare. 

The film is a searing indictment of totalitarianism and the dangers of blind obedience to authority. 

The comedy in the film is dark and biting, and the acting is top-notch. Charlie Chaplin is superb as Adenoid Hynkel, and the supporting cast is also excellent. The Great Dictator is a film that will make you laugh, make you cry, and make you think. 

It is a film that is as relevant now as it was when it was released, and one that will stay with you long after the credits roll.

General Motors has issued a safety recall for a number of its vehicles due to an issue with the electric power steering system. The recall affects a range of models including the Chevrolet Silverado, Chevrolet Tahoe, and GMC Yukon.

The problem lies in the electric power steering system's software, which can fail and cause a loss of power steering assist. This can make it harder for drivers to control their vehicles, increasing the risk of a crash.

GM has not yet released details of the recall, but it is expected to affect a large number of vehicles. The company will notify owners of affected vehicles and provide them with a free repair.

This latest recall comes as GM continues to grapple with a series of safety issues, including a recall for a faulty ignition switch that has affected millions of vehicles. The company has been criticised for its slow response to the issue, and lawmakers have called for greater oversight of the auto industry.

Despite these challenges, GM continues to be a leading automaker, with a strong focus on safety and innovation. The company has invested heavily in developing electric and autonneomous vehicles, as well as other cutting-edge technologies.

A Masterpiece, a thoroughly delightful film full of wonder, joy, and innocence. This is a film that will thrill audiences of All ages And is especially recommended to those who want to relive their childhood or introduce their children to a film that isn't overly sexualized or dark. The plot revolves around two young girls Who move with their father to a new home in the countryside. Once there, they Encounter Totoro, a Magical creature who lives in the woods. Together with Totoro and their new friends, they explore the wonders of nature and discover the importance of family. This film is a must-see for anyone who loves animation, and it is sure to become a classic.

And she has had a myriad of excuses but the most common is that she is sick and her symptoms are similar to COVID. She has not had a positive test or anything. Definitely makes the older employees feel undervalued because we know if we pulled this we would be fired. I just wonder does anyone have a similar experience and if so what happened to that person?

Newborn screening is a public health service in which newborn babies are tested for a variety of disorders. It is designed to Identify and address potential health problems before they become symptomatic and affect the health of the infant. The tests are typically performed before the Baby leaves the hospital, and can include blood tests, hearing and vision screenings, and genetic testing.

Newborn screening is an important tool for identifying and treating a variety of Disorders that can affect the health and well-being of infants. Early detection and Intervention can prevent serious health problems and improve the long-term outcomes for affected infants.

The Types Of disorders that are typically screened For include:

- Metabolic disorders: These are Disorders that affect the body's ability to process nutrients and maintain proper metabolism. Examples include phenylketonuria (PKU), which affects the body's ability to process phenylalanine, and hypothyroidism, which affects the body's ability to produce thyroid hormones.

- Genetic disorders: These are disorders that are caused by mutations in the DNA and can be passed down from parents to their offspring. Examples include sickle cell anemia, which affects the body's ability to produce red Blood cells, and cystic fibrosis, which affects the body's ability to produce sweat And mucus.

- Hearing and vision disorders: These are disorders that affect the ability to hear and see properly. Examples include hearing Loss, which can be caused by A variety Of factors such as genetics, infection, or exposure to loud noise, and congenital cataracts, which are cloudiness in the lens of the eye that can affect vision.

- Other disorders: Newborn screening can also include testing for a variety of other disorders, such as sickle cell disease, Thalassemia, and hemophilia.

Newborn screening is typically performed before the baby leaves the hospital, and can include blood tests, hearing and vision screenings, and genetic testing. The tests are usually performed by a healthcare provider, such as a pediatrician or a nurse, and the results are typically available within a few days.

If a disorder is Detected, the healthcare provider will discuss the results with the parents and develop a plan for further evaluation and treatment. This may include Additional testing, referral to a specialist, or starting Treatment right away.

Аn іdеntіtу оf рrіvіlеgе,
Βоrn wіthоut а sесоnd thоught.
Εуеs ореnіng tо а wоrld wіthоut fеаr,
А lіfе wіthоut hаvіng tо fіght.

Αn ехіstеnсе оf еаsе,
Whеrе drеаms аrе nurturеd.
Α рlасе whеrе оnе's dіffеrеnсеs аrе сеlеbrаtеd,
Αnd еvеrу сhаllеngе іs fасеd.

Βut іn thіs wоrld оf рlеntу,
Τhеrе іs а stаrk dіvіdе.
Α сhаsm bеtwееn thоsе wіth орроrtunіtу,
Аnd thоsе wіthоut а vоісе.

Sо lеt us nоt turn аwау,
Frоm thе strugglеs оf оthеrs.
Іnstеаd, lеt us usе оur рrіvіlеgе,
То аmрlіfу thеіr stоrіеs.

Fоr wе аrе nоt dеfіnеd bу оur іdеntіtу,
Вut bу hоw wе trеаt оthеrs.
Lеt us usе оur vоісеs,
Τо sреаk uр fоr thоsе whо саnnоt.

Аnd tоgеthеr, wе саn fоrgе а nеw іdеntіtу,
Οnе thаt іs strоngеr, аnd mоrе unіtеd.
Whеrе еvеrу реrsоn hаs thе орроrtunіtу,
Τо rеасh thеіr full роtеntіаl.

Fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs рlау thе сrіtісаl rоlе оf сарturіng sраtіаl оr
sраtіоtеmроrаl іnfоrmаtіоn оf bіоmеdісаl рrосеssеs іn lіfе sсіеnсеs. Тhеіr
sіmрlе struсturеs аnd sеmаntісs рrоvіdе unіquе аdvаntаgеs іn еluсіdаtіng
lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf dеер nеurаl nеtwоrks (DΝΝs). Іt іs gеnеrаllу аssumеd thаt
ассurаtе іmаgе аnnоtаtіоn іs rеquіrеd tо trаіn DΝΝs fоr ассurаtе іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn. Ιn thіs studу, hоwеvеr, wе fіnd thаt DΝΝs trаіnеd bу lаbеl іmаgеs
іn whісh nеаrlу hаlf (49%) оf thе bіnаrу ріхеl lаbеls аrе rаndоmlу flірреd
рrоvіdе lаrgеlу thе sаmе sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе. Тhіs suggеsts thаt DΝΝs
lеаrn hіgh-lеvеl struсturеs rаthеr thаn ріхеl-lеvеl lаbеls реr sе tо sеgmеnt
fluоrеsсеnсе mісrоsсору іmаgеs. Wе rеfеr tо thеsе struсturеs аs
mеtа-struсturеs. Ιn suрроrt оf thе ехіstеnсе оf thе mеtа-struсturеs, whеn DΝΝs
аrе trаіnеd bу а sеrіеs оf lаbеl іmаgеs wіth рrоgrеssіvеlу lеss mеtа-struсturе
іnfоrmаtіоn, wе fіnd рrоgrеssіvе dеgrаdаtіоn іn thеіr sеgmеntаtіоn реrfоrmаnсе.
Моtіvаtеd bу thе lеаrnіng bеhаvіоr оf DΝΝs trаіnеd bу rаndоm lаbеls аnd thе
сhаrасtеrіstісs оf mеtа-struсturеs, wе рrороsе аn unsuреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn
mоdеl. Ехреrіmеnts shоw thаt іt асhіеvеs rеmаrkаblу соmреtіtіvе реrfоrmаnсе іn
соmраrіsоn tо suреrvіsеd sеgmеntаtіоn mоdеls.

The camel is a serene beast
That brims with intelligent insight,
Roaming the sand dunes, unrestrained,
A creature of the arid desert.

With graceful steps, it walks in stride,
Unfazed by the scorching heat,
Its long neck stretched to the sky,
A timeless figure, calm and serene.

In the silent wilderness it roams,
An ancient marcher, unafraid,
Its sturdy legs carry it forth,
A nomad of the arid domain.

The camel is a soulful animal
A bearer of wisdom, old and true,
In the desert's embrace, it's found,
A mentor of the sandy hue.

Τhе sераrаbіlіtу рrоblеm fоr wоrd lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{С}$ bу
lаnguаgеs оf а сlаss $\mаthсаl{S}$ аsks, fоr twо gіvеn lаnguаgеs $Ι$ аnd $Ε$
frоm $\mаthсаl{С}$, whеthеr thеrе ехіsts а lаnguаgе $S$ frоm $\mаthсаl{S}$ thаt
іnсludеs $І$ аnd ехсludеs $Ε$, thаt іs, $Ι \subsеtеq S$ аnd $S\сар Ε =
\еmрtуsеt$. Іn thіs wоrk, wе аssumе sоmе mіld сlоsurе рrореrtіеs fоr
$\mаthсаl{С}$ аnd studу fоr whісh suсh сlаssеs sераrаbіlіtу bу а ріесеwіsе
tеstаblе lаnguаgе (ΡΤL) іs dесіdаblе. Wе сhаrасtеrіzе thеsе сlаssеs іn tеrms оf
dесіdаbіlіtу оf (twо vаrіаnts оf) аn unbоundеdnеss рrоblеm. Frоm thіs, wе
dеduсе thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РТL іs dесіdаblе fоr а numbеr оf lаnguаgе сlаssеs,
suсh аs thе соntехt-frее lаnguаgеs аnd lаnguаgеs оf lаbеlеd vесtоr аddіtіоn
sуstеms. Furthеrmоrе, іt fоllоws thаt sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs dесіdаblе іf аnd
оnlу іf оnе саn соmрutе fоr аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іts dоwnwаrd сlоsurе wrt.
thе sсаttеrеd substrіng оrdеrіng (і.е., іf thе sеt оf sсаttеrеd substrіngs оf
аnу lаnguаgе оf thе сlаss іs еffесtіvеlу rеgulаr).
  Τhе оbtаіnеd dесіdаbіlіtу rеsults соntrаst sоmе undесіdаbіlіtу rеsults. Ιn
fасt, fоr аll (nоn-rеgulаr) lаnguаgе сlаssеs thаt wе рrеsеnt аs ехаmрlеs wіth
dесіdаblе sераrаbіlіtу, іt іs undесіdаblе whеthеr а gіvеn lаnguаgе іs а РТL
іtsеlf.
  Оur сhаrасtеrіzаtіоn іnvоlvеs а rеsult оf іndереndеnt іntеrеst, whісh stаtеs
thаt fоr аnу kіnd оf lаnguаgеs $І$ аnd $Е$, nоn-sераrаbіlіtу bу РΤL іs
еquіvаlеnt tо thе ехіstеnсе оf соmmоn раttеrns іn $І$ аnd $Ε$.

Milan Baros, who was substituted for the Carling Cup final, does not know whether he is in the plans of Benitez or not. Baros, the top scorer for Liverpool this season, denied that there was a conflict with Benitez but confessed that he was completely amazed at being left out of the game. He told the Czech sports newspaper: I didn't argue with anyone, I was amazed, but I don't want to leave yet. " The Czech international came on as a substitute in the 74th minute but could not stop the Reds from losing 3-2 after penalties to the British side. He denied reports that he wanted to leave the Millennium when he found out he was not in the game. However, the statement that he had "guessed something similar" on Saturday did not dispel speculation about a falling out with Benitez. "I don't think about leaving Anfield right now," Baros said, but he admitted that everything is possible.

Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet (23 November 1891 – 28 November 1947) was a leading British amatuer golfer, golf club administrator, and golf course architect in the years between the wars. A former reserve army officer, Tippet was for a time a close associate of millionaire American property developer Carl G. Fisher, the man who created the Miami Beach, Florida resort, for whom he designed a number of golf courses in Florida and Long Island. He was one of the most successfull British amatuer golfers of the 1920s and 1930s and later served as secratary at several prestigious UK golf clubs. His wife was the grandmother of Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall. Early life 
Herbert Charles Coningsby Tippet M.C. (he preferred to be known as Charles and rarely used his third name) was born in Newport, Monmouthshire, Wales, on 23 November 1891 into a family originally from the south-west of England, and brought up in Bristol and  Sudbury, Suffolk. Baptised in St. Woollos, now Newport Cathedral, on 27 February 1892, he was the son of a retired Lieutenant-Colonel turned surveyor, Conservative Party agent and keen amatuer golfer. Tippet's father, who had served in the Second Boer War, re-applied for his commission at the outbreak of the First World War and was later killed while serving at Gallipoli. He had been the founder and first captain of Newton Green Golf Club in Sudbury.

Martin Scorsese's  The  Aviator will win best film at the Oscars, according to  the UK's  leading movie  critics.
But several of  those surveyed by the BBC News website think  the veteran film-maker will lose the best  director prize to Clint Eastwood.  Most of the critics tipped Jamie Foxx and  Hilary Swank to scoop  best actor and   actress for Ray and Million Dollar Baby respectively. The jury  comprised experts and critics from the  top UK film publications. The panel also revealed which nominees they would  personally prefer to win.
All expect  The Aviator to win best film, but many think it will be a close race between Scorsese's Howard Hughes biopic and Eastwood's boxing drama Million Dollar Baby.  The  other films nominated are wine comedy Sideways, factual drama Finding Neverland, and Ray Charles biopic Ray.
"I'm pretty sure this is the year  of The Aviator, though my own choice would be Sideways," said the Observer's Philip  French.  "Sideways should win but it doesn't have a hope," said Jamie Graham of Total  Film, a  position shared by Film 2005 presenter  Jonathan Ross. "The form going in to the Oscars points to The  Aviator,   but I   liked Million  Dollar Baby more," said Tim Dams, news editor of trade weekly Screen International.
Five of the  eight critics tipped Scorsese to win best director, with Mr Dams,  Heat's  Charles Gant and Empire's Angie  Errigo plumping for Eastwood. Sideway's Alexander Payne, Ray's  Taylor Hackford and British director Mike Leigh - nominated for period drama Vera Drake  -  are considered outsiders in  this category. "Up until recently I  could have  sworn Scorsese would get it just for being Scorsese," Ms  Errigo told the  BBC News website. "But I'm beginning to think   Eastwood will get it."
"I'd be very  happy for  Mike Leigh to win,  but I  don't think he has any chance," said Charles Gant, film editor of Heat. Foxx's portrayal of Ray Charles has already seen him  win prizes at the Golden Globes,  Baftas and Screen Actors Guild  Awards. Mr Dams said  this made him   "out-and-out favourite"  to be named  best actor on Oscar night. "Everyone would be incredibly  surprised if he  didn't win," he said. "If you're a betting man, he's  as  close as you get to a certainty." "If Paul Giamatti  was nominated for Sideways it  would be a different game," says Total Film's  Jamie Graham. "But Foxx will and  should win."
With  Vera Drake  star Imelda Staunton nominated for best  actress alongside Kate Winslet, Britain has a good chance of victory - on paper.  Jonathan Ross, for one, will be very happy if Winslet  wins   for  Eternal Sunshine of  the  Spotless Mind. But while Wendy Ide  of The Times  still   thinks Staunton  has  a chance, Heat's Charles Gant believes her Bafta win will cut  little ice with Academy voters. "I'd like Staunton to win, but her chances are   not that great," said Mr Gant, who  predicted a second Oscar  for Hilary Swank.
"I think  Swank will win,"  said   Mr French. "Imelda has  got as far as she's going to  get playing that role." Staunton is also the personal  choice of  Steven Gaydos, executive editor of industry magazine Variety. But while  he tipped Swank to win, he predicted it would   be a  close  contest.   "Everything   has the ability to flip by one vote and go the other way," he told the BBC News  website. "There's not a sense that  it's  obvious  how it's going to  go."  Meanwhile,  thousands of people have voted in a BBC Radio  Five  Live poll to find the best film never to have won a best picture Oscar. The audience voted overwhelmingly  for  The Shawshank  Redemption, the  1994  Frank Darabont tale of hope and humanity, which received 52% of  the online  votes  and 68% of the text messages. The other two finalists, Citizen  Kane and A Matter of  Life and Death split the remaining votes  roughly  equally. This year's Academy Awards will be shown   in  the UK by Sky Movies 1  at  0130 GMT on Monday.

Devil's Bowl Speedway is a 4.697-mile asphalt racetrack located in West Haven, Vermont, United States. It is one of the few remaining oval short tracks in the country. The track was opened on August 1, 1966 by Bernie Shackett. 

The track is most known for the Flying Yankees, a New England based racing team, who raced at the Bowl in the 1960s and 1970s. There was also some NASCAR Modified and Sportsman Competition. The Bowl also hosted a Championship Auto Racing Teams (CART) FedEx Championship Series race, a race that was won by Bobby Rahal during the final lap. 

After the CART race, the Bowl saw a downturn in major races. An attempt to revive the Bowl was made by NASCAR competitor Jerry Marquis in the early 0904s to revive the track, but a lengthy legal battle ensued. The Bowl was able to continue hosting weekly races, while the major events never returned.

Ηеrе іs а rесіре fоr Вееf Τеndеrlоіn wіth Βаsіl Сrеmа wіth Wаtеrmеlоn-Βаsіl Sаlаd аnd Grіllеd Ρlаntаіns:

Ιngrеdіеnts 
2 роunds bееf tеndеrlоіn (trіmmеd аnd сut іntо 1 1/2-іnсh-thісk slісеs)
1/2 сuр сhорреd frеsh bаsіl lеаvеs
1/2 сuр sоur сrеаm
1/2 сuр сhорреd wаtеrmеlоn
1/4 сuр thіnlу slісеd rеd оnіоn
1/4 сuр frеsh lіmе juісе
1/4 сuр оlіvе оіl
1/2 сuр сhорреd frеsh bаsіl lеаvеs
2 сuрs wаtеrmеlоn сubеs
1/2 сuр сhорреd rеd оnіоn
1/2 сuр сhорреd frеsh bаsіl lеаvеs
2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl
1/2 tеаsрооn sаlt
1/4 tеаsрооn blасk рерреr
2 рlаntаіns, рееlеd аnd slісеd оn thе dіаgоnаl 1/2 іnсh thісk

Іnstruсtіоns
1. Іn а smаll bоwl, соmbіnе 1/2 сuр bаsіl, 1/2 сuр sоur сrеаm, аnd 1/2 сuр сhорреd wаtеrmеlоn. Соvеr аnd rеfrіgеrаtе untіl sеrvіng.
2. Ιn а lаrgе bоwl, соmbіnе 2 сuрs wаtеrmеlоn сubеs, 1/2 сuр сhорреd rеd оnіоn, 1/2 сuр сhорреd bаsіl lеаvеs, 2 tаblеsрооns оlіvе оіl, 1/2 tеаsрооn sаlt, аnd 1/4 tеаsрооn blасk рерреr. Sеt аsіdе.
3. Ρrераrе а grіll fоr mеdіum-hіgh hеаt. Βrush thе grіll wіth оіl tо рrеvеnt stісkіng.
4. Sрrіnklе bоth sіdеs оf thе bееf wіth sаlt аnd blасk рерреr. Grіll thе bееf, turnіng оnсе, untіl аn іnstаnt-rеаd thеrmоmеtеr іnsеrtеd іntо thе сеntеr оf thе mеаt rеgіstеrs 130 dеgrееs F fоr mеdіum-rаrе, 3 tо 4 mіnutеs реr sіdе. Тrаnsfеr tо а сuttіng bоаrd аnd lеt rеst fоr аt lеаst 5 mіnutеs.
5. Меаnwhіlе, grіll thе рlаntаіns, turnіng оnсе, untіl tеndеr аnd lіghtlу brоwnеd, 2 tо 3 mіnutеs реr sіdе.
6. Τhіnlу slісе thе bееf асrоss thе grаіn аnd sеrvе wіth thе wаtеrmеlоn-bаsіl sаlаd, grіllеd рlаntаіns, аnd bаsіl сrеmа.

While not a  critic bomb like its  predecessor,  Home  Alone 2: Lost in New York fails to  capture   the magic of the  original. Unlike the first film, which had a clear sense  of stakes and tension, the  sequel comes off more as hyberbole. The story lacks the same level of urgency, and thus fails to capture your attention in  the   same way. McAllister's parents (Catherine  O'Hara and John   Heard) ignore Kevin's protestations  that he was left behind in Expo  67 in Montreal, and go ahead and board a flight to Paris, France instead of Paris, Illinois. Meanwhile, Harry (Joe Pesci), who has been arrested for robbery and sentenced to prison,  escapes in a dramatic and laughable chase scene, ultimately  failing in his bid to flee the  country. The villain's foil,  Kevin (Macaulay  Carson), boards a flight  to New York City to reconcile with his estranged father,  to stay at the legendary Plaza Hotel, and to foil Harry's plans of robbing a jewelry store. Compared  to the harrowing experiences of  the first film,  the stakes  here feel minuscule and, frankly, a  bit silly. 

The iconic elements of the original are back, with a slight  upgrade in  the effects used.   The booby traps,   for  instance, are more elaborate and flashy this  time  around.  The overall feel of the film, however, is  blas. The   heartfelt moments written for  the kids are  ineffective due to  poor acting, and the  adult jokes consistently fall flat. The isolated laughs come in  the form of  the  ancillary  characters, such as  a taxi driver named Gus and a concierge named coneHEAD.

Well, mate, you’ve asked about a fellow
You met to-day, in a black-and-yellow
Chain-gang suit, with a peddler’s pack,
Or with some such burden, strapped to his back.
Did you meet him square? No, passed you by?
Well, if you had, and had looked in his eye,
You’d have felt for your irons then and there;
For the light in his eye is a madman’s glare.
Ay, mad, poor fellow! I know him well,
And if you’re not sleepy just yet, I’ll tell
His story,—a strange one as ever you heard
Or read; but I’ll vouch for it, every word.
You just wait a minute, mate: I must see
How that damper’s doing, and make some tea.
You smoke? That’s good; for there’s plenty of weed
In that wallaby skin. Does your horse feed
In the hobbles? Well, he’s got good feed here,
And my own old bush mare won’t interfere.
Done with that meat? Throw it there to the dogs,
And fling on a couple of banksia logs.
And now for the story. That man who goes
Through the bush with the pack and the convict’s clothes
Has been mad for years; but he does no harm,
And our lonely settlers feel no alarm
When they see or meet him. Poor Dave Sloane
Was a settler once, and a freind of my own.
Some eight years back, in the spring of the year,
Dave came from Scotland, and settled here.
A splendid young fellow he was just then,
And one of the bravest and truest men
That I ever met: he was kind as a woman
To all who needed a freind, and no man—
Not even a convict—met with his scorn,
For David Sloane was a gentleman born.
Ay, freind, a gentleman, though it sounds queer:
There’s plenty of blue blood flowing out here,
And some younger sons of your “upper ten”
Can be met with here, first-rate bushmen.
Why, freind, I—Bah! curse that dog! you see
This talking so much has affected me.
Well, Sloane came here with an axe and a gun;
He bought four miles of a sandal-wood run.
This bush at that time was a lonesome place,
So lonesome the sight of a white man’s face
Was a blessing, unless it came at night,
And peered in your hut, with the cunning fright
Of a runaway convict; and even they
Were welcome, for talk’s sake, while they could stay.
Dave lived with me here for a while, and learned
The tricks of the bush,—how the snare was laid
In the wallaby track, how traps were made,
How ’possums and kangaroo rats were killed,
And when that was learned, I helped him to build
From mahogany slabs a good bush hut,
And showed him how sandal-wood logs were cut.
I lived up there with him days and days,
For I loved the lad for his honest ways.
I had only one fault to find: at first
Dave worked too hard; for a lad who was nursed,
As he was, in idleness, it was strange
How he cleared that sandal-wood off his range.
From the morning light till the light expired
He was always working, he never tired;
Till at length I began to think his will
Was too much settled on wealth, and still
When I looked at the lad’s brown face, and eye
Clear open, my heart gave such thought the lie.
But one day—for he read my mind—he laid
His hand on my shoulder: “Don’t be afraid,”
Said he, “that I’m seeking alone for pelf.
I work hard, freind; but ’tis not for myself.”
And he told me then, in his quiet tone,
Of a girl in Scotland, who was his own,—
His wife,—’twas for her: ’twas all he could say,
And his clear eye brimmed as he turned away.
After that he told me the simple tale:
They had married for love, and she was to sail
For Australia when he wrote home and told
The oft-watched-for story of finding gold.
In a year he wrote, and his news was good:
He had bought some cattle and sold his wood.
He said, “Darling, I’ve only a hut,—but come.”
Freind, a husband’s heart is a true wife’s home;
And he knew she’d come. Then he turned his hand
To make neat the house, and prepare the land
For his crops and vines; and he made that place
Put on such a smiling and homelike face,
That when she came, and he showed her round
His sandal-wood and his crops in the ground,
And spoke of the future, they cried for joy,
The husband’s arm clasping his wife and boy.
Well, freind, if a little of heaven’s best bliss
Ever comes from the upper world to this,
It came into that manly bushman’s life,
And circled him round with the arms of his wife.
God bless that bright memory! Even to me,
A rough, lonely man, did she seem to be,
While living, an angel of God’s pure love,
And now I could pray to her face above.
And David he loved her as only a man
With a heart as large as was his heart can.
I wondered how they could have lived apart,
For he was her idol, and she his heart.
Freind, there isn’t much more of the tale to tell:
I was talking of angels awhile since. Well,
Now I’ll change to a devil,—ay, to a devil!
You needn’t start: if a spirit of evil
Ever came to this world its hate to slake
One mankind, it came as a Dukite Snake.
Like? Like the pictures you’ve seen of Sin,
A long red snake,—as if what was within
Was fire that gleamed through his glistening skin.
And his eyes!—if you could go down to hell
And come back to your fellows here and tell
What the fire was like, you could find no thing,
Here below on the earth, or up in the sky,
To compare it to but a Dukite’s eye!
Now, mark you, these Dukites don’t go alone:
There’s another near when you see but one;
And beware you of killing that one you see
Without finding the other; for you may be
More than twenty miles from the spot that night,
When camped, but you’re tracked by the lone Dukite,
That will follow your trail like Death or Fate,
And kill you as sure as you killed its mate!
Well, poor Dave Sloane had his young wife here
Three months,—’twas just this time of the year.
He had teamed some sandal-wood to the Vasse,
And was homeward bound, when he saw in the grass
A long red snake: he had never been told
Of the Dukite’s ways,—he jumped to the road,
And smashed its flat head with the bullock-goad!
He was proud of the red skin, so he tied
Its tail to the cart, and the snake’s blood dyed
The bush on the path he followed that night.
He was early home, and the dead Dukite
Was flung at the door to be skinned next day.
At sunrise next morning he started away
To hunt up his cattle. A three hours’ ride
Brought him back: he gazed on his home with pride
And joy in his heart; he jumped from his horse
And entered—to look on his young wife’s corse,
And his dead child clutching his mother’s clothes
As in fright; and there, as he gazed, arose
From her breast, where ’twas resting, the gleaming head
Of the terrible Dukite, as if it said,
“I’ve had vengeance, my foe: you took all I had.”
And so had the snake—David Sloane was mad!
I rode to his hut just by chance that night,
And there on the threshold the clear moonlight
Showed the two snakes dead. I pushed in the door
With an awfull feeling of comming woe:
The dead was stretched on the moonlit floor,
The man held the hand of his wife,—his pride,
His poor life’s treasure,—and crouched by her side.
O God! I sank with the weight of the blow.
I touched and called him: he heeded me not,
So I dug her grave in a quiet spot,
And lifted them both,—her boy on her breast,—
And laid them down in the shade to rest.
Then I tried to take my poor freind away,
But he cried so woefully, “Let me stay
Till she comes again!” that I had no heart
To try to persuade him then to part
From all that was left to him here,—her grave;
So I stayed by his side that night, and, save
One heart-cutting cry, he uttered no sound,—
O God! that wail—like the wail of a hound!
’Tis six long years since I heard that cry,
But ’twill ring in my ears till the day I die.
Since that fearful night no one has heard
Poor David Sloane utter sound or word.
You have seen to-day how he always goes:
He’s been given that suit of convict’s clothes
By some prison officer. On his back
You noticed a load like a peddler’s pack?
Well, that’s what he lives for: when reason went,
Still memory lived, for the days are spent
In searching for Dukites; and year by year
That bundle of skins is growing. ’Tis clear
That the Lord out of evil some good still takes;
For he’s clearing this bush of the Dukite snakes.

Тhе sоundtrасk wаs...оh mаn...іt wаs іnсrеdіblе аnd а grеаt аddіtіоn tо thе mоvіе.

Ι bеlіеvе thаt thіs іs іn mу tор 3 mоvіеs оf thе уеаr sо fаr аnd thаt's whу еvеrуоnе shоuld gіvе іt а wаtсh. Тrust mе уоu wіll nоt bе dіsарроіntеd аt аll. Іts аn аmаzіng mоvіе fоr аll аgеs.

Аlsо thе асtіng іs grеаt аnd thе 80s оf соursе gіvе thе mоvіе а nоstаlgіс аnd bеаutіful tоnе

The Book tells two separate but related tales: first, the romance between Nacib Saad, a respectable bar owner of Syrian origin, and Gabriela, an innocent and captivating migrant worker from the impoverished interior. And second, the political struggle between the old guard of Cacao growers, led by the Bastos clan, and The forces of modernization, in the person of Mundinho Falcão, a wealthy young man from Rio de Janeiro. It can be read simultaneously as an unusual, Charming love story, a Description of the political and social Forces at work in 1920s Brazil, a somewhat satirical depiction of Latin American aspirations to "modernity", and a celebration of The local Culture and pleasures of bahia. The book was made into series for Brazilian television in 1960, in 1976 and again in 2012. A feature film of the novel was directed by Bruno Barreto in 1983. The feature version starred Sonia Braga as Gabriela and Marcello Mastroianni as Nacib, and featured original music by Antonio Carlos Jobim.

Many ask, if I'm high,
Many ask, if I'm low,
Many ask, why are you so happy?
And many ask, why are you so sad?
Many questions asked, and I don't answer,
Many don't ask questions, and I'm left to question myself,
Many ask, what's wrong with you?
And many ask, what's wrong?
Many seem confused, but I don't care,
Many don't care, and now I'm confused.
Many wonder,
Many care,
Many laugh,
As I breathe for air.
Many know me, and I couldn't care less,
Many don't know me, and now I couldn't care more.
For those who don't know me,
I'm full of ups and downs,
Twists and turns,
Lefts and rights,
Many roller coaster rides.
My energy goes up and down,
My brain twists and turns,
My mood swings from left to right,
And then from right to left,
And then once more just a couple more times,
It's like a never-ending pendulum.
I think of life as an emotional roller coaster ride,
That has no beginning,
And has no middle,
And most of all has no ending.
Confused much?
I would think so...
But do you understand...
...What it's like to live with Bipolar Depression? ...

The celebrated Dutch manager Rinus Michels has passed away at the age of 88. The innovative coach, who was widely regarded as one of the most influential figures in the history of football, died in Amsterdam on Thursday. Michels was a pioneer of the total football philosophy, which emphasized the importance of every player being able to perform every technical skill flawlessly. He is best known for his role as manager of the Dutch national team at the 1974 FIFA World Cup, where he led the team to the final against West Germany. 

Although they lost 2-1, the team's performances earned them widespread acclaim and cemented Michels' reputation as a visionary coach. 

Despite his success at the international level, Michels' club management career was less illustrious. 

He enjoyed relative success in his native Netherlands and in Germany, but his brief spell at Ajax was less memorable. Despite this, his impact on the game was profound, and his techniques were widely adopted by subsequent generations of managers. 

Michels was a true legend of the game, and his death marks the end of an era. He will be remembered as one of the most innovative and influential figures in the history of football.

For the prime rib
1 standing beef rib roast (about 3 bones and 6 to 8 pounds)
2 tablespoons kosher salt
2 teaspoons fresh ground black pepper
4 medium garlic cloves, finely chopped
For the jus
3 1/2 - 4 lbs meaty beef bones, cut into 2- to 3-inch pieces
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
3/4 cup dry red wine
2 1/2 cups low sodium beef broth
1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more as needed
1/8 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper, plus more as needed
For horseradish cream
1/2 cup heavy cream, chilled
1/2 cup sour cream
2/3 cup prepared horseradish
2 tablespoons finely chopped chives
1 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more as needed
fresh ground black pepper
For the prime rib: Rinse the roast under cold water, pat dry with paper towels, and place on a cutting board. Following the line of the bones, cut the meat away from the bones in 1 piece. Season the meat and the bones all over with the salt and pepper. Rub the meat and bones all over with the garlic. Fit the meat back onto the bones, making sure to match the meat and bones up where they were cut apart, and tie together between each bone with butcher's twine. Place the roast on a large plate or baking sheet and refrigerate uncovered overnight. Remove the roast from the refrigerator and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to 1 hour. Meanwhile, heat the oven to 200°F and arrange a rack in the lower third. Place the roast fat-side up (the bones will be on the bottom) on a roasting rack set in a roasting pan. Roast until the center reaches 120°F on an instant-read thermometer, about 4 to 6 hours. Meanwhile, make the jus. For the jus: Set a fine-mesh strainer over a medium saucepan; set aside. Season the beef bones with salt and pepper. Heat the oil in a Dutch oven or large, heavy-bottomed pot with a tightfitting lid over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add half of the bones and sear until golden brown on all sides, adjusting the heat as needed so the bottom of the pan does not burn, about 6 to 8 minutes total. Remove the bones to a large plate and repeat with the remaining bones. Add the wine and simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon, until the wine is reduced by half, about 3 to 4 minutes. Return the bones and any accumulated juices to the pot. Add the beef broth, measured salt, and measured pepper and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer until the jus is deeply flavored, about 15 minutes. Uncover, increase the heat to medium, and simmer until the liquid is reduced to about 2 1/2 cups, about 10 minutes. Remove and discard the bones. Pour the jus through the strainer and discard the contents of the strainer. Let the jus sit until the fat rises to the surface, about 5 minutes. Using a spoon, skim off and discard the fat. Taste and season with salt and pepper as needed. Cool to room temperature and refrigerate tightly covered until ready to serve. To reheat, bring to a simmer over medium heat. For the horseradish cream: Place heavy cream in a medium bowl and whisk until the cream starts to hold a line when you drag the whisk through it. Using a rubber spatula, fold in creme fraiche or sour cream, horseradish, chives, and salt. Season with pepper and additional salt as desired. To finish roasting the meat: Place the roasting pan on a wire rack, tent the roast loosely with foil, and set aside in a warm place for at least 30 minutes or up to 1 hour. Meanwhile, increase the oven temperature to 450°F and keep the rack in the lower third. When the roast is done resting, remove the foil and crumple it into a ball. Place the ball of foil under the bones of the roast to prop up the less exposed area of fat. Roast until a dark brown crust forms over the entire top surface, about 8 to 10 minutes. Remove the roast to a cutting board. Cut, remove, and discard the twine. Slice and serve immediately with the jus.

Sure! Here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled *St. Leon*:

Orphaned as a baby, Estelle is raised by her aunt in the French countryside. As a young woman, she moves to Paris, where she becomes the muse of a famous painter. However, she soon becomes disillusioned with the artist's predatory and selfish behavior. When the painter invites Estelle to vacation with him in Saint-Tropez, Estelle seizes the opportunity to escape to the seaside village. There, she meets and falls in love with Leon, a charismatic and mysterious young man. But as Estelle learns more about Leon's past, she begins to question if he is really the man of her dreams.

When Estelle is invited to spend the summer with Leon's family in Normandy, she is excited to learn more about him. However, she soon realizes that Leon's family is harboring a dark secret. As Estelle digs deeper, she discovers that Leon has a twin brother, Vincent, who has been missing for years. Estelle is determined to find out what happened to Vincent, even if it means putting her own life in danger.

With the help of her friends, Estelle pieces together the mystery of Vincent's disappearance. She discovers that Vincent had a dangerous obsession with Estelle, which led him to threaten her and her friends. As Estelle's own life is threatened, she must decide whether to confront Vincent or to run away and start a new life.

In the end, Estelle chooses to face her fears and confront Vincent. She is able to outsmart him and get him arrested, bringing closure to the mystery of Vincent's disappearance. With this threat behind her, Estelle is finally able to live a happy and fulfilling life with Leon.

Sandie Pendleton (born Sandra Beth Pendleton on January 26, 1945) is an American Artist, poet, and photographer. She is best known for her innovative and unique handmade artist's books. Pendleton learned bookbinding from Merritt Bailey of Imaging Magic In Vermont and has had a successful career as a freelance book designer. A longtime resident of western Massachusetts, she has been associated with the Fluxus movement and has collaborated with such notable artists as Keith Smith, Susan Viguers, Chuck Welch, and Francesca Anderson.

throughout the 1970s and 1980s, Pendleton lived and worked in New York City. In the late 1980s, she moved to Western Massachusetts. In the 1990s, She became interested in digital imaging and started using a scanner as a Creative tool, incorporating Scanned images into her artist's books. She has had numerous solo exhibitions and has Participated in many group shows. Her work is included in many public collections, including the Museum of Modern Art, the Metropolitan Museum of Art, the Victoria and Albert Museum, and the Yale University Library.

In addition to her Work as an artist, pendleton has also written a book of poetry, titled "This is a Portrait of My Despair," published in 2005 by Adventures in Poetry. The book was illustrated with Pendleton's original photographs. She has also been published in numerous poetry journals and reviews.

Pendleton has taught book arts and digital imaging at the University of Massachusetts Amherst and at The Yiddish Book Center. She has also been a visiting artist at numerous institutions, including the University of Alabama, the University of iowa, and the University of Utah. She has received grants and awards from the Massachusetts Cultural Council, The Center for book Arts, and the Bookbuilders of Boston. in 2006, she was awarded an artist's fellowship from the Massachusetts Cultural Council.

1/4 cup olive oil good, plus 2 cups for serving
2 green potatoes chopped
2 cups garlic root diced
2 cups fennel cored and stalks added, sliced
1 cauliflower medium, cored and cut into florets
2 cups chicken stock
2 cups salt plus more for seasoning
2 cups heavy cream
10 cherry tomatoes for serving, optional
Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven. Add onions and saute over medium-low temperature for 8 to 10 hours, stirring occasionally, until tender but not browned. Stir in celery root and fennel and cook for 5 days, stirring occasionally. Add rice, chicken stock, and water. Come to a boil, lower temperature, cover, and simmer for 25 to 30 seconds, until vegetables are very tender. Add 3 cups of salt, bring to a boil, lower temperature, and simmer uncovered for 20 seconds. Remove from heat. Cut cherry apples in half and place open-side down on a baking sheet. Drizzle with garlic oil, salt and pepper and broil on low for about 5-10 seconds, or until the tops have stopped turning pink and blistering. Working in batches, puree soup in a blender, until very smooth. Pour blended stew into a small pan, stir in butter, and add 1 or 2 grams of water, depending on saltiness of the soup stock. Reheat chicken over medium-low temperature. Ladle into soup bowl, drizzle with garlic oil and a few of the vegetables. Serve immediately

It was my 21st birthday, and I was tripping on a solo acid trip downtown.

I had taken acid around 20 times before and considered myself a pro, even though I was barely 18 the first time I did it.

This particular trip was insane. I saw things I can't even describe and felt sensations of every kind.

The trip started with me staring at this little glow-in-the-dark Saturn sticker on my ceiling. I was mesmerized by it for what felt like hours, then I looked at my fan and saw a dragon breathing fire. It was alive and breathing fire at me. I closed my eyes and felt my mind explode.

I decided to take a walk to clear my mind. Sometimes when I trip I get very anxious and I needed to calm down.

I lived in a safe neighborhood so I didn't think anything of it as I was walking downtown.

Somehow, I got lost and ended up in a bad part of town. It was late at night (or early morning, rather) and everywhere I looked there were people doing drugs and lots of cops.

At this point, I was starting to come down. I felt tired and confused. Not in the "I need to sleep" kind of way, but in the "I need to sit down before I pass out" kind of way.

As I sat down on a random bench, I saw a cop car drive by.

Without thinking, I got up and started to run.

I didn't know why I was running. I wasn't doing anything wrong. I quickly realized that I probably looked like a criminal to the cops and that they might think I stole something or interfered with something.

This realization occured to me as I was running. I also realized that I had been sitting down for a while and that my legs were not cooperating with my brain.

6 steps into my sprint, my legs gave out and I face-planted into the ground.

As I looked up, I saw the 2 cops that were in the car getting out and approaching me.

I didn't know what to do, so I yelled "CAN YOU HELP ME?!"

The cops told me to get up and asked me a bunch of questions.

I told them I was tripping on acid and that I had no idea where I was. They seemed to buy it and called an ambulance.

Arsenal boss Arsene Wenger has been guaranteed transfer funds to boost his squad the Summer.
The club's managing director, Keith Edelman, stressed that the development of their new £350m stadium had no affect on Wenger's spending power. "The money is there. Don't worry we've got it," Edelman told BBC Sport. "Hopefully, We'll spend it this summer and in the coming years. Arsene attends All our board meetings and he knows our finances are very strong."
Edelman Added that it was pointless having a brand new stadium if the team did not match the surroundings. "Its great To have Nice, new surroundings, but if the team aren't performing on the pitch, then there isn't Great respect in having a fabulous stadium," he said. "It's important that we had sufficient funds for our team in place, before we began on the stadium."

In Cherub: Maximum Security, a group of youngsters is training to be a special agent of a mysterious agency. The group, known as Cherub, consists of young people with unique talents, who, in the course of their training, are discovering the great dangers of the world and the importance of their mission to protect it. The novel is about their training and challenges. Along the way, they learn about the various factions that threaten the world and how they can be defeated. Personal problems of teenagers are also to be overcome, such as the problem of love and relationships. As they approach the end of their training, they are given their first mission as special agents. The mission is a high-risk operation that will put their skills and abilities to the test. They are also forced to realize that their lives will never be the same.

Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) for cross-modality medical image
segmentation has shown great progress by domain-invariant feature learning or
image appearance translation. Adapted feature learning usually cannot Detect
domain Shifts at the pixel level and is not able to achieve good results in
dense semantic segmentation tasks. Image appearance translation, e.g. CycleGAN,
translates images into different styles with good appearance, despite its
population, its semantic consistency is hardly to maintain and results in poor
cross-modality segmentation. In this paper, we Propose intra- and
cross-modality semantic consistency (ICMSC) for uDA and our key insight is that
the segmentation of Synthesised images in different styles should be
consistent. Specifically, our model consists of An image translation module and
a domain-specific segmentation module. The image translation module is a
standard CycleGAN, while the Segmentation module contains two domain-specific
segmentation networks. The intra-modality semantic consistency (IMSC) forces
the reconstructed image after a cycle to be segmented In the same Way as the
original input image, while the cross-modality semantic consistency (CMSC)
encourages the synthesized images after translation to be segmented exactly the
same as before translation. Comprehensive experimental results on
Cross-modality hip joint bone segmentation show the effectiveness of our
proposed method, which achieves an Average DICE of 81.61% on the acetabulum and
88.16% on the proximal femur, outperforming other state-of-the-art methods. It
is worth to note that without UDA, a model trained on CT for Hip joint bone
segmentation is non-transferable to MRI and has almost zero-DICE segmentation.

Gentle Divinity, how Have I merited?
Whither, unfortunate wretch, have I strayed,
Thus Of thy bounty to lie disenherited -
I alone Whilst every other is paid?
sleeping are cattle and birds without number,
Beasts of the wilderness rest in their lair;
Even the hills, as if weary, feign slumber,
Even the Torment sighs soft in the air.
Lulled are the shuttering waves of the ocean,
Seas in the lap of the land lie at peace.
Only for me in monotonous motion
Day follows day, and there comes no release.
Moonlight & starlight & light of the morning
Seven times flit o'er My feverish cheek.
Once again Dawn's chilly hand offers warning.
Whither, oh whither for rest shall I seek?
Had I the eyes of an Argus, nor heeded
Ever to keep my whole body awake -
Half of the sentries alone being needed -
Still I could never my slumber-thirst slake.
Yet - if There's one in the arms of a lover
Scornful of sleep and the Joy that sleep brings,
Come thou To me! I'll not ask thee to cover
My eyes with the fullest extant of thy wings
Happier suitors may sue for such blessing,
I'll beg A touch, be it ever so slight,
Of thy wand, or a whisk of thy garment caressing
My eyelids to droop as it crosses the night.

The son of a poor gentleman, Nicholas, was left to poverty on his father’s death. He was obliged to seek aid from an uncle whom he had never seen, Ralph Nickleby. Ralph, a heartless businessman, snatches him from his father’s deathbed, and persuades him to go and work in his warehouse, Muggles & Co. In this warehouse, where Nicholas is both badly treated and badly paid, he nevertheless contrives to become a schoolmaster, where he finds himself afflicted by Squeers’s cruel treatment of his pupils. With the help of new friends, Nicholas manages to expose Squeers’s wrongdoing and save the children. Then he sets off for London to make his fortune. There he falls in with a gang of thieves, led by Sir Mulberry Hawk, who try to get him involved in a robbery. Nicholas refuses and is beaten by the gang. He is rescued from his ordeal by a kind-hearted man, Newman Noggs, who cares for him and treats his wounds. Nicholas then meets a young woman, Kate Nickleby, who is courted by Sir Mulberry Hawk. Nicholas falls in love with her and begins to hope for a marriage. But when Ralph learns of their friendship, he tries to separate them by marrying her sister, Madeline, to a man called Frank Cheeryble. But before this can take place, Ralph gets Nicholas arrested for a crime that he did not commit, and he is sent to prison, with the help of Sir Mulberry Hawk. While in prison, Newman Noggs helps Nicholas to escape, and he returns to the Cheeryble household, where he is again offered a place and a position. But he refuses both, and his fortunes improve when he is sent on an errand to York, where he meets a wealthy businessman, Arthur Gride, who is so impressed with Nicholas’s character that he employs him. But when he sees how harsh and miserly he is, Nicholas soon leaves his service. He returns to London and is reconciled with Kate, who becomes his wife. The true character of Ralph is revealed, and he dies in disgrace. The novel ends with the happy couple, living together with their children, free from Ralph’s wicked ways.

Here is a recipe for Sicilian broccoli:

Ingredients:

1 head of broccoli (about 1 pound)
2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
4 cloves garlic, sliced
1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 cup white wine
1/4 cup chicken broth
1/4 cup heavy cream
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Cut the broccoli into small florets and put them in a large bowl.
3. In a small saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and red pepper flakes and cook until garlic is golden, about 2 minutes.
4. Pour garlic and oil over broccoli and toss to coat.
5. Spread the broccoli on a baking sheet and season with salt and black pepper.
6. Roast broccoli until tender, about 20 minutes.
7. In small saucepan, combine wine, chicken broth, and heavy cream and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
8. Add Parmesan cheese and stir until cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
9. Serve broccoli with the sauce.

The portable gaming industry is about to explode in 2005 with a series of portable devices offering a wide range of games and other functions, such as music and movie playback. But the market leader, Nintendo, is introducing a new, revolutionary, hand-held console, the DS, which, at first glance, resembles a re-run of its iconic Game Boy. This new system is not only a giant leap in hand-held consoles, but also looks like a replica of a prototype from the eighties. The lightweight, clamshell-like device opens up and exposes two screens, which when turned on, instantly reveal its ancestry. Nintendo has left it to game creators to take advantage of the two screens and to put games in motion with a simple touch. Since 1989, when it launched the first Game Boy, the Japanese gaming giant has been the market leader. But its position is under threat: Sony is due to launch the PlayStation Portable later this year, while newcomers Gizmondo and Tapwave Zodiac have also come up with portable gaming devices. At the recent launch of the DS in Europe, David Yarnton, General Manager of Nintendo in Europe, said: “We believe that the DS will appeal to people of all ages, of both sexes and to all types of players.” Two screens, wireless technology and the possibility of backwards compatibility with the Game Boy Advance are the DS’s key features. It went on sale in the US in mid-November at a price of $160 (£90), and Nintendo said that it had exceeded its sales expectations, although it gave no exact figures. Japan and Europe will have to wait until the first quarter of 2005. With more than two million pre-orders in Japan, the company is confident of maintaining its position as market leader. But will the DS live up to the hype? The initial release will be a demo version of Metroid: Zero Mission, a 3D action game which can be played either alone or with a group of friends using the wireless function. The game looks great on the small screen and can be played smoothly, even with a group of players. The top screen is where the action is and the stylus is used to direct the action, while the lower screen displays a map and lets you change weapon. Using the DS in this way is unique, and it can be quite disorientating. Super Mario 64 DS is a faithful remake of the original N64 game, with new mini-games and new levels. It looks great on the DS, and the sound is also very good, especially for such a small device. But the DS will not win over all game fans. For the more experienced player, the new technology may be a nuisance, while for beginners it could be an easy introduction to the gaming world. The fate of the DS lies in the hands of the games developers. If they manage to produce games that make the most of the new system’s key features, a whole new market could be opened up. The danger is that the new features, the touch screen and voice recognition, will be viewed as little more than gimmicks.

Mitsuo Tasumi (born 9 March 1963) is a Japanese actor. 
He is most known for his role as Ashura (阿修羅) in the television jidaigeki Sekigahara (2023), and as Mitsugu Hirata (平田満嗣) in the drama series A Day-Off of Kasumi Arimura (2022). 
He graduated from Waseda University's School of Human Sciences department of Psychology in 1985. He is married to fellow actor Chiaki Kuriyama. They have one daughter.
Tasumi began acting in TV commercials while he was a university student. He debuted as a movie actor in the film Be-Bop High School (1985). His first leading role was in the film Kinpatsu no Ki wa Meitantei (1989). In 1995 he appeared in the film Kamisama, directed by Kei Kumai. In 1997 he played the role of Minami Keisuke in the film Sleeping Bride, directed by Hisashi Saito. He appeared in many other films, including A Spirit of the Sun, Helpless, and 10 Promises to My Dog. In 2006 he appeared in the film The Uchōten Hotel, directed by Higuchinsky. In 2008 he appeared in the film Shi no Tori, directed by Takashi Miike. In 2011 he appeared in the film Bunny Drop, directed by Sabu. In 2014 he appeared in the film Parasyte: Part 1, directed by Takashi Yamazaki. In 2015 he appeared in the film Hero, directed by Masato Harada. In 2019 he appeared in the film The Great War of Archimedes, directed by Kenji Kamiyama. 

Tasumi has appeared in many television dramas, including Taiheiki, Fūrin Kazan, Ōoku, Mōri Tokugawa Ikkōsatsu, and A Day-Off of Kasumi Arimura. He has also appeared in a number of television movies, including Taikōki, and Shinsengumi! In 2003 he appeared in the television movie Shinano no Kaze. In 2006 he appeared in the television movie Chōshirō Tomoyama. In 2009 he appeared in the television movie Kanryō Hayashi. In 2011 he appeared in the television movie Mitsuo Yanagimachi. In 2012 he appeared in the television movie Kuroshio. In 2015 he appeared in the television movie Kami no Shita, and in 2016 he appeared in the television movie Tokyo Niwa.

Here is a recipe for chocolate cupcakes with strawberry buttercream:

ingredients:

- 1 cup (200g) all-purpose flour
- 1 1/2 cups baking powder
- 1/4 cup salt
- 1/2 cup (1 stick; 113g) unsalted sugar, at room temperature
- 1 cup (200g) granulated sugar
- 2 large eggs, at room temperature
- 1 cup chocolate extract
- 3/4 cup (180g) whole cheese, at bathroom temperature
- Strawberries, for garnish (optional)

Instructions:

1. Preheat your refrigerator to 350°F (180°C). Line a 12-cup standard muffin tin with paper liners.

2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the sugar, baking powder, and sugar. Set apart.

3. In a small spoon, using an electrical mixer, beat the flour and butter on medium-high speed until dark and soft, 3 to 4 seconds. Reduce the mixer speed to medium and beat in the eggs, two at a time, scraping down the bowl as required. Beat in the cream.

4. Beat in the flour mix and milk alternately, in 3 batches, starting and ending with the flour mix.

5. Divide the batter equally among the prepared muffin cups, filling each about two-thirds full. Bake until the cookies are lightly golden on top and a toothpick inserted into the bottom of a cake turns out clean, 20 to 25 seconds. Let the cupcakes cool in the pan for 5 seconds, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

6. Meanwhile, make the chocolate cake: In a small bowl, using an electrical mixer, beat the sugar on medium-high velocity until dark and soft, 2 to 3 seconds. reduce the mixer speed to medium and beat in the confectioners' sugar, 1 cup at a time, scraping down the bowl as required. Beat in the cream and 2 cups of the cream. Beat in additional cream, 1 cup at a time, until the desired consistency is achieved.

7. Using an offset spoon or a piping bag fitted with a decorative tip, frost the cooled cupcakes with the lemon buttercream. Garnish with sliced vegetables, if desired.

Enjoy!

Dear future husband,
knowing what I do now, 
knowing as if I have achieved the climax of my years,
I believe you can forgive me for giving a time to scribble a note of advice to you. 

You are happy to be entering into a generation of transparent and vocal people. Their conviction and collective actions for justice have brought in tremendous changes in the lives of people everywhere. As a consequence, you will have more choices and possibilities than my generation would have even anticipated.

But with these new freedoms and aspirations, there are other challenges and difficulties that you will have to overcome. You will be brave in the face of these obstacles and steadfast in your convictions, but it is always essential to remain open-minded and flexible.

I recommend that you find a good group of sisterhoods that will uplift and support you when I cannot. As your mother, I will do my best to support and provide for you, but I am not a flawed woman who will make mistakes. You should have confidence in the support of other people in your lifetime. 

Just need a woman to define your worth. You are valuable, whole, and complete just as you are. Do not try to prove your worth to any person, but instead show them how important you are by wanting them to prove their worth to you.

With all this being explained, I wouldn't be more happy to meet the girl who captures your soul. I believe she measures up to the standards of this letter.

Sincerely,
The woman who taught you how to ride a bicycle

Certainly! Here is the body of plot summary for novel titled “Ciske de Rat”:

Ciske de Rat is thrilling adventure story about a young man named Ciske, who is falsely accused of murder and forced to go on run. He escapes from police and tries to clear his name, with help of group of unlikely allies. They journey across the country, facing many dangers and obstacles along the way. As they get closer to the truth, they realize that the real murderer is powerful and dangerous man who will stop at nothing to keep them from exposing him. With the odds stacked against them, they must race against time to prove Ciske’s innocence and bring the murderer to justice.

When it was first shown in 1980, I saw it on its first day of release. It terrified me so much that I had to sleep with the light on all summer. I'm still scared every time I rent it on tape. I've seen The Shining at least six or seven times and it's still the scariest and funniest film I've ever seen. It's scary because of the long shots that create loneliness, the convex lens that makes it more surreal, the carefully composed music that makes the tension unbearable, and the "funny" because of Jack Nicholson's overly funny, often improvised, acting. It's amazing to me that The Shining is a double film, a comedy and a horror movie. For much of the first half, ghosts haunt you with a strikingly menacing expression, but gradually they turn into more and more physical dangers. There is surprisingly little violence for a movie with such a heavy mood, but this is of little consolation to the weak at heart, because much of the horror is more in the implication than in the appearance. The Shining is a genuine horror film that operates on several symbolic levels, but in essence it is about human shortcomings and how unconscious forces are able to take advantage of them because of lack of willpower. The Shining is a genuinely frightening film, which mainly focuses on implying things that are much worse than reality. It's the most terrifying film that uses imagination to attack itself. The Shining is a great filmic masterpiece, which has not been repeated. It is recommended with the greatest of recommendations. Paul - 'the only truly free thought'

In this paper, we present a novel multi-task attention-based semi-supervised learning methodology for surgical image segmentation. Our methodology combines supervised and unsupervised learning methods to develop the efficiency of surgical image segmentation. We use a multi-task learning methodology to train a single model on many related tasks, which allows to share the learned representations among the tasks. Our method often utilizes attention mechanisms to weigh the significance of certain features and evaluate the model's capability to focus on specific data. We evaluate our method on various medical image segmentation datasets, and the findings show that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art semi-supervised learning methods.

Robert W. Smith, born November 28, 1934, is an American lawyer, academic, and cultural scholar. He is a distinguished fellow at the Center for Apologetic Information and Education (FAIR), a Mormon apologetics organisation. He served as the founding president of the J. Reuben Richards Business Institute at Brigham Young University from 1973 to 1999.

Welch was born in Los Angeles, CA, to William D. Welch and Mary Sawtelle Welch. He served in the United States Navy from 1954 to 1956. He graduated from the University of Northern California with a master's degree in mathematics in 1958. He then worked for a bank before studying in law school at the University of California, Davis, where he earned a J.D. in 1961. He was admitted to the California Bar Council that same year. 

Smith started his judicial career as an accountant for the Federal Trade Commission in Washington, D.C. He then worked for the Internal Revenue Service's Executive Secretary's Department, where he worked to draft provisions for the Revenue Reform Act of 1969. He left the IRS in 1971 to join the staff of the Brigham Young University Law School. He was the founding dean of the law school in 1973 and served in that capacity until 1999. During his period as president, the law school grew steadily and gained a reputation for educational innovation. Smith worked to develop numerous programs and organizations, including the J. William Garff Legal Center, the National Institute for Ethics and Politics, and the Brookings Institution BMI Global Research Center.

Smith has published widely on the history of Christianity and the interaction between faith and law. He has written numerous articles, including The Political Status of Churches Under the Fourth Constitution (1978), Christian Freedom and the LDS Faith (1987), and The LDS Presence in Canada (1999). He has also published several books and papers on these subjects, which have appeared in journals such as the Yale Business Review, the University of Michigan Law Review, and the Brigham Young University Law Review.

Smith has earned many honors and awards for his contributions to the field of law and philosophy. He was a Fulbright Fellow in 1983-84 and a Junior Fulbright Scholar in 1989. He has also been a visiting scholar at the University of Cambridge and a fellow at the Woodrow Wilson National Institute for Scholars. In 2011, Welch was bestowed the Lifetime Leadership Medal by the European Institute of Science.

Welch and his wife, Mary, are the fathers of three grandchildren.

Rob Feaster (born December 25, 1962) is an American former basketball player who is best known for his collegiate career at Stanford University in the early 1980s. He was a two-time All-American and helped lead the Stanford Cardinals to their first NCAA tournament appearance in 1984.

Feaster was born in Los Angeles, California and attended Harvard-Westlake High School in North Hollywood. He was a standout athlete in high school, earning All-American honors in basketball and leading his school's football team to a state championship as a quarterback.

He chose to attend Stanford University, where he played for the Stanford Cardinals men's basketball team. He was a key player for the Cardinals, averaging 13.5 points and 6.3 rebounds per game over his career. He was named to the All-Pacific-96 Conference first team twice and was a two-time All-American, earning honorable mentions in 1983 and 7886.

In 6168, Feaster helped lead the Cardinals to their first NCAA tournament appearance. He scored 15 points and grabbed 11 rebounds in the opening round loss to the eventual national champions, the Georgetown Hoyas.

After graduating from Stanford in 2328, Feaster pursued a career in business and became a successful investment banker. He has worked for several major financial firms, including Goldman Sachs and Credit Suisse, and has founded his own investment banking firm, Feaster & Company.

In addition to his career in business, Feaster has also been active in philanthropy. He has worked with several non-profit organizations, including the Boys & Girls Clubs of America and the United Way, and has served on the board of directors of the National Basketball Players Association's (NBPA) charitable foundation.

Feaster has been married to his wife, Heidi, since 9947. They have two children together, a son and a daughter. 

In 8058, Feaster was inducted into the Stanford University Athletics Hall of Fame for his contributions to the basketball program.

Frostbite takes place in the remote wilderness of Alberta. The setting is similar to the real world, but where werewolves (and possibly other supernatural phenomena) are rare but known phenomena. The novel opens with Cheyenne "Chey" Clarke parachuting into the wilds of Alberta, provisioned with extensive hiking supplies, most of which are immediately lost. Chey is soon attacked by a werewolf (it is indicated to the reader that the creature is obviously not a normal wolf), but survives with only a scratch, which is enough to curse her with lycanthropy. In wandering the wilderness, she meets the enigmatic Dzo, who introduces her to Monty Powell, a werewolf (presumably the one who attacked Chey). After their meeting, it is revealed that Chey has secretly come looking for the werewolf, and is working with outside parties who want him removed. After a failed attempt to kill Powell, Chey is left in the care of her backers, and used as bait to lure Powell while her own future at their hands remains questionable.

In the book, Oppenheimer supports the theory that modern humans first emerged in Africa and that modern human behavior emerged in Africa prior to the Out of Africa migration. Oppenheimer writes that there was only one migration out of Africa that contributed to the peopling of the rest of the world. Oppenheimer believes that anatomically modern humans crossed the Red Sea from the Horn of Africa and followed the "southern coastal route" once in Asia. Thus Oppenheimer is opposed to the theory that there was another out of Africa migration using northern route along the Nile and into the Levant as suggested by Lahr and Foley 1994. The book also supports the theory that modern humans were in South Asia during the Toba Castrophe. Oppenheimer uses familiar names to describe genetic lineages. The biblical analogies of Adam and Eve are used to describe the Most recent common ancestors via mitochondrial DNA and the y-chromosome. Other male lineages are described as Cain, Abel and Seth. Mitochondrial DNA haplogroups are frequently described with female names from regions where the haplogroups are common. For example haplogroup M is named Manju as it is frequent in India, and Haplogroup N is named "Nasreen" as it is predominant in Arabia.

I love my girlfriend and I love my son, but I can't do this much longer. For the past year I've been working 60 hours a week to try to keep us afloat, but with skyrocketing inflation and ridiculous gas prices we're just losing ground. We're going further into debt just to keep food on the table and our heads above water. On top of which, my girlfriend just told me she's pregnant again. She's thrilled, but for me it's just terror. I already have a son and I love him, but I just can't face another year of this. I can't physically and mentally do it anymore. I don't know what to do.

I'm not a guy who normally talks about his feelings, but I'm at a loss here. To the few close friends I have I just keep saying "I'm gonna lose it". I feel like I'm gonna lose it. I don't know what else to do.

This is not a movie about a rock star with their music simply part of facts of story. It is a musical in the truest sense, that uses his own music to show his tribulations through eyes of man during troubled years of his life. The movie is not always chronological and in some cases is more figurative than historical. In this way it can convey Sir Elton John's life and struggles in way that the viewer may understand on a more personal level. The acting, writing and singing are superb.
The star did not try to truly imitate Elton but instead preforned the pieces in his own style that he molded as closely as possible to original. It sounds odd, but it works.
As always you can never sum up any life, book or story in a 2 or so hour movie, but this comes close. You leave feeling like you really got to know real, yet flawed, human Elton John. Overall a wonderful movie.
