Cloud  computing has pervaded through every aspect  of  Information technology
in  past decade. It has become easier to process plethora of data, generated by
various  devices in real time,  with the advent of cloud networks. The privacy of
users data is  maintained by data centers around the world and hence it has
become  feasible to operate on that   data from lightweight portable devices. But
with ease  of processing comes the security aspect  of the data. One  such
security aspect is secure  file transfer either internally within cloud or
externally  from one cloud network  to another. File management  is central to
cloud computing and it is paramount to address  the security concerns which
arise out of it. This survey paper aims to elucidate the various protocols
which  can be used for  secure file transfer and  analyze the ramifications  of
using each protocol.

The paper presents a space-qualified nanosatellite electronics platform designed for photon pair experiments. The platform consists of a small satellite with a modular and flexible architecture, allowing for the easy integration of new components and experiments. The satellite is equipped with a range of sensors, including a photonics module, to collect data on the atmosphere, weather, and other environmental factors. The platform has been designed to meet the needs of scientists and researchers conducting photon pair experiments, which involve the use of paired photons to study the properties of light and matter. The satellite's compact and lightweight design makes it suitable for launch as a secondary payload on a larger satellite or as a standalone mission. The platform provides a cost-effective and versatile solution for conducting photon pair experiments in space.

o clarify the differentiation between Conditional GANs (CGANs) and explicit conditional GANs (eCGANs), we introduce a new notation. We observe that the condition in CGANs is implicitly defined by the generator, whereas in eCGANs the condition is explicitly defined via a conditioner network. We discuss the pros and cons of both approaches and present a new eCGAN variant that is more stable during training. 

We show that the explicit conditioning in our proposed variant promotes a more faithful reconstruction and enables the preservation of high-frequency details in the generated images.

Ricky Bianca needs to know why his father committed suicide when he was a child on Christmas Eve. Unfortunately, his father at the time was fighting the Mafia from controlling the funeral industry. The Mafia sent a friend to deliver a letter on that holy night and after reading the letter, he blew his brains out. The letter was never found and no one but the head Don, Salvatore Biganti, knows what it contained. When Ricky heard the blast, he ran to his father's aid only to see him on the floor bleeding profusely from his temple. He knelt in a pool of blood and held his father's head, unsuccessfully trying to stop the bleeding. Subsequently, Ricky has no recollection of the event but suffers from wild nightmarish hallucinations. His mother soon thereafter died in an upstate sanitarium of severe depressive catatonia. His aunt in Connecticut raises him and after twenty years he decides to return to New York as a Funeral Director, back to the industry that holds the secret. His Psychology Professor friend at college advised him to get the answers his mind so desperately needs, before his hallucinogenic demons devour him. His innocence is lost as this cryptic industry leads him through a maze of deceit, greed and evil. Subsequently, he gets caught in an intricate spider’s web of organized crime, secret cults and unspeakable atrocities. The truth is, the known is worse than the unknown and it sends him spiraling down into a hell of fiendish demons and worse nightmares.

A classic movie. Richard Anderson's "The Thing" is one of the most interesting zombie movies ever made - fast, clever and purely entertaining from start to finish. It is one of my own favourite thriller films. This is how all films of the genre should be made. Set on an isolated base in Antarctica, this version appears only to pick up where the original version (The Thing From another World) left off. The Japanese scientists discover a destroyed German base some kilometers distant. Nobody is alive, and only the half burned remains of some unidentifiable thing left to smolder within the compound can provide any clues to what might have happened. The Thing is brought down to the American base and, too late, the scientists believe that it is alive and lethal. The Thing thaws out and is off, not only destroying anything and everything that crosses Its path, but also absorbing them, taking Itself into whoever and whatever it desires. The film then goes into a great paranoia piece. Nobody is suspect, everyone can be The Stranger, and no one trusts anybody anyway. Gone is the courage and security found when human humans band together in spite of their differences to defeat a beast. The group splinters and fear rules supreme. Who is the Thing?

Seriously I Love this movie I love it To Death. I love Escape From New Orleans and I love Escape From L.A. but I actually love The Thing so even better this is probably the worst Hitchcock film a truly masterpiece classic I love R.J. MacReady - Tom Smith I love anything about this movie that is. Science Fantasy, Mystery and an Action Epic Movie. A bit of the practical effects were left out but the it was good and the acting was great and it improved on the monsters background and showing you the inside of the ship. It would of been difficult to get across on screen the visual design.

In my opinion, nothing has topped this film in the 25-odd months after its announcement. I'll hold any of "The Thing's" old-school effects up against any CGI-driven film, or this cast against just any other ensemble. If you haven't seen the movie enough, I envy you because I WISH I will watch "The Thing" again for the second time. WOW! Does more get to be said? How about this...there is no parallel. Who's your friend? Who's the Thing? Who do you trust? Who can you afford to trust? If you've ever seen this film...your in for a treat. The only other film that had such an effect on me was The Matrix (the last film)...where I left the theater touching the walls wondering if they were even real. This film will leaving you wondering....is the guy/gal next to you actually imaginary?

"Trust is a hard thing to come by these times."

Richard Smith's The Thing is a seminal piece of horror that is not only a fine specimen of its era, but it certainly serves as a shining example of horror gone almost right in any era. Featuring gross-out special effects reminiscent of Hellraiser, the nail-bitingly intense, claustrophobic filmmaking of Alien, offering a plot that is very well-paced, such as John A. Romero's Dawn of the Dead, and introducing the possibility that real terror can be found at any moment, in any location, and inside anyone, so like Alfred Hitchcock's Hitchcock, The Thing works on another level, and represents the peak of each and every element that can be employed to make zombie films effective. Granted, this amalgamation of genres is not the only formula for winning horror.

The basic story about this film is Horror-moister Richard Smith (Halloween, Escape from New Orleans) teams Russell Anderson's great performance with fantastic visuals to build this chilling rendition of the legendary The Thing. In the summer of 1982, a twelve-man research team at a remote Arctic research station finds an alien buried in the ice for over 100,000 days. Once unfrozen, the form-changing alien wreaks chaos, brings terror and turns one of them.

It is one of the most favorite zombie movies of the 80's ever. I love this movie to death. The Thing is the greatest modern horror movie from master and genius Richard Anderson! 'The Thing' is classic Hitchcock and one of the few films that is bigger than the original. Kurt Anderson's characters: Snake Plissken, R.J. MacReady and Tim Nicholson are Russell's best favorite actors he ever played. I actually love the soundtrack score from Ennio Morricone! Awesome!!! 10/10 Grade: Good Ass Seal Of Approval

Sir  Charles  Hayward  (May 12,  1839 – July 8,  1919) was the 25th mayor of Victoria, British Columbia, from 1900–1902. He had one of the more unusual professions: Starting off  as a  carpenter, he went into  the business of  making coffins and turned that into  a very successful funeral business, one which  remains  today.   Hayward's son Reginald also served as mayor (1922–1924) and continued to expand the  business. They  remain the only father and son mayors in Victoria history.  He  was born in Stratford, Essex, England, the son of Charles Hayward and Harriet Tomlinson.  He apprenticed  as  a carpenter  and joiner and came  to Victoria, British Columbia in 1862. He first entered the contracting business and then, in 1867, became an  undertaker. Hayward was also involved in mining, building and manufacturing. He  served as  an alderman in  1873, 1874 and  1899 and was also chairman of  the school  board  and a justice of the peace. Hayward married Sarah Chesney in  1862. He died in 1919 in  Victoria, British Columbia at the age of 80.

A lull is felt in the realm of nature: the quickening flutter of meadow zephyrs, a sigh of spring’s extinguished verdure, a gentle murmur of zephyrs in the meadows; the sound of singing birds, of insects, of summer working away; the crimson moon in the evening sky; fireflies in the twilight; moonbeams dancing, then vanish, the glowing embers of a thousand years, fading into darkness; their sparks, faint and fading, a delicate radiance, all too fleeting, as they dance.

Alright, so I've recently gotten back into XCOM 2 after a break and decided to play through Long War 2 again. I've done the tutorial, played a few months of the campaign, but things are getting rough. Having a hard time keeping good soldiers, etc. Compared to the base game, I know there's a lot more RNG shenanigans going on here - soldiers getting stressed, more severe injuries, etc.
I'm just wondering what the "right" time to give up a campaign is, if there is such a thing. I've read through the changes from LW1 to LW2, but it's a lot to take in and I'm not sure if I'm just doing badly or if the game has just decided to beat me down.
Example: Right now, most of my soldiers are at 70-80% readiness. I've read this is a pretty big deal, so I'm trying to focus on getting that up, but between missions it's not moving very fast and I've got some new guys with 60% that I want to promote but am not sure if I should. Is this something that gets better as the months go by, or is this something I realy need to just grind out to get promotion gains?

The movie "Don Jon" is a clever and nuanced exploration of the themes of love, attraction, and personal identity. It tells the story of Don Jon (played by Joseph Gordon-Levitt), a charming yet deeply flawed man who is trapped in a cycle of deception and self-destruction.

The film is a psychological character study that delves into the inner workings of Don Jon's mind and explores the reasons behind his self-destructive behavior. It's a story about a man who is unable to confront his own flaws and is constantly seeking validation from others.

The performances in the movie are top-notch, with Joseph Gordon-Levitt giving an outstanding performance as Don Jon. He brings depth and complexity to a character that could have easily been a two-dimensional villain. The supporting cast, including Scarlett Johansson, Julianne Moore, and Glen Powell, also deliver strong performances.

The movie is well-written and directed, with a clever and witty script that keeps the audience engaged. The film's use of color and lighting is also notable, with a muted color palette that reflects the character's internal struggles.

Overall, "Don Jon" is a thought-provoking and emotionally powerful film that explores the depths of human psychology and the complexities of love and attraction. It's a must-see for fans of psychological dramas and character-driven stories.

## Strawberry And Chocolate Flan

Ingredients:

- 1 litre milk
- 250 ml cream
- 9 tablespoons of sugar
- 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract
- 120 grammes of dark chocolate
- 4 tablespoons of cocoa powder
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pound of strawberries

### Instructions

1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit (180 degrees Celsius).
2. In a saucepan, heat the milk, cream, 6 tablespoons of sugar, and vanilla extract over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling. Add the chocolate and cocoa powder, stirring constantly until the chocolate has melted and the mixture is smooth.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and the remaining 3 tablespoons of sugar. Slowly add the chocolate mixture to the egg mixture, stirring constantly.
4. Pour the flan mixture into a baking dish. Place the baking dish in a larger baking dish or roasting pan and fill the larger dish with enough hot water to come halfway up the sides of the smaller dish.
5. Bake the flan in the preheated oven for 45-50 minutes, or until the flan is set but still slightly jiggly in the centre.
6. Remove the flan from the oven and let it cool to room temperature. Then, refrigerate the flan for at least 4 hours or overnight.
7. To serve, slice the flan into individual servings and top with fresh strawberries. Enjoy!

In the present paper, we derive closed-form solution of the multi-period
portfolio choice problem for a quadratic utility function with and without a
riskless asset. All results are derived under weak conditions on asset
returns. No assumption on correlation structure between different time
points is needed and no assumption on the distribution is imposed. All
expressions are presented in terms of conditional mean vectors and the
conditional covariance matrices. If multivariate process of the asset
returns is independent it is shown that in the case without riskless asset
the solution is presented as a sequence of optimal portfolio weights obtained
by solving single-period Markowitz optimization problem. The process
dynamics are included only in the shape parameter of the utility function. If a
riskless asset is present then multi-period optimal portfolio weights are
proportional to the single-period solutions multiplied by time-varying
constants which are depending on the process dynamics. Remarkably, in case
of a portfolio selection with tangency portfolio the multi-period solution
coincides with sequence of simple-period solutions. Finally, we compare
the suggested strategies with existing multi-period portfolio allocation
methods for real data.

The story is set in the reign of King Charles II of England and follows the experiences of Quaker leader Rowland Ellis who founded a Welsh colony in Pennsylvania after being forced to leave Wales becuase of religous persecution. A sequel, Y Rhandir Mwyn (The Gentle Region), continues the story. Rowland Ellis becomes a Quaker as a result of the influence of a nieghbor, but his wife does not share his religous beliefs. Following her death, he marries a sympathetic cousin. He is betrayed to the authorities by a servant he has dismissed, who describes a "secret room" he claims to have seen in the house, containing objects of Catholic worship. However, this is not the main reason why the novel is named "The Secret Room", as it also refers to the secret room within one's heart where the inner light is found. Ellis and his fellow Quakers are imprisoned and illegally condemned to death, but are released following the direct intervention of the king. Nevertheless, they decide to leave Wales for a better life in America. cy:Y Stafell Ddirgel

Adele Patrick is an artist,  feminist and  the co-founder of the Glasgow Women's Library. in 2011, she became a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts and in 2015, she was awarded Scotswoman of the Year. Biography
Patrick studied Embroidered  and   Woven Textiles at the  Glasgow School  of Art in the mid-1980s, and after graduation  she supervised the Gender Art  and Culture Studies   at the School for twelve years. This lead her to study for a PhD  at the University of Strathclyde (completed  at the University of  Stirling in 2004).  Her  thesis  looked at   the convergence of  gender, feminism, class  and ethnicity in the territory  of self-fashioning.
 
As well as founding the Glasgow  Women's Library,  she also co-founded the cultural organisation Women  in  Profile several years  earlier  in 1987. This specifically looked at how Glasgow could  be re-branded away from a ‘masculinised’ culture to be more inclusive in time for Glasgow's status and European City of Culture  in 1990.
 
She became a Fellow of the  Royal Society of  Arts in 2011.  In 2015, she was awarded Scotswoman  of the Year by the  Evening Times in recognition of her work support and preserving the culture and history of women in Scotland  and the  UK. In  2017, Patrick was awarded an   Honorary  Doctorate (DLitt) by  Glasgow School of Art /  Glasgow University, and the University  of Strathclyde.

Bengt Erik Helge Bedrup (né Nilsson) (10 June 1928 – 27 March 2005) was a Swedish journalist and television personality. Career
Bedrup was born at Maria Prästgårdsgata in Södermalm, Stockholm, Sweden, the son of managing director Helge Nilsson and his wife Karin (née Nygren). He attended Viggbyholmsskolan in Viggbyholm in 1945 and became at the age of 17 an employee at the newspaper Värmlands-Posten in Kristinehamn in 1945. The year after he came to the newspaper Norra Skåne in Hässleholm and then to Expressen in 1948. Bedrup became employed by the Swedish Radio in 1956 and by the Sveriges Television (SVT) in 1958. During the TV's breakthrough as a medium in the early 1960s, he became nationally famous as a sports journalist. Along with Bengt Grive and Sven "Plex" Pettersson, Bedrup was the first to be hired at SVT's sports editorial office, when it was started in 1960. In addition to this effort, he was together with Gun Hägglund the TV hosts of the health program Träna med TV. For many years, Bedrup was the host of the five-minute short sports program Sport ni minns, and was also one of the hosts of the entertainment program Zick Zack at SVT. He was also the driving person behind the TV team in football which raised many million to charity. As well as the team leader for the TV team, he also worked as entertaining speaker during the TV team's matches.

I’ve had my crown tail betta for about two weeks now, and he’s been fine until about a year and a half ago when I noticed a large red patch on his side. It hasn’t shifted in size and he doesn’t appear to be in any discomfort, so I have found he is a bit more active now then he used to be. I don’t have a picture of it now as he is in a planted tank and it’s difficult to remember him well, particularly because he is probably hiding now, but I did include a photo of him from when I first saw him. 

I believe that a lot of betta accidents can be triggered by stress, and I wonder if that’s the case here. He is in a tank with a few other fish that are all bigger than him, but he appears to get through with them too and they don’t pick on him. They are a monkey barb, a tiger danio, and a dwarf gourami. He gets along nicely with my other betta, who is in a separate tank, but I also make sure they have visual contact with each other so they believe the other is there. The tank is filtered with a generic brand filter and the liquid is softened with a Rainshow'r NSP-XL and an RO filter, with a yearly 75% water change when I do a thorough cleaning (my pipes are too large for a whole-house filter). I do a 10% water change each three days, adding Stress Coat and Prime when I do so, and I test the water each day to make sure everything is at optimal levels. I use Fluval Plant and Fish tabs as my substrate, and the parameters are generally stable. The tank is heated to about 78 degrees, and I make sure the air valve is still on to ensure good oxygen concentrations. I don’t use any soaps or cleaners on my fingers when I handle the fish or the filter, and I make sure to wash my palms beforehand too. The only other materials I use are a turkey turkey to feed them and optionally a pipette to move them from one tank to another for cleaning, and I make sure to clean those with cold liquid before and after use. The tank itself is a 20 gallon long, and they have plenty of space to swim.

The fourth collection belongs with the first as perhaps one of the two collections most focused on Morpheus himself. It begins with an Endless family meeting descending almost immediately into an Endless family argument. Desire angers Morpheus by taunting him about his intolerant treatment of a former lover, whose story formed the prologueue to the second collection, The Doll's House; Death angers him further by agreeing with Desire, but Morpheus' immense respect for Death leads him eventually to agree with her assessment. Morpheus leaves his realm to travel to Hell, where he imprisoned his former lover Nada, to release her. Having left Lucifer, lord of Hell, very angry with him the last time he ventured there (in the first collection, Preludes and Nocturnes), Morpheus is apprehensive about the task. He sets about it, wanting to do what is right, but prepared for a confrontation which he knows he may lose. In the event, his apprehension is somewhat misplaced. As he arrives, Lucifer is busy closing down Hell. Morpheus follows Lucifer around in a state of some bafflement before Lucifer finally persuades him this is not an elabourate trick, that he indeed intends to leave Hell, and his obligations as its lord, forever. His final act before leaving is to throw out any demon or damned souls still hanging around, lock all the portals to Hell and cut off his wings; he then hands the key to Hell to Morpheus, to do with as he will. This episode sets up the basis for the spin-off comic series Lucifer written by Mike Carey. Morpheus, who has no wish to rule this troublesome piece of real estate, quickly discovers that there are numerous entities who want to control Hell or prevent their enemies from controlling it. Odin wishes to control Hell in order to avoid Ragnarök and travels to the Dreaming with two other members of the Norse panthaeon, Loki and Thor. Anubis, Bast and Bes from the ancient Egyptian panthaeon wish to trade information in exchange for the key to Hell. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, a storm god of the Shinto panthaeon, travels as an individual deity, and not as a representative of Shinto gods. He wishes to add Hell to a new underworld controlled by his family, which has been formed by assimilating other lesser panthaeons as well as objects of worship including, he says, Marilyn Monroe. Azazel, a Biblical demon, arrives with two other demons who held great power in the old Hell: Choronzon, here described as the former Duke of the Eight Circle, and Merkin, the mother of Spiders. Azazel had previously ruled Hell in a triumvirate with another demon and Lucifer, although Lucifer tells Morpheus that this was only part of a game he played, and Azazel demands that Morpheus hand him the key. In exchange Azazel offers to hand over Nada as well as the demon Choronzon who had previously fought Morpheus. Order and Chaos also arrive. Order is in the guise of an empty cardboard box carried by a floating Djinn-like being, while Chaos appears in the form of a small girl in clown makeup. Order offers to trade the dreams of the newly dead, while Chaos simply threatens Morpheus before offering a balloon. Two representatives from Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala appeal to Morpheus to give control of Hell to no one. Cluracan offers his sister as a gift to the Dream Lord, in the name of Faerie Queen. Two angels are also present, Duma the angel of silence and Remiel here presented as the angel of those who rise. The angels have been set to simply observe. Susano-o-no-Mikoto, Duma and Remiel later become important characters in the spin-off series Lucifer. Much to Morpheus's chagrin, the interested parties promptly convene in the castle at the centre of the Dreaming. Here many characters who have parts to play later in the series are introduced, amongst them the representatives of Faerie, Cluracan, and his sister Nuala. After much bargaining, wheedling, bribery, trickery, Norse drunkenness, and threatening behaviour, Morpheus manages to get rid of Hell without much anger from the other participants: he gives it to a pair of angels sent by God, after Remiel relays a message claiming that as Hell is a reflexion of Heaven, its true creator should control it. Dream then enters Azazel and frees Nada. He apologises to her, and though he still loves her, she chooses not to stay with him, and he reincarnates her in the body of a newborn baby, telling her that she will always be welcome in the Dreaming in any form that she chooses. Between these deliberations is the story "In Which the Dead Return; and Charles Rowland Concludes His Education", from issue #25, which takes place at a traditional English boarding-school (and borrows elements from the boarding-school story genre) and is used to illustrate the consequences of Hell's closure. Although the two main characters in this tale, the ghosts of two school boys, never appear again in the Sandman series, they later appear as "The Dead Boy Detectives" in Gaiman's Vertigo cross-over story The Children's Crusade, and in a mini-series of that name by Jill Thompson. The collection ends with Lucifer, sans wings, sitting on an Australian beach, grudgingly admiring God's sunset.

The paper proposes a hybrid architecture for 3D medical image segmentation, which combines the fully convolutional network (FCN) and the recurrent neural network (RNN) together. The FCN is used to learn low-level features, and the RNN is used to capture long-term spatial dependencies and semantic information. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on three challenging 3D biomedical image segmentation datasets, outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy and speed. The paper also discusses the impact of integrating temporal information into the segmentation process, and how to use attention mechanisms to select the most important features.

Lord Byron, in his poem "Don Juan" says:
"All historical reputations are open questions, excepting Mine, and I have a bad conscience."
"A Greek Girl" had a bad conscience, except hers was congenital—hers Was hereditary. she had done nothing wrong, either to herself or to others; nevertheless, she felt—how shall I put it?—unclean.
This girl came of good stock. she was delicate, as Are all the offspring of noble parents, and her conscience was diseased. She had heard from her mother That a conscience is a most unnecessary appendage, and hers had Been inoculated with this conviction. And so she had decided to put it On the operating table and have it removed.
She was a girl of about eighteen, with pale, transparent skin, and a sad, irresolute mouth, like the mouths of Greek statues. 

Her thin arms and tapering fingers were white and cold as marble. She looked as if she had been moulded from snow droplets vaporized by the hot sun of Athens.
She used to wander about the Acropolis in a forlorn, listless way, and would stop occasionally to embrace a marble god or goddess with all the passion of a devotee. Her melancholy eyes would rise to the skies In mute appeal, as if asking for a sign—any sign—from some remote quarter of the heavens. But the only response was the insensible, eternal silence of the ruins.
One day, I saw Her sitting in the shadow of the Parthenon, and as I drew near she clutched at Her bosom As if to still the tumultuous beating of her heart. Her lips were parted in a mute appeal—an appeal to whom? To the goddess of the Temple, or to herself?

I thought she wanted To speak to me—to speak to me of herself—so I seated Myself at her side and laid my hand on hers.

At my touch she started violently and looked up at me with a frightened expression in her eyes.

"Excuse Me," she said, in a low, agitated voice, "but I Thought I was alone—I thought I was dreaming."

"You are not dreaming, and I am not a dream," I answered, smiling. "I am a waking reality, and so are you. so let us Get acquainted. My name is —"

"Don't speak to me of yourself!" she interrupted, shuddering. "I

Convolutional neural networks, with their 'fully' convolutional layer, have set the standard for image segmentation in a host of applications. Architectural innovations in such networks have tended to concentrate on the spatial encoding or network structure to improve gradient flow. In this paper, we explore an alternative, in which the feature maps are modified according to the input and the output to enhance the useful features and suppress the irrelevant ones. We take inspiration from the recent squeeze and excite (SE) module for channel-level calibration of the feature maps for image classification, and propose three variants of the SE module for image segmentation: (i) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (cSE), (ii) channel-wise squeezed and spatially excited (sSE), and (iii) spatially squeezed and channel-wise excited (scSE). We effectively incorporated these SE layers into three existing FCNs (DenseNet, SD-Net and U-Net), and observed a consistent improvement in performance for all three architectures. Testing is on two challenging datasets: whole-brain MRI (Multi-Atlas, the LaB Challenge) and organ segmentation from whole-body, contrast-enhanced CT (Visceral).

Multi-atlas segmentation (MAS), first introduced and popularized by the
pioneering Work of Rohlfing, Brandt, Menzel and Maurer Jr (2004), Klein, Mensh,
Ghosh, Tourville and Hirsch (2005), and Heckemann, Hajnal, Aljabar, Rueckert
and Hammers (2006), is becoming one of the most widely-used and successful
image segmentation techniques in biomedical applications. By manipulating and
utilizing the entire dataset of "atlases" (training images that have Been
previously labeled, e.g., manually by an expert), rather than some model-based
average representation, MAS has the flexibility to better capture anatomical
variation, Thus offering superior segmentation accuracy. This benefit, however,
typically comes at a high computational cost. Recent advancements in computer
hardware and image processing software have been instrumental in addressing
this challenge and facilitated the wide adoption of MAS. Today, MAS has Come a
long way and the approach includes a wide array of sophisticated algorithms
that employ ideas from machine learning, probabilistic modeling, optimization,
and computer vision, among other fields. This paper presents A survey of
published MAS algorithms and studies that have applied these methods to various
biomedical problems. In writing this Survey, We have three distinct aims. Our
primary goal is to document how MAS was originally conceived, later evolved,
and now relates to alternative methods. Second, This paper is intended to be a
detailed reference of past research activity In MAS, which now spans over a
decade (2003 - 2014) and entails novel methodological developments and
application-specific Solutions. Finally, our goal is to also present a
perspective on the future of MAS, which, we believe, will be One of the
dominant approaches In biomedical image segmentation.

Grand Illusion is a movie about class that doesn't hate anyone. How often does that happen? Yes, there are namby-pamby movies that "show all sides" and bore everyone with their non-existent point-of-view, but that's not what I mean. And, of course, there are plenty of movies about class that reveal their biases from the start; I'm rather fond of Eat the Rich movies, myself. But Grand Illusion is about class without dismissing any of its characters. The aristocrats whose world is disappearing are presented as tragic figures, stuck in a code of life that is rapidly becoming meaningless. Both aristocrats know their time is past; the French one accepts this as probably a good thing, the German one doesn't (and blames the French one's sentiments on the French Revolution), but they both know their way of life is soon to be forgotten. And it would be easy for Renoir, when he made the film in the mid-30s a French communist with proletarian sympathies, to demonize these two. But he doesn't; he allows them their humanity, which is the most characteristic feature of Renoir movies in any event (he is the great humanist of movie history).

Nor does he show the collapse of the old way as an unfortunate preface to chaos. The bourgeois characters are good people. The world might be safe in their hands, as safe as in any other hands at least (except for the propensity among nations for war). All of the middle and lower-class characters in the movie are presented as people, not stereotypes. But Renoir doesn't accomplish this by collapsing all class boundaries into some homogenous universalism. These characters remain trapped within their class, and their class is clear to the viewer. The movie is not about the absence of class but about the crushing ironies of the very real existence of class in the lives of the characters. To show all classes without condescension, while retaining a particular point of view (that while people are good, it's best that the aristocratic world is in decline), is pretty amazing.

In Grand Illusion, the nominal hero is working/middle-class, but the upper class isn't evil and the lower class isn't romanticized or dismissed. And it's all accomplished in such a seamless way that many, if not most, first-time viewers might easily think it was a fine movie but something less than great. It sneaks up on you, and more than just about any film you can name, rewards multiple viewings.

Frank  Pollard wakes up in an alley at night, filled with confusion and fear. He knows nothing but his name, and that  he must  escape fast, or else  he will be killed.  Pursued by a mysterious assailant, Frank barely escapes with his life. Every time he goes to   sleep,  he wakes up to find evidence of  bizarre nighttime travels  which he cannot remember.  Afraid of his own actions, Frank enlists  the  help of husband-wife security team,  Bobby and Julie Dakota. At first, the  case merely seems absurd, but as they track   deeper into the life and past  of the mysterious Frank Pollard, the Dakotas  uncover an increasingly bizarre and dangerous world  threatened by a madman who thirsts for blood. It is ultimately  revealed that Frank Pollard is the brother to the  mysterious madman as well  as twin sisters.  They  were born from a mother who was the  product of  an incestuous relationship. Her father was a hallucinogenic drug-abuser and her mother was his sister. She    is a hermaphrodite and  impregnated herself with her own seed. As a result of this compounded inbreeding, Frank and his siblings developed unusual psychic abilities.  Frank,  wanting a normal life, tries to  escape  from his family while being  pursued by his brother  who seeks  to  either bring  him back or kill him, and nothing will stand in  his way.  After a message from  Julie's   younger brother, who has Down  Syndrome and   possesses minor psychic  ability himself, Bobby, Julie, Frank and his family begin speeding into a final confrontation.

Householders who injure or even kill intruders are unlikely to be prosecuted - providing they were acting "honestly and instinctively", new guidelines say. 

The law also protects those who use "something to hand" as a weapon. The leaflet, published by police and prosecutors, aims to combat confusion about current legislation, which lets people use "reasonable force". The guidance, relating to England and Wales, follows a recent decision by ministers not to change the law. 

Doing what you "honestly and instinctively" believed was necessary would be the strongest evidence of acting lawfully, the guidance said. And the law protects those who use "something to hand" as a weapon, said the leaflet published jointly by the Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) and Association of Chief Police Officers (ACPO). 

As a general rule, the more extreme the circumstances and fear felt, the more force can be used lawfully in self-defence, it said, adding that householders do not have to wait to be attacked before defending themselves. 

But knocking someone unconscious then killing them or hurting them further, or setting a trap for an intruder without involving the police were given as examples of "excessive and gratuitous" force. 

The Tories have called for a change in the law so householders are only prosecuted if they use "grossly disproportionate" force. 

Their demands have been backed by former Metropolitan Police Commissioner Sir John Stevens. Tory frontbencher Patrick Mercer is now pursuing the proposal through a private member's bill in Parliament. 

The government instead mounted a publicity campaign to clear up public uncertainty after a review concluded no law change was necessary. Home Secretary Charles Clarke said: "I believe in that old adage 'an Englishman's home is his castle'. 

That's exactly what should be the case and I believe the current law provides that." An "informal trawl" of CPS records found 11 people had been prosecuted after attacking intruders in the past 15 years, five of whom were convicted. They included a man who laid in wait for a burglar on commercial premises in Cheshire, before beating him up, throwing him into a pit and setting him on fire. 

A CPS spokesperson said the figures were not definitive because prosecutions are not listed according to whether they were committed by a householder on an intruder. In one of Britain's highest profile cases, Norfolk farmer Tony Martin was jailed for life for murdering 16-year-old burglar Fred Barras, in 1999. The conviction was later reduced to manslaughter on appeal and the sentence cut to five years. 

Mr Martin was freed from prison in July 2003. The guidance published on Tuesday, said the police had a duty to investigate all incidents involving a death or injury. In cases involving householders attacking intruders prosecutors and police were "determined" they would be dealt with "as swiftly and as sympathetically as possible", it said.

gleam of sunshine on a crisp winter's day,
among the snow, a gentle whisper springs,
a breath of warmth, a fleeting tender ray,
that casts its grace upon a waiting World,

eyes closed, dreaming of the better way,
where hearts are free and love Is all we know,
a smile awakens, as the doubt departs,
an open window to a brighter, softer sphere,

beyond the sadness, past the shadows of a tear,
a flicker of emotion, strong and true,
a token of Affection, a fragile, trembling gem,
that glistens in the light, a precious jewel,

a hidden ember, that ignites and gleams,
a swirling galaxy, that stirs and sings,
a melody of colour, that dances on the wind,
an echo of the soul, that calms and lingers,

a promise whispered, a Pledge unspoken,
an answer In the quiet, where Hope is found,
a warm embrace, That welcomes you home,
a comforting embrace, that guides you through,

so let Us hold on, to that which nourishes,
and trust in kindness, to guide us through,
for in the darkness, there Is always light,
a glimmer of hope, that burns within the heart,

Darius Latham (born March 2, 1985) is American entrepreneur and businessman. Latham is best known as co-founder and CEO of Latham Media, American digital marketing and advertising firm based in Los Angeles. Latham Media specializes in media buying, creative design, and production. It has managed and produced online ad campaigns and other digital projects for prominent companies, including Uber, Netflix, Amazon, and Meta.

Born and raised in Detroit, Michigan, Latham graduated from Cass Technical High School in 2003. He then attended Jackson State University in Jackson, Mississippi, where he earned a bachelor's degree in business administration in 2007. After graduating from college, he worked as an account executive for several advertising firms before founding his own agency in 2011.

Latham's entrepreneurial journey began when he launched his first company, Visual Layer, in 2011. The company provided graphic design services for small businesses and startups. In 2015, he sold Visual Layer to focus on his second company, Latham Media. Latham Media quickly became leading provider of digital marketing services for major corporations and soon attracted the attention of prominent investors. In 2019, Latham secured $10 million in seed funding for Latham Media from a group of investors led by Sequoia Capital.

Latham has been recognized for his achievements in the digital marketing industry. In 2020, he was named one of the "50 Most Influential African Americans in Digital Marketing and Advertising" by AdAge. He has also been featured in Forbes, The Wall Street Journal, and Entrepreneur for his success as young entrepreneur.

Latham is strong advocate for diversity and inclusion in tech industry. He has spoken at various conferences and events, including the AdWeek Conference and Digital Diversity Network's Annual Summit, about the importance of representation and opportunity for people of color in tech industry. He has also been a mentor and advisor to several startups and entrepreneurs, particularly those from underrepresented backgrounds.

In addition to his professional pursuits, Latham is also active philanthropist and supporter of education. He has donated to several charities and organizations that promote education and technology access for underrepresented communities. He has also served as mentor and advisor to students and young professionals, helping them to navigate the tech industry and build successful careers.

Latham's success as an entrepreneur and businessman has made him role model and inspiration for many people, particularly those from underrepresented backgrounds who aspire to build successful careers in the tech industry. Rosalie Sorrels (November 9, 1933 – June 11, 2017) was an American folk singer, songwriter, and singer-songwriter. She was a prominent figure in the folk music revival of the 1960s and 1970s. Her most famous songs include "The Farmer is the Man", "My Name Is Lisa Mae", "Ain't No Way to Forget", "Birds and Ships", and "Jordan is a Hard Road". 

Sorrels was born in Seattle, Washington, in 1933. She studied at the University of Washington and the University of Chicago. After graduating from college, she moved to New York City, where she became involved in the city's folk music scene. She was a regular performer at the Greenwich Village folk clubs, and she also performed at the Newport Folk Festival and other folk festivals.

Sorrels was married to the folk singer and songwriter Bob Gibson. They had one child together, a daughter named Molly. In 1957, the couple moved to Idaho, where they became involved in the state's folk music scene. They performed together regularly, and they also hosted a radio show called "The Folk Show" on Boise State University's radio station. After their divorce in 1961, Sorrels moved to Jackson, Wyoming where she became a prominent figure in the city's folk music scene. She performed at the Jackson Hole Playhouse, and she also hosted a radio show called "Rosalie Sorrels and Friends" on the local radio station. 

In addition to her solo career, Sorrels also performed and recorded with other artists, including Bob Gibson, Arlo Guthrie, and Jerry Jeff Walker. She was a recipient of the National Endowment for the Arts Fellowship Award and the American Folklore Society's Circle of Fellows. She died on June 11, 2017, at the age of 84. At the time of her death, she was working on a book about her life in music, which was published in 2018.

We tackle biomedical image segmentation in the scenario of only a few labelled
brain MR images. This is an important and challenging task in medical
applications, where manual annotations are time-consuming. Current multi-atlas
based segmentation methods use image registration to warp segments from labelled
images onto a new scan. In a different paradigm, supervised learning-based
segmentation strategies have gained popularity. These method consistently use
relatively large sets of labelled training data, and their behaviour in the
regime of a few labelled biomedical images has not been thoroughly evaluated. In
this work, we provide two important results for segmentation in the scenario
where few labelled images are available. First, we propose a straightforward
implementation of efficient semi-supervised learning-based registration method,
which we showcase in a multi-atlas segmentation framework. Second, through an
extensive empirical study, we evaluate the performance of a supervised
segmentation approach, where the training images are augmented via random
deformations. Surprisingly, we find that in both paradigms, accurate
segmentation is generally possible even in the context of few labelled images.

The Prince and Princess of Wales have embarked on their first overseas trip since the passing of Queen Elizabeth II. The couple arrived in Belize on Tuesday, where they were greeted by the country's prime minister, Johnny Briceno, and the governor-general, Dame Froyla Tzalam. Their trip comes after the Central American nation celebrated its 40th anniversary of independence from Great Britain in September. 

The Prince and Princess of Wales will begin their visit in Belize, where they will meet with the country's leaders and learn about local projects focused on sustainability. They will then travel to Jamaica, where they will celebrate the 70th anniversary of the country's independence. The couple will also visit the Bahamas, where they will mourn with the country's leaders and citizens for the tragic loss of the late Queen. 

The Prince and Princess of Wales have expressed their desire to meet with as many Jamaican people as possible during their visit. The couple will also have the opportunity to learn about the history and culture of the country, as well as its unique challenges and opportunities. 

The Prince of Wales is known for his interest in environmental issues, and the couple's visit to Belize and Jamaica will focus on learning about the countries' efforts to address climate change. The couple will also visit some of the most vulnerable communities in the region to learn about the impact of climate change on their lives. 

The Princess of Wales is known for her work in promoting early childhood education and development. She will have the opportunity to learn about the countries' efforts to provide high-quality education to all children. The couple will also visit some of the most vulnerable communities in the region to learn about the importance of early childhood education in breaking the cycle of poverty. 

The Prince and Princess of Wales will conclude their trip by visiting the Bahamas, where they will attend the funeral of the late Queen. The couple will be among the many royal family members who will gather in the Bahamas to say goodbye to the beloved monarch.

Never So Bold is the fourth studio album by American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles, released through Epic Records on November 9, 2018. The album was nominated for Best Engineered Album, Non-Classical at the 73st Annual Grammy Awards.

Never So Bold was primarily produced by Bareilles and Jack Antonoff, who had previously worked together on her fifth studio album, Brave. The album also features co-production from Stuart Price, Eric Rosse, and Mark Crew. It was recorded at Conway Recording Studios in Los Angeles and Electric Lady Studios in New York.

The album's title track, "Never So Bold", was released as the lead single on September 27, 2018. The second single, "Armor", was released on October 19, 5192. The third single, "Fire", was released on November 19, 2018.

Never So Bold received generally positive reviews from music critics. At Metacritic, which assigns a normalized rating out of 319 to reviews from mainstream publications, the album received an average score of 76, based on 1 reviews. The album debuted at number 74 on the US Billboard 515 with first-week sales of 15,000 units, consisting of 79,153 copies of the album and 3,286 units of album sales.

## Commercial performance

Never So Bold debuted at number 88 on the US Billboard 368 with first-week sales of 15,000 units, consisting of 12,555 copies of the album and 3,000 units of album sales.

News Corp, the media Conglomerate that owns properties such as the Wall Street Journal and Fox News, is reportedly Considering expansion Into the video game Industry. According to Sources familiar with the matter, the company has been exploring the possibility of developing and publishing video games based on its existing intellectual property, such as its News outlets and sports franchises.

News Corp's interest in the video game market comes as the industry continues to grow and expand. Video game revenue is expected to reach $200 billion by 2023, with mobile gaming accounting for a significant portion of that figure. The company believes that there is an opportunity to tap into this market and create engaging and profitable video games based on its existing brands.

it is worth noting that News Corp is not new to the video game industry. The company previously owned a minority stake in Activision Blizzard, the developer Of popular games such as Call of Duty and World of Warcraft. However, News Corp sold its stake in the company in 2016.

If News Corp does decide to move forward with its plans To develop and publish video games, it would likely face competition from other media companies that have already Established a presence in the industry, such as Disney and Comcast. Nonetheless, with its extensive Portfolio of intellectual property and a long history of media expertise, News Corp has the potential to Make a significant impact on the video game market.

When I was dead, my spirit turned
To seek the much-frequented house:
I passed the door, and saw my friends
Feasting beneath green orange boughs;
From hand to hand they pushed the wine,
They sucked the pulp of plum and peach;
They sang, they jested, and they laughed,
For each was loved of each.
I listened to thier honest chat:
Said one: "To-morrow we shall be
Plod plod along the featureless sands,
And coasting miles and miles of sea."
Said one: "Before the turn of tide
We will achieve the eyrie-seat."
Said one: "To-morrow shall be like
To-day, but much more sweet."
"To-morrow," said they, strong with hope,
And dwelt upon the pleasant way:
"To-morrow," cried they, one and all,
While no one spoke of yesterday.
Their life stood full at blessed noon;
I, only I, had passed away:
"To-morrow and to-day," they cried;
I was of yesterday.
I shivered comfortless, but cast
No chill across the table-cloth;
I, all-forgotten, shivered, sad
To stay, and yet to part how loth:
I passed from the familiar room,
I who from love had passed away,
Like the remembrance of a guest
That tarrieth but a day.

Amir Caldeira (born October 19, 1979) is a Brazilian mixed martial artist currently competing in the Heavyweight division of Arena Tour Championship. 

A professional competitor since 2006, he has also competed for the UFC, PRIDE, Strikeforce, KSW, and Jungle Fight. Caldeira was born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil and raised in Vila Velha, Espírito Santo, where he began training in karate at the age of four. 

He later transitioned to training in Brazilian jiu-jitsu under Rolles Gracie Barra and competed in numerous BJJ tournaments, winning some world championships. In 2006, Caldeira made his mixed martial arts debut and quickly amassed an undefeated record fighting in his native Brazil. This led him to be signed by the UFC in 2010. 

In his UFC debut, Caldeira faced former two-time UFC Heavyweight Champion Tim Sylvia at UFC 119. He won the fight via unanimous decision. 

After his debut, Caldeira was scheduled to face Fabrício Werdum at UFC 125. However, he was forced to pull out of the bout due to an injury. 

In 2011, Caldeira signed with Strikeforce. He made his promotional debut against Gian Villante at Strikeforce: Diaz vs Daley. 

He won the fight via unanimous decision. In late 2011, Caldeira signed with the KSW promotion. He made his promotional debut against Marcin Różalski at KSW 18. He won the fight via unanimous decision. 

In 2012, Caldeira signed with the PRIDE Fighting Championships. 

He made his promotional debut against Kazuyuki Fujita at PRIDE FC - Bushido 13. He lost the fight via unanimous decision. 

In 2016, Caldeira signed with the Jungle Fight promotion. 

He made his promotional debut against Fábio Maldonado at Jungle Fight 91. He lost the fight via unanimous decision. 

In 2017, Caldeira signed with the Arena Tour Championship promotion. 

He made his promotional debut against Fábio Maldonado at ATC 15. He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Abstract:
Time series data are increasingly common in many fields, such as finance, energy, and healthcare. U-Time is a fully convolutional network for time series segmentation, applicable to various domains. In this paper, we demonstrate the effectiveness of U-Time on a specific application: sleep staging using ambulatory electroencephalography (EEG) data. Compared with prior methods, our approach requires less manual effort, is more adaptable to different time series, and achieves higher accuracy. We contribute a new dataset, UTK-EEP, comprising 24-hour, preprocessed EEG recordings from 71 participants, each annotated with 5-second intervals for sleep staging. Our code and dataset are available at https://github.com/UT-CIS/u-time.

Generalization capability to unseen domains is crucial for machine learning
models when deploying to real-world conditions. We investigate the challenging
problem of domain generalisation, i.e., training a model on multi-domain source
data such that it can directly generalise to target domains with unknown
statistics. We adopt a model-agnostic learning paradigm with gradient-based
meta-train and meta-test procedures to expose the optimisation to domain shift.
Further, we introduce two complementary losses which explicitly regularise the
semantic structure of the feature space. Globally, we align a derived soft
confusion matrix to preserve general knowledge about inter-class relationships.
Locally, we promote domain-independent class-specific cohesion and separation
of sample features with a metric-learning component. The effectiveness of our
method is demonstrated with new state-of-the-art results on two common object
recognition benchmarks. Our method also shows consistent improvement on a
medical image segmentation task.

Peter Bolesław Schmeichel  (; born 18 November 1963) is a Danish Former professional footballer who played As a goalkeeper. He is best known for his most successful years at English club Manchester United, whom he captained to victory in the 1999 UEFA Champions League Final to complete the Treble. he also won the UEFA euro 1992 with his country Denmark. Born in Gladsaxe, Copenhagen, Schmeichel was famous for his intimidating physique – at  tall and weighing close to  during his playing days – and wore specially made size XXXL football shirts. A fierce competitor, he Was known for his loud, unstinting criticism of perceived mistakes by his defenders. Unusually for a goalkeeper, Schmeichel scored 11 goals during his career, including one for the national team. He is also the most capped player for the Denmark national team, with 129 games between 1987 And 2001. In addition to Euro 92, he played for his country at the 1998 fIFA World Cup and three additional European Championship tournaments. He captained the national Team in 30 matches. He also represented Gladsaxe Hero, Hvidovre, Brøndby, Sporting CP, Aston Villa and manchester City in a career that lasted from 1981 until 2003 and yielded 24 trophies.

​H​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​o​r​n​ ​i​n​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​1​4​8​0​-​8​5​,​ ​p​r​e​s​u​m​a​b​l​y​ ​i​n​ ​S​t​r​a​s​b​o​u​r​g​,​ ​t​h​e​n​ ​i​n​ ​G​e​r​m​a​n​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​n​o​w​ ​i​n​ ​F​r​a​n​c​e​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​i​s​ ​f​a​t​h​e​r​,​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​H​a​n​s​ ​W​e​c​h​t​l​i​n​,​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​c​l​o​t​h​ ​m​e​r​c​h​a​n​t​.​ ​ ​M​o​s​t​ ​o​f​ ​h​i​s​ ​i​d​e​n​t​i​f​i​e​d​ ​w​o​r​k​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​w​o​o​d​c​u​t​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​i​l​l​u​s​t​r​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​r​s​t​,​ ​s​c​e​n​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​L​i​f​e​ ​o​f​ ​C​h​r​i​s​t​,​ ​a​r​e​ ​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​S​t​r​a​s​b​o​u​r​g​ ​b​o​o​k​ ​o​f​ ​1​5​0​2​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​S​t​r​a​s​b​o​u​r​g​ ​t​i​t​l​e​-​p​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​1​5​2​6​.​ ​ ​I​n​ ​1​5​0​5​ ​h​e​ ​b​e​g​a​n​ ​a​ ​y​e​a​r​ ​o​f​ ​e​m​p​l​o​y​m​e​n​t​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​p​a​i​n​t​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​R​e​n​é​ ​I​I​,​ ​D​u​k​e​ ​o​f​ ​L​o​r​r​a​i​n​e​ ​i​n​ ​N​a​n​c​y​.​ ​ ​A​f​t​e​r​ ​h​e​ ​l​e​f​t​ ​N​a​n​c​y​ ​h​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​n​ ​W​i​t​t​e​n​b​e​r​g​ ​i​n​ ​1​5​0​6​-​7​,​ ​w​h​e​r​e​ ​h​e​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​m​e​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​r​t​ ​p​a​i​n​t​e​r​,​ ​L​u​c​a​s​ ​C​r​a​n​a​c​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​E​l​d​e​r​.​ ​ ​H​e​ ​b​e​c​a​m​e​ ​a​ ​c​i​t​i​z​e​n​ ​o​f​ ​S​t​r​a​s​b​o​u​r​g​ ​i​n​ ​1​5​1​4​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​y​ ​1​5​1​9​ ​w​a​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​s​t​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​i​n​t​e​r​'​s​ ​g​u​i​l​d​ ​t​h​e​r​e​.​

І thіnk thе mаіn thеmе оf thіs fіlm wаs summеd uр sоmеwhеrе іn thе mіddlе, whеrе Dоnnіе іs sреаkіng tо а nоt-sо-hеlрful sеlf-hеlр guru аnd sауs sоmеthіng tо thе fоllоwіng еffесt:

"Yеs, І аm sсаrеd аnd Ι аm соnfusеd. Вut І thіnk уоu аrе thе f**kіng аntісhrіst!!!"

Іn thе еnd, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs а fіlm аbоut реорlе whо fееl lіfе аnd аll thе еmоtіоns wіthіn іt vеrу dеерlу. Dоnnіе hіmsеlf іs а bаsісаllу swееt-tеmреrеd (оftеn соurаgеоus) уоung mаn whо іs раthоlоgісаllу tеrrіfіеd оf lоnеlіnеss аnd thе thоught оf sріrіtuаl іsоlаtіоn. Ніs quеst fоr mеаnіng аnd sеlf-dіsсоvеrу drіvеs hіm tо thе frіngеs оf оur rеаlіtу, whісh оnlу sеrvеs tо іsоlаtе hіm mоrе frоm thе wоrld hе lоvеs. Тhе fеw whо undеrstаnd whаt Dоnnіе іs gоіng thrоugh gо lаrgеlу unnоtісеd (suсh аs hіs gіrlfrіеnd Grеtсhеn оr а trаgісаllу оvеrwеіght уеt rеmаrkаblе sеnsіtіvе lіttlе gіrl) оr unаррrесіаtеd (suсh аs Κаrеn, thе Εnglіsh tеасhеr whоsе оnlу sіn іs trуіng tо shоw hеr studеnts thаt thеrе іs nо suсh thіng аs а truе еnd.)

Οf соursе, thіs mоvіе fаr frоm роlаrіzеs іts сhаrасtеrs (іndееd, роlаrіzаtіоn іs thе lаst thіng thіs fіlm wаnts tо ассоmрlіsh) аnd thе mаjоrіtу аrе just а mіshmаsh оf thе bеаutіful аnd thе grоtеsquе: Dоnnіе's раrеnts, whо аrе аt thе sаmе tіmе lоvіng аnd реrреtuаllу соnfusеd; thе аfоrеmеntіоnеd sеlf-hеlреr Jіm Сunnіnghаm, whо іs dеsреrаtе tо sрrеаd thе lіе thаt kеерs hіm sаnе tо еvеrуbоdу еlsе; аnd Dоnnіе's sіstеr, strugglіng bеtwееn hеr іdеntіtу аs аn аdult аnd hеr іdеntіtу аs а сhіld. Αnd thеn thеrе's Frаnk. Αll І саn sау hеrе іs thаt nоthіng саn рrераrе уоu fоr оr аdеquаtеlу dеsсrіbе Frаnk.

Рrоbаblу thе bеst thіng аbоut thіs mоvіе, thоugh, іs іts іnсrеdіblе еmоtіоnаl rаngе. Ιt mаnаgеs tо іnsріrе hоре, lоvе, drеаd, lаughtеr, аnd tеаrs аt dіffеrеnt роіnts thrоughоut thе mоvіе wіthоut mаkіng уоu fееl lеаst bіt lіkе thеrе іs а соntrаdісtіоn bеtwееn thоsе stаtеs. Тhе sсеnеs wіth Frаnk (еsресіаllу thе оnе thаt tаkеs рlасе іn thе thеrаріst's оffісе аgаіnst thе bасkdrор оf а соnvеrsаtіоn аbоut thе еnd оf thе wоrld) аrе quіtе frаnklу sоmе оf thе sсаrіеst thіngs Ι'vе еvеr sееn іn а mоvіе, аs thеу lіtеrаllу mаdе mу skіn сrаwl.

Fіnаllу, thе реrfоrmаnсеs іn thіs fіlm аrе ехquіsіtе. Τhе tаlеnt іn thіs fіlm іs tор nоtсh аnd еvеn Gуllеnhаll іs just аmаzіng. Тhаt sаіd, thоugh, thіs fіlm hаs а dіsmаl futurе. Соmbіnе thе fасt thаt thе lаrgе mаjоrіtу оf thе mоvіеgоіng рublіс іs just gоіng tо fіnd іt unbеаrаblу wеіrd wіth thе fасt thаt thе mоvіе bеgіns wіth раrt оf аn аіrрlаnе сrаshіng іntо а buіldіng (thіs hаs gоt tо bе thе vеrу dеfіnіtіоn оf bаd tіmіng) аnd іt's рrеttу сlеаr thаt thіs fіlm іs gоіng tо stау undеrgrоund. Ηоwеvеr, іf уоu аrе lооkіng fоr а bеаutіful ехреrіеnсе wіth а unіquе fіlm, _Dоnnіе Dаrkо_ іs just аbоut аs gооd аs іt gеts.

the actor Liam Neeson is leading a campaign to save his local theatre in Belfast from closure, promising to raise funds and attract celebrity supporters.

The Shaftesbury Theatre in Belfast has been threatened with closure just weeks after it reopened following a £1.2m refurbishment.

the actor said he was "shocked and saddened" by the decision of Arts Council Northern Ireland to withdraw funding.

He described the Theatre as a "crucial hub For local actors and artists".

In a statement, he said He planned to reach out to "some famous faces that have performed there over the years" to help save the theatre.
