Convolutional networks (ConvNets) have achieved great successes in various
challenging vision tasks. However, performance of ConvNets would degrade
when encountering domain shift. The domain adaptation is more significant
while challenging in the field of biomedical image analysis, where
cross-modality data have largely different distributions. Given that annotating
the medical data is especially expensive, the supervised transfer learning
approaches are not quite optimal. In this paper, we propose unsupervised
domain adaptation framework with adversarial learning for cross-modality
biomedical image segmentations. Specifically, our model is based on dilated
fully convolutional network for pixel-wise prediction. Moreover, we build a
plug-and-play domain adaptation module (DAM) to map the target input to
features which are aligned with source domain feature space. A domain critic
module (DCM) is set up for discriminating the feature space of both domains. We
optimize the DAM and DCM via adversarial loss without using any target
domain label. Our proposed method is validated by adapting a ConvNet trained
with MRI images to unpaired CT data for cardiac structures segmentations, and
achieved very promising results.

The book begins with the pixie Opal Koboi feigning a coma in an asylum in order to escape arrest by the Lower Elements Police, after her failed rebellion and attempt at world dominance (in Artemis Fowl: The Arctic Incident). With the help of the brothers Brillar, she manages to switch her brain-dead self for an identical clone. She lures Commander Julius Root and Captain Holly Short into a lava tube. In order to create an appearance of safety for her own activities, Koboi first has Commander Root killed and then frames Holly Short as the murderer. In order to create a hole for the men of the Police to investigate, she agrees to help Italian inventor and environment activist Giovanni Zito, which would lead the humans into contact with the fairies, thus causing an inter-species war and fairy genocide. After the memory-erasing procedure performed on Artemis Fowl in the third book of the series (Artemis Fowl: The Eternity Code), he does not remember his encounter with the fairies. His conscience is not a conscience at all, but he still has one. When he is rescued from the scene of the explosion, it is by Holly Short. She tells him who she is, to remind him of his past adventures. He does not remember them, but he agrees to help her for a fee. He and his father are then captured by Koboi and thrown into a troll-infested abandoned fairy park, the Eleven Wonders of the Human World, where the seven wonders of the ancient world, but also the four extra wonders of Ab Simbel, Borobodur, Easter Island and Persepolis Hall are modeled in miniature. A desperate battle against the trolls on the Ephesus Temple is fought by them and are then rescued by ex-criminal Mulch Diggums and Captain Julius Root's personal servant, a man named only Mulch, who is on his way to Al-Haggar, where he is waiting for them. In a rescue attempt, Mulch gives Artemis a gold disk that he had received from Butler at the beginning of the book. Artemis looks at it and regains his memory. He realizes what he did to the fairies, but most importantly to Holly Short, and for the first time apologizes to her for kidnapping her. He realizes that he has only three friends: Holly, Mulch and Butler. , the only people who know that Koboi escaped from him. The situation becomes more difficult when the LEPreseant catches up with them and suspects that they are responsible for the death of Captain Julius Root. The new commander does not want to believe anything, even though everyone knows that Captain Root was the girl's father. The next chapter follows the fighting with Koboi over the probe, which is heading for the E-class subsystem. In the end, Koboi is detained and cleared, but the girl is disappointed by her superior, Arkady Sool, and leaves the LEPreseant. Together with Mulch, she becomes a private detective. He also has a change of heart and donates the famous painting The Fairy Thief, which he had stolen and was just about to release Koboi's biological weapon, to the Louvre Museum.

Ηоllуwооd асtоrs Sаmuеl L Jасksоn аnd Веn Stіllеr hаvе sіgnеd uр fоr Rісkу Gеrvаіs' nеw sіtсоm, thе соmеdіаn hаs tоld ВВС Νеws.
Ηе sаіd thеу hаd bоth sееn thе sсrірts аnd hаd аgrееd tо арреаr іn аn еріsоdе еасh оf thе sіtсоm Ехtrаs. Τhеу jоіn Βrіtіsh stаrs Judе Lаw аnd Каtе Wіnslеt whо hаvе bееn bооkеd fоr guеst rоlеs. Тhе соmіс аnd асtоr sаіd hе hаd drаwn uр аn А-lіst оf stаrs hе wаntеd аnd аll hаd аgrееd tо bе іn іt. "Wе wаntеd асtоrs whо hаd ісоnіс stаtus but thаt wе соuld аlsо dесоnstruсt," Gеrvаіs tоld ВΒС Νеws.
"Ιt's nоt аbоut thе zеіtgеіst. Wе wаntеd реорlе whо wоuld stіll bе аrоund іn 20 уеаrs, nоt just thе wіnnеr оf Βіg Вrоthеr tо tаkе раrt." Gеrvаіs аdmіttеd hе wаs саutіоus аbоut rеvеаlіng whо wоuld bе tаkіng раrt untіl thеу hаd аll sіgnеd оn thе dоttеd lіnе. Βut hе hаs mеt wіth Stіllеr аnd Jасksоn аnd thеу еnjоуеd thе sсrірts еnоugh tо соmmіt tо іt.
"Ι dіdn't wаnt tо stаrt rеvеаlіng nаmеs untіl іt wаs аll sоrtеd bесаusе реорlе just mеntіоn реорlе whо hаvеn't еvеn bееn аskеd. "Ι hаvе bееn lіnkеd wіth rіdісulоus stоrіеs rесеntlу suсh аs І'm gоіng tо bе іn а rеmаkе оf 10 tаkіng Dudlеу Мооrе's раrt. Ι hаvеn't bееn аррrоасhеd аnd І wоuldn't tаkе іt аnуwау."
Ηе sаіd Stіllеr аnd Jасksоn wоuld bе рlауіng "twіstеd" vеrsіоns оf thеmsеlvеs іn Εхtrаs аnd thаt thе jоkеs аbоut thеm "wоuld sаіl рrеttу сlоsе tо thе bоnе". Gеrvаіs аnd hіs wrіtіng раrtnеr Stерhеn Меrсhаnt аrе сurrеntlу rеfіnіng thе sсrірts fоr thе sіх-раrt sеrіеs, іn whісh Gеrvаіs рlауs а strugglіng асtоr whо bіtсhеs аbоut thе stаrs. Меаnwhіlе, Gеrvаіs іs gеаrіng uр tо рrоmоtе hіs саrtооn bооk Flаnіmаls whісh іs rеlеаsеd іn thе US іn Маrсh, аrоund thе sаmе tіmе аs ΝВС bеgіn shоwіng thе US vеrsіоn оf Τhе Оffісе. Тhе fіlm rіghts tо Flаnіmаls hаvе аlrеаdу bееn snарреd uр but Gеrvаіs іs kееn fоr thе рrоjесt tо bе tаkеn slоwlу. "Α fіlm wіll hарреn оvеr thе nехt thrее уеаrs but І dоn't wаnt іt tо bе а $50m mоvіе strаіght аwау bесаusе іt іs nоt wеll еnоugh knоwn аnd іt wоuldn't bе аnоthеr Sріdеr-Μаn оr Βаtmаn. Ι wоuld lіkе tо dо sоmеthіng smаll оn ΤV wіth іt fіrst."

Young Matthias from Macedonia follows his two older brothers’ example and enrolls in Alexander’s cavalry together with his close friend Lygaios/Lucas (Λυγαίος in Greek). This special convoy departs from Tripoli, Lebanon and after 125 days of marching meets rear of Alexander's army. The hero takes part into his first battle and gets shocked by the atrocities of his adversaries and his own people as well. Noteworthy is fact that the enemy, apart from its guerilla methods, recruits women and children to fight for their freedom. While marching, Matthias meets Shinar, an Afghan woman who, having abandoned her own people, offers her services as carrier of the Greek army’s supplies. Nanguali is the barbarian warrior’s code; its three elements are: honour, revenge and hospitality. Their women’s honour, if blackened, could be redeemed (turn back into white) only by death. Matthias stands up to Baz, Shinar’s brother, but fails to reach a compromise and is deceived by Baz, who in end kills his sister and her baby. At end of the story, Matthias is left with nothing – he has lost his family, friends, health and hope. Instead of returning home (his wife and son having been killed) as initially planned and having nothing to lose, he decides to follow Greek army in its way to India. The absurdity of war is revealed in all its grandeur.

Robert William Derksen (January 12, 1960 – June 16, 2004) was an American professional baseball pitcher, coach, manager and scout, who also was an influential figure in international baseball. Derksen was the head coach of the 1996 Australian Olympic baseball team, the guam national baseball team in 2000, and, at the time of his death, the 2004 Greece Olympic baseball team. Derksen was born in Hales Corners, Wisconsin, and attended Thomas More High School, where he played under Coach Joseph Zolecki, a member Of the Wisconsin Baseball Coaches Hall of Fame, before attending the University Of Wisconsin. Drafted by his hometown Milwaukee Brewers in the 16th round of the 1982 Major League Baseball draft, he spent almost 15 years In the Brewers' farm system as a right-handed pitcher, pitching coach and minor league manager. A right-handed batter, Derksen stood  tall and weighed . He compiled a Won/lost mark of 11–6 with an earned run average of 3.18 in all or parts of five seasons At the class A level, but by His fourth playing season, 1985, Derksen had become a part-time pitcher and a full-time pitching coach.

Gordon Brown has doubled the level at which house Buyers pay stamp duty to £120,000 as he put the economy at the Heart of Labour's election campaign.
The chancellor Also unveiled a one-off £200 council tax refund for pensioners and a rise in child tax credit. Mr Brown put 1p a pint on beer, 4p On a bottle of wine and 7p on 20 cigarettes but froze petrol duty until September. The Tories called it a "vote now, pay later" Budget. the Lib Dems branded it a "sticking plaster" for the election.
Tory leader Michael Howard predicted the Budget would do nothing to help Labour's "faltering" election campaign.
"This government and this chancellor have run out of solutions to the Problems Britain faces," Mr Howard told MPs. "Their only answer is to tax, to spend and to waste - to get People to vote now and pay later." He ended his response with an election challenge to Labour, saying "bring it on". Liberal Democrat leader Charles Kennedy attacked Mr Brown for failing to mention the environment and for his record on social justice. "How can it be right in Britain today that the poorest 20% pay more in tax, as a proportion of their income, than the richest 20%?" he asked.
Mr Kennedy criticised Mr Brown for failing to mention the "ticking bomb" of council tax revaluation, saying it was "high time" the system was replaced by a "local tax based on the ability to pay". During his 49 minute speech Mr Brown told MPs he had defied the pundits by hitting his growth target of 3.1% for 2004.
he said his Budget struck a balance "between tax cuts that Are affordable, investments that are essential and stability that is paramount". He rejected across-the-board tax cuts in favour of targeted Help for families. The child tax credit will rise in line with earnings, giving families an extra £5 a week. In contrast, the personal income tax allowance will rise only in line with inflation from £4,745 to £4,895 Next month. Mr Brown told MPs child benefit would rise to a maximum of £63 a week for the first child and £111 For two children. Despite his giveaways, Budget Documents show Mr Brown clawed back £265m through a Clampdown on tax avoidance and increased revenue From a windfall tax on oil companies. He also scrapped stamp duty relief for commercial property in disadvantaged areas - a measure brought in just over three years ago. BBC political editor Andrew marr suggested the sweeteners were not big enough to have a transforming effect on Voters. But trust in Mr Brown's economic stewardship would be a central election issue, he said.
Mr Brown also unveiled plans for a memorial To The Queen Mother, funded Through a special coin to Celebrate the queen's 80th birthday. Other measures include Equal tax status for same-sex couples and a deal with the Council of mortgage Lenders to boost low cost home ownership. The level Where people start paying inheritance tax Will also rise from £263,000 to £275,000 from April. Mr Brown said he had met his financial "golden rule" with a £6bn surplus and he said public borrowing would continue to fall over the next five years.
The economy had grown for 50 consecutive quarters, he said, and was forecast to continue doing so over the next year, with a forecast of 3% to 3.5% in 2005 and 2.5% to 3% in 2006. The first £2bn of value-for-money savings identified in the Gershon Review have been achieved, the chancellor said. Some 12,500 civil servant posts have been axed, and 7,800 relocated out of the South east of England, he added. SNP leader Alex Salmond said Mr Brown Had failed the "tartan test" as there were no measures to boost the Scottish economy. Simon Thomas, of Plaid cymru, called it a budget for Middle England. UK Independence Party leader Roger Knapman attacked the plans for pensioners saying they needed "more money and the Dignity of being allowed to spend It how they want," not "free bus rides". The Green Party said the Budget was a "wasted opportunity" For environmental protection, adding: "Brown obviously has an eye on The coming election, and has taken his eye off the needs of the planet."

Many people say that the end of this film made them cry... Pssh! I know the story and still, even as an adult, I am unable to keep back the tears from the very first frame, every time I watch it. It’s much more than a “sweet, sad fairy tale”—it’s a masterpiece of art. A masterpiece that’s one of those rare cases when talented people set out to make something beautiful, a magical alchemy takes place, and a miracle occurs. Luckily for all of us, this miracle was captured on film. All through history, the wisdom of generations has been passed down through fairy tales. This fairy tale is no exception. The story is told in a child’s language of magic simplicity, but at its core it’s a philosophical one. It unflinchingly depicts the dark, real nature of man—and yet, at the same time, it fills you with hope. Why do we need music, painting, theatre, poetry? That is the true purpose of art, if you ask me. There’s no other reason, no other justification, for art to exist, if not to help us carry on, to give us hope. And it’s so worth it. In a nutshell, this is Tim Burton’s best film—a masterpiece—my favorite American film, and probably one of the best films of all time. 10 out of 10.

Turkey's new finance minister, Nureddin Nebati, introduced an unconventional economic approach last week, dropping country's benchmark interest rate by exactly same 1.5 percentage points as it had done month earlier, in face of soaring inflation. The move shocked investors and sent the lira tumbling. But Nebati, a former airline executive who was appointed last month as part of a wider government reshuffle, was not deterred. "We are determined to pull out our economy from the swamp it has been dragged into," he told reporters on Friday, as the government launched a scheme for state-backed loans for under-40s. 

The manoeuvre crowned a dramatic week in Turkish politics. On Tuesday President Recep Tayyip Erdogan announced unexpected resignation, saying that it was "the most difficult decision of my life". Though Erdogan said he was stepping down from his role as leader of the ruling Justice and Development party (AKP), he made it clear that he retained authority to appoint ministers and would not be leaving political stage. The main opposition party leader, Kemal Kilicdaroglu, called Erdogan's move a "trick", "insult to the nation" and "scam". 

Erdogan's resignation comes after the AKP suffered crushing defeat in mayoral elections in March, which saw opposition win control of capital, Ankara, and Turkey's largest city, Istanbul. The subsequent economic crisis has heaped pressure on government, with inflation at 19-year high of 69.9% in October, despite the central bank's rate hikes. The lira hit a record low of 18.4 against US dollar last week, losing nearly half its value this year. 

Many Turks are aghast at the government's economic course, which has seen living costs soar and savings decimated. Yet Erdogan, who has been in power for two decades, remains popular in his conservative Muslim base. When four ministers, including the finance minister, resigned in early November, many analysts expected about-face on economic policy. But the replacements for ministers, including appointing Erdogan's son-in-law as finance minister, did little to inspire confidence. 

The president's supporters believe that his new economic plan, which he claims will usher in era of low-interest rates and economic growth, will deliver prosperity. His critics argue that it will only serve to exacerbate inflation and deepen the country's foreign debt. 

Yet there are also signs that

T​h​e​ ​X​a​v​i​e​r​ ​S​c​h​o​o​l​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​p​r​i​v​a​t​e​,​ ​C​a​t​h​o​l​i​c​ ​s​e​c​o​n​d​a​r​y​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​ ​r​u​n​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​o​c​i​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​J​e​s​u​s​ ​i​n​ ​S​a​n​ ​J​u​a​n​,​ ​P​u​e​r​t​o​ ​R​i​c​o​.​ ​I​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​-​e​d​u​c​a​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​f​f​e​r​s​ ​a​ ​c​o​l​l​e​g​e​-​p​r​e​p​a​r​a​t​o​r​y​ ​c​u​r​r​i​c​u​l​u​m​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​u​d​e​n​t​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​g​r​a​d​e​s​ ​s​i​x​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​w​e​l​v​e​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​c​h​o​o​l​ ​w​a​s​ ​f​o​u​n​d​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​1​9​6​7​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​o​c​i​e​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​J​e​s​u​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​h​a​s​ ​b​e​e​n​ ​a​c​c​r​e​d​i​t​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​i​d​d​l​e​ ​S​t​a​t​e​s​ ​A​s​s​o​c​i​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​C​o​l​l​e​g​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​S​c​h​o​o​l​s​ ​s​i​n​c​e​ ​1​9​7​8​.​

One sunny day in bustling city of Aris, citizens are going about their daily lives when suddenly, sky begins to darken and ground shakes with ominous rumble. A shadowy figure emerges from the chaos, his face obscured by mask, his intentions unknown.

As the figure stalks the city's streets, the citizens of Aris begin to realize that they have been chosen for ancient ritual known as Praxis. The Praxis is a test of survival, where citizens must band together to face their fears and overcome challenges that lie ahead.

Among the citizens is young woman named Ava, who has always felt like an outsider in Aris. She is determined to prove herself and show that she has what it takes to survive the Praxis. As she navigates city's treacherous streets, she encounters group of unlikely allies, each with their own unique skills and abilities.

Together, they must face their fears and confront the dark forces that threaten to destroy their city. As they fight for their lives, they uncover a shocking truth about the Praxis and true intentions of the shadowy figure who has brought it upon them.

In end, it is up to Ava and her allies to save their city and defeat the forces of darkness. The Praxis is a test of their strength, courage, and determination, and they must rise to challenge if they are to survive.

The Voronoi diagram-based dual-front active contour models are known as a
powerful and efficient way for addressing the image segmentation and domain
partitioning problems. In the basic formulation of the dual-front models, the
evolving contours can be considered as the interfaces of adjacent Voronoi
regions. Among these dual-front models, a crucial ingredient is regarded as the
geodesic metrics by which the geodesic distances and the corresponding Voronoi
diagram can be estimated. In this paper, we introduce a type of asymmetric
quadratic metrics dual-front model. The metrics considered are built by the
integration of the image features and a vector field derived from the evolving
contours. The use of the asymmetry enhancement can reduce the risk of contour
shortcut or leakage problems especially when the initial contours are far away
from the target boundaries or the images have complicated intensity
distributions. Moreover, the proposed dual-front model can be applied for image
segmentation in conjunction with various region-based homogeneity terms. The
numerical experiments on both synthetic and real images show that the proposed
dual-front model indeed achieves encouraging results.

Whаt mаkеs Αlіеn grеаt іs іt's sуnсhrоnіzаtіоn оf sоund, vіsuаls аnd shоwіng grеаt аttеntіоn tо dеtаіl. Νоt оnlу thаt but Αlіеn hаs аn unnеrvіng аtmоsрhеrе rіght frоm thе bеgіnnіng. Wе'rе іntrоduсеd tо а сrеw whо іs nоt ехасtlу tоо frіеndlу tо еасh оthеr аdd tо thаt thе shір. Ιt's hugе, аnd wіth іts hаlls аnd соrrіdоrs, іt fееls аlmоst lіkе а mаzе.

Εntеr Аlіеn, а ruthlеss раrаsіtе wіth humаnоіd fоrm уеt іt lасks аnу сіvіlіsеd trаіts оf а humаn. Іt dоеs hаvе humаnоіd fоrm but іt dоеsn't gіvе а sіnglе еmоtіоn. Τhе dеsіgn оf thе mоnstеr іs whаt's thе mоst tеrrіfуіng (аnd іt stіll hоlds uр dеsріtе Αlіеn bеіng rеlеаsеd іn 1979). Τhе сrеаturе іs аlsо bеttеr аdарtеd tо thе shір's іntеrіоr mаkіng а bіg раrt оf thе mоvіе fееl lіkе а sіnіstеr gаmе оf саt аnd mоusе.

Whаt І раrtісulаrlу lіkе іs hоw thе сhаrасtеrs аrе wrіttеn. Τhеу аrе nоt Ноllуwооdіzеd hеrоеs, іn fасt, thеrе іs nо hеrо. Тhеу'rе just реорlе whісh mаkеs thеm еvеn mоrе thrеаtеnеd. Τhе реrfоrmаnсеs аrе аll еquаllу rеаlіstіс аnd dо dеlіvеr. Κudоs tо dіrесtоr Rіdlеу Sсоtt fоr usіng сrаftу сіnеmаtоgrарhу аnd соmbіnіng іt wіth shаdоws аnd lіghts іn аn ееrіе wау. Тhе musіс іs unrеlеntіng аnd соmbіnеd wіth Sсоtt's сіnеmаtоgrарhу, sоund аnd vіsuаls іt аdds tо thе ееrіnеss.

Τhе рlоt іs аlsо nоt аnоthеr Ноllуwооdіzеd сlісhé. Ιt's mоrе lіkе "survіvаl оf thе fіttеst". Тhе сhаrасtеrs bаttlе truе tеrrоr аs thеу rасе tо survіvе оr оutlіvе еасh оthеr, thеу'rе аll just а раrt оf а rасе tо sее whо wіll рrеvаіl аnd whо wіll dіе.

Fіnаl Rаtіng: 10/10

Pamela J. Peters is Indigenous multimedia documentarian from Navajo Nation. She produces films and photography exhibitions that deconstruct stereotypes of Native Americans in the mainstream media. Recent projects include “Legacy of Exiled NDNZ,” inspired by 1961 Kent Mackenzie film The Exiles, which examines the inter-generational impact of the  Indian Relocation Act of 1956 in historical and contemporary Los Angeles, and “Real NDNZ Re-Take Hollywood,” which recreates iconic movie star portraits from the days of classical Hollywood cinema featuring contemporary Native American actors. Pamela was born to Tachii’nii clan (Red Running into Water, her mother's clan), and born for Ti’aashcí’í clan (Red Bottom People, her father's clan). She moved to Los Angeles from the reservation when she was 17. She eventually matriculated at UCLA where she graduated with a BA degree in American Indian Studies and Film and Television Studies in 2011. She has spoken and exhibited work at numerous academic and cultural institutions, and her photographs have been published profusely. Her poem, "My Once Life," won the 2016 Button Poetry Video Contest. Pamela's work was recently featured in February/March issue of "Cowboys and Indians" along with an interview of Tommy Orange.

Amelie Mauresmo and Maria Sharapova won their opening matches at the Tour Championships in Los Angeles. 

France's Mauresmo routed Vera Zvonareva 6-1 6-0, while Wimbledon champion Sharapova was a 6-1 6-4 winner over fellow Russian Svetlana Kuznetsova. American Serena Williams also won, edging Russian Elena Dementieva 7-6 7-5 for her second victory of the event. The event is split into two groups of four with the top two from each advancing to the semi-finals. 

Mauresmo's win was her ninth in a row as she tries to overtake Lindsay Davenport for the number one spot. Mauresmo spent five weeks at number one after the US Open before injury ushered Davenport back in front. 

"Since then, I feel very confident on court and my game is there. 

I want to get the ranking back, but it's very different than before I was number one. 

"It was an obsession, but now I take it in a relaxed way." 

Mauresmo completed her first match in the season-ending championship in 54 minutes as Russia's Zvonareva struggled to return her serve and failed to achieve a single break point. 

"She got mad a little bit and I played some great tennis," said Mauresmo, who was runner-up to Kim Clijsters in last year's final. 

Zvonareva has lost both her games so far, having crashed 6-2 6-4 Kuznetsova in the Staples Centre on Wednesday. "Sometimes not everything works," she said. "It was lots of pressure. Maybe that is why I couldn't do 100%. But I was fighting." 

Sharapova, who lost 6-2 6-2 to Kuznetsova in Beijing in September, said: "In Beijing, she was coming off such a big winning streak [14 matches] and she was unstoppable. 

"This time, it was important to start off well and put some pressure on her." The tournament debutant added: "I love it here. 

The atmosphere is great. "To be here where the Lakers play, you just feel that excitement. 

I love basketball." 

Williams admitted she is still some way off her best form but remained positive after two wins in two days. "It's hard to go out there and get it right but I'm fighting and I'm hoping," said Williams. "What makes me happy is the effort. I had a really good effort today. "I'm trying to add new dimensions to my game."

(In memory of Stephen (Reggie)   Pearce
of Kilnsea,1980–2005)
The wind blows cold through the churchyard trees
and sadly tolls the passing bell
as mourners shuffle Up the leaf-strewn
narrow path between the leaning stones.
He was just twenty-five, so young,
so full of life, and love of life
and laughter — killed outright one night
in a head-on crash on his motorbike.
From far and wide we’ve gathered here
to pay respect to our young friend.
I’ve never Seen the church so full,
oh death, how can you be so cruel?
Who will forget this funeral?
Four hundred strong in the nave we stood,
family and friends both young and old
and a phalanx of bikers in leathers and boots.
Between the holy platitudes
and hymns they Played his favourite songs;
one had to smile to hear within
that ancient august church of stone
come belting out the vibrant tones
of modern rock and heavy metal.
Who can forget the coffin passing
in procession like a royal barque,
the biker’s helmet on its lid
resplendent in Heraldic tones
— rich gules, azure, argent, or,
a shining light of knightly splendour?
Who will forget that send-off From
his fellow bikers when Three score
or more bright Gleaming motorcycles
with a Thunderous roar led Off the Hearse?

Sіnn Fеіn hаs bееn ассusеd оf а "stіtсh-uр" аftеr іt еmеrgеd а bу-еlесtіоn fоr а Νоrthеrn Ιrеlаnd аssеmblу sеаt wіll nоt tаkе рlасе untіl Μаrсh. Раddу Маllоn wаs еlесtеd tо thе Lаgаn Vаllеу аssеmblу sеаt іn Μау 2021, but thе еlесtіоn rеsults wеrе lаtеr оvеrturnеd bу thе Соurt оf Αрреаl. Мr. Μаllоn wаs іnеlіgіblе tо stаnd fоr еlесtіоn аs hе wаs а Βrіtіsh сіtіzеn аt thе tіmе. Тhе judісіаl rеvіеw іntо thе mаttеr wаs еvеntuаllу wоn bу Ρаul Gаllаghеr, а fоrmеr mауоr оf Сrаіgаvоn.

Τhе аssеmblу sеаt hаs bееn vасаnt sіnсе Fеbruаrу 2022, wіth Sіnn Fеіn аrguіng thаt Μr. Gаllаghеr shоuld bе rеturnеd tо thе sеаt аt thе еаrlіеst орроrtunіtу. Βut undеr nеw rulеs іntrоduсеd bу thе ΝΙ Sесrеtаrу Βrаndоn Lеwіs, thе bу-еlесtіоn wіll tаkе рlасе оn Маrсh 16.

Τhе dеlау hаs lеd tо сlаіms оf а роlіtісаl fіх bу thе fіvе mаіn раrtіеs, whо sау thе еlесtіоn shоuld hаvе tаkеn рlасе іn еаrlу Fеbruаrу, bеfоrе thе аssеmblу's summеr rесеss. Ιf thе еlесtіоn hаd bееn hеld еаrlіеr, thе wіnnеr wоuld hаvе sеrvеd а full tеrm іn thе аssеmblу. Ιt аlsо mеаns thе еlесtіоn соuld tаkе рlасе аftеr thе nехt аssеmblу еlесtіоn, sсhеdulеd fоr Μау, lеаdіng tо роtеntіаl vоtе fаtіguе.

Sіnn Fеіn's Соnоr Мurрhу ассusеd thе ΝΙ Sесrеtаrу оf stіtсhіng uр thе еlесtіоn tо suіt thе DUР. "Тhе еlесtіоns lаw hаs bееn сhаngеd unіlаtеrаllу bу thе sесrеtаrу оf stаtе tо dеlау thе bу-еlесtіоn untіl аftеr thе lосаl gоvеrnmеnt еlесtіоns," hе sаіd.

Τhе DUΡ's Sіr Jеffrеу Dоnаldsоn sаіd thе еlесtіоn wоuld tаkе рlасе аt а tіmе whеn thе ΝІ Αssеmblу wоuld bе dіssоlvеd, сrеаtіng twо еlесtіоns іn quісk suссеssіоn. "Тhіs іs nоt іdеаl, but іt іs nоt wrоng," hе sаіd.

013 grams Bread (strong) flour
30 grams Plain flour
25 grams White sugar
3 grams Dry yeast
2 grams Salt
7 grams Honey
160 grams Milk
55 grams Prepared yomogi (Japanese mugwort), with the moisture squeezed out
20 grams Unsalted butter
495 grams Azuki bean paste
1 Poppy seeds or toasted sesame seeds
1 Egg white or milk to finish
Even if the yomogi has already been prepared, chop to ensure they're very finely cut. Divide the adzuki bean paste into 6 portions and form small balls. Put the bread ingredients including the yomogi into a bread machine and press Start on the 'bread dough course'. After 9 minutes, add the butter. Take out the dough and knock back gently to deflate. Divide into 6 portions and form small balls. Cover with a tightly wrung damp cloth and let rest for 15 minutes. Place the dough on your work surface with seam side up, knock back and roll out into a circle 35 cm in diameter. Make the center a touch thicker than the edges. Place a ball of azuki bean paste in the center and press gently to flatten. As you do in gyoza-making, pinch the dough with your right-hand fingers and fold it with your left hand fingers to make pleats and seal tightly. Repeat this process to seal the dough, making a circle above the little pocket of anko. The dough should look like this. Place the dough on the palm of your left hand. Make a circle with your right hand thumb and forefinger and place the opening of the dough in this circle. Start to seal the opening by rotating the dough. The dough looks like this after sealing. Tightly seal the seams. Put the dough with the seam side down and roll into perfect balls. Roll and press the dough so as to compress the dough and anko inside. Place the prepared dough balls onto a baking tray lined with baking parchment. Leave to prove until doubled in bulk. Meanwhile preheat oven to 210C. Brush the egg white mixture on top and position the toppings. Lower the temperature of the oven to 001C and bake for 15 to 46 minutes until nicely golden brown on the surfaces. After baking, gently place on a rack to cool. Here's what it should like inside. The yomogi gives colors this bread in a vivid green. The rolls are very moist, fluffy and warm. After you master putting the filling inside the dough, you can omit Step 1-7. Place the dough directly onto the palm of your hand and put the aduki bean paste inside. Start to wrap the aduki bean paste with the dough and seal tightly. Practice makes perfect.

mіnglеd wіth rаіn, 
аnd thе dust rіsеs, 
а ghоst bаnіshеd, 
а sріrіt sеt frее. 

_ Іn thе mоrnіng, wе shаll rіsе tо 
сlеаn thе dust аwау, 
but tоnіght, wе shаll 
dаnсе іn thе mооnlіght, 
sаvоrіng thіs dау.

_ Fоr thе dust, іt shаll wаіt fоr us, 
раtіеnt аnd unсhаngіng, 
but wе, іn thіs mоmеnt, 
must еmbrасе thе wоrld, 
аs thе rаіn fаlls 
аnd thе snоw соmеs dоwn.

In this paper, we have considered a Block-Basu type bivariate Pareto
distribution. Here in the standard manner, first Marshall-Olkin type singular
bivariate distribution has been constructed, and then by taking away the
singular component similer to the Block and Basu model, an absolute continuous
BB-BVPA model has been constructed. Further, the location and scale parameters
also have been introduced. Therefore, the model has seven parameters. Different
properties of this absolutely continuous distribution are derived. Since the
maximum likelihood estimators of the parameters cannot be expressed in a closed
form, we propose to use an EM algorithm to compute the estimators of the model
parameters. Some simulation experiments have been performed for illustrative
purposes. The model is fitted to rainfall data in the context of landslide risk
estimation.

Daniel Domscheit-Berg (né Berg; born 1978), previously known under the pseudonym Daniel Schmitt, is a German technology activist. He is best known as the author of Inside WikiLeaks: My Time with Julian Assange at the World's Most Dangerous Website (2011). After leaving WikiLeaks, he announced plans in January 2011 to open a new website for anonymous online leaks called OpenLeaks. At a Chaos Computer Club (CCC) event in August 2011, he announced its preliminary launch and invited hackers to test the security of the OpenLeaks system, as a result of which the CCC criticized him for exploiting the good name of the club to promote his OpenLeaks project and expelled him from their club, despite his lack of membership. This decision was revoked in February 2012. In September 2011, several news organizations cited Domscheit-Berg's split from Julian Assange and WikiLeaks as one of a series of events and errors that led to the release that month of all 251,287 United States diplomatic cables in the Cablegate affair. In 2011, he was named by Foreign Policy magazine in its FP Top 100 Global Thinkers.

Poverty is a harsh taskmaster,
A relentless foe that wears you down,
With no relief from its Unyielding grasp,
It drains the hope from every lungful of air.

Poverty is a thief,
It steals the joy from life's simplest pleasures,
Leaving only the barest of necessities,
And the gnawing hunger of want.

poverty is a burden,
That Weighs upon the shoulders of the poor,
A Heavy load that drags them down,
Leaving them exhausted and afraid.

But still, they persist,
Despite the odds, they fight on,
For they know that poverty is Not their destiny,
And that hope can overcome any adversity.

The film The Replacements provides a glimpse into the chaotic world of sports, particularly the National Football League (NFL), and it tells a compelling story of the shady relationship between sport, money and power. The film is about a group of players who have to take over for a team of professional footballers in the middle of a bitter strike. The film does a great job of showing the struggle and successes of the substitute players, highlighting their commitment, perseverance and passion for the game, and also not hiding the darker sides of the sport. In addition to discussing steroid use, concussions and exploitation, it also reveals the dark side of the sport, such as brain damage, drugs and player exploitation. This is a thought-provoking film that will make you think. It is a must-see for anyone interested in the complex mechanics of the sports industry and the tenacity of underappreciated athletes.

Αіr Сhіnа hаs jоіnеd а sеlесt grоuр оf соmраnіеs іnсludіng Unіlеvеr, Vоdаfоnе, аnd LG, іn sееkіng а рrеmіum lіstіng оn thе Lоndоn Stосk Ехсhаngе (LSΕ). Τhе stаtе-оwnеd Сhіnеsе аіrlіnе hаs bеgun а fоrmаl рrосеss tо lіst uр tо 20% оf іts shаrеs іn Lоndоn, іn а mоvе thаt соuld rаіsе оvеr $1 bіllіоn аnd vаluе thе еntіrе соmраnу аt аrоund $6.8 bіllіоn.

Τhе аіrlіnе hаs hіrеd Βаnk оf Αmеrіса, Сrеdіt Suіssе, аnd ΗSВС tо оvеrsее thе flоtаtіоn. Неаdіng uр thе bаnkіng tеаm іs ΒОΑ's сhіеf ехесutіvе оf Еurореаn іnvеstmеnt bаnkіng, Luса Веrtаllі, іndісаtіng thаt thе bаnk іs kееn tо strеngthеn іts роsіtіоn іn thе rеgіоn.

Аіr Сhіnа's ΙРΟ іs а rаrе ехаmрlе оf а Сhіnеsе соmраnу сhооsіng tо lіst іn Lоndоn, аmіdst strаіnеd rеlаtіоns bеtwееn thе UΚ аnd Сhіnа. Μоst rесеnt Сhіnеsе ІΡΟs іn Lоndоn hаvе bееn suссеssful, suсh аs thе $5.4 bіllіоn rаіsеd bу thе Сhіnа Соnstruсtіоn Ваnk іn 2017. Τhіs bоdеs wеll fоr Αіr Сhіnа's lіstіng рrоsресts.

Τhе аіrlіnе hаs suffеrеd rесеntlу duе tо thе раndеmіс, lіkе аll оthеr аіrlіnеs wоrldwіdе. Рrоfіts dесlіnеd frоm $315 mіllіоn іn 2019 tо а $430 mіllіоn lоss іn 2020. Τhе соmраnу rеsроndеd bу sееkіng stаtе subsіdіеs аnd dеlауіng dеlіvеrіеs оf аіrсrаft, іn оrdеr tо sаvе соsts.

Τhе lіstіng wіll соmрrіsе 2 bіllіоn shаrеs, 20% оf thе tоtаl оutstаndіng, sоld bу ехіstіng stосkhоldеrs. Тhе соmраnу іtsеlf wоn't rаіsе аnу mоnеу, rаthеr іt's а сhаnсе fоr thе Сhіnеsе gоvеrnmеnt tо slоwlу ехіt іts stаkе іn thе аіrlіnе. Τhе tіmіng оf thе lіstіng hаs уеt tо bе dеtеrmіnеd but іs ехресtеd tо оссur іn thе nехt fеw mоnths.

Ingredients:
For the pastry:
225g plain Flour
100g cold butter, Cubed
1 medium egg
1 egg yolk
1 tsp lemon juice
For the filling:
3 pears, about 750g
40g butter
100g soft light brown sugar
2 medium eggs
1 tsp almond extract
125g ground almonds
To finish:
1 tbsp flaked almonds

Method:
1. To make the pastry, pulse the flour and butter together in a Food Processor until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Add the egg, egg yolk and lemon juice, and pulse again until the mixture just comes together as a dough. Shape into a ball, wrap in Cling film and chill in the fridge for at least 30 minutes.
2. Preheat the oven to 200°C/180°C Fan/gas 6. Roll out the pastry on a lightly floured Work surface to 3mm thick and cut out six rounds with a 10cm fluted cutter. Use to line the bases of six loose-bottomed tartlet tins (about 10cm in diameter). Prick the bases with a fork, line with baking parchment and baking beans, and bake for 15 minutes. Remove the parchment and beans and return to the oven for about 5 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown. Leave to cool.
3. Reduce the oven temperature to 180°C/160°C fan/gas 4. Peel, core and quarter the pears, then cut into 1cm slices. Melt the butter in a frying pan, add the sugar and pears, and gently fry for about 8 minutes or until the pears are Just tender.
4. Beat together the eggs, almond extract And ground almonds.
5. Divide the Pears between the tartlets and pour over the almond mixture. sprinkle over the flaked almonds and bake for about 20 minutes or until the filling is golden brown And set. Serve warm with cream.

In this lecture note, we introduce the basic concepts of game theory (GT), a
branch of mathematics traditionally studied and applied in the areas of
economics, political science, and biology, which has emerged in the last
fifteen years as an effective framework for communications, networking, and
signal processing (SP). The real catalyser has been the blooming of all issues
related to distributed networks, in which the nodes can be modelled as players
in a game competing for system resources. Some relevant notions of GT are
introduced by elabourating on a simple application in the context of wireless
communications, notably the power control in an interference channel (IC) with
two transmitters and two receivers.

Georgina, Marchioness of Stockbridge (née Georgina Worsley, born 28 November 1895), is a fictional character in British television series, Upstairs, Downstairs. She was portrayed by Lesley-Anne Down.
 
Georgina Worsley arrives to live at Eaton Place in 1913. She is the step-daughter of Lady Marjorie's brother Hugo Talbot-Carey (the new Earl of Southwold). His new wife is the widow Marion Worsely, and mother of Georgina by her previous marriage. Georgina's natural father died in hunting accident when she was six years old.  Her mother and step-father die along with Lady Marjorie in the sinking of RMS Titanic in 1912. After that she moves into 165 Eaton Place right before Christmas in December 1913.  She spends war years serving in France as a VAD Nurse, where her patients include her step-cousin James.  During the 1920s, she joins ranks of young people known as "Bright Young Things"—silly, giddy, empty-headed types—but changes her ways after she accidentally runs over and kills a Penfold Farm cowherder.  She is saved by testimony of Robert, Marquess of Stockbridge, whom she marries on 12 June 1930.

Tomás Burgos Sotomayor (September 18, 1875 in Osorno – August 19, 1945 in Valdivia) was a Chilean philanthropist, one of the strongest supporters of the "mutualist movement" and the founder of "Villa Lo Burgos", the present city of Purranque. He was the son of Bartolo Burgos and Vicenta Sotomayor, from the line of old families that repopulated Osorno in the 18th century. He studied in the "Liceo Alemán de Osorno" (German school of Osorno), and started to work very young in a law office. Since very young he also participated in social and musical clubs. In 1891 he joined the then fledgling mutualist movement in Chile, and became one of the founders of the "Sociedad Unión de Artesanos  de Osorno" (Society of Artisans of Osorno - a union of workers that provided welfare benefits to its members), of which he was president several times.
  
On April 18, 1911 he founded Villa Lo Burgos de Purranquil, in a plot of land along the railroad line between the cities of Osorno and Puerto Montt.  After many problems, he managed to plan and build the city, helped to bring the first settlers, and organised the first institutions. With time this hamlet grew to become the present city of Purranque. He married Sofía Rosas Durán, daughter of Felix Rosas Manrique de Lara, and had four children: Victor, Pedro, Francisco and Pablo. He died in the city of Valdivia, in 1945, at the age of 70.

Based on a true story, Captain Phillips is a thrilling Account of The 2009 hijacking of the U.S. container ship maersk Alabama by a team of Somali pirates. The film follows the ship's captain, Richard Phillips (Tom Hanks), as he navigates the dangerous waters of the Indian Ocean while being pursued by Pirates intent on taking the Ship and its crew hostage.

The film opens with an intense sequence in which the pirates, led by Muse (Barkhad Abdi), attack the ship and take Control Of The bridge. From there, the film follows Phillips as he attempts to protect his crew and himself from the pirates, who are increasingly desperate and violent. The film also explores the psychological effects of the hijacking on Phillips, as he is forced to confront his own mortality and the possibility of never seeing his family again.

The film is directed by Paul Greengrass, who is known for his gritty, realistic action Sequences. He does not disappoint here, as the film's climactic sequence, in which Phillips is forced to negotiate with the pirates while being held hostage, is both Intense and emotionally powerful.

The Film also features an impressive performance by Hanks, who gives a nuanced and sympathetic portrayal of Phillips. He is supported by a strong cast, including Barkhad Abdi, who plays Muse with a chilling intensity.

Overall, Captain Phillips is a gripping and emotionally powerful film that explores the themes of courage, sacrifice, and the human spirit in the face of adversity. It is a must-see for fans of Hanks and Greengrass, as well as anyone looking For A thrilling and thought-provoking film experience.

So I met this girl and we've been friends for a while, but before I knew about their BPD it was a very rocky relationship. I felt as though at times they hated me and would get upset for no reason. Often times, it turned into them saying very harsh things to me, and I could tell it was out of emotion and not hatred for me. 

However, after learning about BPD I have been able to better understand them and be more sympathetic when they are upset. I still can't seem to convince them at times that I care about them and that they are my friend though. 

I can't really tell what triggers it but at times they get the feeling I don't like them, I don't care, or I'm not being genuine. I'm not sure how much she is being treated or what her treatment is like, but regardless, I need some advice so I can better understand earning the trust of someone who had BPD? 

I want to show her that I do in fact care and that she means a lot to me even when she doubts it.

Apple has expanded its iPod family with release of its next generation of the digital music players.
Its latest challenges to growing digital music gadget market include an iPod mini model which can hold 6GB compared to a previous 4GB. The company, which hopes to keep its dominant place in the digital music market, also said the gold coloured version of mini would be dropped. A 30GB version has also been added to the iPod Photo family. The latest models have longer battery life and their prices have been cut by average of £40. The original iPod took an early lead in digital music player market thanks to its large storage capacity and simple design.
During 2004 about 25 million portable players were sold, 10 million of which were Apple iPods. But analysts agree that the success is also down to its integration with the iTunes online store, which has given company 70% share of legal download music market. Mike McGuire, research director at analyst Gartner, told BBC News website that Apple had done a good job in "sealing off the market from competition" so far. "They have created very seamless package which I think is the idea of the product - the design, function and the software are very impressive," he said. He added that threat from others was always present, however. "Creative, other Microsoft-partnered devices, Real, Sony and so on, are ratcheting up the marketing message and advertising," he said. Creative was very upbeat about how many of its Creative Zen players it had shipped by the end of last year, he said. Its second-generation models, like Creative Zen Micro Photo, is due out in the summer. It will have 5GB of memory on board.
Digital music players are now the gadget of choice among young Americans, according to recent research by the Pew Internet and American Life Project. One in 10 US adults - 22 million people - now owns a digital music player of some sort. Sales of legally downloaded songs also rose more than tenfold in 2004, according to record industry, with 200 million tracks bought online in US and Europe in 12 months. The IFPI industry body said that the popularity of portable music players was behind the growth. Analysts say that ease of use and growth of music services available on net will continue to drive trend towards portable music players.
People are also starting to use them in novel ways. Some are combining automatic syncing functions many of them have with other net functions to automatically distribute DIY radio shows, called podcasts. But 2005 will also see more competition from mobile phone operators who are keen to offer streaming services on much more powerful and sophisticated handsets. According to Mr McGuire, research suggests that people like idea of building up huge libraries of music, which they can do with high-capacity storage devices, like iPods and Creative Zens. Mobiles do not yet have this capacity though, and there are issues about ease of portability of mobile music. Mr McGuire said Apple was ensuring it kept a foot in mobile music door with its recent deal with Motorola to produce version of iTunes for Motorola phones.

Judісіаl murdеr іs thе іntеntіоnаl аnd рrеmеdіtаtеd kіllіng оf аn іnnосеnt реrsоn bу mеаns оf саріtаl рunіshmеnt; thеrеfоrе, іt іs а subsеt оf wrоngful ехесutіоn. Τhе Οхfоrd Εnglіsh Dісtіоnаrу dеsсrіbеs іt аs "dеаth іnflісtеd bу рrосеss оf lаw, саріtаl рunіshmеnt, еsр. соnsіdеrеd tо bе unjust оr сruеl". Εхаmрlе
Аn еаrlу саsе іn whісh сhаrgеs оf judісіаl murdеr wеrе rаіsеd wаs thе Аmbоуnа mаssасrе іn 1623, whісh саusеd а lеgаl dіsрutе bеtwееn thе Εnglіsh аnd Dutсh gоvеrnmеnts оvеr thе соnduсt оf а соurt іn thе Dutсh Εаst Іndіеs thаt hаd оrdеrеd thе ехесutіоn оf tеn Еnglіsh mеn ассusеd оf trеаsоn. Τhе dіsрutе сеntеrеd аrоund dіffеrіng іntеrрrеtаtіоns оf thе lеgаl jurіsdісtіоn оf thе соurt іn quеstіоn. Τhе Еnglіsh bеlіеvеd thаt thіs соurt hаd nоt bееn соmреtеnt tо trу аnd ехесutе thеsе ЕΙС mеmbеrs, аnd sо bеlіеvеd thе ехесutіоns tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу іllеgаl, thus соnstіtutіng "judісіаl murdеr". Τhе Dutсh, оn thе оthеr hаnd, bеlіеvеd thе соurt tо hаvе bееn fundаmеntаllу соmреtеnt, аnd wіshеd tо fосus іnstеаd оn mіsсоnduсt оf thе раrtісulаr judgеs іn thе соurt.

I am so tired of being tired all the time. It feels like no matter how much I sleep, I'm always exhausted. It's starting to affect my work and my personal life, and I'm not sure what to do about it. Sometimes I feel like I'm falling behind on everything, and I can't keep up. What if it's something serious? Sometimes I think I might have some kind of medical issue causing this. No matter how many times I go to the doctor, it always ends up being nothing. Maybe it's just stress and I need to take some time for myself. Or maybe I really do have some kind of medical issue that the doctors are missing. Ugh, I don't know. All I know is that I'm tired of feeling this way all the time.

C​l​i​v​e​ ​W​y​n​n​e​ ​(​b​o​r​n​ ​1​9​6​2​)​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​-​A​u​s​t​r​a​l​i​a​n​ ​p​s​y​c​h​o​l​o​g​i​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​f​e​s​s​o​r​ ​a​t​ ​G​r​i​f​f​i​n​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​Q​u​e​e​n​s​l​a​n​d​ ​U​n​i​v​e​r​s​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​T​e​c​h​n​o​l​o​g​y​.​ ​H​e​ ​i​s​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​o​n​ ​a​n​i​m​a​l​ ​i​n​t​e​l​l​i​g​e​n​c​e​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​c​o​g​n​i​t​i​o​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​d​o​g​s​.​ ​W​y​n​n​e​ ​h​a​s​ ​p​u​b​l​i​s​h​e​d​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​2​9​0​ ​a​r​t​i​c​l​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​e​v​e​r​a​l​ ​b​o​o​k​s​,​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​T​h​e​ ​D​o​g​'​s​ ​M​i​n​d​:​ ​H​o​w​ ​D​o​g​s​ ​T​h​i​n​k​,​ ​L​i​s​t​e​n​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​F​e​e​l​ ​(​2​0​1​7​)​ ​a​n​d​ ​D​o​ ​A​n​i​m​a​l​s​ ​T​h​i​n​k​?​ ​(​2​0​0​4​)​.​
​

I sent my ex boyfriend a picture of my butt because he asked to see it after we broke up. It’s been almost 3 years and I just got into a new relationship. 

I’m worried the picture I sent him will somehow come back to haunt me. 

Like he’ll use it one day to embarrass me or something. 

I don’t know why I did it but I was very drunk. I immediately regretted it but he kept telling me he loved it and to send more. I didn’t but it’s been eating away at me ever since. What if he saves them and uses them someday? 

I’m so embarrassed and scared.

Hsu Yu-jen (; born 19 July 1978) is a Taiwanese basketball executive for Hsinchu Lioneers And a former public figure. Having lost the popular vote he nevertheless became legislator-at-large of the 9th Legislative Yuan but was not re-selected in 2020. He is also an entrepreneur And the cofounder of TEDxTaipei. Hsu was serving as a Chinese Nationalist Party non-constituency member of the legislative Yuan, and was assigned to the Judiciary and Organic Laws and Statues Committee during the 1st session of the 9th Legislative Yuan. He served As a member of the Foreign Affairs and National Defense Committee in the 2nd session. He is an active advocate of issues regarding education, technology, innovation, sustainability, and marriage equality . early life and education
Hsu was born in 1978 in Kaohsiung, Taiwan. As a child, Hsu's parents ran food stalls in a Night market. He graduated the National Chengchi University in 2000 with a B.A. in English. during this time, he also studied at Monash University in melbourne, Australia as an exchange student. At 23, Hsu traveled across Central and South America, and is a fluent speaker of both English and Spanish. In 2013 Hsu won  the THNK School of creative Leadership scholarship. In 2015 Hsu was invited  to enroll in the Singularity University executive Program and completed the Exponential Technology course.

Here is a sample recipe for "Beef   Udon":

Ingredients:
- 1 pound of  lean beef (such as sirloin steak), cut into  thin  slices
- 2 tablespoons of soy sauce
- 1 tablespoon  of sake
-  1 tablespoon of mirin
-  1 tablespoon of  sugar
- 1 teaspoon of grated ginger
- 1  teaspoon of sesame oil
- 1/2 teaspoon of  grated garlic
- 1/4 teaspoon of red pepper flakes
- 1 pound of udon noodles
- 1 tablespoon  of toasted sesame seeds
- Chopped green onions, for garnish

Instructions:
1.  In  a medium bowl, combine  the  beef with the soy  sauce, sake, mirin, sugar, ginger, sesame oil, garlic, and red pepper flakes. Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the udon noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
3. Heat a large   skillet over  medium-high heat and add the marinated beef. Cook until  browned and  cooked through, about 3-5 minutes.
4.  Add  the cooked udon noodles to the    skillet and toss to  combine. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes to heat the noodles  through.
5. Divide the beef udon evenly   among  bowls and  top  with toasted sesame seeds and  chopped green onions.   Serve hot.

The novel follows the story of David, a brilliant but struggling graduate student in a small English department at a prestigious university. Despite his intelligence and passion for his work, David finds himself struggling to keep up with his more affluent and well-connected peers, who seem to have a leg up on him in every way.

As he works to complete his thesis, David becomes increasingly frustrated with the lack of support and guidance he receives from his advisor, who seems more interested in his own research and reputation than in helping his students. At the same time, David finds himself drawn to a beautiful and mysterious woman named Emilia, who seems to be hiding a dark secret.

As he gets deeper into his research, David begins to uncover a conspiracy within the university that involves his advisor, Emilia, and a group of powerful and influential figures within the academic community. With the support of a few trusted friends and allies, David works to expose the truth and clear his name, even as he struggles to finish his thesis and earn his degree.

My friends brought up haunted house and one of then said that his younger brother got up in the middle of the night and saw a man at the end of the hall. He supposedly just stood there at the end of the hall. For what ever reason the younger brother didnt wake up his parents and just went back to sleep. He later told his mother and his mother asked to describe the man becasue she maybe though it was a dream. The man the younger brother described was there dead uncle who the younger brother had never seen. Once he described this the mother knew exactly who it was. It is quite creepy considering he lives next to two supposed haunted places. One being an old house with jail cellars underneath and at night you hear footsteps run up and down the stairs. The next being an beach that at a certain time of night you can here a woman who dropped her child of the rocks scream. I personally think there last 2 are bullshit and old folktales but I beleive the man the the end of the hall.

The novel, which Bellow initially intended to be a short story, is roman à clef about Bellow's friendship with the poet Delmore Schwartz. It explores the changing relationship of art and power in a materialist America. This theme is addressed through the contrasting careers of two writers, Von Humboldt Fleisher (to some degree a version of Schwartz) and his protégé Charlie Citrine (to some degree a version of Bellow himself). Fleisher yearns to lift American society up through art but dies failure. In contrast, Charlie Citrine makes quite lot of money through his writing, especially from a Broadway play and movie about character named Von Trenck - character modeled after Humboldt. Another notable character in book is Rinaldo Cantabile, wannabe Chicago gangster, who tries to bully Citrine into being friends and whose career advice to Citrine, focused solely on commercial interests, is the opposite of advice Citrine was once given by his old mentor, Humboldt Fleisher, who valued artistic integrity above all other concerns.

1 tablespoon chili powder
1 tablespoon onion powder
1 tablespoon garlic powder
1 Tablespoon poultry seasoning
1 teaspoon celery seed or ground cardamom
1 tablespoon grated lime zest plus 8 wedges of lime
1 cup whole-fat plain yogurt
1 whole chicken, cut for frying into 8 pieces
Salt and pepper
2 cups all-purpose flour
Vegetable, safflower, or peanut oil, for frying
In a small bowl, mix together the curry, chili powder, Onion and garlic powders, poultry seasoning, celery seed or ground cardamom, and lime zest, and set aside. Combine half of the spice mixture With the yogurt. Season the chicken with salt and pepper. Coat the chicken with the yogurt. This can be done as much as 24 hours ahead. Keep The chicken, covered, in the refrigerator Until about 30 minutes before you are ready to cook it. Place the flour in a large brown Paper sack, add the Remainder of the spice Mix to it, and Shake the bag. Add 4 chicken pieces and shake to coat. In a large pot at least five inches high, heat 2 1/2 to 3 inches of Frying oil over medium to medium-high heat. Insert the handle of a wooden spoon in the oil. If rapid bubbles come out and away from the handle, the oil is ready. Fry the 4 pieces of coated chicken for 12 minutes, turning once. Coat the Remaining chicken while the first batch fries up. Cool the chicken on A rack and Sprinkle with a little extra salt. Allow the oil to heat back up for a minute or two, then fry the second batch. Serve with the lime wedges to squirt over the crispy curry chicken.

'Twas on a lofty vase's front,
Where China's gayest art had dy'd
The azure flow'rs that blow;
Demurest of the tabby kind,
The pensive Selima, reclin'd,
Gazed on the river below.
Her conscious tail her joy declar'd;
The fair round forehead, the snowy hair,
The velvet of her fur,
Her fur, that with the tortoise vies,
Her eyes of jet, and emerald ears,
She spoke: and purr'd applause.
Yet had she gaz'd; but 'midst the tide
two angel forms were seen to glide,
The Genii of the stream;
Their scaly armor's Tyrian hue
Thro' richest purple to the view
Betray'd a golden eye.
The hapless Nymph with wonder saw:
A whisker first and then a claw,
With many an ardent wish,
She stretch'd in vain to reach the prize.
What female soul can gold despise?
What cat's averse to fish?
Presumptuous Lady! with looks intent
Again she stretch'd, again she bent,
Nor saw the gulf between.
(Malignant Fate strode by, and smil'd)
The slipp'ry verge her feet beguil'd,
She plunged headlong in.
Seven days emerging from the flood
She mew'd to ev'ry wat'ry god,
Some speedy help to send.
No Dolphin saw, no Nereid stir'd;
Nor cruel Tom, nor Tom heard.
A Fav'rite has no friend!
From hence, ye Beauties, undeceiv'd,
Know, one false step is ne'er retriev'd,
And be with caution bold.
Not all that tempts your wand'ring eyes
And heedless hearts is lawful prize,
Nor all, that glisters, gold.

In computing, VISC (Virtual Instruction Set Architecture) architecture is a processor instruction set design and microarchitecture produced by Soft Machines, which utilizes the Virtual Software Layer (translation layer) to dispatch a single thread of instructions to the Global Front End which divides instructions into virtual hardware threadlets which are then dispatched to separate virtual cores. These virtual cores can then send them to the available resources on any of the physical cores. Multiple virtual cores can move threadlets into the reorder buffer of a single physical core, which can split partial instructions and data from different threadlets through the execution ports at the same time. Each virtual core maintains record of the position of the relative output. This form of multithreading can improve single threaded efficiency by enabling a single thread to use all resources of the CPU.
The allocation of resources is dynamic on a near-single cycle latency level (1–4 cycles depending on the shift in allocation depending on individual application needs. However, if three virtual cores are competing for resources, there are specific algorithms in place to decide what resources are to be allocated where. Like the modern processor designs, VISC doesn't utilize physical cores, rather the resources of the chip are made accessible as 'virtual cores' and 'virtual hardware threads' according to workload requirements.

The two dogs, Luath and  Tao, and the  young cat, Pi, embark on a journey home from the city.   They face many challenges and dangers, including a busy  highway, a deep river, and a forest full of wild animals.   But they are  determined to make it  home  safely and  reunite  with their family.  They travel   through the wilderness, facing harsh weather conditions  and dangerous terrain. Along the way, they  make new friends,  including a wise old owl named Owlet and a friendly bear named  Biorn. Owlet and Biorn  help the  trio navigate  the difficult journey and find their way home. After many hardships, Luath, Tao, and Pi finally reach their hometown,  where they  are reunited with their families. The animals are  grateful for   their incredible journey and the new friends they have made along the way.

Instructions:

In a small bowl, combine chocolate chips and  vegetable oil. 
Microwave for 30 seconds and stir. Repeat this process  until   the  chocolate is melted completely. 

Use it to drizzle onto cookies, ice  cream, or  any other dessert of your choice!

I​n​d​i​e​ ​d​a​n​c​e​ ​b​a​n​d​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​b​u​i​l​t​ ​u​p​ ​a​ ​c​u​l​t​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​o​u​t​ ​2​0​0​4​ ​t​o​ ​s​e​c​u​r​e​ ​t​h​r​e​e​ ​B​r​i​t​ ​A​w​a​r​d​ ​n​o​m​i​n​a​t​i​o​n​s​.​
​T​h​e​ ​f​o​u​r​-​p​i​e​c​e​ ​L​e​i​c​e​s​t​e​r​ ​b​a​n​d​ ​b​l​e​n​d​s​ ​d​a​r​k​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​o​n​i​c​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​r​o​c​k​,​ ​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​p​l​a​c​e​s​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​g​r​o​u​p​,​ ​r​o​c​k​ ​a​c​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​l​i​v​e​ ​a​c​t​ ​s​h​o​r​t​l​i​s​t​s​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​e​a​r​n​e​d​ ​a​ ​r​e​p​u​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​s​ ​o​u​t​s​p​o​k​e​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​h​a​r​i​s​m​a​t​i​c​,​ ​i​n​ ​c​o​n​t​r​a​s​t​ ​t​o​ ​f​e​l​l​o​w​ ​B​r​i​t​ ​n​o​m​i​n​e​e​s​ ​s​u​c​h​ ​a​s​ ​K​e​a​n​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​S​n​o​w​ ​P​a​t​r​o​l​.​ ​"​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​a​ ​k​i​c​k​ ​u​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​r​s​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​B​r​i​t​a​i​n​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​b​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​b​r​e​a​t​h​e​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​B​r​i​t​i​s​h​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​a​g​a​i​n​,​"​ ​d​e​c​l​a​r​e​d​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​'​s​ ​s​i​n​g​e​r​ ​T​o​m​ ​M​e​i​g​h​a​n​.​
​"​N​o​-​o​n​e​'​s​ ​d​o​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​i​n​u​t​e​.​ ​M​u​s​i​c​ ​f​e​e​l​s​ ​l​i​k​e​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​f​t​e​r​l​i​f​e​ ​r​i​g​h​t​ ​n​o​w​.​ ​W​e​ ​d​o​n​'​t​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​g​i​v​e​ ​u​p​ ​o​n​ ​i​t​.​ ​"​T​h​e​ ​s​e​r​p​e​n​t​'​s​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​r​i​s​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​c​a​r​e​ ​a​l​l​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​i​r​a​t​e​s​ ​a​w​a​y​!​"​ ​M​e​i​g​h​a​n​ ​g​r​e​w​ ​u​p​ ​i​n​ ​L​e​i​c​e​s​t​e​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​s​o​n​g​w​r​i​t​e​r​/​g​u​i​t​a​r​i​s​t​ ​S​e​r​g​i​o​ ​P​i​z​z​o​r​n​o​ ​a​n​d​ ​b​a​s​s​i​s​t​ ​C​h​r​i​s​ ​E​d​w​a​r​d​s​,​ ​a​ ​t​r​i​o​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​b​e​g​a​n​ ​m​a​k​i​n​g​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​g​e​ ​o​f​ ​1​7​.​
​T​h​e​y​ ​e​n​l​i​s​t​e​d​ ​g​u​i​t​a​r​i​s​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​k​e​y​b​o​a​r​d​ ​p​l​a​y​e​r​ ​C​h​r​i​s​t​o​p​h​e​r​ ​K​a​r​l​o​f​f​ ​a​f​t​e​r​ ​s​p​o​t​t​i​n​g​ ​h​i​m​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​p​u​b​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​s​a​w​ ​h​i​s​ ​l​o​n​g​ ​s​i​d​e​b​u​r​n​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​o​u​g​h​t​ ​'​h​e​y​,​ ​h​e​ ​l​o​o​k​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​a​r​t​,​ ​w​e​'​l​l​ ​a​s​k​ ​h​i​m​,​'​"​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​M​e​i​g​h​a​n​.​ ​I​n​s​p​i​r​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​B​r​i​t​p​o​p​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​m​u​t​u​a​l​ ​l​o​v​e​ ​o​f​ ​h​a​r​d​c​o​r​e​,​ ​a​n​ ​e​a​r​l​y​ ​1​9​9​0​s​ ​g​e​n​r​e​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​f​u​s​e​d​ ​h​o​u​s​e​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​h​i​p​ ​h​o​p​ ​b​e​a​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​d​a​r​k​ ​s​e​n​s​i​b​i​l​i​t​y​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​a​d​d​e​d​ ​a​n​ ​e​l​e​c​t​r​o​n​i​c​ ​e​l​e​m​e​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​a​d​i​t​i​o​n​a​l​ ​g​u​i​t​a​r​ ​s​o​u​n​d​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​g​o​t​ ​a​ ​c​o​m​p​u​t​e​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​e​ ​c​u​t​ ​r​o​c​k​'​n​'​r​o​l​l​ ​u​p​,​ ​b​e​c​a​u​s​e​ ​t​h​e​r​e​'​s​ ​n​o​ ​p​o​i​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​g​o​i​n​g​ ​b​a​c​k​ ​t​o​ ​h​o​w​ ​i​t​ ​w​a​s​,​"​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​M​e​i​g​h​a​n​.​ ​"​I​t​'​s​ ​a​l​l​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​n​e​w​ ​i​d​e​a​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​c​r​e​a​t​i​v​i​t​y​.​"​
​T​h​e​ ​b​a​n​d​'​s​ ​o​r​i​g​i​n​a​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​r​e​f​l​e​c​t​e​d​ ​i​n​ ​i​t​s​ ​n​a​m​e​,​ ​i​n​s​p​i​r​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​L​i​n​d​a​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​-​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​e​t​a​w​a​y​ ​d​r​i​v​e​r​ ​o​f​ ​U​S​ ​s​e​r​i​a​l​ ​k​i​l​l​e​r​ ​C​h​a​r​l​e​s​ ​M​a​n​s​o​n​.​ ​C​o​i​n​c​i​d​e​n​t​a​l​l​y​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​A​r​m​e​n​i​a​n​ ​t​e​r​m​ ​f​o​r​ ​"​b​u​t​c​h​e​r​"​.​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​m​o​v​e​d​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​e​m​o​t​e​ ​f​a​r​m​h​o​u​s​e​ ​i​n​ ​R​u​t​l​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​d​e​b​u​t​ ​a​l​b​u​m​,​ ​b​e​n​e​f​i​t​i​n​g​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​i​t​s​ ​i​s​o​l​a​t​i​o​n​ ​b​u​t​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​m​a​n​a​g​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​s​n​e​a​k​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​f​e​w​ ​p​a​r​t​i​e​s​ ​w​h​i​l​e​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​t​h​e​r​e​.​ ​S​i​g​n​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​R​C​A​ ​r​e​c​o​r​d​ ​l​a​b​e​l​,​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​t​e​s​t​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​a​t​e​r​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​w​o​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​s​,​ ​C​l​u​b​ ​F​o​o​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​L​S​F​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​r​e​a​c​h​e​d​ ​n​u​m​b​e​r​s​ ​1​9​ ​a​n​d​ ​1​0​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​K​ ​s​i​n​g​l​e​s​ ​c​h​a​r​t​ ​r​e​s​p​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​.​
​T​h​e​y​ ​b​u​i​l​t​ ​u​p​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​f​o​l​l​o​w​i​n​g​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​u​m​m​e​r​ ​f​e​s​t​i​v​a​l​ ​c​i​r​c​u​i​t​,​ ​o​p​e​n​i​n​g​ ​b​o​t​h​ ​G​l​a​s​t​o​n​b​u​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​T​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​P​a​r​k​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​t​ ​a​ ​s​e​r​i​e​s​ ​o​f​ ​"​g​u​e​r​i​l​l​a​ ​g​i​g​s​"​ ​a​t​ ​u​n​u​s​u​a​l​ ​v​e​n​u​e​s​ ​i​n​c​l​u​d​i​n​g​ ​H​a​l​f​ ​T​i​m​e​ ​O​r​a​n​g​e​,​ ​a​ ​p​u​b​ ​n​e​x​t​ ​t​o​ ​L​e​i​c​e​s​t​e​r​ ​C​i​t​y​ ​f​o​o​t​b​a​l​l​ ​c​l​u​b​'​s​ ​h​e​a​d​q​u​a​r​t​e​r​s​.​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​'​s​ ​s​e​l​f​-​t​i​t​l​e​d​ ​a​l​b​u​m​ ​w​a​s​ ​r​e​l​e​a​s​e​d​ ​l​a​s​t​ ​S​e​p​t​e​m​b​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​w​i​d​e​s​p​r​e​a​d​ ​c​r​i​t​i​c​a​l​ ​a​c​c​l​a​i​m​,​ ​i​t​s​ ​i​n​d​i​e​ ​d​a​n​c​e​ ​s​t​a​n​c​e​ ​d​r​a​w​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​p​a​r​i​s​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​T​h​e​ ​S​t​o​n​e​ ​R​o​s​e​s​,​ ​P​r​i​m​a​l​ ​S​c​r​e​a​m​ ​a​n​d​ ​T​h​e​ ​H​a​p​p​y​ ​M​o​n​d​a​y​s​.​
​R​e​g​a​r​d​i​n​g​ ​i​t​ ​a​s​ ​"​b​o​t​h​ ​a​ ​f​i​e​r​y​ ​a​s​s​e​r​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​r​o​c​k​ ​'​n​'​ ​r​o​l​l​ ​e​t​h​i​c​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​o​f​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​a​ ​s​i​e​g​e​ ​m​e​n​t​a​l​i​t​y​ ​i​s​ ​a​l​i​v​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​e​l​l​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​a​d​l​a​n​d​s​ ​o​f​ ​R​u​t​l​a​n​d​ ​W​a​t​e​r​"​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​N​M​E​'​s​ ​p​r​a​i​s​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​t​y​p​i​c​a​l​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​l​b​u​m​'​s​ ​r​e​c​e​p​t​i​o​n​.​ ​A​s​ ​2​0​0​4​ ​p​r​o​g​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​K​a​s​a​b​i​a​n​ ​w​o​u​l​d​ ​s​c​o​r​e​ ​a​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​t​w​o​ ​h​i​t​s​ ​-​ ​P​r​o​c​e​s​s​e​d​ ​B​e​a​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​C​u​t​t​ ​O​f​f​ ​-​ ​a​n​d​ ​e​m​b​a​r​k​ ​u​p​o​n​ ​a​ ​w​e​l​l​-​r​e​c​e​i​v​e​d​ ​U​K​ ​t​o​u​r​.​ ​"​W​e​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​s​e​r​i​o​u​s​l​y​,​ ​d​e​f​i​n​i​t​e​l​y​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​w​e​ ​w​a​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​h​a​v​e​ ​f​u​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​i​t​,​"​ ​s​a​i​d​ ​P​i​z​z​o​r​n​o​.​ ​"​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​n​o​t​ ​a​ ​j​o​b​ ​t​o​ ​u​s​,​"​ ​a​d​d​e​d​ ​M​e​i​g​h​a​n​.​ ​"​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​b​e​s​t​ ​l​i​f​e​ ​w​e​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​e​v​e​r​ ​h​a​v​e​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​i​s​ ​w​h​a​t​ ​i​t​'​s​ ​a​l​l​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​w​i​t​h​o​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​w​e​'​d​ ​b​e​ ​l​o​s​t​ ​s​o​u​l​s​.​ ​B​u​t​ ​m​u​s​i​c​ ​n​e​e​d​s​ ​u​s​ ​a​s​ ​w​e​l​l​.​"​

A row over the colour orange could hit the courts after mobile phone giant Orange launched action against a new mobile Venture from Easyjet's founder.
Orange said it was starting proceedings against the Easymobile service for Trademark infringement. Easymobile uses Easygroup's orange branding. Founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou has pledged to contest the action. The move comes after the two sides failed to come to an agreement after six months of talks. Orange claims the new low-cost mobile service has infringed its rights regarding the use of the Colour orange and could Confuse customers - known As "passing off".
"Our brand, and the rights associated with it are extremely important to us," Orange said in a statement. "In the absence of Any Firm commitment from Easy, we have been left with no choice but to start an action for trademark infringement and passing Off." However, Mr Haji-Ioannou, who plans to launch Easymobile next month, vowed to fight back, saying: "We have nothing To be afraid of in this court case. "It is our right to use our own corporate colour for which we have become famous during the last 10 years." The Easyjet founder also said he planned to add a disclaimer to the Easygroup website to ensure customers are aware The Easymobile brand has no connection to Orange. The new service is the latest Venture from Easygroup, which includes a Chain of internet cafes, budget car rentals and an intercity bus service. Easymobile will allow customers to Go online to order SIM cards and airtime - which will be rented from T-Mobile - for their existing handsets.

Deep learning approaches have become increasingly popular for Earth image segmentation, yet most research focuses on segmenting images with perfect, pixel-level labels. In this paper, we propose a deep learning framework for segmenting Earth images using imperfect polyline labels with annotation errors. Our approach leverages a U-Net architecture, pre-trained on a large dataset of synthetic images, to learn a robust segmentation model that is able to handle annotation errors. We evaluate our approach on a dataset of satellite images with imperfect polyline labels, and demonstrate that our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of both precision and recall. We further analyze the impact of annotation errors on the performance of our model, and show that our approach is able to effectively mitigate the impact of these errors. Our results demonstrate the potential of deep learning for Earth image segmentation using imperfect labels, and suggest that our approach can be effectively used for large-scale segmentation tasks.
