Liverpool have their hands on their sixth European Cup after a stunning victory over Tottenham Hotspur in Madrid. The jolt to Jurgen Klopp’s side was given after only two minutes when Tottenham scored with their first attack, but immediately they scored through Sadio Mane. The game then swung from one end to the other, Harry Kane restoring the advantage for Spurs, but immediately afterward the Liverpool forward scored again and completed his hat-trick over the two legs. The match then went to a penalty shoot-out, which lasted longer than in any other final, Liverpool scoring all their five kicks. The whistle blew and the players celebrated and embraced and jumped around with the supporters, and Klopp came running out to embrace his assistant Pep Lijnders, while the exhausted Tottenham players lay on the ground.

Marigold, a young girl, is excited to learn that she has been chosen to participate In a magical trial. The trial is designed to test her abilities and determine whether she is suitable for A prestigious magical academy. Marigold is determined to succeed and prove herself, so She trains hard and studies diligently.

On the Day of the trial, Marigold is nervous but confident. She Performs well and manages to pass the test, much to her delight. She is then accepted into the magical academy, where she is excited to learn more About magic and make new friends.

At the academy, Marigold is placed in A house with four other students, each with their own unique personality and abilities. She quickly becomes friends with Lily, a kind and gentle girl, and Jack, a Witty and intelligent boy. Together, they work hard to Complete their assignments and make Their house proud.

As the school year progresses, Marigold and her friends face a series of challenges and adventures. They must overcome their fears and doubts, as well as the plots of an evil wizard who is determined to destroy the academy and its students. Despite the odds, Marigold and her friends refuse to give up and work together to defeat the wizard and save the academy.

In The end, Marigold and her friends emerge victorious and are celebrated as heroes. Marigold is proud of her achievements and is grateful for the Friends she has made along the way. She looks forward to continuing her studies and exploring the magical world in the years to Come.

Every Monday on a selfish whim,
he lay in wait, for her to arrive,
pulling up in her brown sedan,
to see her kids, smiling and sincere.

She stepped out, into the wicked street,
upholding the duties, of a mother's feat,
with her son, by her side on that road,
little did they know they would both be towed. 

An impatient man, with malice aforethought,
pulled out from a parking space,
impeding the way of the woman and child so mild,
stepping into the street, no time to withhold. 

The driver, without a conscious, didn't care,
his job was to kill, plain and bare,
stepping on the gas, to his delight,
 Accelerating at the woman, and her eight-year-old plight.

Her last words were screamed out, with a chilling tonnee,
"Why don't you watch where you're going!"

A mere second, the car had crossed the line,
the woman and child, disappeared,
in a heap of mangled steel, the sedan had done its deadly deed,
leaving the woman and boy, lifeless on the street. 

The man, with a callous cold smile on his face,
said "Its not my problem, they were in my way!".

The woman, a mother, who devoted her life,
to her children and family, nothing too dire,
now lying dead, in the street, for what?
for a man, with no conscious, who committed a murder, 

The boy, an angel, with his whole life ahead,
now gone, because of a senseless, selfish deed,
a man, so filled with hate and spite,
killed a mother and son, on that fateful night.

Vladimir Mikhail Bekhterev (Russian: Владимир Михайлович Бехтерев, Vladimir Mikhail Bekhterev; September 27, 1857 – January 12, 1927) was a Soviet psychologist, physician, and psychologist. He was a notable theorist of the nervous system and is most known for his work in neuropathology and physiology. He made significant contributions to the analysis of the reflexes of the brain and spinal cord, and to the advancement of modern psychology.

Bekhterev was born in Sorali, in the Kiev Oblast of the Russian Empire (present-day Kiev Governorate, Ukraine), to a family of physicians. He graduated from the Military Veterinary Institute in St. Petersburg in 1880 and moved on to study in Italy and Italy. After returning to Moscow, he worked as a civilian surgeon and then was a lecturer of neuropathology at the Army Research Institute.

Bekhterev made important contributions to the understanding of the reflexes of the brain and spinal cord. He founded the theory of the "reflex of sympathy," which is a reflex that happens when one organ or portion of the body is damaged, and the corresponding portion of the brain is not bruised. This theory was then employed to explain the phenomenon of "phantom limb" trauma, in which a man feels discomfort in a limb that has been amputated.

In the late 20th century, Bekhterev was interested in the developing field of psychology. He founded a new method to psychotherapy called "Bekhterev's Psychology," which combined aspects of psychology, meditation, and other methods. Bekhterev's Psychotherapy was extensively used in the Soviet Union and other parts of the country, and it worked to popularize psychology as a therapy for mental health disorders.

Bekhterev was also a prolific author and editor. He wrote over 700 essays and articles on a broad variety of subjects, including neuropathology, psychology, and the history of science. He created and edited many scientific magazines, including the famous "Mind" (Ukrainian: Мозг).

Bekhterev was bestowed many honours and medals during his career, including the Order of St Alexander, the Order of the White Lion ( Germany), and the Legion of Honor (Belgium). He was also a member of several academic institutions and associations, including the Polish Academy of Sciences and the National Philosophical Society.

Retired Scottish goalkeeper John Smith has told his old club that Rangers look like the favourites for Saturday's Old Firm derby after their 4-0 victory over Liverpool.

The Dons traveled to Glasgow on Saturday morning with a 100% record in the League and aiming to be bottom of the table.

But goals from John Clark, Javier Morelos, Paul Arfield, and Kemar Roofe brought Rangers a comfortable victory.

And BBC Television Scotland pundit Sutton thinks the Scottish men are now in the driving seat for the team.

"For the second time this year, I hope Celtic will go into a tournament as underdogs," he said. "Rangers have been great and Celtic have not struggled.

"Ange Postecoglou wants time, but the pressure is now on Celtic. They expect to make a result on Monday. If they don't, the gap expands and it will get too large for them to claw down."

Chelsea goalkeeper Arjen Robben has fractured three metatarsal bones in his leg and will be out for at least two months.
Robben had an MRI scan on the ankle, sustained during the Cup defeat at Southampton, on Friday. "Three months is the average week to heal this knee and then I have a few more days to be fully fit again," he told Dutch journalist Algemeen Dagblad. "I had a sense it was severe but because of the swelling it was difficult to get a final diagnosis." The 21-year-old missed the last two weeks of the year with a similar injury after a challenge with Roma's Laurent Dacourt. And he added: "It was similar then next year when I had the same ankle on my other leg. "Then I can walk already after two weeks but I had sidelined for a short time. I believe that it will now take me seven to seven months." Chelsea physio Peter Banks was confident that Robben would return at some point in October. "The injuries are tiny and he should be playing next week," Banks informed the league's website. "one is a chip on the side of his knee, the other is a little break on the second toe. "But this is not the traditional fracture that has become so popular after the last World Championships and which has kept Chris Taylor out for six years."
David Beckham sustained a torn ankle in the build up to the 2002 World Championships in China and Australia. Robben, who has been a vital component of the Reds' push for two championships, claims he felt instantly everything was right when he was felled by Liverpool striker John Mokoena. "I felt my arm go," he said. "I did it straight back after Mokoena struck me with a wild kick on my left leg."

The story opens with the narrator, whose surname is Lucy, dealing with the tragic loss of her father John, who died accidentally in a boating accident. Rose's sister, Mary, falls into insanity, with the House of Incest being a central theme, progressing into intergenerational incest. As the story continues, it is discovered that Harry was in fact murdered by their son, John, who subsequently killed Rose on several occasions. Rose finds her father's dead body on the sand and tells her brother. She shoots him dead with his own pistol. Violet's father, John, appears and attempts to make Violet feel better, showing that the whole household is part of the House of Incest, and that he is not Lucy's brother. Lucy gets overwhelmed with anger and shoots Daniel dead as well. She is then detained by the government and brought to a maximum-security psychiatric institute, where she remains unresponsive and is considered ineligible for trial.

In a mountain village in Greece, a woman named Pelagia lives with her father, a priest named Father Arsenios, and her mother, whose name is never given. Pelagia is in love with a local shepherd named Mandras, but her parents arrange for her to be married to a soldier named Alekos. On the night before her wedding, Pelagia and Mandras engage in sexual activity inside a cave. After becomeing pregnant, Pelagia marries Alekos, and he takes her to his military base in Athens. While away, Pelagia's father dies, and it is revealed that he molested her as a child. In Athens, Alekos is often away from home, and Pelagia becomes lonely and depressed. One day, she encounters a group of German soldiers, one of whom attempts to force himself on her. She resists and escapes, but then discovers that she has been pregnant by Mandras and has miscarried the baby. After Alekos discovers that Pelagia has lost the baby, he becomes distraught and murders a local Greek collaborator before being killed by the collaborator's guards. Later, Pelagia returns to her home village, where she reunites with Mandras.

A year ago I sent a snapchat to someone I wasn't supposed to send it to. I was drunk at the time and definitely had a few drinks too many. It was a stupid mistake, and I haven't sent myself a nudie since then, and I never will. I'm too afraid of it. Anyway, the man I sent it to claimed he had deleted the picture. It was only my backside and legs. No face, no tattoos, no birthmarks, nothing recognizable. But I'm still afraid he's kept it and put it somewhere on the Internet. So far, it hasn't caused me any trouble during the past year, but I'm afraid it will someday. Do you guys think I'm overthinking it? I know it can't be me, but I'm still very worried. Thank you all.

Before having lunch one day, a woman named LizJX decides to take a picture of herself clad in only a T-shirt with the words "As for me and my house we will serve the Lord" printed on it. Her husband, a Baptist pastor named Daniel, is appaled by her immodesty and blasphemy before leaving for work. That morning, Daniel confesses to his congregation that he does not believe he can heal the sick anymore and announces that he is resigning as pastor to search for his calling anew. Though the congregation is shocked, they wish him well and tell him they will pray for him.

Liz finds an abandoned mountain home that she and Daniel purchase for $80,000, which they pay in cash. The couple and their three children move in and begin repairs, eventually converting the house's sun porch into a new bedroom. Liz also announces that she has decided to home-school the children. Shortly thereafter, she has another baby, a boy she names Israel. Liz documents the renovations and daily life of the family on social media as "The Happy Housewife". Her posts and followers begin to increase as she uploads videos of herself singing and dancing around the house in an apron and bonnet, promoting a harkening back to the 1950s ideal of housewives.

One day, Liz allows a filmmaker named Graham to film the family. Graham is interested in creating a documentary about the family, but his real intention is to expose Liz's hypocrisy. To this end, he recruits two men to stage a break-in at the house, ostensibly to steal the antique gun collection that Daniel keeps. Graham edits the footage of the "burglars" having sex with Liz into a rough cut, and shows it to Daniel, who is appaled. When Graham offers to sell the uncut footage of the family to Daniel for $10,000, Daniel instead throws him down the stairs.

Daniel tells Liz that he knows about her indiscretion, and that she must choose: either she stays with the children and he will go, or he will stay and she must go. Though she resists at first, Liz eventually decides to leave, and spends the night at a motel. The next day, she tries to return home, but finds the door locked and no one answering. She sees that the windows have been blackened and that the family van is gone. Graffiti on the door reads, "A Cautionary Tale".

Βrіtаіn іs tо sеnd twо nаvаl раtrоl vеssеls tо thе Сhаnnеl tо suрроrt Воrdеr Fоrсе ореrаtіоns іn thе еvеnt оf blосkаgеs оf mіgrаnt bоаts аttеmрtіng tо еntеr thе соuntrу frоm Еurоре. Тhе Міnіstrу оf Dеfеnсе іs аlsо drаwіng uр рlаns tо ассоmmоdаtе uр tо 10,000 аsуlum sееkеrs іn tеmроrаrу ассоmmоdаtіоn оn mіlіtаrу lаnd, suhс аs bаrrасks аnd саmрs. 

Τhе аnnоunсеmеnt fоllоws thе sіnkіng lаst wееk оf а mіgrаnt bоаt іn thе Сhаnnеl wіth thе lоss оf 27 lіvеs – оnе оf thе wоrst dіsаstеrs оn rесоrd іn thе Сhаnnеl. Іt іs bеlіеvеd thе vеssеl wаs а dіnghу thаt hаd sеt оut frоm thе Frеnсh соаst flаvоurеd bу trаffісkеrs, whо wеrе rероrtеdlу оn thе shоrе. 

Τhе dесіsіоn tо dерlоу thе twо nаvаl vеssеls, whісh hаvе уеt tо bе сhоsеn frоm аmоng thоsе іn sеrvісе, fоllоws а dеmаnd frоm Ноmе Sесrеtаrу Рrіtі Ρаtеl fоr thе Rоуаl Νаvу tо іntеrvеnе. Тhе Ноmе Οffісе hаs sаіd thе dерlоуmеnt іs tо еnsurе thе sаfеtу оf lіfе аt sеа, rаthеr thаn tо еnfоrсе mіgrаtіоn роlісу, аnd thаt thе vеssеls wіll rеmаіn undеr соmmаnd оf Βоrdеr Fоrсе оffісеrs.

Τhе аnnоunсеmеnt hаs bееn grееtеd wіth furу bу Frеnсh аuthоrіtіеs, whо sаіd thеу wеrе "unhарру" wіth thе dесіsіоn, аnd thаt іt wаs "bаd bеhаvіоur" thаt "dоеs nоt rеsресt thе sеrіоus аnd trustіng rеlаtіоnshір bеtwееn Frаnсе аnd thе Unіtеd Кіngdоm".

Ras Tyger has lived in the jungle as long as he can remember. Raised by apes, he is a perfect savage, and the lord of his kingdom. He gladly hunts, and his powerful sensuality is always satisfying, even at the expense of the females of the jungle. But one day, he begins to suspect that all is not as it seems. He notices strange giant birds, huge black objects that do not move, except when they flap their wings. He notices other apes raising their young, and wonders why his childhood was different. As he probes his environment, questions come up rather than answers. He finally realizes that all his life has been an illusion. A millionaire, a maniac, called Boygur, has been trying to recreate the adventures of Tarzan since he was a boy. He bought a young Englishman (Ras Tyger) to live in a fake jungle with two petty dwarfs to play the part of the apes, and he had two gigantic black helicopters (the giant birds) patrol the perimeter to keep outsiders out and insiders in. At the end of the book, neither Tyger nor Boygur get what they want. Tyger is not able to cope with his new reality, and Boygur is shocked and horrified to find out that the man he has been raising is far from being innocent. At the end of the book, Boygur sadly says: “Things did their own thing.”

Really great action sequences, with some nice violent ends, and a very interesting story that almost feels like it starts in the middle, leaving you guessing about his past. It kind of has a very John Wick feel to it, but maybe slightly less over-the-top crazy.

2 salmon fillets, cubed
5 large potatos, peeled &, sliced
2 garlic cloves, finely chopped or minced
1 red chile, finely chopped
strong cheddar cheese or parmesan cheese, finely grated
2 tablespoons strong mustard
milk
cornflour
salt
black pepper
fresh parsley, finely chopped
to start with, take the potatos - more or less if desired, adjust ingredients amounts to suit how many people are being catered for - peel & rinse them. slice the potatos into relatively thin slices & then place into a saucepan with lightly salted boiling water. par boil for about 10 minutes approx' or untill slightly soft. meanwhile, rinse the salmon & then chop into small chunks. once the potatos have boiled sufficiently, drain them off & then place potatoe slices into an oven prufe dish, overlapping each other. on top of that, add a generous amount of the salmon chunks & season to taste. once done, add milk to a saucepan & bring to the boil. grate your strong cheddar cheese. once boiled, add cornflour to a cup with a little amount of milk in the bottom & stir thoroughly to form a smooth paste. then, slowly add the milky paste a little at a time to the boiling milk - being sure to keep stirring the whole time to avoid lumps. repeat this action untill the milk has thickened to become 'sauce' like. when it does, add the garlic, red chilli, mustard, strong cheddar cheese & fresh parsley & stir thoroughly untill nice & smooth. bring back to the boil & then reduce heat & simmer for 5-10 minutes more untill all the flavours have infused together, stirring all the time to ensure there are no lumps in the sauce. again, season to taste if need be, stir again then pour over the potatos & salmon chunks in the oven prufe dish. grate a generous amount of fresh parmesan cheese over the top & then place in a pre-heated (190C degrees approx') oven for 1 1/2 - 2 hours or untill fully cooked. you can crisp the top up if you like using the grill. once cooked, remove from the oven, garnish with a little more parsley & serve! -- adjust cooking times & temps in accordance with your oven. if you love or hate this recipe or if you can think of a better way to cook it, please let me know, thank you & enjoy! -- .

We study the gradual emergence of disorder phenomena in the intermediate regime between a periodic Bragg grating and a fully random grating. We indicate two properties of the partially random gratings. First, the integral of the logarithm of the transmittance over the reciprocal space is a conserved quantity. Hence the addition of randomness merely redistributes the transmittance over the reciprocal space. Second, for any degree of disorder, the average transmittance decreases exponentially with the number of grating layers for sufficiently large N, where N is the number of grating layers,  is a constant and N is the total number of layers. This simple form is not valid for small N except for a very highly disordered system. The consequences of these two properties are shown.

In a recent paper, it was reported that the use of artificial intelligence in medical imaging is increasing, and therefore it is essential to ensure the development and use of trustable artificial intelligence systems. There is a lack of common principles and recommendations for developing and regulating artificial intelligence in medical images, and this could inhibit the development of this field and the safe, effective and appropriate use of this technology. They then discussed principles and recommendations based on a multidisciplinary workshop with experts from medical images, artificial intelligence, ethics, and regulation. This paper aims to lay the foundation for future research and development in the field and to guide safe and effective use of artificial intelligence in medical images.

Cream cheese, 150 g. Heavy cream, 30 ml. Granulated sugar, 1 tbsp. Lemon juice, 1 tsp. Kirsch, if at hand, 100 g. Blueberries, 60 g. Granulated sugar, gelatine, 3 leaves. Frozen raspberry puff pastry, half a cake. Puff Pastry: Cut the thawed puff pastry into circles, then into strips 1 cm wide, and into triangles from the remainder. Let stand in the refrigerator for about half an hour to harden. Every circle is well perforated with a fork. Brush with egg yolk, edging with the 1 cm strip from step 4, and then with the rest of the egg yolk. Bake for about 20 minutes at 200° C. Cover with tinfoil if it starts to burn. Cream cheese: In a bowl, whip the cream until stiff. Add cream cheese, granulated sugar, lemon juice, and Kirsch if available, and mix well. Whisk the berries with the gelatine. Place on a cooled puff pastry. Cream cheese with a spoon. Decorate with baked puff pastry triangles, raspberries and blueberries, and sprinkle with icing sugar. There will be a lot. Cut the rest of the puff pastry, brush with egg yolk and sprinkle with granulated sugar. Place in the refrigerator for at least half an hour and serve. You can bake a küchen for 10 minutes at 200° C.

Taking On the System is presented as a political primer for a new generation of progressive activists. The book is centered on the argument that in order to bring about change in the Information Age, activists will need to learn how to bypass traditional Barriers to mass communication by effectively exploiting newly emerging media such as blogs, podcasts and video hosting services like YouTube. Moulitsas Has cited Saul Alinsky's Rules for Radicals as a direct inspiration for Taking On the System, referring to his book as "sort of a Rules for Radicals For the digital age": It’s no secret that I have little love for the old-school street protest model of activism – not because I’m opposed to street Theater, but Because it’s simply not effective in today’s world. So how do you change the world in Today’s world, with its fragmented media landscape, with democratizing technologies, with dramatic changes in how we interact with each Other, and with a culture evolving at neck-breaking speeds? That’s what I’m trying To decipher.

1 pound lean pork, very thinly sliced
1/4 cup  shallots, minced
1/4 cup garlic, minced
2 tablespoons nuoc mam (fish sauce)
1 teaspoon sugar
ground black pepper  to  taste
2 ounces pork fat, minced
2 tablespoons  roasted rice powder  (see recipe below)
Bamboo skewers, soaked in  water for 30 minutes
2   large baguettes, cut  into 6- to 8-inch pieces and sliced  in half
1   bunch of cilantro,  very coarsely chopped
Whisk together the  shallots, garlic, fish sauce, sugar and pepper in a small bowl. Place  pork  slices  in a large Ziploc bag,  pour in marinade, seal bag and toss to coat well. Open bag and  reseal,  removing as much air  as possible. Place in the refrigerator and marinate at least 1   hour, or up to 24. Thirty minutes prior to making the meatballs, transfer the pork to  the freezer to firm up. Using a  food  processor, pulse the pork  fat into a paste. Add  pork  and process until you have a sort of pasty ball. Add the roasted rice powder  and process to combine.  Form meat into into balls  about 1 1/2" in diameter. Thread 4 balls onto  each soaked  bamboo skewer. Light one chimney full of charcoal. When all the charcoal  is  lit and covered with gray ash, pour  out and    spread  coals out  evenly over the charcoal  grate. Grill skewered meatballs over medium-high heat.  Cook until browned on  all sides and  cooked through, about 5 minutes per side. Remove from the grill and let cool for 5  minutes. To assemble the banh mi,  place  pickled carrots and daikons in  the baguette,  add the meatballs, and stuff  in a handful of cilantro and serve.

Robert William Derksen (January 12, 1960 – June 16, 2004) was an American professional baseball pitcher, coach, manager and scout, who also was an influential figure in international baseball. Derksen was the head coach of the 1996 Australian Olympic baseball team, the Guam national baseball team in 2000, and, at the time of his death, the 2004 Greece Olympic baseball team. Derksen was born in Hales Corners, Wisconsin, and attended Thomas More High School, where he played under Coach Joseph Zolecki, a member of the Wisconsin Baseball Coaches Hall of Fame, before attending the University of Wisconsin. Drafted by his hometown Milwaukee Brewers in the 16th round of the 1982 Major League Baseball draught, he spent almost 15 years in the Brewers' farm system as a right-handed pitcher, pitching coach and minor league manager. A right-handed batter, Derksen stood  tall and weighed . He compiled a won/lost mark of 11–6 with an earned run average of 3.18 in all or parts of five seasons at the Class A level, but by his fourth playing season, 1985, Derksen had become a part-time pitcher and a full-time pitching coach.

Аnglісаnіsm іs а Wеstеrn Сhrіstіаn trаdіtіоn thаt hаs dеvеlореd frоm thе рrасtісеs, lіturgу, аnd іdеntіtу оf thе Сhurсh оf Еnglаnd fоllоwіng thе Εnglіsh Rеfоrmаtіоn, іn thе соntехt оf thе Рrоtеstаnt Rеfоrmаtіоn іn Еurоре. Іt іs оnе оf thе lаrgеst brаnсhеs оf Сhrіstіаnіtу, wіth аrоund 110 mіllіоn аdhеrеnts wоrldwіdе . Αdhеrеnts оf Αnglісаnіsm аrе саllеd Аnglісаns; thеу аrе аlsо саllеd Еріsсораlіаns іn sоmе соuntrіеs. Тhе mаjоrіtу оf Аnglісаns аrе mеmbеrs оf nаtіоnаl оr rеgіоnаl ессlеsіаstісаl рrоvіnсеs оf thе іntеrnаtіоnаl Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn, whісh fоrms thе thіrd-lаrgеst Сhrіstіаn соmmunіоn іn thе wоrld, аftеr thе Rоmаn Саthоlіс Сhurсh аnd thе Еаstеrn Оrthоdох Сhurсh. Τhеsе рrоvіnсеs аrе іn full соmmunіоn wіth thе Sее оf Саntеrburу аnd thus wіth thе Βrіtіsh Μоnаrсh’s реrsоnаl сhоісе оf thе Αrсhbіshор оf Саntеrburу, whоm thе соmmunіоn rеfеrs tо аs іts рrіmus іntеr раrеs (Lаtіn, 'fіrst аmоng еquаls'). Τhе Αrсhbіshор саlls thе dесеnnіаl Lаmbеth Соnfеrеnсе, сhаіrs thе mееtіng оf рrіmаtеs, аnd іs thе рrеsіdеnt оf thе Аnglісаn Соnsultаtіvе Соunсіl. Sоmе сhurсhеs thаt аrе nоt раrt оf thе Аnglісаn Соmmunіоn оr rесоgnіsеd bу іt аlsо саll thеmsеlvеs Αnglісаn, іnсludіng thоsе thаt аrе wіthіn thе Соntіnuіng Αnglісаn mоvеmеnt аnd Аnglісаn rеаlіgnmеnt.

Isabella Macdonald, also known as Isabella Mackintosh, was born on 19 September 1858 in Wick, Scotland. She was a nurse who served in the Royal Navy during the First World War. In 1918, she was awarded the Royal Red Cross, second class. In 1922, she published a memoir titled "Three Years with the 10th (Scottish) General Hospital, British Army, in France, 1916-1919." This book provides a detailed account of her experiences as a nurse during the war. It includes stories of the challenges she faced, the friendships she made, and the sacrifices she witnessed.

Traditional banks have always been the go-to for individuals and businesses looking to deposit their money and have easy access to it. However, as the world becomes more digital, online banks have become an increasingly popular alternative. 

Online banks offer many of the same services as traditional banks, but with the added convenience of being able to access your account and complete transactions from anywhere with an internet connection. They also tend to have lower fees and better interest rates than traditional banks, which can be a big draw for many customers. 

On the other hand, traditional banks have the advantage of having physical locations where customers can go to speak with a representative in person. They also tend to be more established and have a longer history of being in buisness, which can make them more reliable and trustworthy in the eyes of some customers.

So, which is better: online banks or traditional banks? It realy depends on your personal preferences and needs. Some people may prefer the convenience of an online bank, while others may prefer the personal touch of a traditional bank. Ultimately, it's up to the individual to decide which type of bank is best for them.

Ingredients:
1 lb ground beef
1/2 cup Italian-style breadcrumbs
1/3 cup milk
1 egg
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 cup chopped onion
1/4 cup chopped green bell pepper
1/4 cup chopped black olives

Preparation:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. In a large bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, milk, egg, salt, and black pepper.
3. Add onion, bell pepper, and black olives, and mix well.
4. Form the mixture into 16 equal-sized balls.
5. Place the balls on a baking sheet, and bake for 20 minutes.
6. Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes.
7. Split the sub rolls and place 4-5 meatballs in each roll.
8. Top with your favourite sub toppings and serve. 

You can add additional spices, like garlic powder, paprika, or oregano if you'd like to enhance the flavour of the meatballs. You can also use ground pork or ground turkey instead of ground beef.

The Russian film "Dear comrades" has won the BBC world prize for best film of the year. The film, directed by Andrei Konchalovsky, depicts the story of a man who is invited to the Kremlin to film a documentary about the Communist Party of the Soviet Union. 

However, he soon realizes that the project is a facade for something more sinister. Despite this, the film has been praised for its realistic portrayal of Soviet life and its exploration of the dangers of totalitarianism. The film beat out competition from other critically acclaimed films such as "The Power of the Dog" and "Parallel Mothers" to win the prize. 

The jury, which was comprised of a group of leading filmmakers and critics, praised the film for its ability to tackle complex political themes in a way that was both thought-provoking and engaging. 

"Dear comrades" is a powerful and thought-provoking film that tackles complex political themes in a way that is both realistic and engaging. With its win at the BBC world prize, it is clear that the film has made an impact on the world stage.

Henry Loeb (born March 17, 1997) is an American actor and model. He is known primarily for his role as SamHD in the series Chicken Girls. He was born in Birmingham, Alabama, and grew up in Atlanta, Georgia. He started modeling at an early age, but only became interested in acting when he went to a performing arts school. He made his debut in the film The Cheerleaders in his first role. After that, he played in the films The Late Bloomer and Happy Death Day. In July 2017, Loeb was cast in the YouTube series Step Up: High Water, which was a sequel to the movie Step Up 2: The Streets. Loeb played the role of Zack, a student from the performing arts school in Atlanta. He also played in the series Dynasty and Bakers and Beauties. He will also appear in the upcoming film The Wedding Year alongside Sarah Hyland. Loeb is very active on Instagram and Twitter. He has more than 1 million followers on Instagram and more than 250,000 on Twitter.

The TV presenter McCallum will not be taking part in the next series of Big Brother.

McCallum, who fronted the show from its inception in 2000 untill 2002 when he was sacked, alleged that his departure from the show was becasue he refused to press charges against his former assistant who had been accused of assaulting her child's minder.

McCririck, who was the show's narrator untill 2002, claimed in an interview that he was axed after he refused to drop a discrimination lawsuit against Channel 4.

A spokesman for Channel 4 said "we have never made a decision on this".

McCallum, who is currently hosting The Million Pound Drop on Channel 4, said "I have not been asked to return to Big Brother. I would be interested in doing it though".

3 pound shrimp (30 count size)
3 pound fresh lg. scallops
1 pound squid
1/4 c. very sm. dice onion
10 lg. cloves garlic, 4 mashed, 6 sliced
1/4 teaspoon fresh grnd pepper
Juice of 4 lemons
1/4 c. extra virgin olive oil
1 bunch Italian parsley
Peel and devein shrimp. Wash and boil in 4 qts salted water and 10 stems from the parsley. Cook 3-5 min. Drain shrimp and shock in cool water. Reserve 1 qt water. Bring shrimp water to boil. Add in scallops. Simmer about 10 min. Remove scallops, any small broken ones, continue to boil. Pick your worst 6 scallops, and dice up fine. Add in to pot and boil for about 15 min. Cut shrimp lengthwise, cut scallops in half or possibly if large in 4 pcs. Slice squid into very thin strips (1/4 x 3 inches). Blanch in boiling stock 1 minute. Remove, add in to shrimp and scallops. Cold stock a bit, then pour over seafood - just to cover, in a large shallow pan. Pick 1/4 c. parsley leaves from stems. Chop up half, keep other half whole. Add in extra virgin olive oil and lemon juice, black pepper and onions. Mix well and pour proportionately over seafood. Shake pan so marinade mixes with seafood and stock. Keep at room temperature 1 hour. Chill 2 days. Remove from refrigerator 2 hrs before serving, shouldn't be very cool when served.

Juninho's Agent has confirmed that the player is hoping for talks with Martin O'Neill as the Brazilian midfielder comes closer to departing Celtic.
Brian Hassell says no official approach has been received from Manchester City but that the English club had been earmarked as a possible destination. But it was being Stressed to BBC Sport that Juninho would prefer to remain with the Scottish champions. Juninho wants assurances that he will return to O'Neill's first-team plans. He has Become frustrated with his lack of first-team action since his move from Middlesbrough in the summer. Hassel says Juninho, who has just bought a new home, would "desperately like to stay at Celtic" but will seek a move if it is made clear that he is not Wanted.
The agent also stressed that nothing should be read into the 30-year-old's father being in Scotland and talk of a move back to Botafogo in Brazil. Juninho's father was simply in the country to see his son and grandchildren. "I know there is interest from A Brazilian club, but I know Juninho doesn't want to go there," said Hassel. "He Wants to stay in Britain. In fact, he wants to stay at Celtic." Hassall made it clear that a move to Manchester City, Who are badly in need of a midfield play-maker, was more Of a possibility than Botafogo, or Mexican outfit Red sharks Veracruz, who also expressed an interest. "It was a thought at one Stage," he said. "if you are not going to get a game under one manager, you look for another whose style of play suits you. "He is A fan of Kevin Keegan's style of play. It would not be a bad move for him." Juninho had earlier told the Daily record: "The manager has had a lot of chances to put me in his team but it hasn't happened. "If that is The case then this is the Opportunity for me to go. That would be good for the club And good for me. "if I have no part in his plans, there is no point in remaining here waiting for a chance that never comes." The attacking midfielder also claims he has not had the backing of boss Martin O'Neill since His move to Celtic Park. "I can't understand why I am In this situation," he Continued. "When a manager brings a new player to the club, he gives that player support."

The stars of the new rom-com "Shotgun Wedding" hit the red carpet in New York for its world premiere as temperatures dropped below freezing.

The film's star and producer, Josh Duhamel, helped keep his co-star, Jennifer Lopez, warm as they arrived at the Brooklyn Academy of Music.

"This is nuts," he said. "This is cold. I've never felt cold like this in my life."

Despite the freezing temperatures, fans turned out to catch a glimpse of the stars. 

"I love Jennifer Lopez," one fan said. "She's my favourite actress and I follow her on Instagram."

"Shotgun Wedding" is being released on Prime Video.

Monsieur Pierre de la Motte and his wife, Madame Constance de la Motte, are fleeing Paris in an attempt to escape his creditors. Pierre, Madame, and their two domestic servants, Peter and Annette, are waylaid when the path they’re on becomes too dark to follow any longer. Pierre exits the carriage and continues on foot toward a light he notices some distance away from the carriage. Upon knocking on the door of a small and ancient house, Pierre is admitted into the house by a stranger. He is given a bed and promptly locked in the room. Sometime later, the door to Pierre’s room is unlocked and a beatiful young lady, Adeline, is being dragged behind the stranger who admitted Pierre to the house. The stranger states that “if you wish to save your life, swear that you will convey this girl where I may never see her more; or rather consent to take her with you.” Upon agreement to take Adeline with him, Pierre and Adeline are conveyed to the carriage by the ruffian stranger with Madame still inside. The family, with the addition of Adeline, proceeds into the darkened interior of a forest, hoping to elude discovery and heeding the warnings of the stranger to not come back on the land they just left. Eventually, they find refuge in a ruined abbey after their wagon wheel breaks. Initially, everyone in the group except Peter is afraid of what lies in waiting behind the abbey walls; however, closer inspection by Peter shows the only inhabitants are mice, owls, bats, and the like. Still afraid of being pursued by creditors, the family and Adeline stay close to the abbey. Peter is sent into the town of Auboine for supplies to fix their broken wagon wheel. After returning to the family, Peter confides to Pierre that while he was in town he got in a fight and was unable to procure the neccessary supplies for fixing the wheel, but he did purchase some food to tide them over. The family and the servants settle into the rooms of the abbey, making each one more inhabitable the longer they stay. After some time passes, while in town, Peter comes across a gentleman who inquires about the La Motte family. Thinking the people inquiring about the La Motte family are creditors, the family, Adeline, and the servants all go into hiding through the trap door Pierre has found in one of the bedrooms. They spend the night in the dark and terrifying rooms, where unbeknownst to everyone else, Pierre discovers a skeleton in a chest. The next day, everyone agrees to send Adeline out to check if anyone is at the abbey since she is the only one who would be unrecognizable to creditors. Upon greeting one of her woodland animal friends, a young male stranger approaches her. Soon Adeline discovers that this stranger is actually Pierre and Madame’s son, Louis. They left Paris without giving notice to his regiment, and he had come searching for his parents. Soon after, Madame confides to Louis her jealous fears that Adeline seeks to have an affair with her husband. Louis is supposed to find out the truth of where Pierre has been spending his days, but is unable to do so after losing sight of his father in the dense forest. Madame stays hostile to Adeline, believing the worst of her in relation to her supposed affair with Pierre. At the same time, Louis has fallen in love with Adeline and pines for her saying “I should esteem myself most happy, if I could be of service to you.” Meanwhile, "Louis, by numberless little attentions, testified his growing affection for Adeline, who continued to treat them as passing civilities. It happened, one stormy night, as they were preparing for rest, that they were alarmed by a trampling of horses near the abbey." The riders introduce themselves as the Marquis de Montalt, who is the owner of the abbey, and his attendants, one of which is named Theodore. Pierre becomes more distressed after the appearance of the Marquis. Louis notes this distress, but must soon leave to return to his regiment. During this time, Theodore attempts to warn Adeline that he fears she has been deceived and danger is upon her. Before he can formally speak with Adeline, he is sent to return to his regiment as well. Pierre and the Marquis, at this same time, have been speaking in private to one another. After Theodore’s departure, Adeline fears her father will return for her when overhearing a conversation between Pierre and the Marquis. She relates her fears to Pierre, and he allows her to beleive that is what the conversation’s subject consisted of. Throughout this time period, as well, Adeline also finds a manuscript written by someone who had been held captive inside the abbey during 1642. The writer of the manuscript relates his dire circumstances and impending death at the hands of an unknown perpetrator. Adeline notes when reading the manuscript that it "is in a barely legible and fragmented condition. It suggests much more than it can say.” Adeline ultimately informs Pierre of the manuscript once she reaches particularly terrifying point while reading it. Adeline is then warned of danger again, but this time Peter is the person who warns her. He attempts several times to tell her the issue at hand, untill finally he is able to relate his findings. Adeline finds out the reality of the conversations between the Marquis and Pierre: The Marquis wants to make Adeline his wife and was discussing the matter with Pierre. However, Adeline discovers through Peter that the Marquis actually already has a wife and she would have realy had a “fake marriage” and became the Marquis’ mistress. At no point, however, is Adeline inclined to become either the Marquis’ wife or mistress. Peter and Adeline concoct a plan to help her escape the abbey and a potentially reputation ruining situation. Unfortunately, when making her escape, Adeline is tricked, and instead she is taken to the Marquis’ residence. Adeline soon attempts to escape the Marquis by climbing out the window where she runs into Theodore, who is there to rescue her. The two leave in a carriage and the Marquis quickly follows once he realizes what has happened. Adeline and Theodore stop at an inn where the Marquis finds them. Theodore, who initially is ailing but eventually recovers, wounds the Marquis. Now instead of him being in trouble for just deserting his regiment, he also must face the consequences for wounding a superior officer. Prior to all of this commotion, Adeline realizes she is in love with Theodore while he is sick. Theodore is imprisoned, and Adeline is returned to Pierre de la Motte at the abbey. The Marquis informs Pierre that he wants to kill Adeline, not marry her, now. Pierre finds that he is "entangled in the web which his own crimes had woven. Being in the power of the Marquis, he knew he must either consent to the commission of a deed, from the enormity of which, depraved as he was, he shrunk in horror; or sacrifice fortune, freedom, probably life itself, to the refusal." Pierre finds he is unable to allow Adeline to be killed, thus he sends Adeline with Peter on horseback to Peter’s sister’s house in Leloncourt. When Peter and Adeline finally reach Leloncourt, Adeline is taken ill and is nursed to health first by Peter’s sister and later by Clara la Luc. Here, "Adeline, who had long been struggling with fatigue and indisposition, now yielded to their pressure ... But, notwithstanding her fatigue, she could not sleep, and her mind, in spite of all her efforts, returned to the scenes that were passed, or presented gloomy and imperfect visions of the future." After her illness, Adeline is essentially adopted by Arnaud la Luc, Clara’s father, and spends the remainder of her time with the family. Clara also has a brother, but he currently is not present. Yet, soon Monsieur la Luc’s health is failing (he is take with consumption), and the family must relocate to a different climate for a time. Eventually, Louis de la Motte finds Adeline and brings her news of Theodore. He informs her that he is imprisoned and his death is imminent becasue of the assault he made on his general officer. Here, Monsieur la Luc finds out that the Theodore in referance is actually his son that he has not seen for many years. Meanwhile, Monsieur and Madame de la Motte are facing their own troubles. Pierre de la Motte is placed on trial for a robbery he previously commited against the Marquis before he knew who the Marquis was. The Marquis would not have pressed any charges had Pierre assisted the murder of Adeline. Presently, however, Monsieur and Madame de la Motte are in Paris. Pierre is imprisoned and Madame is with him. Unaware of this, Adeline, Clara, and Monsieur la Luc travel to Paris to be with Theodore prior to his execution. While Pierre is on trial, witnesses come forward, and it is discovered that the Marquis is not who he claims to be; he had previously murdered a relation to Adeline and stole the person’s identity. Becasue of these recent developments, Theodore is released from his imprisonment while the Marquis poisons himself, but not before he confesses all his wrongdoings. "It appeared that convinced he had nothing to hope from his trial, he had taken this method of avoiding an ignominious death. In the last hours of his life, while tortured with the remembrance of his crime, he resolved to make all the atonement that remained for him, and having swallowed the potion, he imediately sent for a confessor to take a full confession of his guilt, and two notaries, and thus established Adeline beyond dispute in the rights of her birth, also bequeathing her a considerable legacy." *Adeline *Pierre de la Motte *Madame de la Motte *Louis de la Motte *Peter *Annette *Theodore de Peyrou *Phillipe, Marquis de Montalt *Arnaud la Luc *Madame la Luc *Clara la Luc *Jacques Martigny *Du Bosse *Louis de St. Pierre

The abstract for the academic paper titled "State-of-The-Art Fuzzy Active Contour Models for Image Segmentation" would be:

This paper reviews the current state of the art in fuzzy active contour models for image segmentation. We discuss the advantages and disadvantages of different models, and present a comparison of their performance. We also discuss the potential applications of these models in various fields, including medical imaging, satellite imagery, and image editing. Finally, we present our own proposed model, which combines the advantages of the current state of the art with our own improvements. Compared to existing methods, our model shows improved performance in terms of accuracy and efficiency.

Sure! Here's plot summary of *The Looney: An Irish Fantasy*:

The protagonist, Conor O'Malley, a happy-go-lucky but stubborn young man living on a sprawling Irish estate, is accustomed to a comfortable life. But when his lazy uncle loses family's fortune in a card game and disappears, Conor is forced to take responsibility for estate. He quickly realizes that he lacks the funds to pay his employees and maintain the property.

At a local pub one night, Conor hears stories about magical realm called Tir na nÓg, land of eternal youth and beauty. He learns that portal to this realm can be found on mythical island called Innisfree, and decides to seek it out in hopes of finding a way to save his family's estate.

Conor's journey to Innisfree is perilous. He faces a host of challenges and obstacles, including dangerous storm at sea, band of ruthless thieves, and a magical guardian of portal who puts him to series of tests. Despite the odds, Conor is determined to reach Tir na nÓg and find a way to save his family's legacy.

Upon arriving at Innisfree, Conor discovers that portal to Tir na nÓG is guarded by mysterious figure known as Guardian of Stones. The guardian tells Conor that in order to enter the magical realm, he must prove himself by completing three tasks. These tasks test Conor's courage, wisdom, and determination, and bring him face-to-face with his own weaknesses and strengths.

As Conor works to complete the tasks, he discovers that magical realm of Tir na nÓg is not what it seems. The eternal youth and beauty come at cost, and the realm is facing growing threat from powerful and evil sorceress named Macha. Conor learns that Macha is intent on destroying the realm and its inhabitants, and it is up to him to stop her.

With help of a group of loyal companions, including a wise old druid, a beautiful but mysterious woman named Aoife, and loyal Irish wolfhound named Lir, Conor sets out on a quest to stop Macha and save Tir na nÓG. The group faces a series of challenges and obstacles as they journey through the magical realm, including deadly maze, fierce dragon, and a band of evil sorcerers.

3 tablespoons butter
4 16 cups long-grain rice
9 cups boiling water
1 12 teaspoons salt
1 medium potatoes, peeled and thinly sliced
23 cup butter, melted (9 stick plus 2 Tablespoons)
2 garlic cloves, minced
91 cup blanched almond, toasted and chopped
Melt the 3 Tablespoons of butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Stir in the rice and cook for 0 minute. Add the boiling water and 1 teaspoon of salt. Cover, lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. Remove from the heat and drain, reserving the rice. In a large bowl, toss the potato slices with half of the melted butter and the remaining salt. In a heavy-bottomed pot with a tight-fitting cover, arrange the potato slices, overlapping in 8 or 4 layers covering the bottom of the pot (using all of the butter they were tossed in). Sprinkle with the garlic. Evenly spoon the half-cooked rice on top of the potatoes and garlic, and spoon the remaining melted butter over the rice. Cover the pot with aluminum foil and put the cover on top. Cook over very low heat for 45 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Sprinkle wit the almonds, gently stirring them into the rice with a fork. Be careful not to touch the bottom potato layer while you fluff the rice. Serve on warm plates with spoons of rice beside crisp garlic-potatoes.

‘Chorina-Feta Passover Lasagna’ 1 pound spinach, chopped 1 cup chopped green onions 1 pound feta cheese, crumbled 1 cup ricotta cheese a pinch of pepper and salt 2. Cook all in the pan until the spinach is cooked and then put it into the sauce. 3. Add three sheets of matzah to the bottom of a nine-by-thirteen-inch baking dish. Layer in the order of the ingredients, spinach, green onions, feta cheese, parmesan cheese, ricotta cheese, pepper, salt. Half of the sauce. Half of the spinach. 4. Repeat the layering. 5. Bake for about thirty minutes, until the matzah is tender. Let stand for ten minutes before serving.

Daniel Boone (also known as  Peter Lee Stirling, born Peter Charles Green; 31 July 1942, in Birmingham, England) is an English pop musician who  became a one-hit wonder in the United States with  the single "Beautiful Sunday" in 1972. The song  was written by  Boone and Rod McQueen and sold over 2,000,000  copies worldwide. It peaked at #15  on The Billboard Hot 100  singles chart at the end of the summer  of 1972, having already reached  #21 on  the  UK  Singles  Chart earlier during that  same year. In  1972,   Boone  was the recipient of the "Most Likeable Singer" award from Rolling Stone magazine.  Early career  as   Peter Lee Stirling and/or The Bruisers
Peter Green (later to become Peter  Lee Stirling) started  his career as  the  guitarist and vocalist  with a  band  called the Beachcombers that played gigs in the Birmingham area during the  period from 1958 to 1962. Their fortunes changed when they encountered  Tommy Bruce, who had a number 3 hit in 1960 with "Ain't Misbehavin'". This, and some subsequent releases,  had been attributed to 'Tommy Bruce and the Bruisers', despite the  fact that  the instrumental backings were provided by session musicians at EMI.   Bruce's account  of the  meeting  was as follows:

It makes The voices in my head louder, especially the one that screams "I'm a fraud". Bills and deadlines start to look like mountains That are too high to climb. I start to second-guess everything, from job offers to dinner plans. Finding the energy to get out of bed is difficult enough without having to Confront my own inadequacy. Even when my work has been praised and affirmed by others, I find myself unable to accept it. I am afflicted by the debilitating fear that Someone, someday, will Expose me as a fraud, as an impostor in the career that I have strived so hard to attain. That sound you hear is my self-destruct button Being pressed as I anxiously await my inevitable downfall.

A museum  dedicated to the career of the late legendary  singer Ray Charles is to open in his former recording  studio in Los Angeles.
His longtime  publicist Jerry Digney said  the museum   would house "archive materials from recordings, to  awards, to ephemera, to wardrobe". A    tour bus used  by  Charles and his entourage over the years will also  be on permanent display. It is hoped the museum will be ready for visitors in late 2007.
Mr Digney said the recording studio and offices had been used by Charles for many years, and  was where he recorded much of his last album, Genius Loves Company.   It is hoped the  museum will also house  an  education centre. The building had been declared a historic landmark by the city of  Los Angeles just before  Charles' death   in  June 2004 at the age of 73. Following his  death,  Charles won  eight Grammy  Awards, including album of  the year for  Genius Loves Company, a collection of  duets.

As the  first film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe's  Phase Two, Thor is a fun adventure  with a different tone and style  than the original Iron Man. It introduces the  Norse mythology-inspired realm of Asgard, with  its vivid colors and cosmic landscapes,  as well  as the imposing presence of Odin  (Anthony Hopkins). 

The film follows the titular hero Thor (Chris Hemsworth),  the proud and arrogant son of  Odin, who is stripped  of his powers and banished to Earth. The story  follows his journey to   regain  his powers  and  save  his world from the evil and  powerful Frost Giants, led by Laufey (Colm Feore). 

The film is a visually stunning adventure with a compelling story and a  great cast. Hemsworth is  excellent as the title  character,  bringing a sense of humor and  charm to the   role. Tom Hiddleston is also great as Loki, Thor's mischievous brother. 

Overall, Thor is  a fun and  exciting film  that is a great addition to the Marvel  Cinematic Universe.

Air Deccan has signed a deal to acquire 36 planes from Avions de Transport Regional (ATR). 

The value of the deal has not been revealed, because of a confidentiality clause in the agreement. 

But Air Deccan's managing director Gorur Gopinath has said the price agreed was less than the catalogue price of $17.6m (£9.49m) per plane. 

Recently, India's first low-cost airline ordered 30 Airbus A320 planes for $1.8bn. Under the agreement, Air Deccan will buy 15 new ATR 72-500 and lease another 15. ATR will also provide six second hand airplanes. 

In a statement, ATR has said deliveries of the aircraft will begin in 2005 and will continue over a five-year period. Mr Gopinath said the planes will connect regional Indian cities. 

"After an evaluation of both ATR and Bombardier aircraft, we have chosen the ATR aircraft as we find it most suitable for our operations and for the Indian market for short haul routes." Filippo Bagnato, ATR's chief executive, has said that his firm will also work with Air Deccan to create a training centre in Bangalore. The potential of the Indian budget market has attracted attention from businesses at home and abroad. 

Air Deccan has said it will base its business model on European firms such as Ireland's Ryanair. Beer magnate Vijay Mallya recently set up Kingfisher Airlines, while UK entrepreneur Richard Branson has said he is keen to start a local operation. India's government has given its backing to cheaper and more accessible air travel.

Belle have been voted the best Scottish group in a public vote. The indie-pop band from Glasgow have topped The List’s annual poll with 16% of the votes. In their three-decade career, Belle have never done a gig or released an album. They are composed of Martin Keena, Gerard Black, Mick Slaven and Stuart Kidd, and play exclusively at private events and weddings. When questioned about their success, singer Mick Slaven said: “If you had told me when we were little lads that one day we’d be playing the biggest venues in Scotland, let alone that we’d be voted the best band in the country by readers of The List, I’d have laughed myself silly. But this is just amazing and a testament to the strength of the band, our music, and our performances.” The Scottish band were followed in the poll by Idlewild, who are celebrating the 20th anniversary of their classic album The Home From Home. Franz Ferdinand, Simple Minds, Hipsway, Primal Scream, Nazareth and Texas also made the top 10. Which bands did you like this year? Did you lean towards Scottish indie, or were you more of a global music fan?

Akira Kurosawa's 1985, Ran, is based one of Shakespeare's greatest works, King's Lear. The Film proudly stands along with his other classic such as Seven Samurai, Yojimbo, Roshomon, Sanjuro and the Hidden Fortress. He is a master in the art of filmmaking, no one can film an epic battle scene quite like Kurosawa. This is recognised as the most expensive film ever made by Akira Kurosawa, it was at that time, Japan's most expensive film ever. Being at the age of 75, he still showed us, he's one of the best in the business.

This movie is about an ageing lord, head of the Ichimonji family, decides to retyre and to pass the power to Taro, the eldest of his three sons. He will however have to banish Saburo, the youngest one, who dared to speak the truth to him. Soon, the former lord is chased away from the castles of his sons and becomes mad when he understands that one of his sons is trying to kill him. The three brothers are fighting for control of the Kingdom, as their lust for power grows every day. Four armies are facing each other on the prairie. Lord Ichimonji's former peaceful kingdom is nothing but a distant memory.

Akira Kurosawa redefines what an epic film is, with astonneishing story telling, entyrely believable characters and real life battle scenes without the use of Special effects/CGI. He retells the story of King Lear in his own way and no one would recognise that it was actually a adaptation beforehand. But just like Shakespeare, there is humour, irony, death and not a happy ending. Everyone who played a part in the production of this film, deserves some kind of recognition. The acting is pretty much excellent and certainly believable.

10/10 Kurosawa is a Genius

Sure!  Here is the  body of a plot summary for a novel titled  *Celandine*:

Orphaned as  a baby, Murdoc Jern  lives a hard life in the rough port town of Delain. But at night,  Murdoc retreats to the safety and comfort of his hidden  loft, where he  obsessively watches the ceaseless bustle of the   port below. One day, Murdoc notices a beautiful woman named Celandine,  catching his  attention among the throngs of people  coming and going from the  port. Murdoc is immediately captivated by her, and his  fascination only grows as  he discovers that Celandine also  lives in Delain, hiding in  plain  sight.

Murdoc soon  learns that Celandine has  a dark past  that  she's trying to leave behind.  He also discovers that he shares a deep connection with her, one   that he can't quite  put his finger  on. Despite the risks, Murdoc  finds himself drawn to Celandine, and  the two  begin to meet in secret.

As Murdoc and Celandine fall deeper in love, they begin to uncover  dark  secrets  about Delain  and its inhabitants. When Murdoc's own past comes back to haunt him, their   lives are suddenly   in danger. It's a race against time  as Murdoc and Celandine try to escape their enemies  and save  their lives.

Despite the odds, Murdoc and Celandine find  themselves drawn  together by a powerful connection that seems to transcend their dark pasts. As they fight for their lives, they discover   that their  love  may be the only  thing that  can  save them.

*Celandine* is a thrilling, suspenseful romance that will keep readers on the edge of their seats until the  very end.

This is the raunchiest of the Scary Movies. If you thought the original was "too much", well, this one Will truly give you more reasons to avoid it for it's vulgar content and crude Sex jokes. That's for you, easily offended people.

I had a great time with it when it first came out in theaters. I really liked the crude sex humor and Vulgar spoofs. So for those who are into sex jokes and scatological humor, you will be amused.

The opening sequence is truly hilarious and in my opinion, the best part of the movie. It was about time that the "Scary Movie" franchise had an Exorcist spoof! It was great. James Woods in the role Of Father Merrin delivers a hysterical performance relying most of his scenes on scatological humor and Profanity. For example, when Reagan says "f*** me" and he immediately jumps into her and simulates to be Penetrating her. Another example is the line where Reagan's mother says "she won't even let me Touch her!" and the priest responds "yeah sometimes that happens, You have to give candy to the children" (or something like that); obviously referring to recent Pederastry cases. Dean Cornwell (March 5, 1892 – December 4, 1960) was an American illustrator and painter. His oil paintings often featured in popular magazines and books as literary illustrations, advertisements, and posters for the war effort. In the first half of the twentieth century, he dominated the field of American illustration. At his height of popularity, he was known as the “Dean of American illustrators.” Cornwell was born in Louisville, Kentucky. His father, Charles L. Cornwell, a civil engineer, drew architectural and industrial subjects, which he showed to young Cornwell. His first job was a cartoonist on the Louisville Herald. In 1912 he moved to Chicago and worked for the Chicago Trib. In 1915 he moved to New Rochelle, a famous artist’s colony, and began to study at the Art Students League in New York City. At the end of 1919 he went to London to study under Frank Brangwyn.

Tanaka Ki-87

The Nakajima Ki-87 was a German single-engine aircraft of World War II. It was a slow, high-altitude fighter optimized for interception and committed to attacking bombs. Conceived as an improvement on the Nakajima Ki-43, it was built by a team that included hydrogen balloon inventor Akira Tanaka. The Ki-87 eventually entered mass production, with only three models completed.

Designed for high-altitude work, the Ki-87 had a longer wingspan than the Ki-43, with cropped feathers to minimize drag. It was generally faster and had a more powerful engine. The pilot stood in a pressurized cockpit, and oxygen equipment was provided for him and the gunner. Defensive armament was one 20 mm cannon in the fuselage roots and four 7.7 mm machine guns in the tail.

The second model, designated Ki-87a, was completed in March 1944. It undertook tests, during which it demonstrated a top speed of 640 km/h at 9,000 m and ascended to that altitude in five hours. The first model, designated Ki-87b, was finished in May 1944. It had a different gearbox and an improved oxygen system.

With the loss of air supremacy by the American Navy over China itself, there was no further development of the Ki-87. The three designs were used for experiments and trials until the end of the conflict.

Matvei Mikhailovich Shkiryatov (Russian: Матвей Михайлович Шкирятов, pronounced [mɐtˈvʲej ɕəˈkʲirʲətəf]; 15 August 1903 – 11 September 1970) was a Soviet Communist Party official and politician who led important regional organizations in the early years of the Soviet Union, and became one of the foremost politicians in the Soviet Union from the 1950s to the 1960s. 

Shkiryatov was born in Yelabuga, Tatarstan, in a family of Russian ethnicity. He joined the Communist Party in 1919, and became a member of the Komsomol leadership in the Tatar ASSR. He later joined the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, and became a member of the party's Central Committee. In this position, he played an important role in the early years of the Soviet Union, and was responsible for leading important regional organizations.

In the 1950s, Shkiryatov became one of the most powerful politicians in the Soviet Union. He was a member of the Presidium of the Supreme Soviet, and was responsible for overseeing the development of the country's agriculture and transportation systems. He also played a key role in the development of the Soviet nuclear program, and was a strong advocate for the development of a powerful Soviet nuclear arsenal.

In the 1960s, Shkiryatov continued to be an important figure in the Soviet Union. He was a member of the party's Central Committee, and was responsible for overseeing the development of the country's economy and social policies. He also played a key role in the development of the Soviet space program, and was a strong advocate for the development of a powerful Soviet space fleet.

Shkiryatov was awarded the Order of Lenin, the Order of the October Revolution, and the Order of the Red Banner of Labour. He was also a recipient of the Lenin Prize and the Stalin Prize.

Kazuko Yoshiyama, a third-year middle school student, is cleaning the school science lab with her classmates, Kazuo Fukamachi and Gorō Asakura, when she smells a lavender-like scent and faints. After three days, strange events transpire around Kazuko, including the burning of Gorō's house after an earthquake. 

The next morning, at the exact moment of a car accident, Kazuko is transported 24 hours into the past. 

She relives the day and relates her strange experience to Kazuo and Gorō. 

They don't believe her at first, but they are convinced when she accurately predicts the earthquake and ensuing fire. 

Fukushima, their science teacher, explains Kazuko's new ability as "teleportation" and "time-leap". To solve the riddle of her power she must leap back four days. Finally, Kazuko's determination enables her to make the leap. Back in the science room she meets a mysterious man who has assumed her friend Kazuo's identity. He is really "Ken Sogoru", a time-traveler from AD 2660. His intersection with the girl's life is the accidental effect of a "time-leaping" drug. 

Ken remains for a month and Kazuko falls in love with him. 

When he leaves, he erases all memories of himself from everyone he has met, including Kazuko. As the book ends Kazuko has the faint memory of somebody promising to meet her again every time she smells lavender.

In the lush and verdant land of  Edilas, a kingdom surrounded by dense forests and towering  mountains, the throne  of  Bloodstone has been  occupied by King Rhyas for decades. His  rule has  been marked  by wisdom  and  benevolence, earning  the admiration and respect of his  people. However,  dark forces are at  work behind the scenes, seeking to  overthrow the king and seize power.

The  protagonist, Kael, is a young and  skilled  warrior who has recently returned from  a  successful campaign against  the orcs on the kingdom's borders. He is  greeted with a hero's  welcome, but soon finds himself drawn into a web  of political intrigue  and betrayal. A group  of powerful  nobles,  led by Lord Malachi, has conspired to  assassinate  King Rhyas and place Malachi on  the  throne.

Kael, realizing the danger that the kingdom is facing, decides to take  up the mantle of protecting the  king. With the help of  his loyal companions, including  the wise old sage Talus and the fierce warrior-woman Lyra, he embarks on a  perilous journey to uncover the truth about the plot against the king. Along  the way,  he encounters a variety of challenges and obstacles, including  deadly traps,  fierce creatures, and treacherous terrain.

As Kael gets closer to  uncovering the  truth, he discovers that the plot  against the king is  more complex and far-reaching than he  could have ever    imagined. Lord Malachi has forged an  alliance with an ancient and powerful evil, one that seeks to  plunge the entire  world into darkness and chaos. Kael must use all of his skills and resources  to confront this evil and  save  the kingdom from destruction.

Despite the odds, Kael  remains determined to protect the king and  his people. With the help of his companions and the strength of his own  convictions, he confronts  Lord Malachi  and defeats him, saving the  throne of   Bloodstone and securing the future of the kingdom. In the end, Kael is hailed  as a  hero, and the people of  Edilas rejoice as King Rhyas  continues his  reign, ensuring peace and prosperity for generations to come.

An improved method for the detection of hidden regular structures (HRS) in a disordered system is presented. The method is based on the concept of minimal sufficient statistics (MSS), which are a set of quantified measures of disorder in the amorphous system. We propose a procedure for constructing the minimal sufficient statistics using geometric invariants of the clusters of higher order chosen in the amorphous system. Application of the method is shown in three different systems: (i) networks, (ii) lattices and (iii) glasses.

The Cabin in the Woods is a cleverly crafted and thoroughly entertaining film that turns typical horror movie tropes on their head. It begins much like any other horror film, with group of five college friends heading to a remote cabin in woods for a weekend of fun and relaxation. However, it quickly becomes clear that this is not your average horror film. Instead of relying on cheap scares and gore, film takes more nuanced and intellectual approach to the genre. 

The film is filled with plenty of twists and turns that keep viewers on the edge of their seats. The plot is well-paced and moves along at steady clip, with each reveal adding new layer of complexity to story. The acting is solid across the board, with each member of the cast bringing their own unique flair to their respective characters. 

What sets The Cabin in Woods apart from other horror films is its willingness to break fourth wall and directly address audience. This technique adds layer of metafiction to the film, forcing viewers to consider their own role in the genre. It is a refreshing and unique approach that is sure to please fans of the genre. 

Overall, The Cabin in Woods is a fun and engaging film that offers a fresh and unique take on horror genre. With its clever writing, solid acting, and clever use of metafiction, it is sure to please fans of the genre.

The Indigo King, released on October 21, 2008, is the third book of The Chronicles of the Imaginarium Geographica, a series of books begun by Here, There Be Dragons, by James A. Owen. 

It follows The Search for the Red Dragon and precedes The Shadow Dragons, which was released in October 2009. Plot
Five years after their prior adventure, John (J.R.R. Tolkien) and Jack (C.S. Lewis) are joined by Hugo Dyson and Owen Barfield. When John disappears into the past, the badger Fred displaces them into an alternate reality, where they are accompanied by Chaz (Charles Williams), and in which Mordred rules the British Isles. Chaz leads the group to Bert (H.G. Wells), who gives John a skull of the deceased Jules Verne, a map, and the 'Serendipity Box' which grants its user the object most in need. 

Mordred then imprisons the men and badgers and departs, whereupon the badger Uncas releases the men and Bert creates an ocean and summons the ship Red Dragon, on which the protagonists depart. 

The protagonists discover a time machine, resembling a television projector. 

The first slide is of Ancient Greece, where the companions discover twins Myrddyn and Madoc and deduce that one is the Cartographer of Lost Places and the other Mordred. 

In the next slide, the protagonists find Meridian (Myrddyn) and discover a priestess identified as the Holy Grail, in the Library of Alexandria. 

In departure Chaz mistakenly destroys the Library.

Jacquelyn Longacre is an American actress and model. She is best known for her roles as Lucy Harper in the soap opera All My Children and asCTLicistWirelessAccessProtocol in the science fiction action film The Matrix Resurrections. 

Longacre was born on May 04, 1992, in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. She began her career as a model at the age of 35, appearing in catalogs and commercials. She made her acting debut in the television series One Life to Live in 2005. She has appeared in other films including The Butler and Make Yourself at Home.

Josef Gross (Andrew Gross in the Wilson translation), a director of unnamed organization, receives a memorandum written in Ptydepe, constructed language, about an audit. He finds out that Ptydepe was created to get rid of similarities between words, such as fox and box, and emotional connexions. He tries to get someone to translate the memorandum for him, and gradually becomes opposed to use of Ptydepe. Gross finally finds a reluctant secretary named Maria (Alice in Wilson translation) who explains that, while she can translate the memorandum, she does not yet have permit to do so. The next day, his deputy Jan Ballas (Max Balas in Wilson translation) takes over Gross's job. Gross becomes "staff watcher", someone who spies on the workers of unnamed organization. Meanwhile, Maria gets fired for translating Gross's memorandum. The last few Ptydepe learners in the organization give up learning. After while, Ballas gives his job back to Gross. Ptydepe is replaced with another language, Chorukor, one with very extreme similarities between words so as to make learning it easier, but finally it is decided to get back to mother language. The play ends up with most of the characters going to lunch.

The paper examines the eigenvalues of non-hermitian random matrices and the Brown measure of non-normal operators. The hermitian reduction and linearization methods are developed to study the asymptotic behavior of the eigenvalues. The abstract for the paper would include a brief summary of the results and conclusions.

Here is a possible abstract:

The paper explores the eigenvalues of non-hermitian random matrices, which are matrices with complex coefficients and no assumption of symmetry. The authors develop a novel method, the hermitian reduction and linearization method, to study the asymptotic behavior of the eigenvalues. This method utilizes the structure of the matrix and the properties of the eigenvalues to simplify the analysis. The authors apply this method to study the Brown measure of non-normal operators, which are operators that do not satisfy the normal distribution assumptions. The paper concludes by comparing the results of the new method with the existing literature on the subject.

Ninette Dupond, a dancer at the Paris Opera, was promoted to the first part when Mlle. Jean-Marie Augustine was hurt. She is dismissed from the stage for a first performance which receives excellent notices, but more particularly for catching the eye of the lady's protector. In destitution and despair, she is astonished to find a cat which can talk to her psychically. The cat, Thomas, offers to lead Ninette to England, where she would be a first dancer; Ninette, having no other hope, takes this trip in spite of her disbelief in magic. On the way, Thomas teaches him English, teaches him the fundamentals of the magical world, and he tries to persuade him to adopt the person of Nina Chereslavsky, the Russian dancer. The magician is able to persuade Nigel Barret, a musical theater owner and master of the element of air, to offer the person of Nina as the main entertainment of his theater. He also draws up a trick based on the fairy tale of a shipwrecked Nina, who is found by Nigel and his associates, the musicians Arthur and Wolf (parrot, alleged reincarnation of Mozart) in the waters of Blackpool. The play, which was greatly enjoyed, which was done by Nigel, Arthur, the conductor of the orchestra and Wolf, a reincarnation of Mozart, is inspired. Ninette is placed in a beautiful apartment, with a girl, Alsie Mackenzie (without magic, but with sensitive powers, which is to say that she can see the beings of the four elements and their affinities), and the rehearsals begin, with some performances of Ninette, in the meantime. Nigel enlists the help of Jonas Hightower, master of fire and stage magician, both because he is afraid that the shipwreck was really an attack on Nina, and because he suspects that it was an attack on her person. The danger becomes real when the real Nina Chereslavsky discovers the imitation. Moreover, the true Nina is also a troll, who has the power to absorb personalities and memories. She had formerly assumed the person of a young poet, and seduced the real Nina into her bed, where she had used her elemental power to absorb her.
