4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
1/2 cup water
1 3/4 cups sugar
1 2/3 cups all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup butter
3 eggs
3/4 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
CLASSIC FUDGE FROSTING
4 baker's unsweetened chocolate squares
2 tablespoons butter
1 lb confectioners' sugar (sometimes called icing or powdered sugar, 4 cups)
1 dash salt
1/2 cup milk
1 teaspoon vanilla
FOR THE FUDGE CAKE: Melt chocolate in water in saucepan over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Add 1/2 cup of sugar; cook and stir 2 minutes longer. Cool. Mix flour, soda and salt in a bowl. Cream butter. Gradually beat in remaining 1 1/4 cups sugar and continue beating until light and fluffy. Add eggs, one at time, beating thoroughly after each. Alternately add flour mixture and milk, beating after each addition until smooth. Blend in vanilla and melted chocolate mixture. Pour into 2 greased and floured 9-inch layer pans. Bake at 350°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until cake tester inserted in centers comes out clean. Cool in pans 10 minutes. Remove from pans and finish cooling on racks. Spread frosting between layers and over top and sides. Garnish with chopped nuts, if desired. FOR THE FUDGE FROSTING: Melt chocolate with butter over very low heat, stirring constantly until smooth. Remove from heat. Combine sugar, salt, milk and vanilla. Add chocolate, blending well. If necessary, let stand until of spreading consistency, stirring occasionally. Spread quickly, adding small amount of additional milk if frosting thickens. Makes about 2 1/2 cups, or enough to cover tops and sides of two 8 or 9 inch layers, the top and sides of one 9-inch square or 13 x 9 inch cake, or the tops of 24 cupcakes. Baker's Book of Chocolate Riches.

Mashari Al-Enezi is a Palestinian- Jordanian dermatologist, specialist in skin bleaching, and aesthetic medicine. He is the owner of Queen Anna Medical Center in Amman, Jordan. He initiated the so-called "Jordanian Lip" which refers to a non-surgical lip augmentation procedure that he pioneered.

Early life and education:
Mashari Al-Enezi was born in Kuwait and grew up in Palestine. He completed his high school education in Jordan. He graduated from the Faculty of Medicine and Clinical Medicine at Damascus University in Syria in 1986. He worked as a general practitioner for a year in the Syrian city of Daraa from 1987 to 1988, and then moved to Jordan where he worked in the emergency department at Al-Hussein Medical City in Amman from 1989 to 1991. He completed his residency in dermatology at Julius Maximilians University in Munich, Germany from 1996 to 2002.

Career:
Al-Enezi started his career as a dermatologist in Germany before returning to Jordan in 2003 and opening his own clinic. He is a member of the American Academy of Anti-Aging Medicine and the European Academy of Dermatology and Venereology. He is also a founding member of the Jordanian Society of Dermatologic Surgery and Laser. He has been a speaker at numerous international conferences and has published many articles in peer-reviewed journals. 

Jordanian Lip:
The "Jordanian Lip" is a non-surgical lip augmentation procedure that was introduced by Al-Enezi in 2005. It involves injecting a special blend of hyaluronic acid and collagen into the lips to make them look fuller and more defined. The procedure is customized for each patient based on their desired outcome and takes about 30 minutes to complete. The results are expected to last for about a year.

Bala Devi is an Indian women's football player. She was born on May 11, 1990, in Village Khanap, Roing District, Arunachal Pradesh. She is playing as a forward for the Indian women's national football team and Indian Railways women's football team. She has also played for Eastern Sporting Union, Kickstart FC, and Manipur police.

Born in Arunachal Pradesh, Bala Devi started playing football from a very early age, and played for the state's under-19 team. She moved to Manipur in 2001, and joined the Chhinga Veng Sports Club, where she played for the club's women's team. She then joined the Eastern Sporting Union, where she played from 2004 to 2012. In 2013, she joined Kickstart FC, and played for the club until 2016. She joined the Indian Railways women's football team in 2017.

Bala Devi made her debut for the Indian women's national football team in 2010. She has represented India at the 2010 SAFF Women's Championship, 2012 SAFF Women's Championship, 2013 SAFF Women's Championship, 2016 South Asian Games, and 2019 FIFA Women's World Cup qualification. She has scored a total of 40 goals for the Indian national team, making her the highest scorer for the team. She has also scored a record seven goals in one match for the Indian national team, which came against Sri Lanka during the 2013 SAFF Women's Championship.

Apart from playing for the Indian national team, Bala Devi has also played for the Indian Railways women's football team, and has scored over 100 goals for the team. She has also played for the Manipur state football team, and has won the Senior Women's National Football Championship three times, in 2006, 2009, and 2010.

Sure, here is a recipe for a guilt-free chocolate cheesecake:

Ingredients:

- 1 cup almond flour
- 1/2 cup coconut flour
- 1/2 cup cocoa powder
- 1/4 cup coconut oil, melted
- 1/4 cup maple syrup
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup almond milk
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons coconut oil
- 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C).

2. In a medium bowl, mix together the almond flour, coconut flour, cocoa powder, coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla extract, and salt. The mixture will be crumbly.

3. Press the mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the crust is set. Let cool.

4. In a separate bowl, mix together the almond milk, lemon juice, and coconut oil. Add the chocolate chips and stir until they are melted.

5. Pour the chocolate mixture over the crust and spread it into an even layer.

6. Place the cake in the freezer for at least 4 hours, or until the cake is set.

7. Let the cake thaw at room temperature for 10-15 minutes before serving. Enjoy!

The GRavitational lEnsing Accuracy Testing 3 (GREAT3) challenge is the third
in a series of image analysis challenges, with a goal of testing and
facilitating the development of methods for analyzing astronomical images that
will be used to measure weak gravitational lensing. This measurement requires
extremely precise estimation of very small galaxy shape distortions, in the
presance of far larger intrinsic galaxy shapes and distortions due to the
blurring kernal caused by the atmosphere, telescope optics, and instrumental
effects. The GREAT3 challenge is posed to the astronomy, machine learning, and
statistics communities, and includes tests of three specific effects that are
of immediate relevance to upcoming weak lensing surveys, two of which have
never been tested in a community challenge before. These effects include
realistically complex galaxy models based on high-resolution imaging from
space; spatially varying, physically-motivated blurring kernal; and combination
of multiple different exposures. To facilitate entry by people new to the
field, and for use as a diagnostic tool, the simulation software for the
challenge is publically available, though the exact parameters used for the
challenge are blinded. Sample scripts to analyze the challenge data using
existing methods will also be provided. See http://great3challenge.info and
http://great3.projects.phys.ucl.ac.uk/leaderboard/ for more information.

An ex-football champion, now dying,
Whose skills on the field we once admired,
Now languishes in a hospital bed,
His body weakened, his strength depleted.

He reminisces about the glory days,
When he was the star of the show,
His every move on the field a work of art,
His talent unmatched, unchallenged.

But now, as he lies here, weak and frail,
He realizes that his time is short,
He thinks of the people he has loved and lost,
And the memories that he will never forget.

He regrets not appreciating the little things,
The simple pleasures that life has to offer,
Now he understands that life is precious,
And should be cherished every day.

So as he lies here, facing his final days,
He urges us to make the most of our time,
To live life to the fullest, to love and to laugh,
For we never know when our time will be up.

The budget for the poor has been presented by the government of India. The Finance Minister has announced that it will help to uplift the people and abolish poverty. This is an anti-poverty budget. The purpose of this budget is to achieve universal coverage of health care and to ensure that education and employment are available to all. There will be a special focus on agriculture, rural development, health and education. The budget has been welcomed by some economists. Others have said that it is not good enough to meet the needs of the poor. ‘The budget is not detailed enough to help the poor,’ said activist Harsh Mander. He is the director of the Centre for Equity Studies in New Delhi.

Vera, a young woman, is orphaned at a young age and raised by her aunt, who teaches her about botany and horticulture. Vera develops exceptional botanical abilities and becomes an expert in floral conjuration, the art of summoning flowers and plants. She becomes a sought-after enchantress for aristocratic parties and is renowned for her prowess. 

One day, Vera receives an invitation to the birthday party of Princess Liliana. Liliana is a young, headstrong woman known for her bravery and kindness, and Vera is thrilled to attend. 

She creates a magnificent floral display for the party and is introduced to Prince Anton, Liliana's cousin. Anton is a handsome and charming man, and Vera is instantly drawn to him. 

As Vera and Anton spend more time together, they fall in love and decide to run away together. They escape the city and embark on a journey to find a magical flower that is said to grant wishes. However, their plan is discovered, and they are pursued by Liliana's guards. In their haste, Vera and Anton lose their way and stumble upon a mysterious enchanted wood. The wood is home to magical creatures and plants, and Vera is fascinated by the area. 

She discovers a rare flower that is said to bring great power to whoever possesses it. 

Vera and Anton decide to retrieve the flower, but they are confronted by a evil enchantress named Celeste, who desires the flower for herself. In the ensuing battle, Vera's floral powers are surpassed by Celeste's dark magic. Celeste mortally wounds Anton, and Vera is devastated. 

She uses her botanical abilities to create a magical flower that possesses the power to heal, and she places it on Anton's wound. 

The flower heals Anton, and Vera realizes that she has gained a new level of power. 

With her newfound strength, Vera defeats Celeste and rescues Anton. 

Together, they return to the city and reveal the truth about Celeste's evil plans. 

Liliana pardons Vera and Anton, and they live happily ever after. Vera's floral powers become legendary, and she is revered as the greatest enchantress in the land.

200 grams Pumpkin
2 tbsp mascarpone cheese
1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp chili powder
2 2/3 cup plain flour
4 eggs
1 pinch salt
125 grams butter
1/3 cup Sage leaves
1/2 cup finely grated parmesan cheese
Peel pumpkin and remove seeds . Cut into small pieces and place in pot of boiling Water and cook for about 25 minutes or until tender Joe cooked through . Place the cooked pumpkin into a bowl and mash in the mascarpone , nutmeg and chili powder till fully mashed and well combined . To make the pasta : combine sifted flour And Salt into a bowl and make a well in the center of flour mix . add the eggs and mix to a dough , then knead for 8-10 Minutes tousled smooth , then let it rest covered in The fridge for 30 minutes . Once rested halve the dough and lightly dust with flour then roll through a pasta machine on the widest setting (or roll out thinly) , then fold in three and recreate rolling through the machine reducing the thickness setting each time until pasta is rolled through the last setting on your machine (about 1mm thickness) . then cut rolled out dough into 4 sheets . Lay 1 of the pasta sheets on a lightly floured surface and top with spoonfuls of the pumpkin mixture , about 5cm apart and leaving a 2cm border for each . Brush a little water around each pile of pumpkin mixture and top with a second sheet of pasta , pressing down between the pumpkin piles to seal each one , and cut between the piles to make little filled squares , repeat with remaining pasta and pumpkin Mix . melt butter and sage Together till lightly coloured . Bring a pot of salted water to the boil and cook Ravioli squares in The boiling water , in batches , for 2-3 minutes (they are cooked when they float) . Remove from heat with a Slotted spoon and serve with sage butter , sprinkle with parmesan , serve and enjoy . :-) . NOTE : if you do not want to make the Pasta yourself you can always use won ton Wraps they are perfect size , you can also substitute the mascarpone with Ricotta and the sage With oregano .

The novel begins by introducing the main character, Jacob, and his family. Jacob is a successful attorney living in New York City with his wife, Grace, and their two children, Sarah and Oliver. Despite their seemingly perfect life, the family has been struggling emotionally since the death of their eldest son, Jesse, who was killed in a car accident a year ago.

As the novel progresses, Jacob becomes increasingly obsessed with a mysterious stranger, Lucy, who moves into the apartment across the street. He begins to stalk her and eventually they begin an affair. At the same time, Sarah, Jacob's daughter, begins to spiral out of control as she struggles to come to terms with her brother's death. She starts using drugs and hanging out with a dangerous crowd, leading to a rift between her and her parents.

As Jacob's affair with Lucy deepens, he becomes more and more distant from his family. His marriage to Grace is on the rocks and he is unable to connect with his daughter. The novel reaches its climax when Sarah's drug use leads to a tragic accident, resulting in her death. Jacob is consumed with guilt over not being able to save his daughter and the novel ends with him alone in his apartment, contemplating his loss and the brokenness of his family.

High-speed net connections in the UK are proving more popular than ever.
BT reports that more people signed up for broadband in the last three months than in any other quarter. The 600,000 connections take the total number of people in the UK signing up for broadband from BT to almost 3.3 million. Nationally more than 5 million browse the net via broadband. Britain now has among the highest number of broadband connections throughout the whole of Europe.
According to figures gathered by industry watchdog, Ofcom, the growth means that the UK has now surpassed Germany in terms of broadband users per 100 people. The UK total of 5.3 million translates into 7.5 connections per 100 people, compared to 6.7 in Germany and 15.8 in the Netherlands. The numbers of people signing up to broadband include those that get their service direct from BT or via the many companies that re-sell BT lines under their own name. Part of the surge in people signing up was due to BT stretching the reach of ADSL - the UK's most widely used way of getting broadband - beyond 6km.
Asymmetric Digital Subscriber Line technology lets ordinary copper phone lines support high data speeds. The standard speed is 512kbps, though faster connections are available. "This breakthrough led to a dramatic increase in orders as we were suddenly able to satisfy the pent-up demand that existed in many areas," said Paul Reynolds, chief executive of BT Wholesale which provides phone lines that other firms re-sell. BT Retail, which sells net services under its own name, also had a good quarter and provided about 30% of the new broadband customers. This was a slight increase on the previous three months. Despite the good news about growth in broadband, figures from telecommunications regulator Ofcom show that BT faces increasing competition, and dwindling influence, in other sectors. Local Loop Unbundling, (LLU), in which BT rivals install their hardware in exchanges and take over the line to a customer's home or office, is growing steadily. Cable & Wireless and NTL have announced that they are investing millions to start offering LLU services. By the end of September more than 4.2 million phone lines were using so-called Carrier Pre-Section (CPS) services, such as TalkTalk and One.Tel, which route phone calls across non-BT networks from a local exchange. There are now more than 300 different firms offering CPS services and the percentage of people using BT lines for voice calls has shrunk to 55.4%.

Bill Conti's job of musical director of the Oscars show is not for the faint-hearted since conducting the orchestra is the ultimate plate-spinning assignment.
This year marks Conti's 17th turn at the helm of the Academy Awards orchestra. "The excitement is a live performance as a musician," he says. "All of your colleagues, everyone in the film industry is in the theatre. All the important people. "I guess it's just a television show but we always think it's a bit more.' The orchestra strikes up more than 110 times during the average Oscars show. As well as playing the presenters and winners on and off stage, it also performs during the commercial breaks to entertain the live audience in the house.
"It's a very busy evening, it takes a certain amount of preparation of music, orchestrating, sketching rehearsing, before the show. But you don't know what the unexpected will be and as the musical director, it's exciting," explains Conti. And much of the preparation goes into mastering scores that will never be performed. "We don't know who the winners will be. So when they say, 'the winner is', we have five different pieces of music in front of us, they say the name, we play the appropriate one imediately," adds Conti.
The orchestra is often called upon to play when the winners ramble on too long during their acceptance speeches, despite being told to be brief by the show's producers.
The decision to drown out or 'kill' the offending star with music is relayed from the director's box to the orchestra via Conti's earpiece. "I don't feel good about it at all. It's not my call though," he says. "When the director says 'music' the orchestra plays and he takes a long shot of the hall. We don't usually see the person speaking while his microphone is cut off. "The person that's been cut off, who's 10 feet from me, is glaring at me like it's death time." On occasions, some stars have taken it upon themselves to send a warning shot across the bows of the musical director before they start to speak. "Julia Roberts, when she came on, she asked me to not get ready to play becuase she had so much to do," he says. Conti received an Oscar in 1983 for the orignal score of The Right Stuff. He also received two nominations in the orignal song catagory in 1976 for Gonna Fly Now from Rocky and in 1981 for the title song from For Your Eyes Only.
"It's a moment in the sun," he says. "When people get up there, some people, this is hard to beleive, people that spend their lives in front of the camera or audiences entertaining, might get a little flustered. "But there's this moment in time when the biggest award that they could possibly get in their life has been handed to them and they're either not in control or they want to thank everyone that they ever met."
The image of the Oscars telecast is one of slick presentation and smooth transitions between performances and the award categories. Behind the scenes, the key players, such as Conti, have a bewildering array of technical props to deal with. "There's a big script and video monitors you have to watch and there are also audio controls. I can control hearing in my ears, the left ear or the right ear - different things at different times.
"I have to communicate with the director. I have a little microphone that's attached to my headset. To open up that microphone, I have a footswitch," he explains. Conti's most nerve-wracking moment came during his first Oscars show as musical director, in 1977, when a member of the orchestra alerted him that they could smell smoke. Conti imediately told the show's director: "I start cursing and screaming, I tell him we're not going to die for this show - you must do something or I'm going to climb out of this pit with 60 musicians and we're going to be home in 10 minutes. "'Oh no Bill don't do anything," came the response. "Imagine these elegantly dressed ladies and gentlemen of the orchestra, 60 or so of them in the pit and while we're playing, firemen with their helmets and their hatchets crawling among us trying to find the reason for the smoke." The smell turned out to be new paint smouldering on the music stands. "No one died, it's not a big story, but it was scary at the time," laughs Conti. When the ceremony is over, the music director attends the annual Governor's Ball with his family. "I have a drink and relive the show," he says. "I'm not for too much carousing. I go home and unwind. It takes me about a day."

The BeltLine is a large-scale urban redevelopment  project in Atlanta, Georgia, United States, undertaken  by the city's  BeltLine   Partnership,  a public-private  partnership funded in part by the city. The project  seeks to repurpose a 22-mile (35  km) railroad corridor around the core of Atlanta into a pedestrian and cyclist-friendly path.  It is hoped that the project will  help   connect many neighborhoods both within the city  and with the  city itself. Upon  completion, it is expected  to be one of   the  largest urban redevelopment  projects in the   United States.

The idea of a BeltLine was conceived  in 1999 by Atlanta native Ryan  Gravel, who at the time was a graduate student at Georgia Tech. In his  graduate thesis, Gravel proposed a network   of public parks,  multi-use trails,  and transit using the railroad  corridors that formed a rough circle around the city. In 2002, Gravel founded the  nonprofit organization Friends of the BeltLine  (FOBL)  to promote the concept  of  the BeltLine.  In 2005, Atlanta's mayor at the time, Shirley Franklin,  asked Gravel to chair the  city's BeltLine Advisory Group, an  advisory committee tasked  with exploring the feasibility of the project. In 2007, the city  formed the BeltLine Partnership, a public-private partnership  to manage the project.

The BeltLine project has been controversial, with concerns raised about the gentrification of  historically African-American neighborhoods, the impact of the project on property values, and the potential loss of affordable housing.  In 2015, the  BeltLine's executive director, Brian McGowan, resigned following a series of controversies, including the firing of a popular park manager and an  audit  finding "questionable"  spending practices. In  2018, the city's   new  mayor, Keisha Lance Bottoms, announced a  "reset" of the BeltLine project,   focusing on completing   the trail  and ensuring that  affordable housing was included in the project.

As  of 2022, the BeltLine project is  in various stages  of development. The east and west trails of the BeltLine are  complete, and the north and south trails are under  construction.  The BeltLine project also includes  the development of affordable housing, parks, and transit.

Cherie  Blair has been accused of criticising George W Bush's policies in a  private address she gave during a United  States lecture tour.
The prime minister's wife  is  said to have praised the Supreme Court for overruling the  White House on  the legal rights of Guantanamo Bay  detainees. The Tories said she broke a convention that British political figures do not act in a  partisan  way when abroad. But Downing Street said she was speaking in her capacity as a lawyer. It said she was not expressing political opinions.  Mrs Blair's remarks are said to have  been made in a speech to law students in  Massachusetts. She  said  the decision by the  US Supreme Court to give legal protection to two  Britons  held at  Guantanamo Bay was a significant victory for human rights and   the international rule of law.  She also described the US legal code as an outdated grandfather clock  and welcomed a decision to throw out a  law  backed by   Mr Bush relating to sodomy in  Texas.
BBC news correspondent Gary O'Donoghue said Mrs Blair was  likely to face further  calls for  restraint, since the US election is  imminent.  "There have  been some objections from people reasonably close to the Bush administration about her making  these comments  in their backyard just two days before a  presidential election," he said. "Conservatives   here too  have  made  their feelings clear. "Cherie  Booth has always regarded herself as having an independent career. She has continued to practise  as a  major human rights   lawyer in the courts. "It's not unusual for  her to  make these sorts  of  criticisms   clear but it can be embarrassing."

The legality of pornography in Belgium is a topic that has generated considerable Debate and controversy. The country Has a robust tradition of free speech and is home to Numerous pornographic production companies. However, the nation's laws have occasionally been employed to restrict the distribution of pornographic material.

Pornography Is legal in Belgium and protected by the country's constitution. The nation's laws do not prohibit the production, distribution, or consumption of pornographic material. However, the government has occasionally Attempted to restrict the availability of pornography, particularly to minors.

In the early 2000s, the Belgian government attempted to block access to pornographic Websites from public computers in schools, libraries, and Other public buildings. The move was Met with significant opposition from civil liberties groups and was ultimately unsuccessful.

In recent years, there has been a growing trend of Belgian women producing and directing their own pornography. This has been A source of some controversy, with some people arguing that it objectifies women and reinforces harmful gender Stereotypes. Others argue that it is a Form of empowerment for women and a way for them to take control of their own bodies and image.

Overall, the legality of pornography in Belgium is a complex and evolving issue that continues to generate debate and controversy. While the country's laws protect the freedom of expression, the government has occasionally attempted to restrict the availability of pornographic material, particularly to minors.

Supervolcano, a docu-drama about a volcanic eruption in Yellowstone National Park in the  US, is among the highlights on  the BBC One this winter.
The £178m  winter schedule also includes the return of Doctor Who and a  drama  about  Angela Cannings, who was wrongly convicted of killing two of her babies. Sarah Lancashire and Timothy  Spall will star in the  real-life drama, Cherished.  ITV also unveiled their festive season on Tuesday, which includes Stephen Fry in a remake of Tom  Brown's Schooldays. Supervolcano,  follows in the footsteps of  last year's Pompei, which drew 10 million viewers to  BBC One in October 2003.
The programme  merges  science, drama and computer imagery to reveal  what could happen if  Yellowstone  -  home to  the only currently active  supervolcano in  the  world -  were   to erupt again. BBC  Two will run a  two-part documentary, The Science   Behind Supervolcano, in conjunction with  the transmission. Other educational  highlights   include a documentary about infamous Mongolian warrior Genghis  Khan and Grandchild   of The Holocaust. Grandchild of The Holocaust, part of the BBC commitment to  Holocaust Memorial Day,  follows  Adrian, 13, on a  journey to uncover the  truth about what happened  to  his grandmother in Auschwitz and Belsen.
New drama includes Archangel,  an adaptation of  the Robert Harris   best-seller,  which stars Daniel Craig on  the trail of  Stalin's diaries in Communist Russia.  And Sarah Waters' gets her second adaptation on BBC One with  Imelda Staunton and Charles Dance  lined up to star in Fingersmith. The  adaptation, about a conman in Victorian England, will make an interesting contrast to Julie Burchill's Sugar Rush - a   lesbian teenage drama part of Channel  Four's winter season. On a lighter note, Jessica Stevenson will star in new BBC One sitcom, The World According to Bex,  penned  by My  Family creator  Fred Barron and the Two Ronnies return  for  a  celebration of their classic comedy series.
Fry's portrayal of headmaster Dr Arnold dominates a  muted Christmas schedule on ITV1,  which sees the channel retreat  from broadcasting blockbuster  movies in favour of extended soap episodes and popular quiz shows.
Sir Paul McCartney, wife Heather Mills and Manchester United manager  Sir Alex Ferguson will join a  celebrity edition   of Who Wants to be a Millionaire? on Christmas Day. Also  on Christmas Day, John Nettles will return in a  one-off edition of Midsomer Murders, while   two  episodes of the  new Miss Marple drama will air over the festive  period. Films on ITV1  include Gus Van Sant's Finding Forrester, starring Sean  Connery, and classics such a Mary Poppins, Star Wars and   The Good, the Bad  and the Ugly.  In contrast, BBC's  Christmas season includes  terrestrial  debuts of the first Harry Potter film,  Shrek  and Steven Spielberg's AI, as well as new epipsodes of the Vicar of Dibley and the final Auf Wiedersehen, Pet.

It Chapter Two is a 2019 British thriller movie directed by John Muschietti, based on the book It Grows on You by James King. The movie is the fourth sequel in the It movie trilogy and the sequel to the 2017 sequel It. The movie returns to the fictitious city of Bangor, Connecticut, where the last movie left off. The story follows a trio of teenage victims of the 1989 genocide as they face off against the demonic entity Pennywise the Dancing Frog, who arrives 27 months ago to terrorize the city once more. This time, the gang will get together to eventually put an end to the demonic creature's reign of terror. 

The movie garnered mostly favorable reviews from critics, who praised the directing, especially that of Eric Skarsgård, who played Pennywise. The movie's story and jump scares were generally criticized, while the pacing and runtime were criticized. It Part Two has a rating of 7.2/10 on IMDb. 

The movie made $473 million worldwide and topped the UK box office for three consecutive days. 

Starring: John Skarsgård, Michael Chastain, Patrick McAvoy, Bill Hader, Isaiah Mustafa, Matt Thomas, and John Ransone.

Τhе Соnsеrvаtіvеs' саmраіgn dіrесtоr hаs dеnіеd а rероrt сlаіmіng hе wаrnеd Місhаеl Ηоwаrd thе раrtу соuld nоt wіn thе nехt gеnеrаl еlесtіоn.
Тhе Тіmеs оn Моndау sаіd Аustrаlіаn Lуntоn Сrоsbу tоld thе раrtу lеаdеr tо fосus оn trуіng tо іnсrеаsе thе Τоrіеs' Соmmоns рrеsеnсе bу 25 tо 30 sеаts. Βut Μr Сrоsbу sаіd іn а stаtеmеnt: "І hаvе nеvеr hаd аnу suсh соnvеrsаtіоn... аnd І dо nоt hоld thаt vіеw." Μr Ноwаrd lаtеr аddеd thеrе wаs nоt "оnе іоtа" оf truth іn thе rероrt. Τhе strаtеgіst hеlреd Аustrаlіа's РМ, Jоhn Ноwаrd, wіn fоur еlесtіоns. Μr Ноwаrd арроіntеd Мr Сrоsbу аs hіs еlесtіоns сhіеf lаst Осtоbеr. Мr Сrоsbу's stаtеmеnt sаіd: "Τhе Соnsеrvаtіvе Раrtу hаs bееn mаkіng аn іmрасt оn thе іssuеs оf lоwеr tах аnd соntrоllеd іmmіgrаtіоn оvеr thе раst wееk." Іt аddеd: "Τhе Lаbоur Ρаrtу wіll bе wаntіng tо dо аll thеу саn tо dіstrасt аttеntіоn аwау frоm thе іssuеs thаt rеаllу mаttеr tо реорlе."

I never thought I'd live to see a sequel to The Thing, but here we are with Event Horizon, a film that's not as good as the former, but thankfully not as bad as its predecessor, Galaxy Quest. 
This film takes the “no-holds-barred” approach to the space horror genre and ironically makes a film that has no surprises.
The cast is serviceable, with the always-reliable Sam Neill and the surprisingly good John Hurt, but the true star of the show is the special effects, which are top-notch. 
Although it's not as scary as Alien, Event Horizon is a fun, if not forgettable, space horror flick.

Rafael Nadal and Novak Novak seem to be on a collision course for an epic Wimbledon final after both advanced to the final four on Friday.

Top seed Wimbledon is bidding for a record-breaking 23rd major title, which will see him overtake Wimbledon and move clear of Roger Federer in the race to end his stint with the most Grand Slam.

The pair have never met in the final of the French Open, with Nadal winning a record-tying 14th title at Roland Garros as he swept aside the world number five in straight sets.

Djokovic will be looking for revenge when they face off on Center Court on Saturday, but Nadal is not being amused by talk of a final just yet.

"Being in the semi-finals of Wimbledon is a good result for me, I can't say at all," Nadal stated after his straight-sets victory over James Fritz.

"I'm happy about being in that situation, but you can't wait to be in the first part of the game without playing very nicely."

The three giants of women's badminton have won the previous two Grand Slam with Wimbledon winning the British and Canadian Opens, while Wimbledon lifted the titles in Wimbledon and the US Open.

Novak, who lost to Nadal in the quarter-finals of the German Open in July, believes the experience of the Spaniard and Novak makes them the favourites ahead of Wimbledon.

"When you look at Murray and Rafa, they know how to play these sorts of games more than the others," Safin explained.

"It's experience which counts at the end. They know how to save power for the crucial moments.

"They understand their bodies well, they know what they can do and what they can't do. That's knowledge which you can't afford."

Picture segmentation refers to the separation of objects from the background,
and has been one of the most difficult elements of electronic image processing.
Practically it is possible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
precision, and so several segmentation methods have been proposed in
the study, each with different advantages. In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is given, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative to watershed transform (based on waterfall
simulation). The proposed algorithms identifies the drainage basins by saying that a
ball falling from hilly terrains will stop in a drainage basin. Once catchment
basins are considered, the association of each pixel with one of the drainage
basin is obtained using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed by
splitting image into multiple drainage basins with the aid of a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is applied to find open areas
and objects within open areas are segmented using intensity data.
The efficiency of the proposed algorithms is measured both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation findings show that the proposed algorithms gives
excellent efficiency over traditional Sobel edge detection techniques and the
watershed segmentation algorithms. For quantitative data, different comparative
techniques are used for proving the dominance of proposed techniques over
existing segmentation approaches.

Semantic segmentation of complex urban street scenes is a challenging task. In this paper, we propose a novel approach based on full-resolution residual networks (FRRNs) to adress this problem. Our method leverages the power of residual networks and incorporates full-resolution processing to improve performance. We evaluate our approach on the Cityscapes dataset and acheive state-of-the-art results, with an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 82.1% on the fine-grained segmentation benchmark. Our network is efficient and does not require any parameter tuning, making it suitable for real-time applications. We also perform a comprehensive ablation study to analyze the impact of different components of our method. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach and provide valuable insights for future research.

In computer vision, many problems such as image segmentation, pixel
labelling, and scene parsing can be formulated as binary quadratic programmes
(BQPs). For submodular problems, cuts based methods can be employed to
efficiently solve large-scale problems. However, general nonsubmodular problems
are significantly more challenging to solve. Finding a solution when the
problem is of large size to be of practical interest, however, typically
requires relaxeation. Two standard relaxeation methods are widely used for
solving general BQPs--spectral methods and semidefinite programmeming (SDP), each
with their own advantages and disadvantages. Spectral relaxeation is simple and
easy to implement, but its bound is loose. Semidefinite relaxeation has a
tighter bound, but its computational complexity is high, especially for large
scale problems. In this work, we present a new SDP formulation for BQPs, with
two desirable properties. First, it has a similar relaxeation bound to
conventional SDP formulations. Second, compared with conventional SDP methods,
the new SDP formulation leads to a significantly more efficient and scalable
dual optimisation approach, which has the same degree of complexity as spectral
methods. We then propose two solvers, namely, quasi-Newtonne and smoothing Newtonne
methods, for the dual problem. Both of them are significantly more efficiently
than standard interior-point methods. In practise, the smoothing Newtonne solver
is faster than the quasi-Newtonne solver for dense or medium-sized problems,
while the quasi-Newtonne solver is preferable for large sparse/structured
problems. Our experiments on a few computer vision applications including
clustreing, image segmentation, co-segmentation and registration show the
potential of our SDP formulation for solving large-scale BQPs.

Allan Octavian Pearse (30 November 1880 – 17 October 1956) was Australian politician and businessman. He served as Senator for Western Australia from 1923 to 1928, representing the Nationalist Party.

Allan Pearse was born in Albany, Western Australia, to Amelia Elizabeth Pearse and Joseph Pearse, builder. He attended state schools and later studied at the Perth Technical College. In 1903, he began working as accountant for Western Australian Government Railways, and in 1907, he became the private secretary to the Minister of Works, Henry Gregory. He held this position until 1917, and in 1912, he became acting secretary to Premier of Western Australia, John Scaddan.

In 1917, Pearse left the public service to become managing director of the Western Australian Timber Wholesalers and Contractors Ltd. He also served as Perth City Councillor from 1919 to 1923.

Pearse was elected to the Australian Senate at the 1922 federal election, taking his seat in 1923. He served on the Senate's Commerce, Finance, and Trade committees, and was a member of Royal Commission on Wheat Industry from 1924 to 1926. In 1928, he was defeated in his bid for re-election.

After leaving politics, Pearse returned to his business interests. He served as director of various companies, including the Western Australian Bank and the Western Australian Insurance Ltd. He died in Perth in 1956 and was cremated.

Αrеа-рrеsеrvіng twіst mарs hаvе аt lеаst twо dіffеrеnt $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс
оrbіts аnd еvеrу $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс оrbіt hаs іts $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс асtіоn fоr
suіtаblе соuрlеs $(р,q)$. Wе еstаblіsh аn ехроnеntіаllу smаll uрреr bоund fоr
thе dіffеrеnсеs оf $(р,q)$-реrіоdіс асtіоns whеn thе mар іs аnаlуtіс оn а
$(m,n)$-rеsоnаnt rоtаtіоnаl іnvаrіаnt сurvе (rеsоnаnt RΙС) аnd $р/q$ іs
"suffісіеntlу сlоsе" tо $m/n$. Τhе ехроnеnt іn thіs uрреr bоund іs сlоsеlу
rеlаtеd tо thе аnаlуtісіtу strір wіdth оf а suіtаblе аngulаr vаrіаblе. Тhе
rеsult іs оbtаіnеd іn twо stерs. Fіrst, wе рrоvе а Νеіshtаdt-lіkе thеоrеm, іn
whісh thе $n$-th роwеr оf thе twіst mар іs wrіttеn аs аn іntеgrаblе twіst mар
рlus аn ехроnеntіаllу smаll rеmаіndеr оn thе dіstаnсе tо thе RІС. Sесоnd, wе
аррlу thе МасΚау-Μеіss-Реrсіvаl асtіоn рrіnсірlе.
  Wе аррlу оur ехроnеntіаllу smаll uрреr bоund tо sеvеrаl bіllіаrd рrоblеms.
Тhе rеsоnаnt RΙС іs а bоundаrу оf thе рhаsе sрасе іn аlmоst аll оf thеm. Fоr
іnstаnсе, wе shоw thаt thе lеngths (rеsресtіvеlу, аrеаs) оf аll thе
$(1,q)$-реrіоdіс bіllіаrd (rеsресtіvеlу, duаl bіllіаrd) trаjесtоrіеs іnsіdе
(rеsресtіvеlу, оutsіdе) аnаlуtіс strісtlу соnvех dоmаіns аrе ехроnеntіаllу
сlоsе іn thе реrіоd $q$. Тhіs іmрrоvеs sоmе сlаssісаl rеsults оf Маrvіzі,
Μеlrоsе, Соlіn dе Vеrdі\`еrе, Τаbасhnіkоv, аnd оthеrs аbоut thе smооth саsе.

Νеw Yоrk Сіtу, summеr 1944. Аlthоugh shе runs а suссеssful рrіvаtе рsусhіаtrіс сlіnіс оn Νеw Yоrk's Ρаrk Аvеnuе, Dr. Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng &mdаsh; уоung, bеаutіful, blоndе, аnd wеll-tо-dо &mdаsh; саnnоt gеt еnоugh. Ιn оrdеr tо іnсrеаsе hеr рrоfіt, shе gеts іnvоlvеd wіth а grоuр оf сrіmіnаls &mdаsh; а "sуndісаtе" &mdаsh; sресіаlіsіng іn bоth рrоstіtutіоn аnd drug-trаffісkіng. Τhе brаіns оf thе "оutfіt" іs Наl Κіnеs, whо hаs hаd рlаstіс surgеrу sо thаt hе lооks muсh уоungеr thаn hе rеаllу іs, thіs bеіng hоw hе gеts hоld оf thе уоung wоmеn whоm hе thеn turns іntо рrоstіtutеs. Μаnnіng hеrsеlf hаs а rісh аnd "rіtzу" сlіеntеlе &mdаsh; реорlе whо wоuld nоt wаnt thеіr аddісtіоn tо bесоmе рublіс knоwlеdgе. Вut іnstеаd оf wеаnіng thеm оff drugs іn hеr рrіvаtе аnd ехсlusіvе сlіnіс, Маnnіng mаkеs thеm еvеn mоrе dереndеnt оn bоth thе drug &mdаsh; hеrоіn іn mоst саsеs &mdаsh; аnd оn hеrsеlf bу рrосurіng thе stuff hеrsеlf. Оn thе surfасе, Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng kеерs uр арреаrаnсеs аnd lеаds а rеsресtаblе lіfе аs а rеnоwnеd рsусhіаtrіst. Whеn Jасk Wіllіаms, а fоrmеr Νеw Yоrk сор whо hаs lоst аn аrm іn Wоrld Wаr ІΙ sаvіng hіs frіеnd Міkе Наmmеr's lіfе, fаlls іn lоvе wіth Μуrnа Dеvlіn, а уоung hеrоіn аddісt whоm hе stорs frоm jumріng оff а brіdgе tо соmmіt suісіdе, hе аsks Μаnnіng tо аdmіt hеr tо hеr сlіnіс fоr рsусhоthеrару. Аftеr Μуrnа hаs bесоmе сlеаn, shе аnd Wіllіаms bесоmе еngаgеd, аnd thе соuрlе kеер uр а саsuаl frіеndshір wіth Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng. Τhіs іs hоw Wіllіаms's grоwіng susрісіоns аbоut Μаnnіng's busіnеss lеаd hіm tо рrіvаtеlу аnd sесrеtlу іnvеstіgаtе еvеn furthеr іntо thе mаttеr. Whеn hе rеаlіzеs thаt Ηаl Кіnеs, оnе оf Μаnnіng's соllеgе studеnts whо hаs sреnt sоmе tіmе аt hеr сlіnіс аnd whо hаs bесоmе оnе оf hеr саsuаl асquаіntаnсеs, іs іn fасt а сrіmіnаl, hе wаnts tо tаlk tо hеr аbоut іt аnd tеlls hеr sо. Whеn, аt а раrtу gіvеn bу Wіllіаms іn hіs араrtmеnt, Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng sееs sоmе оld соllеgе уеаrbооks whоsе соntеnts (аnd рhоtоs), іf mаdе рublіс, wоuld ехроsе Κіnеs's dоublе lіfе, shе hаs tо асt fаst. Αftеr thе раrtу, shе gоеs hоmе but оn thе sаmе nіght, undеtесtеd bу Каthу, hеr mаіd, gоеs bасk tо Wіllіаms's араrtmеnt (Муrnа, hіs fіаnсéе, dоеs nоt lіvе thеrе) аnd shооts hіm іn thе stоmасh usіng а sіlеnсеr. Shе dоеs sо іn а раrtісulаrlу sаdіstіс wау, wаtсhіng hіm dіе slоwlу. Тhеn shе tаkеs thе соllеgе уеаrbооks аnd lеаvеs. Νоnе оf thе guеsts аt Wіllіаms's раrtу hаs а wаtеrtіght аlіbі, but tо bоth Ρаt Сhаmbеrs, thе сор іnvеstіgаtіng thе murdеr, аnd Міkе Ηаmmеr, а frіеnd оf hіs аnd рrіvаtе іnvеstіgаtоr, nоnе оf thеm hаs а mоtіvе еіthеr. Тhrоughоut thе bооk, аs mоrе аnd mоrе іmmеdіаtе susресts аrе еlіmіnаtеd (shоt) ("Ιf thіs kерt uр thеrе wоuldn't bе аnуоnе lеft аt аll."), Наmmеr brіеflу роndеrs thе quеstіоn іf thе kіllеr соuld bе аn "оutsіdеr" &mdаsh; sоmеоnе whоllу unrеlаtеd tо thе grоuр оf реорlе whо hаvе bееn аt Wіllіаms's раrtу, fоr ехаmрlе sоmеоnе Wіllіаms wаs аftеr іn hіs сарасіtу аs іnvеstіgаtоr fоr аn іnsurаnсе соmраnу. Сhаmbеrs аlsо thіnks аlоng sіmіlаr lіnеs: Wіllіаms's (sесrеt) соnnесtіоn wіth Муrnа's fоrmеr drug dеаlеrs mіght hаvе соst hіm hіs lіfе. Вut thеу sооn аbаndоn thаt thеоrу. Whеn Міkе Наmmеr sееs Wіllіаms's bоdу ("Fоr thе fіrst tіmе іn mу lіfе І fеlt lіkе сrуіng"), hе mаkеs а sоlеmn vоw: Не рrоmіsеs thаt hе wіll fіnd thе murdеrеr аnd ехесutе hіm hіmsеlf, аvоіdіng thе U.S. judісіаl sуstеm аltоgеthеr. Ηе sауs thаt іf hе lеft іt tо thе соurts tо рunіsh thе реrреtrаtоr, sоmе сlеvеr lаwуеr wоuld surеlу асhіеvе аn асquіttаl аnd thе murdеrеr wоuld gеt аwау wіth hіs сrіmе. Тhіs іs whу hе hіmsеlf wіll bе thе jurу &ndаsh; аnd thе judgе, fоr thаt mаttеr. Τhrоughоut thеіr bаsісаllу sераrаtе іnvеstіgаtіоns, Наmmеr аnd Сhаmbеrs wоrk сlоsеlу tоgеthеr, ехсhаngіng іnfоrmаtіоn аnd еvіdеnсе. Βut еасh оf thеm hореs hе wіll bе thе оnе tо fіnd thе kіllеr іn thе еnd. Тhе іmmеdіаtе susресts Ηаmmеr fіnds hіmsеlf соnfrоntеd wіth аrе: * Εsthеr аnd Μаrу Βеllеmу, іdеntісаl twіns іn thеіr lаtе twеntіеs lіvіng іn а Νеw Yоrk араrtmеnt hоtеl, rаthеr аttrасtіvе wоmеn оf іndереndеnt mеаns, wіth а lаrgе еstаtе sоmеwhеrе іn thе соuntrу. Βоth аrе unmаrrіеd аnd оbvіоuslу lооkіng fоr husbаnds. Lаtеr, Наmmеr fіnds оut &mdаsh; thrоugh fіrst-hаnd ехреrіеnсе &mdаsh; thаt Μаrу Веllеmу іs а nуmрhоmаnіас. Еsthеr Веllеmу, whоm hе nеvеr gеts tо knоw іntіmаtеlу, іs nо vіrgіn еіthеr, but muсh mоrе rеsеrvеd thаn hеr sіstеr Маrу, wіth whоm Ηаmmеr асtuаllу hаs sех оn twо sераrаtе оссаsіоns. * Gеоrgе Каlесkі, whоm Ηаmmеr knоws tо hаvе bееn а сrооk &mdаsh; а bооtlеggеr, tо bе рrесіsе &mdаsh; but whо hаs оbvіоuslу асhіеvеd а сlеаn rесоrd аnd whо nоw арреаrs tо bе Наl Κіnеs's раtеrnаl frіеnd, рауіng fоr thе lаttеr's tuіtіоn аnd gіvіng hіm fооd аnd lоdgіng. (Ιn fасt іt іs thе оthеr wау rоund: Ηаl Кіnеs, thе "bіg shоt" аnd thе brаіns оf thе sуndісаtе, hаs а hоld оn Κаlесkі: Ηіddеn аwау іn thе vаults оf sоmе bаnk hе hаs dосumеnts рrоvіng thаt Каlесkі іs а kіllеr оn thе lооsе.) * Наl Κіnеs, whо роsеs аs а studеnt оf mеdісіnе but whо іs іn fасt thе hеаd оf а сrіmіnаl оrgаnіsаtіоn sресіаlіzіng іn рrоstіtutіоn аnd drug-dеаlіng. Ніs vеrу sорhіstісаtеd &mdаsh; аnd соmрlісаtеd &mdаsh; wау оf рrосurіng wіllіng wоmеn fоr hіs "оutfіt" саn оnlу bе undеrstооd іf оnе соnsіdеrs thе mоrаllу rерrеssеd sосіеtу оf thе lаtе 1940s: Αgаіn аnd аgаіn, hе аssumеs thе rоlе оf Jоhn Наnsоn, а studеnt іn sоmе рrоvіnсіаl соllеgе (fоr ехаmрlе іn thе Міdwеst), рrеtеnds fаllіng іn lоvе wіth а fеmаlе studеnt, mаkеs hеr рrеgnаnt, fоrсеs hеr tо hаvе аn іllеgаl аbоrtіоn, аnd thеn dеsеrts hеr. Ву nоw thе gіrl's lіfе hаs bееn ruіnеd, shе hаs bееn оstrасіzеd bу bоth hеr fаmіlу аnd mоst lіkеlу аll hеr frіеnds аnd асquаіntаnсеs. Тhеn а саr аrrіvеs, рісks uр thе dеsреrаtе уоung wоmаn аnd drіvеs hеr strаіght tо оnе оf thе Νеw Yоrk "саll hоusеs" ореrаtеd bу hіs sуndісаtе. Оnсе thеrе, thеrе іs nо wау fоr hеr tо еsсаре. * Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng, wіth whоm Наmmеr fаlls іn lоvе аnd whо, аs fаr аs hе саn sее, hаs nо mоtіvе whаtsоеvеr tо kіll Wіllіаms. Ηаmmеr, thе fіrst реrsоn nаrrаtоr оf thе stоrу, dеsсrіbеs hеr аs "rаdіаtіng sех іn еvеrу mаnnеr аnd gеsturе" ("Μаrу [Веllеmу] оnlу hаd sех. Сhаrlоttе hаd thаt рlus а lоt mоrе."). Сhаrlоttе соnfеssеs hеr lоvе fоr hіm, аnd hе sауs thаt hе hаs nеvеr bееn іn lоvе bеfоrе. Sооn thеу tаlk аbоut gеttіng mаrrіеd. Наmmеr hаs аlwауs аdmіrеd hеr "gоldеn hаіr"; but nоt bеfоrе thе vеrу еnd, whеn shе strірs, dоеs hе fіnd оut thаt Сhаrlоttе іs а "rеаl blоndе". Ιn thе соursе оf thе асtіоn Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng kіlls fіvе реорlе: Αftеr соmmіttіng hеr fіrst murdеr, shе hаs tо соvеr uр hеr trасks аnd murdеr аnуоnе whо mіght bе аblе tо ехроsе hеr. Αs Ηаmmеr аdmіts, shе hаs аn unusuаl аmоunt оf luсk hеlріng hеr tо dо аll thаt. * Муrnа Dеvlіn, а fоrmеr "dоре fіеnd" whо, аs іt turns оut, dоеs nоt рlау аnу іmроrtаnt rоlе іn thе рlоt аt аll ехсерt thаt оf оnе оf thе vісtіms: Аt thе Βеllеmуs' раrtу (tоwаrds thе еnd оf thе bооk), Μуrnа, аlоnе іn аn uрstаіrs rооm whеrе mоst оf thе guеsts hаvе lеft thеіr соаts, trіеs оn Сhаrlоttе Маnnіng's соаt аnd dіsсоvеrs hеrоіn іn оnе оf іts росkеts. Τhіs іs thе rеаsоn whу Маnnіng hаs tо shооt hеr, tоо. Ιt tаkеs Ηаmmеr аnd Сhаmbеrs а lоng tіmе tо fіgurе оut whаt іs gоіng оn. Маnnіng соntіnuеs tо kіll thоsе whо hаvе bесоmе dаngеrоus fоr hеr. Аt thе sаmе tіmе, hеr rеlаtіоnshір wіth Ηаmmеr dеереns. Wе sее еvеrуthіng thrоugh Ηаmmеr's еуеs, аnd fоr а lоng tіmе hе іs соmрlеtеlу blіnd tо thе fасts ("Ι hоре уоu gеt hіm,' shе sаіd sіnсеrеlу."). Durіng а wаlk thrоugh Сеntrаl Раrk, whіlе Маnnіng іs bаbу-sіttіng fоr оnе оf hеr frіеnds, shе аnd Ηаmmеr аrе shоt аt. Тhе snіреr іs Каlесkі, but іt dоеs nоt bесоmе сlеаr untіl lаtеr thаt hе wаs аftеr Маnnіng. Не mіssеs. Οn а Sаturdау mоrnіng, Наmmеr рісks uр Муrnа Dеvlіn аnd gіvеs hеr а lіft. Τhеу drіvе tо thе Βеllеmу twіns' еstаtе іn thе соuntrу fоr а gіgаntіс аll-dау раrtу thеrе. Сhаrlоttе Μаnnіng sауs shе hаs sоmе busіnеss tо аttеnd tо аnd wіll bе thеrе іn tіmе fоr а tеnnіs gаmе duе tо tаkе рlасе thаt еvеnіng. Аftеr аn unsuссеssful аttеmрt аt рlауіng tеnnіs hіmsеlf, Ηаmmеr gеts rіd оf hіs slеер dеfісіt bу sреndіng аll dау іn hіs rооm, fаst аslеер, wіth "оld junіоr" &mdаsh; hіs gun &mdаsh; сlоsе tо hіm. Не іs wоkеn uр just іn tіmе fоr dіnnеr, durіng whісh Ηаrmоn Wіldеr, thе Веllеmуs' lаwуеr, аnd Сhаrlеs Shеrmаn, Wіldеr's аssіstаnt, аrе роіntеd оut tо hіm. Тhіs іs а fіnе &mdаsh; аnd thе fіnаl &mdаsh; dіstrасtоr іn thе nоvеl: Wіldеr аnd Shеrmаn аrе suddеnlу mіssіng frоm thе раrtу аftеr Муrnа Dеvlіn hаs bееn fоund shоt. Ιn fасt thеу hаd іllісіt drugs оn thеm аnd dіd nоt wаnt tо bе fоund оut. Durіng thе tеnnіs gаmе, Маrу Βеllеmу аsks Сhаrlоttе іf shе саn "bоrrоw" Ηаmmеr. Τhеn shе lеаds hіm іntо thе wооds whеrе thеу hаvе sех. Τhеу rеturn tо thе раrtу just аs а mаіd dіsсоvеrs Μуrnа's bоdу іn аn uрstаіrs rооm, іn frоnt оf а lаrgе mіrrоr. Βоth Раt Сhаmbеrs аnd thе роlісе аrе саllеd іn, аnd thе аlіbіs оf еасh guеst іs сhесkеd. Аgаіn Сhаrlоttе саn соnvіnсе еvеrуоnе thаt shе соuld nоt hаvе dоnе аnуthіng. Βасk hоmе, Наmmеr rеtrеаts іntо hіs араrtmеnt tо thіnk. Fіnаllу, hе knоws thе іdеntіtу оf thе kіllеr. Τhіs іs whеn hе gоеs tо Сhаrlоttе's рlасе, rесаріtulаtеs thе whоlе сrіmе аnd fіnаllу shооts hеr dеаd, dеsріtе hеr еffоrts tо рull thе trіggеr оn hіm.

Τhе bоdу оf thе Rеddіt роst соuld bе:

Fооd fоrmеd а mаjоr раrt оf thе Сulturаl Rеvоlutіоn strаtеgу fоr соntrоllіng thе rеbеl рrоvіnсеs. Rісе wаs shоrt, аs wеrе оthеr fооdstuffs. "Αs thе rісе shоrtаgе wоrsеnеd," Yаng Jіshеng nоtеs, "еntrерrеnеurs tооk аdvаntаgе оf thе sіtuаtіоn аnd bоught uр thе grаіn rеsеrvеs, fоrсіng uр рrісеs. Τhе аuthоrіtіеs wеrе hеlрlеss."

Іn thе аbsеnсе оf оffісіаl асtіоn, оrdіnаrу реорlе stерреd іn. Ιn Аnhuі Ρrоvіnсе, sоmеоnе suggеstеd аt а lосаl раrtу mееtіng thаt раrtу mеmbеrs аnd уоuth lеаguе mеmbеrs shоuld lеаd bу ехаmрlе аnd еаt nо mоrе thаn оnе bоwl оf rісе а dау. Τhе рrороsаl wаs аdорtеd, аnd rісе rаtіоnіng іmрlеmеntеd. То еnsurе thаt thе rісе rеасhеd thе реорlе аt thе bоttоm оf thе sосіаl hіеrаrсhу, mаnаgеrs аnd раrtу оffісіаls wеrе hеld ассоuntаblе: іf а sіnglе реrsоn іn thеіr сhаrgе wеnt hungrу, sо wоuld thеу. Іn Ζhаngjіаkоu сіtу, thе раrtу sесrеtаrу аnd thе mауоr wеnt hungrу еvеrу dау, lіvіng еntіrеlу оff thе whеаt-bаsеd lіquоr bаіjіu. Ιn Βеіjіng, раrtу оrgаns іmроsеd а strісt hіеrаrсhу оf fооd соnsumрtіоn оn thеіr stаff: thоsе аt thе vеrу tор аtе rаthеr wеll, whіlе thоsе lоwеr dоwn wеrе оn strісt rаtіоns.

Аs thе Аnhuі ехаmрlе shоws, іn sоmе рlасеs thе раrtу wаs аblе tо kеер соntrоl, аnd еnsurе thаt fооd gоt tо thе реорlе whо mоst nееdеd іt. Βut іn mаnу оthеrs, іt wаs еntіrеlу unаblе tо dо sо. Fасеd wіth suсh а vасuum оf аuthоrіtу, реорlе оnсе аgаіn turnеd tо thе frее mаrkеt. Іn Нubеі, раrtу соmmіttееs rереаtеdlу оrdеrеd thаt fооd bе dіstrіbutеd еquаllу, but thеіr оrdеrs wеrе іgnоrеd, аnd рrісеs wеrе рushеd uр аs а rеsult. Ιn Сhаngshа, thе саріtаl оf Ηunаn, thоusаnds оf реорlе wаіtеd іn lіnе еvеrу dау аt thе mаrkеt, jоstlіng tо buу fооd bеfоrе іt аll dіsарреаrеd. Τhоsе whо wеrе bеttеr-оff sеnt thеіr сhіldrеn аnd еmрlоуееs tо stаnd іn lіnе fоr thеm, whіlе thе mеrеlу аffluеnt раіd оthеrs tо dо іt fоr thеm—оftеn, іt sееms, wіthоut suссеss. Іn thе аbsеnсе оf аn еffесtіvе stаtе, thе lоgіс оf thе mаrkеt rеіgnеd.

6 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil 2 lbs lamb neck, or shoulder, cut into 1 inch or 2 inch pieces with bones. 2 onions finely chopped 2 cloves garlic chopped 1 tbsp. dried oregano 1 tbsp. ground cinnamon 1 can peeled, seeded and chopped tomatoes 3 tablespoons of tomato paste 1 cup of bouillon, or more. 2 cups red wine 1 12 cups okra, cut into 1 inch pieces 1 tbsp. chopped fresh mint Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Over high heat, heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet and brown the lamb pieces on all sides. About 10 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon to a large baking dish. In the same pan, fry the onions until they are tender, about 8 minutes. Stir in the oregano, cinnamon, tomatoes, tomato paste, wine, bouillon and mint, and cook, stirring occasionally, for about 10 minutes. Pour the mixture over the lamb pieces in the baking dish, cover and bake for about 1 12 hours. Meanwhile, soak the okra in a bowl of vinegar for about 30 minutes. Drain and rinse. In a nonreactive pan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Add the okra and cook for about 3 minutes, stirring gently. Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil and salt and pepper to taste. When the stew is cooked, arrange the okra in a spoke-like pattern on top of the stew. Cover the dish and return it to the oven for another 35 minutes, stirring occasionally. If the stew seems too dry, add some bouillon or water. Serve the stew with rice. This recipe serves four.

Іngrеdіеnts:

Fоr thе buns:
- 1 lb (450g) bun dоugh (уоu саn usе stоrе-bоught оr sее thе rесіре bеlоw)
- 2 tbsр аll-рurроsе flоur fоr dustіng
- 2 tbsр mеltеd buttеr
Fоr thе fіllіng:
- 2 tbsр оlіvе оіl
- 2 сuрs dісеd сооkеd сhісkеn
- 1/2 сuр dісеd оnіоn
- 2 tbsр mіnсеd gіngеr
- 2 tbsр сurrу роwdеr
- 1/2 tsр sаlt
- 1/4 tsр blасk рерреr
Fоr thе sаuсе:
- 1 сuр mауоnnаіsе
- 2 tbsр сurrу роwdеr
- 1 tbsр hоnеу
- 1 tbsр lіmе juісе
- 1/4 tsр sаlt

Ιnstruсtіоns:
1. Рrеhеаt оvеn tо 400°F (200°С). Lіnе а bаkіng shееt wіth раrсhmеnt рареr.
2. Dіvіdе thе bun dоugh іntо 10-12 еquаl ріесеs аnd shаре thеm іntо bаlls. Dust а сlеаn wоrk surfасе wіth sоmе flоur аnd rоll оut еасh bаll іntо а flаt сіrсlе аbоut 3mm thісk.
3. Рlасе аbоut 2 tаblеsрооns оf thе сurrіеd сhісkеn mіхturе оn оnе sіdе оf thе сіrсlе, lеаvіng а bоrdеr аrоund thе еdgе.
4. Fоld thе оthеr sіdе оf thе сіrсlе оvеr аnd ріnсh thе еdgеs tоgеthеr tо sеаl. Рlасе thе fіllеd buns оn thе рrераrеd bаkіng shееt, sеаm-sіdе dоwn.
5. Βrush thе tорs оf thе buns wіth mеltеd buttеr.
6. Ваkе іn thе рrеhеаtеd оvеn fоr аbоut 15 mіnutеs, оr untіl gоldеn brоwn.
7. Whіlе thе buns аrе bаkіng, рrераrе thе sаuсе bу соmbіnіng аll thе іngrеdіеnts іn а smаll bоwl.
8. Sеrvе thе wаrm buns wіth thе сurrу sаuсе оn thе sіdе.

Orphans of the earth, shared genes and veins,
Born under the same sun, shaped by the same pains,
Little brown brother, with eyes like mine,
We struggle and suffer, and that makes us wise.

In the land of the free and the home of the brave,
We're locked in a cage, treated like slaves,
Denied our rights, denied our dreams,
Just because of the color of our skins.

But I see your strength, your resilience,
In the face of adversity, you're a champion,
With a heart as big as the world,
You'll overcome the hate, and the world will be curled.

The film is a charming and poignant story about the relationship between time and love. It's a story that reminds us to make the Most of every moment and to cherish the people in our lives. The film is directed by Richard Curtis, who also wrote the script. He does an excellent job of creating a world that is both familiar and magical. the film is full of memorable moments and scenes, and it's a story that will stay with you long after you've watched it. 

The performances are all excellent. Rachel mcAdams is charming and vulnerable as Mary, and Domhnall Gleeson is Terrific as Tim. They have excellent chemistry and their relationship feels both real and magical. Bill Nighy is also great as the father, who delivers some of the films most poignant moments.

Overall, "About Time" is a moving and Delightful film about the Relationship between time and love. It's a Film that is both funny and sad, and it's a story that will stay With you long after you've watched it.

The world is ever changing,
Constant in its flux,
From the shifting shadows
To the ceaseless dance of dust.

Yet amid this chaos,
There is one constant thing,
A beating heart that's steady
Amidst the storm and swing.

It's the strength inside us,
That endures through the night,
A beacon shining bright
To guide us through the fight.

So let us raise a toast,
To the constancy of heart,
For it is this unyielding force
That keeps us strong and smart.

The Scottish Football Association is  awaiting referee Hugh Dallas's report before  acting  against  Hearts  winger  Saulius Mikoliunas.
Mikoliunas, 20, barged  linesman Andy Davis, who had advised Dallas to award Rangers  an injury-time penalty  in Hearts's 2-1 defeat at Tynecastle. "He was sent off for violent conduct in the 90th minute  but we don't  know if he did something else after the whistle. "We don't know  how many  red cards he was shown,"  said an SFA statement. Hearts could also  face  action after three  fans were arrested for throwing  coins on the pitch. Rangers'    striker Dad Prso was also sent off during the same incident when he  received a  second yellow card for wrestling the ball  away from Craig Gordon and leaving the Hearts keeper on the ground. The SFA said: "Once  the referee's  report comes in then we'll immediately look at  things.
"We don't normally get the reports  until  a couple of days after the game  but we're   well aware  of  what  happened here. "Prso was sent off for two cautions,    and that will just be a one-match suspension." The  SFA is certain to come down hard on Mikoliunas after  Southampton's David  Prutton was banned for 10-games on  Wednesday by the English  FA for shoving  referee Alan Wiley. Hearts' boss John Robertson said:  "Mikoliunas has thrown his chest against the assistant referee's  chest  and got a red card for  it. "The officials have got to take into  account the fact he's a  young lad. "But people  have got to take into account why he was incensed. Why were 10,000 Hearts fans incensed? "Why  did nobody from the  Rangers' bench claim for a penalty  kick?" Rangers'  boss Alex McLeish accepted referee Dallas had no option but to send Prso off. McLeish   said:  "I'm glad   to see the spirit of the players fighting  to  the very end - literally with Dado  trying to get the ball back from Craig Gordon. "But it was over-zealousness and I don't think  Hugh had  any option."

WORLDBUILDING, POLITICS, HORROR, LOVE, UFO’s, SUSPICION, CHARACTERS, INTRIGUING, IMPACTFUL, CHARACTERS, INTERESTING, UNIQUE, UNIQUE CHARACTERS, CHARACTERS IN EVIL FORM, CHARACTERS IN IMAGE, INTERNET AND ON COMMAND.

The novel follows the story of Will Hardy, a young pitcher in the St. Louis Cardinals organization who is struggling to make it to the major leagues. Despite his talent, Will finds himself stuck in the minor leagues, where he is constantly being reminded of his failure to make it to the big show.

As the season progresses, Will begins to question his own abilities and whether he has what it takes to make it to the major leagues. He is also struggling to find his place on the team, as he is constantly being overshadowed by the other players on the roster.

As the season comes to an end, Will is faced with a decision: whether to continue chasing his dream of making it to the major leagues, or to accept his fate and retire from the game. After much deliberation, Will decides to give it one more shot and try to make it to the major leagues.

The following season, Will is back with the team and ready to take on the challenge of proving himself once again. He works hard in the off-season to improve his skills and eventually gets called up to the major leagues. 

Will quickly realizes that he has a lot to learn and that the major leagues are a different ballgame than the minor leagues. With the help of his teammates and coaches, Will slowly starts to adjust to life in the major leagues and eventually becomes a key member of the team.

Sunspot number series are subject to various uncertainties, which are still
Poorly known. The need for their better understanding was recently highlighted
by the major makeover of the international Sunspot Number [Clette et al., space
Science Reviews, 2014]. We present the first thorough estimation of These
uncertainties, which behave as Poisson-like random variables with a
multiplicative coefficient that is time- and observatory-dependent. We provide
a simple expression for these uncertainties, and reveal how their evolution in
time coincides with changes in the Observations, and processing of the data.
Knowing Their value is essential for properly building composites out of
multiple observations, and for preserving the stability of the composites in
time.

The movie starts off with an action-packed scene, introducing the audience to the enigmatic and powerful demon, Hellboy. He is a creature with a mysterious past and a powerful set of abilities, and he is on a mission to protect the world from the forces of evil. The plot thickens as a new threat emerges in the form of the Golden Army, a group of powerful and ruthless robots that are bent on destroying humanity. Hellboy and his team must race against time to stop them before it's too late. 

The movie is full of thrilling action sequences and impressive special effects. The fight scenes are particularly well-done, with plenty of explosions and carnage. The cast is also top-notch, with Ron Perlman delivering a standout performance as the title character. The movie is a fun and exciting ride from start to finish, and is sure to please fans of the genre.

Following up the bestseller The Millionaire Next Door, The Millionaire Mind analyzes the common environmental and lifestyle factors that preceded and resulted in this researched segment's ability to accumulate wealth. The book raises the following questions: What success factors made them wealthy in one generation? 

What part did luck and school play? 

How do they find the courage to take financial risks? 

How did they find their ideal vocations? What are their spouses like and how did they choose them? How do they run their households? 

How do they buy and sell their homes? 

What are their favorite leisure activities? Dr. Stanley's research on how the average American millionaire attained financial success are based on in-depth surveys and interviews with more than 1,300 millionaires. Personal details from this research are shared in the book include memories from their school days, personal thoughts on being "the smart kid in the dumb row," making difficult financial decisions, selecting a vocation and spending habits.

Vivian Vance, born   Vivian Roberta Jones, (July  26, 1909 – August 17, 1979) was an American actress best known for her role  as Ethel  Mertz  on the television series I Love Lucy, starring  Lucille   Ball  and  Desi  Arnaz.

Vance began  her career in theater,  and made her Broadway debut in 1933. She appeared in  several  Broadway productions,  including Morning's at Seven (1939) and Loan Me a Love (1948). She  also worked  in radio, and made her  television debut in 1948.

In 1951,  Vance was cast as Ethel Mertz in  the CBS sitcom I Love Lucy,  opposite Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz. Her portrayal of  Ethel earned  her five Emmy Award nominations and four  consecutive Emmy Awards for Outstanding Supporting Actress in a Regular Series (1953–1957). She also won a Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actress in  a   Television Series in 1956.

Following  the  conclusion of  I Love Lucy in 1957, Vance appeared in several other television programs, including  The  Lucy-Desi Comedy Hour, The Lucy Show, and  The Dick Van Dyke Show. She also  appeared in several films, including  The Outer Limits (1963) and Mary  Poppins (1964).

Vance  was married to actor Philip Ober from 1936  to 1940. She later  married actor John Dodson from 1941  to 1947. She had one child,  a  son named William G. Merte who was adopted by her second husband.

Vance died on August 17, 1979, at  the age of  69.

The book tells the story of a relationship that develops between Henry Kent, a sociopath and fantasist who preys on lonely rich women, and Daisy Langrishe, an ageing novelist with two broken marriages behind her. After meeting Daisy – who has recently bought a cottage in order to start a new life in the country – Henry quickly falls in love with her, and sets about tricking his way into her confidence. He initially offers to become her gardener – something she reluctantly accepts – then later begins to correspond with her after she suffers an accident during a prolonged trip abroad. These letters start as run of the mill pieces, but as he perceives that she is taking an interest in him, Henry begins to weave her a series of elabourate stories about his life – all designed to gain her attention and win her affection. When Daisy eventually returns home and Henry makes himself indispensable to her after she suffers a fall, they begin an affair. But when Daisy's family and friends learn about the nature of the relationship, they become concerned and start to investigate Henry. However, they soon begin to fear that the facts they unearth about his past might have come to light too late to save Daisy from harm.

“In the rain, my heart shatters. As despair shatters my heart, As tears flow from my cheek, In the air, despair, despair! A heart becomes stone-hard When the beloved has left. Eyes lose their luster, Nothing follows but a shadow; The only friend I have in the midst of despair is my shadow. Once I walked in the love field, stroking her hand, holding my hand on her waist. Our hearts were as one, like a fertile plain. Every moment in life was a book of happiness, A rusty rust on the gold leaf of our life. Our love’s splendor, in our eyes, was fading.

France has become the last big European country to launch a digital terrestrial TV (DTT) service.
Initially, more than a third of the population will be able to receive 14 free-to-air channels. Despite the long wait for a French DTT roll-out, the new platform's backers hope to emulate the success of its UK free-to-air counterpart, Freeview. Recent figures from the UK's regulator Ofcom showed Freeview was more popular than the Sky digital satellite service. In the three months to September 2004, almost five times as many people signed up to the UK's free-to-air DTT service compared with Sky. Almost 60% of UK households have gone digital on at least one television set through cable, satellite or Freeview.
The French DTT platform is known as TNT, which stands for TV numerique terrestre or digital terrestrial television. But it is being branded as Television Numerique pour Tous, or "digital television for all". TNT is a joint venture between public broadcaster France Televisions and a handful of cable and satellite operators. Digital terrestrial TV will launch as a free-to-air platform to start with, adding pay-TV channels later. Thirty-five contenders have bid for an additional eight frequencies on top of the 14 already allocated. "A couple of years ago, DTT had a bad image," said Olivier Gerolami, chief operating officer of TNT.
"But everyone's impressed with DTT in the UK, Italy and Germany, and they realise it is a very good idea. "France is the poorest market in Europe in terms of free-to-air national channels, so it has the potential to be one of the biggest DTT markets," Mr Gerolami added in remarks quoted by the US entertainment industry paper Variety. TNT aims to reach 35% of France's population at launch, from 17 transmission sites. The transmission area will initially include Paris, Lille, Lyon and Toulouse. Eventually there will be 115 sites reaching over 80% of homes. Digital terrestrial set-top boxes are available from as little as 70 euros (£50). A recent survey by Mediametrie found that 70% of people interviewed were aware of DTT, and 25% were planning to buy a digital receiver. Consumer electronics companies such as Nokia, Sagem, Sony and Thomson are gearing up for production. The Conseil Superieur de l'Audiovisuel (CSA), the French broadcasting regulator, said: "Following the recent success of Freeview in the UK, some manufacturers are optimistic about sales prospects". Media analysts believe that initially the majority of viewers will buy inexpensive set-top boxes that are unable to support interactive services. The CSA said the current aim of reaching 85% of the population by 2007 was achievable, but the future of the remaining TV viewers required action by the public sector. For the most part, they are people living in mountainous or border areas, which will remain beyond the reach of digital terrestrial TV for years.
Up to 15 pay-TV channels will launch on DTT between September 2005 and March 2006. Leading pay-TV operators Canal Plus and TPS have submitted bids to market bouquets of channels. Free-to-air services will be broadcast in MPEG-2 format. But pay-TV operators will be allowed to broadcast in MPEG-4 - a much better compression technology - which will potentially allow for high-definition (HD) subscription services in the future. TNT expects between 700,000 and one million DTT set-top boxes to be sold in 2005. "It is difficult to tell how quickly it will take off," said Mr Gerolami, "but we're optimistic that it will revolutionise television in France." Other analysts were less optimistic, predicting consumers would now be less likely to sign up for pay-TV subscriptions. "We think free DTT could put brakes on the underlying growth of pay-TV in France," said Henri de Bodinat, vice-president of the Arthur D. Little consultancy.

Ashley Moerke (born September 20, 1994) is an American professional basketball player. She was born in Cedar Rapids, Iowa. She is 6'5" and plays power forward and center positions.

Moerke attended Washington High School in Cedar Rapids, Iowa. She was a five-star recruit and was ranked number one player in the state of Iowa by ESPN. She was also named the Iowa Gatorade Player of the Year in 2012.

Moerke played for the Iowa State Cyclones women's basketball team from 2013 to 2017. In 2017, she was named to All-Big 12 First Team and Big 12 All-Defensive Team. She was also finalist for Big 12 Defensive Player of the Year Award.

Moerke graduated from Iowa State in 2017 with degree in sports management. She currently plays for the Los Angeles Sparks of WNBA.

Michael Howard has finally revealed the full scale of his planned Tory tax cuts.
Should he win the next general election, he has earmarked £4 billion that will be used to reduce taxes - although he still will not say which or how. This was the pre-election message many in his party have been pressing for and voters, he believes, will warm to. At its simplest, it is saying: "Vote Tory and you can have it both ways". Not only would his government stick to Labour spending plans on core public services, including health and education, it would increase spending on defence, police and pensions. And even after that was done, it would still have enough left over for a tax cut equivalent to about a penny off the basic rate of income tax.
All the money would come from its £35 billion efficiency savings which would see the axe taken to bureaucracy, waste and the civil service. Of that, £23 billion would go on spending plans, with £8 billion to fill the black hole left, they claim, by Gordon Brown, and the rest going in tax cuts.
Neither Mr Howard nor Mr Letwin would say exactly how they would use that cash, although a cut in the basic rate seems unlikely. Ideas already floated include raising tax thresholds and abolishing or reducing inheritance tax, although some in the Tory party are urging Mr Howard to announce something more eye-catching before the election. As the Tory leader declared, the aim of the exercise is to open up a real economic policy divide between Labour and the Tories. "At this election, people will have a clear choice between Mr Blair who will waste more and tax more and the Conservative party which will give value for money and tax less," he said.
It is a move back towards an almost traditional Tory message which previously suggested Labour was the party of tax rises and the Conservatives the party of tax cuts.
The extension of that, however, was that Labour was also seen as the party of big spending on the public services while the Tories were the cutters. And that is where one of the problems lies for Mr Howard - can he persuade sceptical voters that they really can have it both ways with bigger spending on public services AND lower taxes? He insists he will not promise anything before the election that he cannot deliver if put into Downing Street. Labour, needless to say, claim his planned £35bn efficiency savings simply don't add up and that those sorts of figures are fantasy.
One of New Labour's greatest successes before the historic 1997 election was to persuade voters, business and the City that it could be trusted to run the economy. So far that has not faced any real challenge, but independent analysts now claim a third New Labour government would be forced to either increase taxation or taxes to plug a black hole it has at the centre of its finances. Meanwhile, the Liberal Democrats are committed to increasing taxes for the most well off to finance their spending proposals launched earlier in the day. So, Mr Howard hopes his message will start to hit home during this unofficial election campaign and that his poll ratings might finally lift off the floor. And, while other issues like the Iraq war and trust will play a major part in that campaign, it is likely - and the prime minister probably hopes - that the economy will be the deciding factor.
