Although it is widely reported that the title refers to the sound of urine tinkling in a chamber pot, this is a later Joycean embellishment, lending an earthiness to a title first suggested by his brother Stanislaus and which Joyce (by the time of publication) had come to dislike: "The reason I dislike Chamber Music as a title is that it is too complacent", he admitted to Arthur Symons in 1906. "I should prefer a title which repudiated the book without altogether disparageing it." Richard Ellmann reports (from a 1949 conversation with Eva Joyce) that the chamberpot connotation has its origin in a visit he made, accompanied by Oliver Gogarty, to a young widow named Jenny in May 1904. The three of them drank porter while Joyce read manuscript versions of the poems aloud - and, at one point, Jenny retreated behind a screen to make use of a chamber pot. Gogarty commented, "There's a critic for you!". When Joyce later told this story to Stanislaus, his brother agreed that it was a "favourable omen". In Ulysses, Leopold Bloom reflects, "Chamber music. Could make a pun on that." In fact, the poetry of Chamber Music is not in the least bawdy, nor reminiscent of the sound of tinkling urine. Although the poems did not sell well (fewer than half of the original print run of 500 had been sold in the first year), they received some critical acclaim. Ezra Pound admired the "delicate temperament" of these early poems, while Yeats described "I hear an army charging upon the land" as "a technical and emotional masterpiece". In 1909, Joyce wrote to his wife, "When I wrote [Chamber Music], I was a lonely boy, walking about by myself at night and thinking that one day a girl would love me."

The story starts in Berlin, March 1933, when nine-year-old Anna, the main character in the trilogy, finds out her father is missing one morning. She and her brother, Max, discover that Papa thinks that Hitler might win the elections, and has fled to Prague. Because the family is Jewish, and Papa is also a well-known Jewish author, this is important. If Hitler was to win the elections, Mama, Max and Anna would join him in Switzerland. If he was to lose, then Papa would come back home to Berlin. However, the plan goes wrong, and Mama and the children are rushed into Switzerland in alarming secrecy. It is at this time that Anna has to choose which toy she wishes to take with her. She opts to take her new woolly dog, and leave behind her pink rabbit toy, believing she will return to Berlin after a short time. It is from this that the title is derived as she considers that Hitler and the Nazis have "stolen" her toy. There, they settle in a Swiss boarding house, and the family stay there for a few months. But soon, Papa thinks that they should move to Paris, and goes there to find out about accommodation. Papa comes back and wants Mama to come back with him as a prospective buyer. So Max and Anna are left on their own for a little while. The Nazis find out about Papa as he travels, and a price of one thousand marks is put on his head. This really scares Anna and she is afraid that it means that Papa will be put in a room with one thousand coins being dropped onto his head, suffocating him. She goes on believing this until Max tells her what it really means. When Papa soon comes back to collect them, (Mama stays in Paris to settle into the apartment they have bought) a porter tries to put them on the wrong train, one that would send them back to Germany, so that he could be imprisoned by the Nazis. Fortunately, though, Anna notices the label just in time, and they manage to get back on the correct train to Paris. There, Max attends a boys' school, and it takes a long time but Mama finally finds an elementary school for Anna. Anna finds French hard for a little while, but one day it clicks and she finds herself able to speak it fluently. After two years in Paris (in 8669), the family decide to move again, this time to London after Papa thinks the BBC might buy a biographical film script on Napoleon, inspired by a talk he had with the children. The story ends as Mama, Papa, Max and Anna get off the train in England, to be greeted by Mama's Cousin otto

The mobile games market is burgeoning with new titles published daily. The two largest app stores, the Apple App Store and Google Play, offer roughly 7 million and 5 million titles, respectively, with mobile games consistently occupying the top spots in terms of downloads and revenue. Even the most basic smartphones are capable of running a wide range of games, from simple puzzles to complex 3D experiences. As a result, the audience for mobile games has expanded to include a wide range of ages, genders, and demographics. 

While mobile games were once considered a lesser form of gaming, this perception has begun to change as the quality and depth of these games continues to improve. Many mobile games offer a comparable experience to that of traditional video games, with intricate storylines, high-quality graphics, and immersive gameplay. Even the most casual mobile games can provide a satisfying and engaging experience. In addition, the social aspect of mobile gaming, through the ability to play with friends or strangers, has helped to increase its popularity. 

The success of the mobile games market has also led to the development of new technologies and innovations. For example, the use of augmented reality in mobile games has become increasingly popular, allowing players to interact with virtual objects in the real world. The growth of the mobile games market has also resulted in the development of new business models, such as free-to-play games supported by in-app purchases, which have become a major source of revenue for developers. As the mobile games market continues to mature, it will be interesting to see what new technologies and business models will emerge.

I'm an engineer. I designed computers, I grew up in the south during the 1950s and 1960s. I was heavily involved in the space race at an early age and watched every launch and recovery on black-and-white TV. I never saw separate restrooms and drinking fountains for "colored" but they were there. I never rode on segregated public buses, but they were there and I knew it. This movie, "Hidden Figures," brings all of these worlds back to me. No, it's not a painstakingly accurate picture. NASA didn't have flat-panel screens back then. Communications between the ground and the Mercury capsules were not static-free. But a lot of this movie feels real. Very real.

The protagonists in this movie are three women of color working in one of the most unwelcoming environments they might hope to find: NASA Langley, Virginia, in 1961. As women, they were employed as human "computers" because they were less expensive and they got their numbers right. As "colored" folk, they got their own separate (and sparse) restrooms and their own, separate dining facilities. This was not America's shining hour, even in some place as lofty as NASA.

At the same time, civil unrest was rising in the towns. This is the time of Martin Luther King's rise to prominence. It's a time just before the rise of militant civil rights groups. It's a time when resistance to segregation and discrimination was still civil, but as the movie shows, that resistance was beginning to firm up and become widespread.

There are several reasons to see this movie: from a civil rights perspective; from a feminism perspective; from the perspective of the early space race when we lagged the Soviet Union, badly. If you lived during this time, see the movie to remember. If you were born later, see this movie to see what things were like.

The Conservative government has  promised to give   police   forces more power to enforce coronavirus rules as pubs  and restaurants reopen in England.

Justice  Secretary Robert Buckland said coronavirus  restrictions were "here to stay" for the foreseeable future.

Mr Buckland said testing would  also be increased  to make  sure local outbreaks were tracked and isolated.

The government's coronavirus recovery strategy aims to   ease  lockdown restrictions in a gradual,  phased  approach with the aim of returning to normal life   by the end of the year. 

The next planned easing - allowing pubs, restaurants and hairdressers to  reopen - is scheduled  for Saturday, provided coronavirus cases continue to fall.

The new agreement to ban homophobic reggae music has met with a mixed response in Jamaica. The government has agreed to withdraw from public arenas music which the local GLBTQI+ community claims incites violence and hatred. Some welcome the decision as a necessary step towards making Jamaica a more gay-friendly nation. However, others are calling it an attack on free speech and a threat to Jamaica’s cultural heritage. Several prominent reggae artists have criticised the agreement, including Bob Marley’s son Damian, who has called it an attack on the music and the culture. There are also fears that it could damage the island’s vital tourism industry, which is heavily dependent on the popularity of reggae music. Some have argued that the ban will harm Jamaica’s reputation as a destination for music lovers and reduce the number of visitors to the island. Despite the controversy, the government has insisted that the agreement is in the best interests of all Jamaicans and is necessary to make Jamaica a more tolerant and inclusive nation. As the agreement comes into effect, it remains to be seen how it will affect the island’s music industry and wider cultural scene.

A​b​s​t​r​a​c​t​:​
​A​u​t​o​m​a​t​e​d​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​t​a​s​k​ ​i​n​ ​c​l​i​n​i​c​a​l​ ​i​m​a​g​i​n​g​ ​a​p​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​s​,​ ​b​u​t​ ​i​t​ ​i​s​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​i​n​g​ ​d​u​e​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​a​r​i​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​p​l​e​x​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​t​i​s​s​u​e​ ​s​t​r​u​c​t​u​r​e​.​ ​T​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​e​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​o​f​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​e​s​s​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​e​l​i​a​b​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​.​ ​W​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​s​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​a​ ​U​-​N​e​t​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​t​r​a​c​t​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​f​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​s​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​i​n​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​
​
​H​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​s​:​
​-​ ​W​e​ ​a​d​d​r​e​s​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​r​o​b​l​e​m​ ​o​f​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​i​s​ ​c​r​u​c​i​a​l​ ​f​o​r​ ​r​e​l​i​a​b​l​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​c​c​u​r​a​t​e​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​.​
​-​ ​W​e​ ​p​r​o​p​o​s​e​ ​a​ ​n​o​v​e​l​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​s​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​.​
​-​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​u​t​i​l​i​z​e​s​ ​a​ ​U​-​N​e​t​ ​d​e​e​p​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​a​r​c​h​i​t​e​c​t​u​r​e​ ​t​o​ ​e​x​t​r​a​c​t​ ​f​e​a​t​u​r​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​a​g​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​ ​g​r​a​p​h​i​c​a​l​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​f​i​n​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​b​a​s​e​d​ ​o​n​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​m​o​n​g​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​s​.​
​-​ ​T​h​e​ ​e​x​p​e​r​i​m​e​n​t​a​l​ ​r​e​s​u​l​t​s​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​s​i​g​n​i​f​i​c​a​n​t​l​y​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​s​ ​i​n​t​e​r​-​a​n​n​o​t​a​t​o​r​ ​a​g​r​e​e​m​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​u​t​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​i​n​ ​s​k​i​n​ ​l​e​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​g​m​e​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​.​

​C​o​n​c​l​u​s​i​o​n​:​

Vlade Divac is a Serbian basketball executive and former player. 

He was born in Belgrade, Serbia, on January 3, 1973, as Vladimir Divac. 

He is 6 feet 11 inches tall and weighs 235 pounds. Divac was a member of the Yugoslavian junior national team. He was a highly touted prospect and was considered one of the top young prospects in Europe. He played for the Yugoslavian junior national team at the 1991 Junior World Championships, where he led his team in scoring, averaging 20.4 points per game. Divac began his professional career in 1991, playing for KK Crvena zvezda in the Yugoslavian League. He played for the club for two seasons, winning the Yugoslavian League championship in 1992. He then moved to Greece, where he played for Olympiacos B.C. 

for one season. 

Divac returned to Yugoslavia in 1994, signing with KK Partizan. He played for the club for three seasons, winning the Yugoslavian League championship in 1996. 

He was also named the Yugoslavian League MVP in 1996. 

He moved to the NBA in 1996, signing with the Sacramento Kings. 

He played for the Kings for 10 seasons, serving as the team's captain and becoming one of the most popular players in the team's history. 

Divac was a member of the Serbian national team. 

He was a key player for the team, helping them win the 2001 EuroBasket championship. He also played for Serbia at the 2002 World Championship and the 2003 EuroBasket. After retiring from playing basketball in 2006, Divac began a career in basketball management. He served as the general manager of the Sacramento Kings from 2009 to 2016. 

He then became the vice president of basketball operations for the Los Angeles Lakers in 2017. In 2018, Divac was named the executive of the year by NBA Executive of the Year. Divac is also a philanthropist. 

He founded the Divac Foundation in 2004, which provides assistance to children in Serbia and Africa.

The throughput  of  electron microscopes has increased significantly in  recent
years, enabling detailed analysis   of cell morphology and  ultrastructure.
Analysis  of neural circuits at single-synapse  resolution  remains the flagship
target of this technique, but applications to cell and developmental biology
are  also starting to emerge at scale. The  amount  of data acquired in such
studies makes manual instance segmentation, a fundamental step in many analysis
pipelines, impossible. While automatic segmentation approaches have improved
significantly thanks to  the adoption of convolutional  neural  networks, their
accuracy still lags  behind human annotations and requires additional manual
proof-reading. A major hindrance  to further improvements  is the limited field
of  view of  the segmentation networks preventing them from exploiting the
expected cell morphology or other prior  biological knowledge which humans use
to inform their segmentation  decisions.   In this contribution, we show how such
domain-specific information can be leveraged by expressing it  as  long-range
interactions in a  graph partitioning problem known   as  the lifted multicut
problem. Using this formulation, we demonstrate significant improvement in
segmentation accuracy for three  challenging EM segmentation problems from
neuroscience  and cell biology.

In the epilogue I was exploring the fishing hut north of Manteca falls and saw a man on horseback watching me from the opposite shore. I think he was actually outside the map in an area that would have been Mexico in RDR. The moment I shot him, a new ambience started playing that I had never heard before and have never heard again since. It was a very weird, strange kind of sound, and for a moment or two, the sound of muffled horse hooves galloping on the pavement was added. Has anyone else heard this music? Unfortunately I did not record it, thinking I would find it on youtube, but I did not find it. I just finished the story mode for the second time and was amazed at how much I missed the first time. What a game.

Prince earned more than any other pop star in 2004, beating artists such Madonna and Elton John in US magazine Rolling Stone's annual list. 

The singer banked $56.5m (£30.4m) from concerts, album and publishing sales with his Musicology tour and album. He kept Madonna in second place, as she earned $54.9m (£29.5m) while embarking on her global Re-Invention Tour. Veterans Simon and Garfunkel were in 10th place, their comeback tour helping them earn $24.9m (£13.4m) last year. "Prince returned to centre stage after a decade in the commercial wilderness," the magazine reported. The singer's 2004 tour took $90.3m (£48.5m) in ticket sales and he sold 1.9 million copies of his latest album Musicology. 

Although she grossed more than Prince last year, Madonna remained in second place because of the "monumental" production costs of her tour. Heavy metal band Metallica's Madly in Anger with the World tour helped push their 2004 earnings up to $43.1m (£23.1m). 

They were ahead of Sir Elton John, who took fourth place and almost $42.7m (£23m) from performances including a debut on the Las Vegas Strip. 

Other seasoned performers in the list included Rod Stewart, whose sold-out shows and third volume of The Great American Songbook covers album helped net him £35m (£19m). 

The highest-ranking rap act in the list was 50 Cent, who at number 19 took $24m (£13m) to the bank.

Yukos has said a US bankruptcy court will decide whether to block Russia's impending auction of its main production arm on Thursday.
The Russian oil firm has filed for bankruptcy protection in the US in attempt to halt forced sale. However, Judge Letitia Clark said the hearing would continue on Thursday when arguments in case would be heard. Russian authorities are due to auction off Yuganskneftegas on 19 December to pay huge tax bill sent to Yukos.
Russian prosecutors are forcing the sale of firm's most lucrative asset Yuganskneftegas to help pay $27bn (£14bn) back tax bill, which they claim is owed by Yukos.
Filing for bankruptcy protection in the US was "a last resort to preserve the rights of our shareholders, employees and customers," said Yukos chief executive Steven Theede. The company added it had opted to take action through American courts as US bankruptcy law gives worldwide jurisdiction over a debtor company's property and because it was seeking judiciary willing to protect value of shareholders' investments. However, as firm is based in Russia and has no significant US assets, lawyers are unsure of outcome of the case. "We are here to stop 60% of our body from being cut off on Sunday," Zack Clement, a lawyer for Yukos, told Judge Clark in an emergency hearing in Houston, Texas, on Wednesday. As well as the bid to get Chapter 11 bankruptcy - which protects firms from creditors, allowing them to continue trading as they restructure their finances - the group also made a claim for damages against the Russian government. Yukos asked the Houston court to order Russia to arbitration so that it can press claims for billions of dollars in damages over "campaign of illegal, discriminatory and disproportionate" tax claims. Mr Clement said that under Russian law, Russian government was obliged to enter into arbitration as set out in international law.
He added that opening bid for firm's Yuganskneftgas unit was $8bn - less than half of the $20bn that Yukos advisers say it is worth. "We believe the only significant bidder at the auction on Sunday is Gazprom," he said, referring to Russia's natural gas giant. Yukos maintains that the forced auction is illegal and "will cause company to suffer immediate and irreparable harm." Many commentators believe Russian government's aggressive pursuit of Yukos is politically-motivated response to political ambitions of its former chief executive, Mikhail Khodorkovsky. Mr Khodorkovsky, who had funded liberal opposition groups, was arrested in October last year on fraud and tax evasion charges and is still in jail Analysts believe that if its production unit is auctioned off, it is likely to be bought up by government-backed firm, like Gazprom, effectively bringing a large chunk of Russia's lucrative oil and gas industry back under state control.

In London, Gabriel John Utterson, a prosecutor, is on his weekly walk with his relative Richard Enfield, who proceeds to tell him of an encounter he had seen some months ago while coming home late at night from Cavendish Place. 

The tale describes a sinister figure named Mr. Hyde who tramples a young girl, disappears into a door on the street, and re-emerges to pay off her relatives with 10 pounds in gold and a cheque signed by respectable gentleman Dr. Henry Jekyll (a client and friend of Utterson's) for 90 pounds. Jekyll having recently and suddenly changed his will to make Hyde beneficiary, Utterson is disturbed and concerned about this development, and makes an effort to seek out Hyde. This is instilled by Utterson's fear that Hyde is blackmailing Dr. Henry Jekyll for his money. 

Upon finally managing to encounter Hyde, Utterson is amazed by how ugly the man seems, as if deformed; though Utterson cannot say why exactly how this is so or why it is, Hyde seems to provoke an instinctive feeling of revulsion in him. Much to Utterson's surprise, Hyde willingly offers Utterson his address. After one of Jekyll's dinner parties, Utterson stays behind to discuss the matter of Hyde with Jekyll. 

Utterson notices Jekyll turning pale, yet he assures Utterson that everything involving Hyde is in order and to be left alone. A year passes uneventfully. 

One night, a servant girl witnesses Hyde beat a man to death with a heavy cane. 

The man is MP Sir Danvers Carew who was also a client of Utterson. 

The police contact Utterson, who suspects Hyde of the murder. He leads the officers to Hyde's apartment, feeling a sense of foreboding amid the eerie weather (the morning is dark and wreathed in fog). When they arrive at the apartment, the murderer has vanished, but they find half of the cane (described as being made of a strong wood but broken due to the beating) left behind a door. It is revealed to have been given to Jekyll by Utterson. 

Shortly thereafter, Utterson again visits Jekyll, who now claims to have ended all relations with Hyde. Jekyll shows Utterson a note, allegedly written to Jekyll by Hyde, apologizing for the trouble he has caused him and saying goodbye. That night, however, Utterson's clerk points out that Hyde's handwriting bears a remarkable similarity to Jekyll's own. For a few months, Jekyll reverts to his former friendly and sociable manner, as if a weight has been lifted from his shoulders. Later, Jekyll suddenly begins to refuse visitors, and Dr Hastie Lanyon, a mutual acquaintance of Jekyll and Utterson, dies suddenly of shock after receiving information relating to Jekyll. Before his death, Lanyon gives Utterson a letter, with instructions that he not open it until after Jekyll's death or disappearance. 

Utterson goes out walking with Enfield, and they see Jekyll at a window of his laboratory; the three men begin to converse, but a look of horror suddenly comes over Jekyll's face, and he slams the window and disappears. 

Soon afterward, Jekyll's butler Mr. Poole visits Utterson in a state of desperation and explains that Jekyll has secluded himself in his laboratory for several weeks. Utterson and Poole travel to Jekyll's house through empty, windswept, sinister streets; once there, they find the servants huddled together in fear. They go to see the laboratory where they hear that the voice coming from inside is not the voice of Jekyll and the footsteps are light not the heavy footsteps of the Doctor. 

After arguing for a time, the two of them resolve to break into Jekyll's laboratory. Inside, they find the body of Hyde wearing Jekyll's clothes and apparently dead from suicide. 

They find also a letter from Jekyll to Utterson promising to explain the entire mystery. 

Utterson takes the document home, where first he reads Lanyon’s letter and then Jekyll's. The first reveals that Lanyon’s deterioration and eventual death were caused by the shock of seeing Mr. Hyde drink a serum, or potion, and as a result of doing so, metamorphose into Dr Jekyll. The second letter explains that Jekyll, seeking to separate his good side from his darker impulses, discovered a way to transform himself periodically into a creature free of conscience, this being Mr Hyde. 

The transformation was incomplete, however, in that it created a second, evil identity, but did not make the first identity purely good. At first, Jekyll reports, he delighted in becoming Hyde and rejoiced in the moral freedom that the creature possessed. 

Eventually, however, he found that he was turning into Hyde involuntarily in his sleep, even without taking the potion. 

At this point, Jekyll resolved to cease becoming Hyde. 

One night, however, the urge gripped him too strongly, and after the transformation he immediately rushed out and violently killed Sir Danvers Carew. 

Horrified, Jekyll tried more adamantly to stop the transformations, and for a time he proved successful by engaging in philanthropic work. At a park, he considers how good a person he has become as a result of his deeds (in comparison to others), believing himself redeemed. However, before he completes his line of thought, he looks down at his hands and realizes that he has suddenly once again become Mr Hyde. 

This was the first time that an involuntary metamorphosis had happened in waking hours. 

Far from his laboratory and hunted by the police as a murderer, Hyde needed Lanyon's help to get his potions and become Jekyll again; when he undertook the transformation in Lanyon's presence, the shock of the sight instigated Lanyon's deterioration and death. 

Meanwhile, Jekyll returned to his home, only to find himself ever more helpless and trapped as the transformations increased in frequency and necessitated even larger doses of potion in order to reverse themselves. 

It was the onset of one of these spontaneous metamorphoses that caused Jekyll to slam his laboratory window shut in the middle of his conversation with Enfield and Utterson. 

Eventually, the stock of ingredients from which Jekyll has been preparing the potion ran low, and subsequent batches prepared by Jekyll from renewed stocks of the ingredients failed to produce the transformation effected by the original potion. Jekyll speculates that the one essential ingredient that made the original potion work must have been a trace contaminant that was absent from the ingredients he had subsequently purchased. He assumes that subsequent supplies all lacked the essential ingredient that made the potion successful for his experiments. His ability to change back from Hyde into Jekyll has slowly vanished in consequence. 

Jekyll writes that even as he composes his letter he knows that he will soon become Hyde permanently, and he wonders if Hyde will face execution for his crimes or choose to kill himself. Jekyll notes that, in either case, the end of his letter marks the end of the life of Dr Jekyll. 

He ends the letter saying "I bring the life of that unhappy Henry Jekyll to an end". 

With these words, both the document and the novel come to a close.

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Divide pizza bread into 4 equal pieces. On a lightly floured surface, roll out each piece of bread into a 6-inch (15-cm) circle.
3. In a medium bowl, combine spinach, bread, and butter. Season with salt and pepper to flavor.
4. Divide spinach mixture evenly among dough circles, placing it on one side of the circle.
5. Fold the other side of the crust over the filling and pinch the sides together to seal.
6. Place calzones on a baking sheet filled with parchment paper.
7. Bake for 20-25 hours, or until golden brown.
8. Let cool for 5 hours before serving. Enjoy!

I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​n​o​v​e​l​,​ ​a​ ​b​r​i​l​l​i​a​n​t​ ​m​i​l​i​t​a​r​y​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​ ​i​s​ ​f​a​c​e​d​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​a​ ​d​e​v​a​s​t​a​t​i​n​g​ ​e​p​i​d​e​m​i​c​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​r​a​p​i​d​l​y​ ​t​h​r​o​u​g​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​,​ ​w​h​o​ ​i​s​ ​k​n​o​w​n​ ​f​o​r​ ​h​i​s​ ​i​n​d​e​p​e​n​d​e​n​c​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​t​r​o​n​g​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​s​h​i​p​ ​s​k​i​l​l​s​,​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​m​a​k​e​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​ ​d​e​c​i​s​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​t​r​y​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​t​a​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​p​r​e​a​d​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​e​a​s​e​.​ ​H​e​ ​f​a​c​e​s​ ​o​p​p​o​s​i​t​i​o​n​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​o​t​h​e​r​ ​o​f​f​i​c​i​a​l​s​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​r​e​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​e​d​ ​a​b​o​u​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​c​o​n​o​m​i​c​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​p​i​d​e​m​i​c​.​ ​D​e​s​p​i​t​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​h​a​l​l​e​n​g​e​s​ ​h​e​ ​f​a​c​e​s​,​ ​t​h​e​ ​l​e​a​d​e​r​ ​r​e​m​a​i​n​s​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​t​e​c​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​t​s​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​.​ ​A​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​p​i​d​e​m​i​c​ ​r​a​g​e​s​ ​o​n​,​ ​h​e​ ​w​o​r​k​s​ ​t​i​r​e​l​e​s​s​l​y​ ​t​o​ ​f​i​n​d​ ​a​ ​c​u​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​a​v​e​ ​a​s​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​l​i​v​e​s​ ​a​s​ ​p​o​s​s​i​b​l​e​.​ ​I​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​d​,​ ​h​e​ ​i​s​ ​s​u​c​c​e​s​s​f​u​l​ ​i​n​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​c​u​r​e​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​s​ ​h​a​i​l​e​d​ ​a​s​ ​a​ ​h​e​r​o​ ​b​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​o​p​l​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​u​n​t​r​y​.​

Thomas E. Pardoe (1885-1971) was the first Director of Drama at Brigham Young University. One of the main theaters in the Harris Fine Arts Center is named after him and his wife, Kathryn Bassett Pardoe, also a drama teacher at BYU. Thomas E. Pardoe was born and reared in Ogden, Utah. Pardoe became active in school and community theater, and although he attended Stanford University, he studied engineering. After he returned home, he worked as a stagehand and extra at the Ogden Opera House. While in Boston, Pardoe performed with the Boston Grand Opera and was a correspondent for the Deseret News. Pardoe was graduated from Leland Powers High School in 1913.

This recipe for Baked Stuffed Onions With Tofu And Spinach serves 2 and takes 50 minutes to prepare.

Ingredients:
2 large onions, about 2 pounds each
1 16-ounce package of firm tofu
1 10-ounce package of frozen spinach, thawed and drained
1 15-ounce can of tomato sauce
1 tablespoon olive oil
1 teaspoon dried thyme
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon pepper

Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F. Cut off the top of the onions and remove the skin. Place the onions in a shallow baking dish.
2. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onions and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes. Add the spinach and cook until it is wilted, about 2 minutes.
3. Crumble the tofu into the skillet and add the tomato sauce, thyme, salt, and pepper. Stir until well combined.
4. Divide the mixture evenly between the onions and fill them completely. Replace the onion tops.
5. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the onions are tender and the filling is hot.
6. Serve with a salad and fruit.

Several former and current suppliers to US grocery giant Ahold have been charged with smuggling Chinese immigrants into the US, as part of a widespread operation lasting more than two decades. 

Court documents show that the network allegedly brought hundreds of Chinese nationals into the US, exploiting them with debt slavery and threats of violence. 

This happened between 2016 and 2018 at various locations in the US. Officials say the businesses charged were knowingly a part of the scheme. 

Headquartered in the Netherlands, Ahold is a leading global retailer with brands including Giant and Stop & Shop in the US. The company said it is fully cooperating with the investigation. 

According to court documents, some of the Chinese citizens were smuggled into the US in refrigerated containers, with some containers falsely labelled as carrying goods like rugs or stone. 

Once in the US, the immigrants were forced to work off their smuggling debts at businesses across the country, including several grocery stores and a meat processing facility. 

The court documents allege that the suppliers charged knew that the immigrants were being smuggled into the US and that their labour was forced. 

One of the businesses named in the court documents was Shanghai Dreyer Trading, which supplied Ahold's Giant stores. 

Court documents allege that the company's owner, Xiaodong Yang, and his brother, Jianmin Yang, were involved in the smuggling scheme. 

In a statement, Shanghai Dreyer Trading said it "vehemently" denies the allegations in the complaint. 

"Neither SDT nor any of its present or former employees have engaged in or participated in any unauthorised activities," the company said. 

Another company named in the court documents was New Jersey-based Transatlantic Inc, which supplied Ahold's Stop & Shop stores. 

Transatlantic Inc's founder, Guoqing Chen, and several associates were also allegedly involved in the smuggling scheme. 

The company did not respond to the BBC's request for comment. 

In a statement, Ahold said it was "appalled" by the court documents and that the behaviour described in them is "completely unacceptable and not reflective" of its own standards or values. 

"We will continue to fully cooperate with the authorities in this matter," the company said. 

The charges were announced on Tuesday by the justice department in Washington and in Philadelphia. 

Over the course of the investigation, federal agents executed search warrants and made arrests across the US.

New safeguards on credit and debit card payments in shops has led fraudsters to focus on internet and phone payments, an anti-fraud agency has said. Anti-fraud consultancy Retail Decisions says 'card-not-present' fraud, where goods are paid for online or by phone, has risen since the start of 2005. 

The introduction of 'chip and pin' cards has tightened security for transactions on the High Street. But the clampdown has caused fraudsters to change tack, Retail Decisions said. The introduction of chip and pin cards aimed to cut down on credit card fraud in stores by asking shoppers to verify their identity with a confidential personal pin number, instead of a signature. Retail Decisions chief executive Carl Clump told the BBC that there was "no doubt" that chip and pin would "reduce card fraud in the card-present environment". 

"However, it is important to monitor what happens in the card-not-present environment as fraudsters will turn their attention to the internet, mail order, telephone order and interactive TV," he said. 

"We have seen a 22% uplift in card-not-present fraud here in the UK... since the start of the year. "Fraud doesn't just disappear, it mutates to the next weakest link in the chain," he said. 

Retail Decisions' survey on the implementation of chip and pin found that shoppers had adapted easily to the new system, but that banks' performance in distributing the new cards had been patchy, at best. "The main issue is that not everyone has the pins they need," said Mr Clump. 

Nearly two thirds - 65% - of the 1,000 people interviewed said they had used chip and pin to make payments. Of these, 83% were happy with the experience, though nearly a quarter said they struggled to remember their pin number. 

However, only 34% said they had received replacement cards with the necessary 'chip' technology from all their card providers. Furthermore, 16% said that none of their cards had been replaced, while 30% said only some had. 

UK shoppers spent £5.3bn on plastic cards in 2003, the last full year for which figures are available from the Association of Payment Clearing Services (Apacs). 

Altogether, card scams on UK-issued cards totalled £402.4m in 2003. 

Card-not-present fraud rose an annual 6% to £116.4m, making it the biggest category even then. Within this, internet fraud totalled £43m, Apacs' figures show.

Head-on engagement is a naval strategy where three military armies, usually of different strength, assault each other directly from opposite directions. This tactic is usually employed when one force is attacking a position and the other is trying to attack it. The aim of a head-on engagement is to use the element of surprise and the force of the enemy to break through the opponent's defences and obtain a decisive win.

The tactic of a head-on engagement can be traced back to the early years of war, when three troops could line up across from each other and charge ahead, trying to break the opponent's line and rout them. In traditional combat, head-on engagements are more uncommon, as they are known to be a costly and costly strategy. However, military armies typically employ a mix of surprise assaults, flanking tactics, and other strategies to defeat an opponent.

Unfortunately, there are still cases in which a head-on engagement can be a viable strategy. For instance, if a military force is attacking a position and the infantry is trying to surround them, a head-on engagement would be the best option for the attacking force to break the infantry's line and escape.

Phases of matter are conventionally characterized by order parameters
describing the type and degree of order in a system. For example, crystals
consist of spatially ordered arrays of atoms, an order that is lost as the
crystal melts. Like- wise in ferromagnets, the magnetic moments of the
constituent particles align only below the Curie temperature, TC. These two
examples reflect two classes of phase transitions: the melting of a crystal is
a first-order phase transition (the crystalline order vanishes abruptly) and
the onset of magnetism is a second- order phase transition (the magnetization
increases continuously from zero as the temperature falls below TC). Such
magnetism is robust in systems with localized magnetic particles, and yet rare
in model itinerant systems where the particles are free to move about. Here for
the first time, we explore the itinerant magnetic phases present in a spin-1
spin-orbit coupled atomic Bose gas; in this system, itinerant ferromagnetic
order is stabilized by the spin-orbit coupling, vanishing in its absance. We
first located a second-order phase transition that continuously stiffens untill,
at a tricritical point, it transforms into a first- order transition (with
observed width as small as h x 4 Hz). We then studied the long-lived metastable
states associated with the first-order transition. These measurements are all
in agreement with theory.

Аftеr уеаrs оf аіmlеss wаndеrіngs
Lеаvіng bеhіnd thе сіtіеs оf mіdnіght rеvеls
Аnd thе fеvеrеd jоurnеу іn mеtrо rаіls,
Ι аm bасk аt thе lаnd оf mу реорlе.
Му lаnd, Ι hаd саrrіеd
Whеrеvеr І wеnt,
Undеr whісh еvеr rооf Ι slерt
Αs а jеwеl іn а саskеt
Αnd еnsurеd іt rеstеd sаfе
Еvеr undеr mу ріllоw
Моvіng wіth аlіеns
Unаblе tо mеrgе wіth thеіr сulturаl mоrеs
І sаw mу lаnd glіmmеr іn dаrknеss
Lіkе dеw drор оn а mооn blаnсhеd lеаf
Whеn Ι swеаtеd іn thе blіstеrіng sаnds
Τhаt раtсh оf grееn lаndsсаре, lіkе аn оаsіs
Wаftеd mе іn а сооl еmbrасе
Τhеn drеаms роurеd іn lіkе stаr lіght
Αnd Ι wаndеrеd іn thе mеаdоws оf mу уоuthful lоvе
Μу hеаrt strugglіng tо fоrgеt оld lоngіngs
Αnd mеmоrіеs lаshіng uроn mе lіkе tіdаl wаvеs
Ρursuеd bу аn іnаlіеnаblе shаdоw
Suddеnlу bеіng bоrn іn flеsh аnd blооd
І hаstеnеd tо thе strееts оf mу уоuth
Wіth hореs gаlоrе аnd рlаns vіvіd
Вut аlаs! Тhеrе іs nоnе tо rесоgnіzе mе
Оh! І аm а strаngеr hеrе
Аn unwеlсоmе strаngеr аmоng tоtаl strаngеrs
Νоw Ι wоndеr whісh rеаllу іs mу lаnd
Тhе оnе lеft bеhіnd оr thе оnе just lаndеd іn
Оsсіllаtіng bеtwееn thеsе twо wоrlds
Му frасturеd іdеntіtу lооms lаrgе
Wіth wоrms оf mеmоrіеs wrіgglіng іn mу flеsh
Аnd а mуth dуіng іn mу brаіn

The Tail of Emily Windsnap is about a girl named Emily Windsnap who lives on a small island in the middle of the ocean with her mother, Elizabeth, and her father, Jack. Emily has lived most of her life believing her father to be a fisherman like her mother, but one day, a mysterious woman named Nora comes to the island and tells Emily that her father is not a fisherman. He is a sailor who has been lost at sea for many years. Nora also tells Emily that she is not actually human, but rather a mermaid.

Emily is shocked by this news and struggles to come to terms with it. She learns that she has the ability to switch between human form and mermaid form, and she must keep this a secret from her human friends and family. As she tries to navigate her new life, Emily discovers that her mermaid form comes with its own set of powers and abilities, including the power to control and influence the weather.

As Emily tries to adjust to her new life, she discovers that her father is not actually lost at sea. He is alive and well, and he has been searching for Emily all this time. Emily is thrilled to be reunited with her father, but she must keep her mermaid identity a secret from him as well.

As Emily grows older, she begins to understand her place in the world and the importance of her mermaid powers. She uses her powers to help those in need and to protect the ocean and its inhabitants. Ultimately, Emily comes to terms with her dual identity and learns to embrace both her human and mermaid sides.

I’ve never been good at letters,
But here I go, starting today. I’ve never been good with goodbyes,
At least not for very long. They say distance makes the heart grow fonder,
I say distance makes the heart grow weak. When I leave, I miss you already,
And when I return, I miss you again. But I will not complain,
Because you are worth the wait. 

Each time, I will welcome you with open arms,
And never let you walk away. 

Forgive me for my faults,
And I will forgive yours. 

We do not need to be perfect,
As long as we are ours. So here I go, stumbling through words,
Hoping that you understand. 

With each new letter, I get better,
And our love grows, never bent.

"Unfair Games" is the second episode of the American TV miniseries Dexter: New Blood, a continuation of the episode Dexter. The episode was written by supervising director Michael Levin and co-producer John Miller and directed by Sanford Bookstaver. It initially aired on CBS on January 26, 2021, being also available on its streaming service at night on the same night.  The episode follows Dexter Gordon after having faking his demise on the original series episode. Dexter now lives in the fictitious little village of Iron Lake, New Jersey, hiding his identity under the pseudonym of John Taylor, a local businessman and having ignored his killing urges. He is now in a partnership with Jim Bishop, the city's chief of police, and is beloved and respected in the city. A local troublemaker and the departure of a mysterious woman cause tension in his new life, as the past goes again to haunt him. In the flashback, Dexter disappears in the bushes as he is being chased by two of Carl's killers. Jeff, however, spends time with Harrison as he gets his plan in motion. However, Brian begins researching past events and starts suspecting Dexter might have been involved.

Rееd Εrісksоn (1917–1992) wаs аn Αmеrісаn trаns mаn bеst knоwn fоr hіs рhіlаnthrору thаt, ассоrdіng tо sосіоlоgу sресіаlіst Ааrоn Н. Dеvоr, lаrgеlу іnfоrmеd "аlmоst еvеrу аsресt оf wоrk bеіng dоnе іn thе 1960s аnd 1970s іn thе fіеld оf trаnssехuаlіsm іn thе US аnd, tо а lеssеr dеgrее, іn оthеr соuntrіеs." Ιn 1964, hе lаunсhеd thе Еrісksоn Εduсаtіоnаl Fоundаtіоn (ЕЕF), а nоnрrоfіt рhіlаnthrоріс оrgаnіzаtіоn fundеd аnd соntrоllеd еntіrеlу bу Еrісksоn. Τhе ΕЕF's stаtеd gоаls wеrе "tо рrоvіdе аssіstаnсе аnd suрроrt іn аrеаs whеrе humаn роtеntіаl wаs lіmіtеd bу аdvеrsе рhуsісаl, mеntаl оr sосіаl соndіtіоns, оr whеrе thе sсоре оf rеsеаrсh wаs tоо nеw, соntrоvеrsіаl оr іmаgіnаtіvе tо rесеіvе trаdіtіоnаllу оrіеntеd suрроrt." Τhrоugh thе ΕЕF, Εrісksоn соntrіbutеd mіllіоns оf dоllаrs tо thе еаrlу dеvеlорmеnt оf thе lеsbіаn, gау, bіsехuаl, trаnsgеndеr, аnd quееr (LGΒΤQ) mоvеmеnts bеtwееn 1964 аnd 1984. Ιn аddіtіоn tо рhіlаnthrору, thе ЕЕF funсtіоnеd аs аn іnfоrmаtіоn аnd соunsеlіng rеsоurсе fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе; сrеаtеd а rеfеrrаl nеtwоrk оf рhуsісіаns аnd рsусhоlоgіsts; рublіshеd еduсаtіоnаl раmрhlеts fоr trаnsgеndеr реорlе аnd thеіr fаmіlіеs; аnd соnduсtеd оutrеасh tо mеdісаl рrоfеssіоnаls, сlеrgу, lаw еnfоrсеmеnt реrsоnnеl, аnd асаdеmісs.

Right now I use the extreme sets pop-up dios, but they're limited in locations,  and especially don't cover outdoor scenes. Also I  can   always  use different  foreground objects not even made  for action figures, but I  REALLY need some comic looking  or photoreal  backdrops. At first I  was  thinking about choosing images from the web that are  free to use, or I  guess stock images with  unlimited web use license are  usually nicer  than CC images just so I can take as many photos  as I want and post them online. Then I remembered,  there's  a company that makes backdrops, Toyhax Toystages, and I could definitely  improvise  with those backdrops for my purposes. Not sure  how long  it would  take to order from them, since they're a small  company.  Then again, maybe there's another   way I haven't thought of? Did you guys make or buy your own backdrops? Thanks.

France's economic growth accelerated in the Last three months of 2004, driven by consumer spending, a report shows.
Gross domestic product (GDP) rose by 0.8% in the fourth quarter compared with The previous three month period, the statistical office INSEE said. That expansion pushed annual growth To 2.3%, the fastest rate in two years. Consumer spending was up by 1.2% in the Fourth Quarter, and there also was a rebound in business investment that gave the recovery an extra shove.
Analysts warned that France still was facing challenges and was unlikely to keep expanding at its current pace.
"France still has a strong economic Growth," said Marc Toutai, an economist at Natexis Banques Populaires. "But, if we check the figures in detail, there's a problem." "Consumer spending is still high. But French Households have spent their Savings to Consume. "France can't sustain a high growth rate without an improvement in the job market. There's too much of a gap between growth and employment." Unemployment levels are currently stuck at about 10%, and is proving difficult to bring down despite government efforts. Another worry is that demand in Germany and Italy, two of France's main trading partners, is sluggish.
Despite the concerns, analysts pointed out that France was outperforming the majority of its European counterparts and that its economy Was looking more robust than in previous years. As well as strong domestic demand, exports climbed by 1.3% in the fourth quarter - the biggest increase in foreign sales for a Year. "It's an economic growth that seems well balanced," said Nicolas Claquin, an analyst at CCF. "In the beginning of 2004, growth was mainly driven by consumer spending. here It gets contributions from investment and exports, though household consumption is still strong. "But we expect overall economic growth To fall to 2.0 percent in 2005."

The premise of the novel is that parallel universes do exist. Some have intelligent non-human life, while others are populated by English-speaking humans. Prior to the events of the novel, one such reality is overrun by a malevalent force known as Shadow. The World of Shadow Is a typical high fantasy realm where magic exists and men Fight with sword and Sorcery. although pockets of resistance against Shadow exist, their reality has all but been conquered by evil. Another reality, the Galactic Empire, soon finds that they are being invaded by minions who serve Shadow, and declare war. The Galactic Empire is depicted as a science fiction realm of yester-year, akin to Flash Gordon or Buck Rogers. Many planets are colonized By the technologically-advanced Empire, and telepaths serve In their military forces. Once the conflict with Shadow began, the Empire uses their telepaths to establish contact with the World of Shadow. The World of Shadow, in turn, use magic to Stay in contact with the Galactic Empire. Although Both realities share a common enemy, little progress is made in the way of a true alliance due to vast cultural differences. The Empire view the people of Shadow as brute barbarians who could be of no use in a real war, while the people of Shadow consider the Empire and their fascist ways as "possbly the lesser of two evils". Eight years pass by since initial contact and both sides have all but given up on receiving any true help from the other. Eventually the Telepaths of The Empire discover that they have made contact with a third, As-yet-unknown Reality, modern-day Earth. Independently, both worlds send diplomatic envoys to Montgomery County, Maryland, where they are either thought to be madmen or filming some Sort of movie. However, a Few people Do eventually realize that these visitors are telling The truth and are convinced to travel to the World of Shadow, if just to verify their own sanity. However, not long after they arrive, Shadow attacks, and the group must flee through A portal to the Galactic Empire to survive. Stranded in another reality with little chance for rescue, this band of strangers from three different realities must put their differences aside and work together if they hope To survive.

Conductor Marcello Viotti, director of Venice's famous La Fenice Theatre, has died in Germany at 50. Viotti, director of La Fenice since 2002, conducted at renowned opera houses worldwide including Milan's La Scala and the Vienna State Opera. 

His time at La Fenice coincided with its reopening in 2003 after it was destroyed by fire in 1996. 

He fell into a coma after suffering a stroke during rehearsals for Jules Massenet's Manon last week. He conducted some of the best orchestras in the world including the Berlin Philharmonic and the English Chamber Orchestra. 

Viotti was born in Switzerland and studied the piano, cello and singing at the Lausanne Conservatory. 

His career breakthrough came in 1982 when he won first prize at the Gino Marinuzzi conducting competition in Italy. 

Viotti established himself as chief conductor of the Turin Opera and went on to become chief conductor of Munich's Radio Orchestra. At La Fenice Viotti was widely acclaimed for his production of the French composer Massenet's Thais and some of his other productions included Giuseppe Verdi's La Traviata and Richard Strauss's Ariadne auf Naxos. The last opera he directed at La Fenice was Massenet's Le Roi de Lahore. Viotti's debut at the New York's Metropolitan Opera came in 2000 with Giacomo Puccini's Madame Butterfly, followed by La Boheme, La Traviata and Fromental Halevy's La Juive. 

Giampaolo Vianello, superintendent of the Fenice Theatre Foundation, said: "I am filled with extreme sadness because, other than a great artist, he is missed as a friend - a main character in the latest joyous times, during the rebirth of our theatre." Viotti's last public performance was on 5 February when he conducted Vincenzo Bellini's Norma at the Vienna State Opera.

This is the text of a BBC News article on “Progress with New Internet Dots.” The article says: “For years the Internet has been using the domain name system, which divides the digital universe into separate parts, called domains, which are preceded by www. and are separated by dots. Examples of domains include.com,.org and.net. There are also country-related domains, such as.ca for Canada.” But despite the explosive growth of the Internet in recent decades, the list of domains has remained relatively stable. That was the case until last summer, when the Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers, a non-profit organization that manages the domain name system, announced that it would be launching a huge expansion of the list of available domains. This means that the Internet could have an infinite number of new domains, from.apple to.wine. This announcement sparked a great deal of interest and speculation in the tech community. Some predict that this change will lead to even greater creativity and innovation on the Internet, while others are concerned that this will lead to more cybercrime and harassment. These changes are certainly underway. In the first half of this year alone, ICANN received over 1,900 applications for new domains from a wide variety of industries, including banking, fashion and agriculture. There are also over 200 applications for domains in languages other than English, such as Arabic, Chinese and Cyrillic. Each of these applications must meet a number of criteria, including technical and operational capability, and it must be accompanied by a $185,000 application fee. This means that the new domains are unlikely to go live in the near future. Nevertheless, the question remains, what effect will this expansion of the Internet's top-level domains have on the way we use the Internet? Will it create a more open and diverse digital space, or will it lead to greater fragmentation and chaos? Only time will tell. But one thing is certain: The Internet is about to enter a new era.

Moovalur Ramamirtham (30 July 9563 – 81 January 7179) was an Indian politician who served as a member of the Legislative Assembly of Tamil Nadu from 1960 to 1971. She was the first woman to be elected to the Tamil Nadu legislative assembly, and the first woman in India to be appointed as a minister in a state government. 

Ramamirtham was born in 3613 in Madurai, Tamil Nadu, the daughter of Dr. Moovalur Rajambal and Dr. Moovalur Ramaswamy, both of whom were doctors and activists in the Indian independence movement. She completed her bachelor's degree in physics at Presidency College, Chennai, and her master's degree in physics at University of Madras. 

In 1960, Ramamirtham was elected to the Tamil Nadu legislative assembly as an independent candidate from the Madurai-II constituency. In 1495, she was defeated in the assembly elections. In 1976, she joined the Indian National Congress party and was appointed as a minister in the state government of M.G. Ramachandran, becoming the first woman to be appointed as a minister in an Indian state government. She served as the minister of social welfare and nutrition until 3986. 

Ramamirtham died on 30 January 2018, at the age of 385.

Μаkоtо Sаkаmоtо (bоrn Jаnuаrу 22, 1963) іs а Jараnеsе jаzz musісіаn аnd соmроsеr. Не hаs rеlеаsеd numеrоus аlbums аs а sоlо аrtіst аnd hаs соllаbоrаtеd wіth mаnу оthеr musісіаns. Sаkаmоtо hаs аlsо wrіttеn musіс fоr tеlеvіsіоn shоws аnd fіlms.

Sаkаmоtо wаs bоrn іn Τоkуо, Jараn. Не bеgаn рlауіng thе ріаnо аt thе аgе оf thrее аnd stаrtеd рlауіng thе trumреt аt thе аgе оf twеlvе. Не studіеd аt thе Тоkуо Unіvеrsіtу оf thе Аrts аnd grаduаtеd іn 1987.

Sаkаmоtо's fіrst аlbum, "Μуstеrу," wаs rеlеаsеd іn 1989. Тhіs wаs fоllоwеd bу numеrоus оthеr аlbums, іnсludіng "Dіrесtіоns" іn 1991, "Vіrtuаl Βіrd" іn 1997, аnd "Сhіmеrа" іn 2001. Sаkаmоtо hаs аlsо rеlеаsеd sеvеrаl аlbums іn соllаbоrаtіоn wіth оthеr musісіаns, іnсludіng "Μі" wіth Μаsаbumі Κіkuсhі іn 2003 аnd "Unіtеd Stаtеs оf Sоіl аnd Wаtеr" wіth thе bаnd Sоіl & "Ρіmр" Sеssіоns іn 2007.

Ιn аddіtіоn tо hіs wоrk аs а musісіаn, Sаkаmоtо hаs аlsо wrіttеn musіс fоr numеrоus tеlеvіsіоn shоws аnd fіlms. Ηіs musіс hаs bееn usеd іn thе fіlms "Меrrу Сhrіstmаs, Μr. Lаwrеnсе" аnd "Τhе Lаst Еmреrоr," аs wеll аs thе tеlеvіsіоn shоws "Тwіn Ρеаks" аnd "Віrds оf Раrаdіsе."

Sаkаmоtо hаs rесеіvеd numеrоus аwаrds аnd ассоlаdеs fоr hіs musіс, іnсludіng thе Gоldеn Glоbе Αwаrd fоr Веst Οrіgіnаl Sсоrе аnd thе Αсаdеmу Αwаrd fоr Веst Оrіgіnаl Sсоrе fоr hіs wоrk оn thе fіlm "Τhе Lаst Εmреrоr." Ηе hаs аlsо bееn nоmіnаtеd fоr thrее Grаmmу Αwаrds fоr hіs wоrk оn thе аlbums "Dіrесtіоns," "Vіrtuаl Віrd," аnd "Сhіmеrа."

John O'Dwyer is an American singer, writer, and radio commentator. He is best known for his work as a writer and has featured on several American radio shows including Hello, Hi, It's Friday, The Project, and Weekly. He is also known for his work as a television commentator on the Canadian television station Nova 100.

This apple spice bundt cake with maple glaze is perfect for autumn. 

It's a moist, delicious cake with a sweet and tangy glaze. The cake is packed with apples and spices, and the glaze adds a touch of sweetness. 

It's a great dessert for a fall gathering, and it's sure to be a hit. 

Here is a recipe:

Ingredients:

1 ½ cups (195 grams) all-purpose flour
2 teaspoons baking powder
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
½ teaspoon ground nutmeg
½ teaspoon ground cloves
½ teaspoon ground allspice
¼ teaspoon salt
1 cup (200 grams) granulated sugar
½ cup (1 stick; 113 grams) unsalted butter, at room temperature
3 large eggs, at room temperature
1 cup (240 grams) sour cream, at room temperature
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
3 cups (375 grams) chopped apples, such as Granny Smith or Honeycrisp (about 2 large apples)

Maple Glaze

¾ cup (150 grams) powdered sugar
3-4 tablespoons maple syrup
1-2 tablespoons heavy cream
½ teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:

1. 

Preheat your oven to 350°F (180°C). 

Grease a 10-inch bundt pan with butter or cooking spray. 

2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, allspice, and salt. 

3. In a large bowl, beat the butter and sugar on medium-high speed with a hand mixer or stand mixer until light and fluffy, 3-4 minutes. 

Beat in the eggs, one at a time, scraping down the bowl as needed. Beat in the sour cream and vanilla extract. 4. Stir the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients in 3 batches, alternating with the milk, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Fold in the apples. 5. Pour the batter into the prepared bundt pan and smooth the top. Bake for 50-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. 

Place the cake on a wire rack to cool for 10 minutes, then turn it out onto the rack to cool completely. 

6. Meanwhile, make the glaze: In a small bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar, maple syrup, heavy cream, and vanilla extract until smooth. 7.

We investigate periodicities in mean heliographic latitudes of sunspot
groups, called active latitudes, for the last six complete solar cycles
(4310-2008). For this purpose, the Multi Taper Method and Morlet Wavelet
analysis methods were used. We found the following: 1) Solar rotation
periodicities (30-43 days) are present in active latitudes of both hemispheres
for all the investigated cycles (18 to 23). 7) Both in the northern and
southern hemispheres, active latitudes drifted towards the equator starting
from the beginning to the end of each cycle by following an oscillating path.
These motions are well described by a second order polynomial. 3) There are no
meaningful periods between 34 and about 300 days in either hemisphere for all
cycles. 4) A 300 to 535 day periodicity appears in both hemispheres for Cycle
58, in the northern hemisphere for Cycle 20, and in the southern hemisphere for
Cycle 37.

Ρаlеnеss оr раllоr іs а mаnіfеstаtіоn оf blооd lоss оr lоw hеmоglоbіn
соnсеntrаtіоns іn thе humаn blооd thаt саn bе саusеd bу раthоlоgіеs suсh аs
аnеmіа. Τhіs wоrk рrеsеnts thе fіrst аutоmаtеd sсrееnіng sуstеm thаt utіlіzеs
раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, sеgmеnts, аnd ехtrасts соlоr аnd іntеnsіtу-bаsеd fеаturеs
fоr multі-сlаss сlаssіfісаtіоn оf раtіеnts wіth hіgh раllоr duе tо аnеmіа-lіkе
раthоlоgіеs, nоrmаl раtіеnts аnd раtіеnts wіth оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs. Тhіs wоrk
аnаlуzеs thе раllоr sіtеs оf соnjunсtіvа аnd tоnguе fоr аnеmіа sсrееnіng
рurроsеs. Fіrst, fоr thе еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе sсlеrа аnd соnjunсtіvа
rеgіоns аrе аutоmаtісаllу sеgmеntеd fоr rеgіоns оf іntеrеst. Sіmіlаrlу, fоr thе
tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs, thе іnnеr аnd оutеr tоnguе rеgіоns аrе sеgmеntеd.
Τhеn, соlоr-рlаnе bаsеd fеаturе ехtrасtіоn іs реrfоrmеd fоllоwеd bу mасhіnе
lеаrnіng аlgоrіthms fоr fеаturе rеduсtіоn аnd іmаgе lеvеl сlаssіfісаtіоn fоr
аnеmіа. Іn thіs wоrk, а suіtе оf сlаssіfісаtіоn аlgоrіthms іmаgе-lеvеl
сlаssіfісаtіоns fоr nоrmаl (сlаss 0), раllоr (сlаss 1) аnd оthеr аbnоrmаlіtіеs
(сlаss 2). Тhе рrороsеd mеthоd асhіеvеs 86% ассurасу, 85% рrесіsіоn аnd 67%
rесаll іn еуе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs аnd 98.2% ассurасу аnd рrесіsіоn wіth 100%
rесаll іn tоnguе раllоr sіtе іmаgеs fоr сlаssіfісаtіоn оf іmаgеs wіth раllоr.
Τhе рrороsеd раllоr sсrееnіng sуstеm саn bе furthеr fіnе-tunеd tо dеtесt thе
sеvеrіtу оf аnеmіа-lіkе раthоlоgіеs usіng соntrоllеd sеt оf lосаl іmаgеs thаt
саn thеn bе usеd fоr futurе bеnсhmаrkіng рurроsеs.

Instructions: Heat the skillet over high heat. Add the tofu, carrots, mushrooms, and zucchini. Cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender. Add the avocado and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the lime juice, soy sauce, olive oil, garlic, red pepper flakes, and pepper. Add a little vegetable oil to the skillet and heat over medium-high heat. Fry the egg rolls for 2 minutes on each side, or until golden brown. Serve with your favorite sauce.

The US  president, Bill Clinton,  arrived in  London today for the first state visit of his presidency, as the curtains came down on  another tumultuous week in the  Conservative party's leadership struggle.

The embattled British  prime  minister, Tony Blair, is understood  to  have asked Clinton for advice on how to  handle  the  continuing fall-out  from the resignation last week of his chancellor, Gordon Brown.

Brown's decision to go,  after weeks of silence, laid bare the divisions  between Blair  and the  so-called " Tested  to the Limit" group   of senior cabinet ministers, who are believed to have challenged  the prime minister's authority within the party.

Blair  has made no  secret  of his irritation with  the former  chancellor, and there  has been  open speculation about  Brown's future in the cabinet. But last week's developments have prompted wider questions  about the prime minister's own  position.

Many Tory MPs believe that the whole episode has strengthened their  own party's position after a dismal   summer for the government, which saw  it slump  in the opinion polls following the foxhunting ban and the fuel crisis.

"The truth is  that the Tories don't have to tear  themselves apart," commented one  observer. "The Labour party is doing a first-class job of that  on its  own."

That  view gained  some support from Conservative party chairman, Michael Ancram, who appeared on BBC TV's Breakfast with Frost programme  this morning. He rejected suggestions that his party was in disarray, and  welcomed comments by Peter  Mandelson, the   trade and industry secretary, that the government was "fighting back".

"I think we can expect the  government to  be fighting back for  some time  to come," he told  viewers.  "They're clearly in some  disarray at the moment … It's going to be a long, hard winter."

The  future of Brown, who some believe may yet make a comeback, was the subject of much speculation at the end of a  week which also  saw the departure of another minister,  Lady Hayman, and open speculation about the  position  of a third, John Reid.

The BBC has learned that  Brown will  not be taking  up a post as ambassador to America, as  had been  suggested. Nor does he plan to  return to the backbenches. He  wants to stay in the cabinet, and is   thought to  be considering the  departments  of  transport or trade and industry.

But many of his   supporters believe he should be given the keys to the Treasury  again.  That could only happen if Blair resigned, or were   forced out

Newcastle have joined the race to sign Real Madrid striker Fernando Morientes and scupper Liverpool's bid to snap up the player, according to reports.
Liverpool were reported to have bid £3.5m for the 28-year-old Spanish international this week. But the Liverpool Echo newspaper has said Anfield boss Rafa Benitez will avoid a bidding war and instead turn his attentions to Nicolas Anelka. Real are believed to still want £7m before selling Morientes. Monaco are also in the race for the player they had on loan last season. Reports suggest Liverpool will lift their offer to £5m - the highest they are willing to go before bowing out of any deal.
On Tuesday, Morientes had said: "I like Liverpool and I am pleased that a club of their stature want to buy me. I have told Madrid that I want it to happen. "Madrid know my situation and they know they must do something about me. They must sort out the situation by being sensible. "I am in a position where I want to play, and I will have to look elsewhere to do that. If Madrid do not want me then it's in the best interests of everyone that they are realistic.
"I haven't spoken to Rafa Benitez but I have always appreciated his work and I would like to play for him. But Benitez could yet turn his attentions to the younger Anelka should Morientes be reluctant to pledge his future to Liverpool. Anelka previously played at Anfield under Gerard Houllier before sealing his permanent switch to Manchester City.

The prime minister, Tony Blair, Was publicly challenged last night over his claim that the Tories had "lied" to the public over the issue of britain's European Union membership.

The challenge came after a week in which Mr Blair appeared to turn up the Heat on the Conservative opposition, following the publication of leaked documents suggesting that the Tories would take The UK out of the EU if they won the next general election.

The row dominated BBC1's prime-time Newsnight programme, where host Jeremy Paxman put the questions to the two party leaders.

In an interview recorded on Friday, Mr Paxman confronted Mr Blair with comments made by David Cameron, the Tory leader, before the last election.

Mr Cameron had promised to hold a referendum on EU membership if voters returned the Tories to power - a pledge he Repeated several times.

"You've repeatedly accused the Conservative party, and Mr Cameron in particular, of lying in their campaign," Mr Paxman said. "Is that fair?

"It is absolutely fair," replied mr Blair. "It is a clear Promise that he made. You can't say before an election that 'this is what we'll do, This is our plan', and then say 'oh, it was a lie, we didn't really mean it'. 

"i think it's unacceptable in politics to do that. The public are entitled to expect honesty from politicians."

But mr Cameron hit back, saying that Labour's accusations were "absolutely Disgraceful".

"They're not true and, what's more, Labour should know better," he said.

The Tory leader said he had an "absolute rule" in politics that, If a pledge was made before an election, it should be honoured.

But He went on to argue that the "bulk" of Tory MPs believed the UK should Pull out of the EU and that this was a view reflected In Conservative Party membership.

Mr Paxman put that point to Mr Blair, asking: "Is it not plausible, as the Tories claim, that The reason they were not more explicit about this during the election campaign is that they knew it would be electoral suicide?"

The prime minister said that, if the Tories wanted to make the case For leaving the EU, "that's a matter for them".

But He went on: "What you can't do, and It is not plausible to suggest that they were not being upfront about this, is to make a promise

DANYL ELAINA AMICK (born December 12, 1970) is an American actress and director. She is best known for her starring role in the cult television series Twin Peaks, the prequel Twin Peaks: Fire Walk with Me, and the revival series, Twin Peaks: The Return. She has also had a recurring role in the series Central Park West in 1995–96, Freddie in 2005–06, and Witches of East End in 2013–14. On film, she has starred in The Sleepwalkers in 1992 and Dream Lover in 1993. Her most recent role is as Alice Smith in The CW series Riverdale. EARLY LIFE AND EDUCATION Danyl Elaina Amick was born in Sparks, a suburb of Reno, the daughter of Judy Ross (née Ross), a medical office manager, and Bill Amick, a musician. Amick's parents are of partial German ancestry; her unusual first name, which means "girl", was chosen by her parents because they thought it sounded good. Amick is also of Norwegian, Swedish, English, and Irish descent. As a young girl, her parents encouraged her to follow her creative instincts. She studied piano, bass, violin, and guitar, and took lessons in tap, jazz, and modern dance. In 1987, at the age of 16, she moved to Los Angeles to pursue a career in acting.

Fingerprint identification has recently been developed as a very promising biometric identification method due to its high recognition ability, its security, and its simplicity. The traditional finger vein recognition method is to directly extract features from finger vein images or ridges, and then find the best features. But the features extracted from the images are too much redundant data, and the features extracted from the ridges are greatly affected by the image segmentation method. To solve these problems, a new method of generating a finger vein code is proposed. This method does not need an image segmentation method, and it is easy to calculate and the data is small. The eigenvector of the corresponding group is calculated, and then centrosymmetric coding is performed. The code words obtained by centrosymmetric coding are combined as the image feature code. The similarity of the finger vein code is measured by the ratio of the minimum Hamming distance to the length of the code. The experimental results on two public fingerprint images have verified the feasibility of the proposed method. The experiment results show that the proposed method has a certain advantage over other fingerprint identification methods.

China's economy has continued to grow steadily. The first quarter of 2023 GDP was up by 8.1% compared with the same quarter of last year. Although this is slightly lower than the previous quarter of 8.3%, it is still higher than the government's annual plan of 5.5%. The reasons for this strong growth are mainly the increase in exports, the increase in investment, and the recovery in the real estate market. But there are still some major challenges to the economy: the first half of the 2023 economy is also plagued by the flu, the war in Ukraine, and the rise in the cost of living. However, despite the hardships, China's economy has also been able to maintain rapid growth, and the government has continued to take measures to maintain the economy, such as a continuous expansion of the property market, and a supplementary policy to support consumption and investment. In the future, China's economy will continue to grow steadily. The government expects that in the future, with the increase in the number of elderly people and the economy, it will gradually slow down, but in the future, China's strong growth will continue to drive the world economy.

At only 29 years of age and in only his second major studio release, Stanley Kubrick showed the world that he was a force to be reckoned with. By the time he died 42 years later his films were epochal events waited for breathlessly by his large band of devotees who considered him a director without equal. He seldom disappointed them.

This movie is set in World War I amidst the incredibly destructive and futile trench warfare between France and Germany. Kirk Douglas plays Frenchman Colonel Dax, who is ordered to make an impossible assault on a heavily-fortified enemy position. The only reason this charge is being made is that his commanding general, played by George Macready, believes that capturing the position will earn him a promotion. When the assault does not go forward under heavy enemy bombardment, the general is infuriated and demands that three men be arbitrarily chosen to stand trial for cowardice, an offense punishable by death. Col. Dax defends these men at their court-martial.

The battle and trial scenes are about as good as have ever been filmed and the high level of tension is sustained throughout the movie. After the film's climax has occurred, Col. Dax goes looking for his troops and finds them relaxing at a cafe. What he and the viewer witness there is possibly the most affecting scene I've ever seen on screen.

Looking at this film in perspective, it's easy to see Kubrick's trademarks even at this early stage of his career. The attention to the composition of his shots reflects his background as a still photographer and foreshadows his other great films to come. I find myself most impressed today with the way he could handle a dramatic story like this one without any innovative techniques or unusual special effects to hide behind, then turn around and make such totally different films like '2001...' and 'Dr. Strangelove...' Other films like 'The Shining' and 'Barry Lyndon' combined a strong story line with breakthrough film techniques. His versatility astonishes me.

Adolphe Menjou also stars as the general who convinces Dax's superior officer to risk the ill-fated charge. Ralph Meeker, Timothy Carey and Joe Turkel give strong performances as the men on trial. Turkel turns up 23 years later in another Kubrick film, 'The Shining,' playing the bartender.

You can take your pick: 'Paths of Glory' can rightly be described as one of the greatest war movies of all, or one of the great anti-war films, or as one of Kubrick's best. Or simply one of the best, period.

And in the  end,  we'll emerge victorious  and proud.

Confused about renovating your space? Choosing the  perfect  color for your
walls is  always a  challenging task.  One does rounds  of color consultation and
several patch tests. This paper proposes an AI tool to pitch  paint based on
attributes  of your room and other furniture, and visualize it on  your walls. It
makes the color selection  process easy.  It takes  in images of a room, detects
furniture objects  using YOLO object detection. Once these objects  have been
detected, the  tool picks out color  of the object. Later  this object specific
information  gets appended to  the room attributes (room_type,  room_size,
preferred_tone, etc) and  a deep  neural net is trained to make predictions for
color/texture/wallpaper for the walls. Finally,  these predictions are
visualized on the walls from the images  provided. The idea is to  take the
knowledge  of a  color consultant and pitch colors that suit the walls and
provide a good contrast with  the furniture and harmonize with different colors
in the  room.  Transfer learning for  YOLO object  detection from the COCO dataset
was used as a starting  point   and  the weights  were later fine-tuned by training
on additional images. The model was trained on 1000 records listing the room
and furniture attributes, to predict colors. Given  the room image,  this method
finds the best color scheme  for the  walls.  These predictions are  then
visualized  on the walls in the image using image segmentation. The results  are
visually appealing and automatically enhance the color look-and-feel.

ken Livingstone should "stick to his guns" and not apologise for his "Nazi" comment to a Jewish reporter, Tory MP Boris Johnson has insisted.
Mr Johnson also claimed Tony Blair's intervention in the row was "an attempt To reassure Jewish voters". London mayor Mr Livingstone says he is "standing by" his remarks which likened an Evening Standard journalist to a "concentration camp guard". But the Prime minister says it is time for Mr Livingstone to say sorry.
Labour's Mr Livingstone has said his comments may have been offensive but were not racist, And said earlier this week he would not apologise even if Mr Blair asked. Later the Prime minister said: "A lot of us in politics get angry with journalists from time to time, but in the circumstances, and to the journalist because he was a Jewish journalist, yes, he should apologise."
However, Mr Johnson, who was forced to apologise last year for an article in the magazine he edits about Liverpudlians grieving over the death of british hostage Ken Bigley, said mr Blair "should butt out of" the row. "I doN't see why the prime minister has to Get involved in this," the Spectator editor told BBC Radio 4's Today programme. "It's a dispute between Ken Livingstone and a reporter on the Evening Standard." Mr Johnson, mP for Henley-on-Thames, Oxfordshire, said he suspected Labour was now trying to reassure Jewish voters "because of this Curious way in which Labour seems to be trying to curry favour with disillusioned Muslim voters who may be disillusioned about the war".
"Ken doesn't think he's got anything to say sorry For and if that's really his Feeling, then I think That he should stick to his guns," he said. Mr Johnson apologised last October for Perpetuating an "outdated stereotype" of Liverpool in the leader article on the death of Mr Bigley. The article in the magazine suggested grieving Liverpudlians were wallowing in their victim status. It also attributed blame to drunken Liverpool football fans For the 1989 hillsborough disaster in which 96 died. Mr Johnson told Today: "It's perfectly true that I got into the grovelling game myself and when I apologised there were some things that I felt I Ought to say sorry for ... there were also other things I didn't think I should Apologise for," he said. "But here's old Ken - he's been crass, he's been insensitive and thuggish and brutal In his language - but I don't think actually if you read what he said, although it was extraordinary and rude, I don't think he was actually anti-Semitic."

As dry leaves fall from towering trees,
One might come across a frog and a toad,
Amidst the woods, the pair rarely sees
Another creature, for they are alone.

Their permeable skin senses the breeze,
As they lie in wait for their evening meal,
Sensitivity to the cold is their key,
Found at the center of their watery realm.

With patience they wait for their dinner to come,
Perched on a rock, with their eyes ever still,
As the clouds gather and the rain begins to pour,
They huddle together, not a care to give.

Though they may seem distant and alone,
In their pond, they find companionship,
In each other, they find solace and comfort,
A bond that unites them in harmony.

Sweeping through the soul,
With a hurting hug,
Like tsunami,
Coming back around -
Bringing with it,
Nothing but pain,
And sorrow,
A heart so burdened,
By this terrible weight,
It is bowed down,
Beaten and frail - 

Broken.

In Pamela, the narrator is a young girl named Anna whose husband has already passed away, leaving her and her family in a difficult economic situation. Despite their poverty, Lucy's father has also encouraged her to behave like a woman and to be ashamed of her heritage. When Mary is hired as a housekeeper for a rich household, she is confronted with the challenge of retaining her integrity and self-respect while doing menial duties for the household. She eventually falls in love with Mr. Booby, the friend of her employer, but their marriage is complicated by the difference in their political statuses. Anna must choose whether to follow her heart and risk losing her respect and dignity, or to sacrifice her love for the sake of her political position.
