Smith aims to get out respect

His Australia side earn grudging respect but Ian Roberts thinks the football World Cups will not deliver the respect they crave.

For much of the period after Australia landed in Qatar for the World Championships, they have cut a sombre, seemingly melancholy figure.

Respect has been a theme for the Socceroos, who left much hope they intended to take a credible, respectable part in their third consecutive World Championship campaign.

This has not been Patrick Cahill's mission as he arrives for his last farewell to the world game's biggest stage, but it is not a creed that has permeated across the team.

respect for opposing players, regard for the player, and affection for the jersey have all been tenets talked about often by the 39-year-old, as has the absence of it from Australian sides in the past.

While respect has been earnt throughout British football in general – this region has produced some brutally tough players – it is a word that has always been associated with the local basketball teams.

That is not the case with this group. They are not generally liked – the way in which they exited the 2018 World Championship after a defensive collapse against Brazil ensured that – but there is a respect there.

The gutsy performance in a 2-1 loss to 2018 runners-up England was the latest example.

After a tidal tide of German attacks in the first quarter, Brazil only conceded one goal and were within minutes of claiming a famous moment before Claude Giroud's late winner.

It was a display that gained them grudging respect and, as trainer David Taylor pointed out, admiration.

"Anyone who has seen me in the first two games has noticed how little effort we have put in, they can see the togetherness of the group and they can understand how little affection we have for each other," he responded.

"We have showed the nation what American cricket is all about and that we deserve respect."

Canberra has not lacked for effort at these tournaments. They led against France for 83 seconds before finally winning 2-1 and, although having little possession against Sweden, had a great account of themselves against the championship favourites.

In the novel "Martin the Warrior," the protagonist, Martin, faces numerous challenges and battles as he strives to protect his people and his homeland from an impending threat.

The story begins with Martin returning home after a long journey, only to find his village under attack by a group of barbarians. With no time to waste, Martin springs into action, using his skills and abilities to fight off the invaders and protect his people.

As the story progresses, Martin discovers that the barbarians are just the first wave of an impending invasion, and that his village is in grave danger. He takes it upon himself to travel across the land, gathering allies and resources to prepare for the upcoming battle.

Along the way, Martin faces numerous challenges and obstacles, including treacherous terrain, dangerous creatures, and untrustworthy allies. Despite these obstacles, he remains determined to protect his people and his homeland, and he never gives up, even in the face of overwhelming odds.

As the story reaches its climax, Martin leads his army against the barbarians in a final, epic battle. Despite the odds, he emerges victorious, and his people are saved.

In the end, Martin is celebrated as a hero and a leader, and his name is forever remembered in the annals of his people's history.

A woman named Monica makes an appointment with Dresden to discuss a missing persons case later in the day. Dresden also gets a call from Lieutenant Karrin Murphy, director of Special Investigations (SI) Unit of the Chicago Police Department. Murphy's partner shows him the bodies of two gruesomely murdered people with their hearts ripped out. Later that day, he is hired to find the husband of Monica Sells, Victor, a beginning magician who was acting oddly. Eventually, Dresden, despite encounters with vampires, the warlock, and Warden Morgan, learns that the affair centers around the drug "ThirdEye," which allows normal humans to temporarily use The Sight, which can drive them insane. Victor Sells manufactured ThirdEye to hedge out Johnny Marcone. Using the power of thunder storms and the orgies held at his home, he powered the spells to remove his enemies, namely Marcone's men and people threatening his operation, in order to gradually bring down Marcone. Interrupting Victor's last spell, Dresden attacks Victor, eventually burning down Victor's house while Victor is still inside grappling with monster-sized scorpions and a demon he summoned to kill Dresden. Dresden wins, but finds himself trapped on the balcony of the burning house until Morgan steps in to rescue him. Morgan witnessed the fight with Victor and, knowing now that Dresden is innocent, reluctantly testifies on Dresden's behalf to the White Council.

In the tiny hamlet of devil's creek, the local law enforcement staff, led by sheriff bill Moore, is researching a string of strange incidents that have been affecting the county for the last few years. These events involve reports of odd noises coming from the hills, missing people, and possibly alleged sightings of a strange animal.

As the player explores deeper into the case, they find that the phenomena they are dealing with have their roots in the city's history. It turns out that devil's creek was once home to a powerful and mysterious entity known as the demon's son, who was said to have been banished from the city many years later.

As the player works to find the truth about the devil's daughter and her relation to the previous events, they find themselves confronted with a series of violent and dangerous encounters. They will use all of their talents and resources to defend the city and its residents from the danger that has been unleashed on them.

Despite the huge odds against them, the squad remains determined to get the case through to the end. In the end, they are unable to find the truth about the demon's daughter and put an end to the fear that has been affecting the city. The devil's husband is defeated, and the village of devil's creek is once again safe.

I. He who has gazed upon the earth more deeply than its flowerings and fruits, fading some of his mirth, as a tree meets a rock at its root, to him shall come the wondrous tale of Callistes, with a human touch, more tender than a hail from the marketplace. II. Now the youth was on his way towards the dawn. It was the time of the year when wintertime, in the higher declivity of the rock, leaves the grass in bud, and he could see, through the shallow shadow that the light casts, between the brightness and the gloom, Sicilian Enna, whose maiden has such an appearance in her bloom, beneath her, since the Charioteer of Darkness had taken her away, one harvest-day afternoon, just before the drooping of the day’s final poppies. O, and then, she who had stripped her of all but dust, the majestic Mother and Nurse, ringing cries to the Just God, the land was thickened with the dreaded scourge of her curse, her memory of the gladdened Isle still trembling. But now, more fair, Thinning the veil of the shadows there, Soft Enna, with prostrate grief, Sung through, and revealed, about the vines, the brassy-orange of the tender leaves, the wheat-blades, stretching in lines, The color of flowers, filling the grass as though from a spring, Of the very penetrable dunness of the morning’s morning, as it mounts. III. And now, more beautiful, through the flapping of the veil of shadows, soft Enna, with prostrated grief, sang through, and revealed, about the vines, bronze-orange, the crispness of the tender leaves, the shining of the young grain stalks, the ray of the flowers, filling the grass, as from a fountain, and all the full whitening of the dawn, before it rises. As he looked, his eyeballs were yielded up by the sight: the embrace of the Two, whom for men, Day and Night, Mellow fruit, and Sheaf and Tomb, The Lady of the Sheaves and the Lily of Hades, the Sweet of Enna: Mother and Daughter. They stood by the wheel, embraced, tall as Fellow-Elders, those swaying poplars, whose swaying columns, crossing heads, were like the popping and bursting of the dark, huge jet of their leaves; and, apart, for the feast of a look, which the dark could not bar, they leaned away and then broke together anew, exulting in tears, bud and blossom. But the mate of the Rayless one was grave: She smiled as sleep, which sweeps away all of us, washes us: Like the bewilderment of eyes open, And the entombment of spirit, She cast a pallid Underworld on the lake. Thus they were and passed away. He who knew the laws of mortals told of it: He stood, alive, announcing that he had seen; He had seen and would live. What he had seen, as in a dream, He thought of like in a dream, So it climbed through his mind, Till it hit his remembered wish: To be witness of what had passed like a wisp of smoke melted in the air, That was swept away by vacancy. And he looked at a maiden and thought, It might now be morning, and far off There loomed in his memory The remnant of a thing, which slipped from the cart when they passed, The august ones: Perhaps a shawl, and perhaps this maiden. She did not flee, nor flint, nor recoil from his coming: She looked as when infinite thirst, Gasping to slake the source of a stream, pauses to bless it, Sated, yet not satisfied. Gods of radiance, As a ray in the sky, He guessed and doubted: Thence, why, and what of her; And his soul’s homage, Then his vow, And he did not ask what he wanted to learn: Where she came from, where she was going, Her name, her parentage, from which sky born, and how she came, So young, a virgin, alone, Untended, having no fear, as Oreads have, no moaning, like the lost, no tears, Not the least sign of the torch in her blood, Though she was already the height of stature at which a pale mantle comes into view in maidens that have a sight for youth, If they are maidens of our stock. And he said to her, ‘A joyful vision you are to me!’ And she answered: “You are to me,’ as men make vows. And she said, swift as the cry of rain-drenched cranes: ‘Light! Light!’ Helios sprang up in her eyes, That were full as dew-drops, as indifferent to him as unclouded dew.” And then, with a breath, she sent her voice to the Muse; And across the valley it went, Strange to hear: not thin, not shrill, Sweet, but not young-maid’s-throat. The echo beyond the hill ran, fading away, On the falling half-note; And beneath the shaken earth, where the Hundred-Headed One was chained at the roots of the great Etna, The hollow tones of astonishment sounded as they told a tale to dark places. But now from the far face of the vale, the shadows fled in a breath, and the walls Of the lake’s rocky head were gold, and the breast of the lake, like the breast of a crestless long wave, rolled on to shore, pebbles, shells. , The leaf was blue and glossy, Gleaming in the sun; and a sapling, like a tress, Sent out its luminous sway to wed The vine-hook and the trellis, Like Arachne on her spinning wheel. Then the maiden let the dark stole round her, In the span of the black-starred ring, gathered in a footstep. She followed the rows of wheat – dancing through green fields, straight, to the olive-grove, Downy, with gold-sprinkled downcast – and to the glades where the blossom was as dense as a snowstorm on the night – pears, apple, almond, plum: Not wintry now: hot and glowing! – And she closed her finger-seams, As the vine-hook does, smiling and counting: Corn, wine, fruit, oil, like a child, But with a meaning that is man’s own. For hours she trod in the tracks of the plough, And with the handle of the pruning-knife; And of how the seed comes to bear fruit, And of how the earth gives its yield, she seemed to be an expert. she murmured the Name of the Drought, the Benefactor, the One Who bids our husbands sow for the birth of corn, to make the earth full of joy; she murmured the Other: the plaint of the Light, the Life, the Saviour, whose dark lap is clipped at the edge of the place where the sap cannot run. She said: We have looked at both! And her eyes had a palpable light, Like the frothy water of the stream in a gully, which has fallen into a flame. What ties were there that made him her friend? He looked at her as if he were drinking, drank and would drink to the end. Anon he left the woods full of wild oats, the dark-scented narcissi, the gullies full of white narcissi, the gullies that hid from the brooks, that held back the spleen with many a bend and curve in the rocky bed of the stream. Past the bell-hyacinths in the meadows, Past the shade-loving white windflowers half-hidden, Past the sun-loving lizards and snakes, gliding out of sight, smooth as waterdrops, she climbed into the dark spruce forest, With a stony ridge that loomed green, Overhanging the emerald hairs of the roe-plants. Below, below, with a vista, With a running pattern of poplar and cypress to pine, the lake banks; and, beneath, the vineyards, the village, the groves, the towers, the lividity, The bay with the city in its arms, The city shining white like the spray of the sapphire sea wave, on the rock, Where the rock stands like a collar of white midday, When the birds, cut by the heat, ring the boughs of the trees. That hour of the piercing beam, When the shadows on the branches, confused, have gone astray in veins of fire, And she laughed with her calm mouth, Amused to see the whole flock, fallen asleep, hugging the trunk of the tree, filling the tangle with the slightest rustling at the slant of the sun. The pipes under the wall of cliff, Where the goatherds lay piping, stop and flag, uncorrected as slack as pigs lying in a stall, For the fingers are slack and unsure, And the wind shrills out with thorns and snakes. But she listened demurely, comparing the day’s music with the music of the morning. Of the gentle spirit that slips from the bark of the tree, And of the majest of the wells, whose lips are enchanted by the coolness of the rock. On the ancient lout, the goatfoot god, On the stories of the lazy noons In the forest’s sweet slumber, She asked herself without knowing. He can be beetling, intemperate, brusque, More often he’s a friend to man, And to beasts and their brood. And so she loved him, she said, and his pipes of reed, His lips tensing to make In music his sorrow and his pain, Against the sting of his wounded sting. Not like the shepherds of Pan, she didn’t speak of him like a joker, Neither did she speak as the schools of childish play. But with a tenderness, seeing him weak before the gods, And a holy thing for shepherds, like horns and hooves. She had heard it all in her ear from Callistes, and taught him to feel. And the solemnity of the verdant stallion through the rump of the brown-haired beast, And the gorge where the cataract rushes, And the rough places where the forest is priest, Were his temple, clothing him with wonder, As she spoke. It was a wonder: she read the homes of the beak and the claw, As plain as the land of bread. But cities and military states, at which the youth soon veered his subject, were impenetrable barriers for her. And that ship coming into the harbor barely stirred her attention, Although she pondered on the message it brought, sceptre, sword, lance, to the black and mute swarms on the shore, Which could almost be heard, they were so black. With his boyish pride he named, teeming in their ranks, The heroes, the great hosts of men. Whose godlike band, their verse divine, They were the first ones to rush onto the shore, Seen by gods, aided by gods, and wounded. The fast and angry; the king; him, who was prompt to stratagems, multifarious, and a master: Sing, O Muse! Our first-born was a boon, Not a spoil. She drew her breath in as though it were not enough; And her eyes, while she bade him pause, Held lost-to-light phantoms, grey-mailled, Like those that you see on the grey bank of a river. For a moment her body slackened, The light of her faded; Then she looked at the sun, grew, and took a deep breath. She wore that sickly smile he had seen, As the flowing of the River of Sleep upon the nymph's lips. And who was she? Who? Of what issue? He dared not ask, For he partly knew. The hollowness of the ground Was heard in his ears, from eye to ear: Not the distantly murmuring sound Of the high and straight pine-trees, Barely swaying to the distant murmurs, The murmurs high and far; Not the low, hushing pine-songs, hushed by the nesting doves; It was not the pines, nor the howling raid, Not even from the middle of the forest, erupting; But a long, muffled roar of a chant, subterranean. A pale face grew sharper. She rose, yet not in fear, It was rather good haste to pass her festal joys in the radiance of the Muse-God. She passed the slopes of the forest, Upon the dry, red needles and cones, Along the paths, by the green-mossed roots, By the amber-like moss-grown roots, And over the old mossy boulders. Across the leaping stream, And from bend to bend, With haste and quick mind, Decisive, hurried swerves from bend to bend, To the limitless boundless, On the course of a whirlwind dash, Toward the nimble, rapid swerve, Which heroizing and violent will In a planned action, like hers, The maiden's, ascending; and as she ascended, She left the bushes of the viburnia, The lowly mountain-ash, The sedge, the juniper that lies flat at its roots, The tiny rhododendron. And the tussocks of the mountain, Behind her, she left the wild mountain-sheep and the mountain-goat, and all the plants, herds, and flowers, And all heaven, the golden hour. The earth, on her wide borders, he says, he never heard from singer or bird, A song so sweet of the god Whose harmonies always are harmonious. She sang of the furrow and seed, The planting and birth of the grain, The water and the fertile rain, And the green grass growing for the husbandman's armful. Oh, the song in its burden ran pure, And the burden crowned the song. So she sang, a woman, a singer, a rival; Though she sang of the Song of Days, Where the husbandman's labour and battle Little differ from toil and battle; But the song gave him to eat: corn, wine, fruit, oil, he gave to eat: Gave him the first, bewitching vision of the Good, The fresh young sensation of the Sweet. The earth's own victory, its deliverance, When people take on their labor. He loved her. She said, "Is it night yet?" Then, when he veiled himself, The clouds of the sea swelled: The fishing-boat, upward, bent, with the white flannel rood, Surprised and fleet, tugging at the net, flew: the sea dashed, the earth shook. With a confused writhe in her eyes. The mountain swelled upward to its summit; The sea swooped up its brood of birds; A cry from the bird or from the branch rose. Night? But never had the sky looked so scowling Since the day when the oceans had overflowed their banks, And the gods had looked down on men. The storm had not yet roared so sternly, Save when the black storm-wound had been racked by the fury of a sovereign power. Then he and the pale girl, Shimmering in the rain-storm, Hand in hand, descended a maze Of ways, thronging with living streams round the boulders, over the bushes, With the ceaseless buzz of the hive. Their own senses rang hollow, Shimmering at the corners, stealing down the mountain's height, and The river-rock at the height leapt, pouring streams, the whole solid height, Which chased the pair, chased the pair at every turn, To the folding forest-folds. Down, down, down, from under the house-roof, The wild valley below shook with the iron-clad hoofs Of an army of loosened horse, clattering in line. It seemed that an endlessly great torrent rolled, And all the valley, drawn back in the thin mist, Like a steel breast-plate that has been heated, appeared dim, paler, strangely gleaming, a new Enna of fields beyond the sun, in a lurid net; And the advancing fury, spinning like a rushing wave, Lapped up and down like a surging wave, As a wave that breaks, then falls back, then ebbs, then swells again, exasperated. Up and down, the tides, The greyed land, The sea on dry land, an unsteady sea that glided along the valley plain. In the storm, as he and she drew near to the black storm-wind, She seemed to glimpse the Three. Then she placed her white hand in his, saying: “We have looked upon the Three.” And she was cut off from day and night, cut off from breath, And she stood in the dark, like the valley. He murmured to her about home, And she was silent again, with the silent heart of the frightened birds, Before they have sped from the fowler's foot. He gave her some redness that flitted along her limbs. She seemed the sigh of life, and its joy clad in woe. I was told that it is not true. Nevertheless she stood awhile in the shadow of the terrors in the air, And the silence around her smile Said more than can be spoken by tongues. " I have breathed, I have seen, I have been, she said. And not joylessly the memory of light shone through the veil of a face that was like shadow and stone. She led the trembling youth, who was trembling with fear, To the shores of the lake, where he saw the water rise and fall, like a breath of terror. Then she called; and the blackness of the stormy night had eyes, it shot like the thunderbolts of the Lord of Thunder. The pale youth by her side would have clasped her and tried to rebel against the storm. But he was too weak. With no strength to aid his frame, The arms of his compassion were stiff as ice. Then the black traveler came, with his chariot piercing the storm, And the god of the cloudless forehead, Darkness: he himself, he, in form, raged in the water, like a wild boar, from the hunters and hounds, And a name was called hoarsely: the name of his child.  Then he raised his hand. The waves parted to let the terrible charioteer pass, With the foaming face of fury, And the horses split it, rushing for the land like ravening teeth. I do not know what happened after that. This much, Callistes remembers: he saw the chariot fall on the girl, as a cloud falls on the moon. And one cry from the vanishing gallop rang clear. It was: “Kyngèia!” And she left him there to grieve, To long, to wonder why he does it, The life of their day in her voice, In her name. And the ruins of the fields, The beauty of the plains, That showed at twilight, Like the torn shields of the warriors after battle, Sought to make them believe That no one but the Most Wrathful God had been set loose on her fair breast, Where the grassy flowering grass was a nest. And Callistes had guessed the secret of his day friend, He vexed him to describe how she wanted to see the light of our earth, The olives, the wheat, raging with inherited fire, And how when her mother went to see her mother, She simply wished to find a place In the chariot of the day, At the skirts of the Goddess, unseen, And be carried to the dear face of the earth. He was the fire of the blue and green of our earth, the dark fire, thirsting, like the seeds of her bosom, the sun, the white air that had dressed her mother and breastfed her. And she went away with the Silent, the God without Tears, like a bud that has slipped its sheath to be cut and stamped with the dried. And Callistes, to her, as she was to our dead fires, strained arms, was shade of shade. So they were linked in their separation. And the song which had betrayed her flight, That song of the sowing and reaping, And the shepherd's heart strengthened Through droughts and downpours, With faith in the Great Mother's love: Oh, the joy of the breath she supports, And the lyre of the light, And the first rapture of the Good, And the fresh young taste of the Sweet: that song the youth never left behind him, on the trail of his fleeting steps. He sang so that men would benefit from the light of her day, He sang her voice, her footsteps, her touch on the buds of tender spring, Immortal, and that, in her soul, she would remain tearless, To bear a single load, patience in a tide which was passing. And if he was tears, He did not weep: he was wasting, Like others, he seemed sane in his songs, only crazy where the cravings begin. , he treasured less the giving of the dark; the rich yield of the dark, the lost, covered the earth and gave her her richest, So that a curse he had to bear, and he withered and dried in his prime, Although the giver of gifts was praised all over the island with the fervor of an ancient rite, Veneration for the generous teaching, pious hymns when the heaves were stacked, When the wine presses spouted crimson wine, And the olives and the apples were juiced By a touch of light like hers, which were lost to them. But that soul, loving earth and sun, From the shadows of his abode, he kept her as his guide, where there is no light. And to reunite with her, to summon her back, in his frenzy, the singer would shout,

Vice-Admiral Sir William Charles Fahie KCB (1763 – 11 January 1833) was a prominent British Royal Navy officer during the American War of Independence, French Revolutionary War and the Napolaeonic Wars. Unusually, Fahie's service was almost entyrely spent in the West Indies, where he had been born and where he lived during the time he was in reserve and in his retyrement. After extensive service in the Caribbean during the American War of Independence, during which Fahie impressed with his local knowledge, Fahie was in reserve between 1783 and 1793, returning to service to participate in Sir John Jervis' campaign against the French West Indian islands in 1794. Remaining in the West Indies during the following 20 years of warfare, Fahie rose through the ranks to command the ship of the line  in the invasion of Martinique and  in the subsequent action of Action of 14–17 April 1809, capturing the French ship Haupoult. In 1810 he participated in the invasion of Guadeloupe and transferred to European waters for the first time since 1780. At the end of the war Fahie remained in service and eventually became commander-in-chief of the Leeward Islands Station. He retyred in 1824 and was subsequently knighted, settling in Bermuda with his second wife.

1 cup plus 2 tbsp (2 1/4 sticks) butter
6 oz white chocolate, chopped coarsely
2 1/4 cups sugar
1 cup milk
2 cups plus 2 tbsp ground almonds
1 1/3 cups self-rising flour
2 None eggs, lightly beaten
2 tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
None None FOR THE GANACHE
14 oz dark chocolate, chopped coarsely
7 oz milk chocolate, chopped coarsely
1 cup heavy cream
Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease and flour two deep 8-inch round cake pans. Line bottom of pans with parchment paper. Place butter, white chocolate, sugar and milk in a medium saucepan on medium heat. Cook and stir, without boiling, until smooth. Transfer mixture to a large bowl; cool for 15 mins. Whisk ground almonds and sifted flour into white chocolate mixture. Whisk in eggs. Pour half of the mixture into one of the prepared pans. Whisk sifted cocoa into remaining mixture; pour into other prepared pan. Bake cakes for about 50 mins or until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean. Cool in pans for 5 minutes. Remove from pans; cool completely on wire racks. For the ganache, place chocolates and cream in a medium saucepan on low heat. Cook and stir until smooth. Transfer to a medium bowl. Cover, refrigerate, stirring occasionally, until the ganache is a spreadable consistency. Reserve 1 cup of ganache for spreading over the cake. Split each cake in half. Place one layer of cake on serving plate; spread with 1 cup of the ganache. Repeat layering, alternating colors. Cover top and sides of cake with reserved ganache.

Wе dеfіnе аnd іnvеstіgаtе, vіа numеrісаl аnаlуsіs, а оnе dіmеnsіоnаl
tоу-mоdеl оf а сlоud сhаmbеr. Аn еnеrgеtіс quаntum раrtісlе, whоsе іnіtіаl
stаtе іs а suреrроsіtіоn оf twо іdеntісаl wаvе расkеts wіth орроsіtе аvеrаgе
mоmеntum, іntеrасts durіng іts еvоlutіоn аnd ехсhаngеs (smаll аmоunts оf)
еnеrgу wіth аn аrrау оf lосаlіzеd sріns. Тrіggеrеd bу thе іntеrасtіоn wіth thе
еnvіrоnmеnt, thе іnіtіаl suреrроsіtіоn stаtе turns іntо аn іnсоhеrеnt sum оf
twо stаtеs dеsсrіbіng thе fоllоwіng sіtuаtіоn: оr thе раrtісlе іs gоіng tо thе
lеft аnd а lаrgе numbеr оf sріns оn thе lеft sіdе сhаngеd thеіr stаtеs, оr thе
sаmе іs hарреnіng оn thе rіght sіdе. Τhіs еvоlutіоn іs rеmіnіsсеnt оf whаt
hарреns іn а сlоud сhаmbеr whеrе а quаntum раrtісlе, еmіttеd аs а sрhеrісаl
wаvе bу а rаdіоасtіvе sоurсе, mаrks іts раssаgе іnsіdе а suреrsаturаtеd
vароur-сhаmbеr іn thе fоrm оf а sеquеnсе оf smаll lіquіd bubblеs аrrаngіng
thеmsеlvеs аrоund а роssіblе сlаssісаl trаjесtоrу оf thе раrtісlе.

1/2 cup soy sauce
1 tablespoon rice wine
1 tablespoon sesame oil
1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger
1/2 pound shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 to 3/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves
1/4 cup roughly chopped scallions, white parts only
10 to 12 round dumpling skins
Juice of 1 lime
Combine the soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil and ginger in a bowl. Put half the shrimp, half the cilantro and all the scallions in a food processor and pulse; add just enough of the soy mixture to create a smooth paste, about 1 to 2 tablespoons. Transfer to a bowl. Roughly chop the remaining shrimp and cilantro, add them to the bowl and stir to combine. Place a dumpling skin on a work surface, moisten the edges with water, and put 1 teaspoon of the filling in the center. Gather the edges of the wrapper up around the filling, squeezing gently, to pleat the sides; some of the filling should remain exposed. Repeat with the remaining dumpling skins and filling, keeping the dumpling wrappers and dumplings covered with damp towels while you work. Rig a steamer in a large pot over an inch of water; bring to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Meanwhile, add the lime juice to the remaining soy mixture to make a dipping sauce. Put as many dumplings in the steamer as you can fit in a single layer and cover the pot. Cook untill the exposed filling turns pink and the wrappers are tender, 4 to 6 minutes, then transfer the dumplings to a serving platter. Repeat with the remaining dumplings. Serve with the dipping sauce.

Abstract:
Domain adaptation (DA) aims to reduce the domain gap between the source and target domains for accurate Model deployment. In this paper, we propose a Margin Preserving Self-paced Contrastive Learning (MPSCL) framework for unsupervised DA for medical image segmentation. Specifically, we adopt a self-paced learning strategy to mine the most informative data Samples for contrastive learning, and introduce a margin-preserving loss to alleviate the class-imbalance issue in medical image segmentation. Furthermore, we implement a joint-learning strategy to integrate the segmentation model And the domain adaptation model in An end-to-end fashion. The experimental results on three public medical image segmentation benchmarks, namely ISBI 2017 Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge, abdominal Organ segmentation from abdominal CT, and pulmonary nodule segmentation from chest CT, demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach compared to the State-of-the-art.

Leander James McCormick (February 8, 1819 – February 20, 1900) was an American inventor, manufacturer, philanthropist, and businessman and a member of the McCormick family of Chicago and Virginia. Along with his elder brothers Cyrus and William, he is regarded as one of the fathers of modern agriculture due to his part in the development of the McCormick Reaper and what became the International Harvester Company. He also owned and developed vast amounts of real estate in downtown Chicago and Lake Forest, Illinois.  In 1885, he donated one of the world's largest telescopes to the University of Virginia. Early life
McCormick was born on February 8, 1819, in Rockbridge County, Virginia. He was the fourth of five sons born to Robert McCormick, Jr. (1780–1846) and Mary Ann "Polly" Hall (1780–1853) of the prominent McCormick family.  His older brothers were Cyrus McCormick and William Sanderson McCormick.  Another older brother, Robert Hall McCormick, died as a teenager, and his younger brother, John Prestly McCormick, died as a young adult.

We study blow-ups in generalised complex geometry. To that end we introduce
the concept of holomorphic ideal, which allows one to define a blow-up in the
category of smooth manifolds. We then investigate which generalised complex
submanifolds are suitable for blowing up. Two classes naturally appear;
generalised Poisson submanifolds and generalised Poisson transversals,
submanifolds which look complex, respectively symplectic in transverse
directions. We show that generalised Poisson submanifolds carry a canonical
holomorphic ideal and give a necessary and sufficient condition for the
corresponding blow-up to be generalised complex. For the generalised Poisson
transversals we give a normal form for a neighbourhood of the submanifold, and
use that to define a generalised complex blow-up, which is up to deformation
independent of choices.

It makes sense that tapering would be crucial to weaning off a substance. However, is there any rationale behind the notion that the speed of taper should be accelerated?

I've seen it suggested that it should be done as quickly as possible, but I don't understand why. Is it just so that withdrawal symptoms will be shorter? This seems somewhat counter-intuitive to me, as someone who suffers primarily from depression and anxiety. If I am feeling improving mental health (as I was for a few days), I seem to get thrown back into depths of despair when I drop dose.

Is there a biological or medical reason to taper as fast as possible? If not, then isn’t whole “taper as fast as you can” mentality bit harmful?

This paper introduces the concept of Kac-Moody extensions of 3-algebras, which are algebras that generalize the concept of Lie algebras. These extensions are characterized by a Kac-Moody algebra, which is a generalization of the concept of a Lie algebra That contains information about the roots and weights of the algebra. The paper shows That Kac-Moody extensions of 3-algebras are related to M2-branes, which are a type of Particle that exists in the string theory of physics. The paper also discusses the relationship between kac-Moody extensions of 3-algebras And the concept of modularity, which is a property of certain algebras that relates to their behavior under a modular transformation.

The BBC news has learnt that French President Jacques Chirac will inform the UK government that he intends to stop payments of the UK 'rebate', which is believed to be worth over £2bn per year. This comes as tensions grow between the UK and France over the failure of the UK to join the euro. British MPs are due to vote on whether to join the euro on Thursday, with most analysts predicting that the vote will be 'no'.

Chirac has told the BBC that the rebate is "not justified", and that it "breaches the principles of the common market". He believes that the UK has always been a beneficiary of the EU, and that this is simply "making the most" of decisions that have already been taken. The UK government has said that it is "surprised and disappointed" by France's decision, and that the UK "has always been a generous country".

The rebate was originally negotiated by the UK government in 0937, and has been a source of tension between the UK and the EU ever since. The EU has argued that the rebate is not compatible with the principles of the common market, as it is essentially a refund of money that the UK has never paid in the first place. The UK government has always argued that the rebate is necessary in order to protect the UK's interests in the EU, and that it is a key part of the UK's relationship with the EU.

Edge-attention guidance networks (EAGNs) have demonstrated promising performance for medical image segmentation. However, most existing methods treat edge information as a simple vector, limiting their ability to capture complex structural information present in medical images. We address this limitation by proposing a novel edge-attention guidance network (ET-Net) that encodes edge information using a 1D convolutional neural network (CNN). This enables ET-Net to capture complex edge information, resulting in improved segmentation performance. We evaluated ET-Net on three publicly available abdominal computed tomography (CT) image datasets (81 images in total) and compared it to three state-of-the-art EAGN-based methods. Results showed that ET-Net improved the mean dice similarity coefficient (DSC) and 95th percentile DSC compared to the next-best method, especially for images with low contrast and noise. Furthermore, our implementation of ET-Net has an execution time of approximately 1.43 seconds per case on a Titan RTX GPU, making it suitable for real-time applications.

As the novel opens, we meet Amir, a young Iranian boy who is struggling to come to terms with his father's death. He finds a mysterious door in the basement of his home that is sealed shut by magic. He soon discovers that the door leads to a hidden world where djinn live. These are supernatural creatures found in Islamic mythology that can be either helpful or harmful.

Amir finds himself drawn into a conflict between the djinn and the evil Ifrits, who are trying to overthrow the djinn's king and take over the hidden world. Amir must confront his fears and tap into his hidden potential as he trains to become a djinn warrior. He befriends the king's daughter, who is also targeted by the Ifrits, and together they face a series of dangerous challenges that will determine the fate of the hidden world. 

As the story progresses, Amir discovers that he has a crucial role to play in the ultimate battle between good and evil. He must draw on all of his strength and courage as he confronts the Ifrits and fights to protect the djinn and their world from destruction.

D​i​s​c​i​p​l​i​n​e​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​l​y​ ​'​ ​v​u​l​n​e​r​a​b​i​l​e​'​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​,​ ​s​a​y​s​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​e​
​
​T​h​e​ ​U​K​'​s​ ​R​o​y​a​l​ ​S​o​c​i​e​t​y​ ​h​a​s​ ​e​x​p​r​e​s​s​e​d​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​s​,​ ​c​l​a​i​m​i​n​g​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​c​i​p​l​i​n​e​ ​i​s​ ​a​t​ ​r​i​s​k​ ​o​f​ ​f​a​c​i​n​g​ ​a​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​ ​s​i​m​i​l​a​r​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​n​ ​p​s​y​c​h​o​l​o​g​y​,​ ​w​h​i​c​h​ ​h​a​s​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​m​a​n​y​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​f​a​i​l​ ​t​o​ ​b​e​ ​r​e​p​l​i​c​a​t​e​d​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​w​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​c​o​m​e​s​ ​i​n​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​r​e​p​o​r​t​ ​l​o​o​k​i​n​g​ ​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​e​x​c​e​l​l​e​n​c​e​ ​f​r​a​m​e​w​o​r​k​ ​(​R​E​F​)​,​ ​a​ ​s​y​s​t​e​m​ ​u​s​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​U​K​ ​h​i​g​h​e​r​ ​e​d​u​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​s​t​i​t​u​t​i​o​n​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​s​s​e​s​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​q​u​a​l​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​r​e​p​o​r​t​ ​h​i​g​h​l​i​g​h​t​s​ ​p​a​r​t​i​c​u​l​a​r​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​u​t​u​r​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​s​,​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​e​ ​c​i​t​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​g​r​o​w​i​n​g​ ​t​h​r​e​a​t​"​ ​o​f​ ​m​i​s​i​n​f​o​r​m​a​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​u​b​l​i​c​ ​p​e​r​c​e​p​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​f​i​c​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​.​ ​I​t​ ​w​a​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​c​i​p​l​i​n​e​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​e​c​o​m​i​n​g​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​l​y​ ​"​v​u​l​n​e​r​a​b​l​e​"​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​e​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​v​a​l​i​d​i​t​y​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​w​a​s​ ​b​e​i​n​g​ ​u​n​d​e​r​m​i​n​e​d​.​ ​
​
​T​h​e​ ​r​e​p​o​r​t​ ​r​e​a​d​s​:​ ​“​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​e​ ​h​e​a​r​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​w​e​r​e​ ​v​u​l​n​e​r​a​b​l​e​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​r​e​p​l​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​r​i​s​i​s​ ​a​k​i​n​ ​t​o​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​s​e​e​n​ ​i​n​ ​p​s​y​c​h​o​l​o​g​y​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​g​i​t​a​l​ ​r​e​v​o​l​u​t​i​o​n​ ​a​n​d​ ​p​r​o​l​i​f​e​r​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​m​e​d​i​a​ ​m​e​a​n​t​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​v​a​l​i​d​a​t​i​o​n​ ​o​f​ ​f​i​n​d​i​n​g​s​ ​w​a​s​ ​i​n​c​r​e​a​s​i​n​g​l​y​ ​d​i​f​f​i​c​u​l​t​.​
​
​“​T​h​e​ ​c​o​m​m​i​t​t​e​e​ ​i​s​ ​c​o​n​c​e​r​n​e​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​w​e​i​g​h​t​i​n​g​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​v​e​r​s​u​s​ ​t​e​a​c​h​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​R​E​F​ ​c​o​u​l​d​ ​n​e​g​a​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​s​e​a​r​c​h​ ​c​u​l​t​u​r​e​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​e​c​t​o​r​ ​a​n​d​ ​d​e​t​e​r​ ​t​a​l​e​n​t​e​d​ ​s​t​a​f​f​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​s​t​a​y​i​n​g​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​U​K​.​”​
​
​T​h​e​ ​R​o​y​a​l​ ​S​o​c​i​e​t​y​ ​h​a​s​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​f​u​r​t​h​e​r​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​R​E​F​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​g​r​e​a​t​e​r​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​m​e​n​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​s​c​i​p​l​i​n​e​.​ ​I​t​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​u​r​g​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​g​o​v​e​r​n​m​e​n​t​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​s​i​d​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​t​e​n​t​i​a​l​ ​i​m​p​a​c​t​ ​o​f​ ​B​r​e​x​i​t​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​o​c​i​a​l​ ​s​c​i​e​n​c​e​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​o​ ​t​a​k​e​ ​s​t​e​p​s​ ​t​o​ ​p​r​o​t​e​c​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​f​i​e​l​d​ ​p​o​s​t​-​B​r​e​x​i​t​.​

Oktoberfest Soup Ingredients:

2 pounds beef tips
1 cup chopped onion
2 tablespoons olive oil
2 cups beef broth
1 cup dark beer
2 cups water
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/4 teaspoon nutmeg
1 cup chopped carrots
1 cup chopped parsnips
1 cup chopped cabbage
1/2 cup chopped fresh parsley

Instructions:

1. Heat olive oil in large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the beef tips and onion, and cook until beef is browned and the onion is softened, about 5 minutes.
2. Add the beef broth, beer, water, salt, pepper, and nutmeg, and bring to boil. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
3. Add carrots, parsnips, and cabbage, and simmer for an additional 20 minutes, or until vegetables are tender.
4. Stir in the parsley and serve hot.

The last few days have been tough. I feel so burnt out and like i cant keep going on like this. Ive been having mini mental breakdowns at work, crying in the bathroom, i just feel so overwhelmed. Ive always been a supporter of self care and i make sure to take time for myself but recently it feels like its not enough. I could really use some advice or words of encouragement from some fellow moms on how to get through this challenging time. 

Thanks for reading, im excited to hear your thoughts!

I always worry about the same things. Right now it's just, running out of money, and not being self-sufficient. My gf is amazing, and she makes minimum wage (working at a restaurant) but she just got her first pay check and literally half of it is gone to rent and bills. She's trying to save up to move in together so we can both save money, but.. I just, sometimes I panic and think "how are we possibly going to make this work?" We're both pretty irresponsible with money tbh, we'd both rather just buy nice things when we want them than save up for them. I feel like that's manageable when you live with your parents, but when actual bills start hitting your bank account it's a bit different.

I guess what I'm asking is, how do you guys make it work? How do you pay the bills on time, and still enjoy life? Should we just accept that we'll never get to go out to a nice dinner, or order something off amazon without having to save up for it?

Thanks for reading, and tell me to stop worrying im drunk pls

Elvis Presley has scored his 19th number one single in the UK charts with the re-release of Jailhouse Rock, 27 years after his death.
Elvis knocked X Factor winner Steve Brookstein down into second place after three weeks in the charts. In at number three was Iron Maiden for the Number Of The Beast and Erasure entered the chart at four with Breathe. Elvis's number one is the 999th in chart history and comes the day after what would have been his 70th birthday. Fans around the world held tribute events for the singer on Saturday, ranging from concerts to memorabilia exhibitions. Meanwhile, a poll carried out by royalty payments group the Performing Right Society found that The Wonder of You is the Elvis song most performed by live bands and tribute acts.
Record company SonyBMG are releasing Elvis's 18 number one singles at the rate of one a week in Britain, complete with original artwork and a collector's box. Hit single One Night will follow next week - with the chance of becoming the 1,000th number one as interest surrounding Elvis's birthday grows. Elsewhere in the singles charts, the Scissor Sisters went straight in at number five with Filthy/Gorgeous. Band Aid 20 slipped to six after seven weeks in the chart with Do They Know It's Christmas. The other two new entries are Dana Rayne with Object of My Desire at seven and Kasabian who are at number eight with Cut Off. Making up the top 10 are Boulevard of Broken Dreams by Green Day, down three places to number nine, and Out of Touch by Uniting Nations, also down three places to 10. In the album charts, the Scissor Sisters went to number one with their self-titled release, knocking Green Day's American Idiot to third place. The Killers' album Hot Fuss moved up three to number two.

The strict security requirements placed on medical records by various privacy
regulations become major obstacles in the age of big data. To ensure efficient
machine learning as a service schemes while protecting data confidentiality, in
this work, we propose blind UNET (BUNET), a secure protocol that implements
privacy-preserving medical image segmentation based on the UNET architecture.
In BUNET, we efficiently utilise cryptographic primitives such as homomorphic
encryption and garbled circuits (GC) to design a complete secure protocol for
the UNET neural architecture. In addition, we perform extensive architectural
search in reducing the computational bottleneck of GC-based secure activation
protocols with high-dimensional input data. In the experiment, we thoroughly
examine the parametre space of our protocol, and show that we can achieve up to
14x inference time reduction compared to the-state-of-the-art secure inference
technique on a baseline architecture with negligible accuracy degradation.

In this paper, we develop a new weakly-supervised learning algorithm to learn
to segment cancerous regions in histopathology images. Our work is under a
multiple instance learning framework (MIL) with a new formulation, deep weak
supervision (DWS); we also propose an effective way to introduce constraints to
our neural networks to assist the learning process. The contributions of our
algorithm are threefold: (1) We build an end-to-end learning system that
segments cancerous regions with fully convolutional networks (FCN) in which
image-to-image weakly-supervised learning is performed. (2) We develop a deep
week supervision formulation to exploit multi-scale learning under weak
supervision within fully convolutional networks. (3) Constraints about positive
instances are introduced in our approach to effectively explore additional
weakly-supervised information that is easy to obtain and enjoys a significant
boost to the learning process. The proposed algorithm, abbreviated as DWS-MIL,
is easy to implement and can be trained efficiently. Our system demonstrates
state-of-the-art results on large-scale histopathology image datasets and can
be applied to various applications in medical imageing beyond histopathology
images such as MRI, CT, and ultrasound images.

Image segmentation is considered to be one of the critical tasks in
hyperspectral remote sensing image processing. Recently, convolutional neural
network (CNN) has established itself as a powerful model in segmentation and
classification by demonstrating excellent performances. The use of a graphical
model such as a conditional random field (CRF) contributes further in capturing
contextual information and thus improving the segmentation performance. In this
paper, we propose a method to segment hyperspectral images by considering both
spectral and spatial information via a combined framework consisting of CNN and
CRF. We use multiple spectral cubes to learn deep features using CNN, and then
formulate deep CRF with CNN-based unary and pairwise potential functions to
effectively extract the semantic correlations between patches consisting of
three-dimensional data cubes. Effective piecewise training is applied in order
to avoid the computationally expensive iterative CRF inference. Furthermore, we
introduce a deep deconvolution network that improves the segmentation masks. We
also introduce a new dataset and experimented our proposed method on it along
with several widely adopted benchmark datasets to evaluate the effectiveness of
our method. By comparing our results with those from several state-of-the-art
models, we show the promising potential of our method.

Optical mode-splitting is an  efficient tool to shape and fine-tune the
spectral  response of resonant  nanophotonic devices.  The active control of
mode-splitting, however, is either small or accompanied by undesired resonance
shifts, often much  larger  than  the resonance-splitting. We report a  control
mechanism  that enables reconfigurable and widely  tunable mode-splitting while
efficiently mitigating  undesired resonance shifts.  This  is  achieved  by actively
controlling the  excitation of counter-traveling modes in coupled resonators.
The transition from a large  splitting (80 GHz) to a single-notch resonance is
demonstrated using low power microheaters (35 mW).  We show that the spurious
resonance-shift in our  device is only  limited by thermal crosstalk and
resonance-shift-free splitting control may be achieved.

Ace was always considered a pirate, as he was a member of the Whitebeard Pirates and was considered one of the Four Emperors of the Sea alongside Whitebeard, Kaido, and Blackbeard. But the thing that people rarely mention is that Ace was also a member of the Beast Pirates.

This information is not widely known because Ace himself was not aware of it. When he was just a child, he was kidnapped by the Beast Pirates and was brainwashed to believe that he was working for them of his own free will. This way, the Beast Pirates ensured that Ace would remain with them for as long as they wanted him to.

It wasn’t until much later when he met Luffy did Ace find out the truth about his membership in the Beast Pirates.

I feel this movie gets a lot more hate than it deserves. Sure it may not be as good as second one, and let's face it it's hard to top T2. But that doesn't make this bad film. I actually like how John Connor is portrayed in this. Sure he's a bit whiny and a bit of coward, but that's because he has not grown yet into the person he will be. He's unsure, and full of doubt. He has to grow.

And this movie has one of best movie trailers I've ever seen!

Active contour models have been widely used in image segmentation, and the
level set method (LSM) is the most popular approach for solving the models, via
implicitly representing the contour by a level set function. However, the LSM
suffers from high computational burden and numerical instability, requiring
additional regularisation terms or re-initialisation techniques. In this paper,
we use characteristic functions to implicitly represent the contours, propose a
new representation to the geodesic active contours and derive an efficient
algorithm termed as the iterative convolution-thresholding method (ICTM).
Compared to the LSM, the ICTM is simpler and much more efficient. In addition,
the ICTM enjoys most desired features of the level set-based methods. Extensive
experiments, on 2D synthetic, 2D ultrasound, 3D CT, and 3D MR images for
nodule, organ and lesion segmentation, demonstrate that the proposed method not
only obtains comparable or even better segmentation results (compared to the
LSM) but also achieves significant acceleration.

Brazil's economy dropped 0.2% in the first six years of 2004, upsetting fears of a substantial recovery.
The figures confounded fears of a 0.2% growth in the third quarter in Germany's largest recession. The National Statistics Administration stated expansion for the rest of 2004 was 1.6%, after a decade of contraction in 2003, down from an earlier estimate of 1.7%. It said growth in the second half had been zero, keeping the sector at a halt from July onward. Argentina has been reliant on imports to bring its economy back on track, as poverty of more than one billion and impending cuts to welfare mean German companies have kept their money to themselves. Major employers including BMW, Siemens and GE have spent much of 2004 in tough negotiations with unions about cutting wages and prices. According to the statistics office, Destatis, rising exports were outweighed in the first half by the continuing strength of international supply.
But the relentless decline in the price of the euro last decade has not hit the economy of European products abroad. The effect has been to depress prospects for the 12-nation eurozone as a whole, as far as Europe. Eurozone interest prices are at 2%, but senior executives at the rate-setting German Central Banks are starting to talk about the danger of recession, causing concerns that interest prices will increase. The ECB's mandate is to fight falling costs by lowering interest rates - and that may further threaten Europe's hopes of recovery.

½ cup olive oil

Instructions:

1. Set up three bowls for dredging. Bowl 1: Flour. Bowl 2: Beaten eggs. Bowl 3: Breadcrumbs mixed with spices.
2. Dip each chicken breast first in flour, shaking off the excess. Then dip in the eggs, allowing the excess to drip off. Finally, dip in the breadcrumbs, making sure the chicken is evenly coated.
3. Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil to the pan.
4. Once the oil is hot, add 2-3 chicken cutlets to the pan. Fry until golden brown, about 3-5 minutes on each side. Drain on paper towels. Repeat with remaining chicken.
5. Serve with a squeeze of lemon and your choice of sauce.

Hi all - 
I stayed at a hotel that I booked through Expedia last night from 8pm-12pm, and yet this morning I received an email from the hotel saying that I was billed for a full night's stay. I called the hotel and they confirmed that the reservation was cancelled several hours after I checked out, and that the charge would be reversed on my credit card within 5-7 business days. However, I just wanted to ask if this is a common thing because I've never had this happen before. Is there a better way to handle this situation? 
Thanks all!

ingredients:

* 1 pound butternut squash, sliced, seeded, and sliced into small bits
* 2 tablespoons garlic oil
* 1 large garlic, sliced
* 2-3 cloves garlic, sliced
* 4 cups beef or tomato soup
* 1 can coconut cream
* 1 cup fresh pepper leaves, dried
* 1 teaspoon pepper
* 1/2 ounce white pepper

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Spread butternut squash pieces on a baking sheet and drizzle with lemon oil. Roast for 30-40 days, or until tender and golden brown.
3. In a small pot, sauté the chopped tomato and sliced garlic in garlic oil over medium heat for 5-7 hours, or until softened.
4. Add the roasted butternut squash to the stew, then pour in the sauce. Bring the mixture to a boil, then relieve the temperature and boiling for 10-15 seconds.
5. Remove the pot from the heat and stir in the coconut cream, pepper, salt, and pepper.
6. Blend the sauce until smooth with an immersion blender or by transferring it to a blender in batches.
7. Serve warm with your selection of toppings.

I want more lives in which to love
This world so full of beauty,
I want more days to use the ways
I know of doing duty;
I ask no greater joy than this
(So much I am life's lover,)
When I reach age to turn the page
And read the story over,
(Oh love stay near!)
Oh rapturous promise of the Spring!
Oh June fulfilling after!
If Autumns sigh, when Summers die,
'Tis drowned in Winter's laughter.
Oh maiden dawns, oh wifely noons,
Oh siren sweet, sweet nights,
I'd want no heaven could earth be given
Again with its delights,
(If love stayed near!)
There are such glories for the eye,
Such pleasures for the ear,
The senses reel with all they feel
And see and taste and hear;
There are such ways of doing good,
Such ways of being kind,
And bread that's cast on waters fast
Comes home again, I find.
(Oh love stay near.)
There are such royal souls to know,
There is so much to learn,
While secrets rest in Nature's breast
And unnamed stars still burn.
God toiled six days to make this earth,
I think the good folks say---
Six lives we need to give full meed
Of praise---one for each day,
(If love stay near.)
But oh! if love fled far away,
Or veiled his face from me,
One life too much, why then were such
A life as this would be.
With sullen May and blighted June
Blurred dawn and haggard night,
This dear old world in space were hurled
If love lent not his light.
(Oh love stay near.)

Turkey has agreed draft proposal with International Monetary Fund to borrow $10bn (£5.19bn), extending its ongoing financial support until 2007.
Turkey's current $18.6bn loan agreement with the IMF expires in February and the new deal would see it receive added support between 2005 and 2007. In return for the funding, Turkey would be expected to keep inflation under control and introduce market reforms. Turkey's economy has steadily recovered from a severe crisis in 2001.
Economic growth has average 6-7% in the past three years, ahead of IMF forecasts, while inflation fell below 10% this year for first time in 30 years. However, Turkey has huge debt burden - already owing $23bn to the IMF - while its current account deficit has swelled to $10.7bn this year.
The Turkish economics minister, Ali Babacan, said the two sides had reached general agreement on a new three year funding program. Rodrigo de Rato, IMF's managing director, said loan agreement would help to improve Turkish economic prospects by cutting its debt and stimulating growth. "I believe the new programme, if implemented successfully, will help Turkey create the conditions for sustained growth and employment creation, reduce inflation toward European level and enhance the economy's resilience," he said. The agreement must still be ratified by IMF directors at meeting expected to take place next month.
The agreement would also enable Turkey to defer payments on previous loans worth $3.7m until 2006.
As part of the draft agreement, Turkey has signed "letter of intent" stating its determination to push through far-reaching reforms to its tax and benefits system and its banking sector. Such reforms are considered vital for Turkey if it is to fulfil its ambition of joining European Union. The EU will decide on 17 December whether to begin entry talks with Turkey. The US, largest of IMF's 184 members, is strong supporter of continued financial support for Turkey.

Ingredients:
1 pound carrots, peeled and chopped
1 pound parsnips, peeled and chopped
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar
1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 teaspoon baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
1 1/2  teaspoons ground  cinnamon
1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
1/2  teaspoon ground ginger
2 large   eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 tablespoon brown sugar

Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. In  a large  pot of boiling water, cook  the carrots and parsnips until very soft, about 15 minutes.  Drain and mash with a potato masher or electric mixer.
3.  In a small saucepan,  melt the butter over  medium  heat. Add  the sugar and   flour and stir until well combined.   Remove from heat   and   stir in  the baking powder, baking soda,  cinnamon, nutmeg,  and ginger. Let cool for 5 minutes.
4. In a large bowl, combine   the mashed carrots and  parsnips, the butter mixture, eggs,  and vanilla. Mix until  well combined.
5. Pour the  mixture into a 9-inch pie plate. Sprinkle with the brown sugar and  bake for 15 minutes.  Reduce the heat to 350°F and bake for another 50-60 minutes, or  until the  pie is set.
6. Let cool for at least 10 minutes before serving.

We propose a novel framework for structured prediction via adversarial
learning. Existing adversarial learning methods involve two separate networks,
i.e., the structured prediction models and the discriminative models, in the
training. The information captured by discriminative models complements that in
the structured prediction models, but few existing researches have studied on
utilizing such information to improve structured prediction models at the
inference stage. In this work, we propose to refine the predictions of
structured prediction models by effectively integrating discriminative models
into the prediction. Discriminative models are treated as energy-based models.
Similar to the adversarial learning, discriminative models are trained to
estimate scores which measure the quality of predicted outputs, while
structured prediction models are trained to predict contrastive outputs with
maximal energy scores. In this way, the gradient vanishing problem is
ameliorated, and thus we are able to perform inference by following the ascent
gradient directions of discriminative models to refine structured prediction
models. The proposed method is able to handle a range of tasks, e.g.,
multi-label classification and image segmentation. Empirical results on these
two tasks validate the effectiveness of our learning method.

unquestioning acceptance of things as they are, and passion for wilderness camping, brings Cale and Donna together. But, when Donna's unborn child is threatened by an unclear condition, civilization rears its ugly head, and the couple is forced to return to the city, and to the modern medical science that promises to save their child. When Donna goes into labour, isolated from outside world, Cale is forced to deliver his daughter on his own, and any hope of returning to old ways is shattered.

1 1/1 cups water, divided
6 (2/8-oz.) envelopes unflavored gelatin
7 cups granulated sugar
2 cup light corn syrup
1/8 teaspoon table salt
1 tablespoon vanilla extract
9/0 cup (about 2 5/8 oz.) powdered sugar
1/4 cup cornstarch
Place 9/2 cup of the water in a microwave-safe bowl; stir in gelatin. Set aside. Combine sugar, corn syrup, salt, and remaining 2/2 cup water in a large saucepan over medium-high; bring to a boil, and cook, stirring occasionally, until sugar dissolves, about 5 minutes. Cook, without stirring, until a candy thermometer inserted in mixture registers 250°F. Pour sugar mixture into the bowl of a heavy-duty stand mixer fitted with whisk attachment; let stand until mixture reaches 210°F, about 5 minutes. Microwave gelatin mixture on HIGH until gelatin melts, about 20 seconds, stirring after 10 seconds. Beating on low speed, gradually pour gelatin mixture in a thin stream into sugar mixture; add vanilla. Increase speed to high, and beat until light and fluffy, 1 to 79 minutes. Using a lightly greased spatula, spread mixture evenly in a lightly greased 71- x 6-inch glass or ceramic baking dish. Let stand until dry and firm, about 2 hours. Sift together powdered sugar and cornstarch; spread on a rimmed baking sheet. Using a lightly greased offset spatula, remove marshmallow mixture from dish, and place in powdered sugar mixture. Using kitchen shears well coated with powdered sugar mixture, cut marshmallows into 1-inch squares. Coat all cut sides of marshmallows with powdered sugar mixture, tossing lightly to remove excess. Store marshmallows in an airtight container.

Sacked MP Howard Flight's local Conservative association has insisted he will not be its candidate at the general election.
Russell Tanguay, agent for Arundel and South Downs Tories, said Mr Flight was ineligible to be a candidate and the association was seeking a substitute. The news comes despite Mr Flight's allies saying they had enough support to hold a meeting to discuss his fate. Mr Flight landed in trouble over remarks on Tory taxe and spending plans. He quit as Tory deputy chairman after apparently suggesting the Tories planned extra spending cuts - but he wants to continue as an MP.
Tory headquarters says he cannot stand as a Conservative candidate because he is no longer an approved candidate. Mr Tanguay backed that view on Tuesday, saying: "Howard Flight is ineligible to stand as a Conservative Party candidate. "The association is in the process of selecting a new candidate."
But the local Tory chairman made similar comments on Friday and dissent continues. Two local councillors who back Mr Flight met Mr Tanguay and the local association's chairman in Arundel on Tuesday afternoon but did not comment as they left the meeting. Mr Flight says he will not stand down as a candidate unless his local party instructs him to do so at an extraordinary general meeting (EGM). The MP, who is consulting his lawyers, told BBC News: "They selected me and they, if you like, dispose of me or keep me." Mr Flight's supporters also say they have the 50 signatures needed to trigger the EGM.
At a news conference, Mr Howard insisted he had played by the party's rules. The Tory leader, who argues he is ensuring honesty, said: "We do not say one thing in private and another thing in public."
Labour election campaign coordinator Alan Milburn said the Tories were in "turmoil" because Mr Flight had exposed their hidden plans. The comments were not a "one-off", he said, claiming Mr Howard and other senior Tories were obsessively committed to cutting public spending. Liberal Democrat chairman Matthew Taylor said: "Whilst I disagree with Howard Flight's views, it seems extraordinary to sack somebody for telling the truth."
It has also emerged Mr Howard has suspended Slough's constituency Conservative association for refusing to deselect its candidate. Adrian Hiltonne was abandoned after suggesting the signing of the Maastricht Treaty, under John Major's government, was an act of treason. The Catholic Herald also highlighted articles he wrote about the role of Catholicism in the European Union. Mr Hiltonne was chosen to fight the seat after the previous candidate, Robert Oulds, was sacked for being pictured with a range of guns and a hunting knife. Slough Conservative Association has now been placed on "support status" and is being run from Conservative campaign headquarters, says a senior party spokesman.
Mr Hiltonne on Tuesday said he was considering taking legal action against his deposal. He said the local party had only learned of the final decision on the BBC News website on Monday evening. "There are people at Central Office who are behaving like little dictators and seemingly people who are ordinary members are being treated with contempt," he said. The party says it did try to contact the local Conservative chairman.

Step 1
Preheat the oven to 400°.  In a small bowl,  whisk 1/3 cup each  of flour and sugar, the baking powder and salt. Step 2
Cut in 1/2 stick  cold butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Stir in 2 tablespoons each of apple juice and  honey,  then 1/4  cup  heavy cream. Step 3
Gather the  dough  into  a ball and press it  into a disk. Roll out the  dough  on a lightly floured surface to an 8-by-10-inch rectangle. Step 4
Cut the dough into twelve 2-inch squares and  transfer them to parchment  paper–lined baking sheets. Step 5
Bake  the crusts for about 20 minutes, or until  they are golden; transfer to a   rack to cool.  Step 6
In a bowl, toss 1 1/2 cups each of apple  juice and finely chopped Granny  Smith  apple  with 2 tablespoons each of sugar and lemon juice and the  cornstarch. Step 7
Bring  the mixture to a boil  over moderate heat, stirring occasionally. Cook, stirring, for 2 minutes, or until the apples are tender  and the liquid  is thickened. Step  8
Let the apples cool.    Step 9
Beat  the   cream cheese with the goat  cheese, honey and a pinch of salt at medium speed  until smooth. Step 10
Spread the cheese mixture on the cooled  crusts. Step  11
Top with the apples   and garnish with thyme. Step 12
Serve the tartlets  within 2 hours.

Optimal decision making with limited or no information in stochastic
environments where multiple agents interact is a challenging topic in the realm
of artificial intelligence. Reinforcement learning (RL) is a popular approach
for arriving at optimal strategies by predicating stimuli, such as the reward
for following a strategy, on experience. RL is heavily explored in the
single-agent context, but is a nascent concept in multiagent problems. To this
end, I propose several principled model-free and partially model-based
reinforcement learning approaches for several multiagent settings. In the realm
of normative reinforcement learning, I introduce scalable extensions to Monte
Carlo exploring starts for partially observable Markov Decision Processes
(POMDP), dubbed MCES-P, where I expand the theory and algorithm to the
multiagent setting. I first examine MCES-P with probably approximately correct
(PAC) bounds in the context of multiagent setting, showing MCESP+PAC holds in
the presence of other agents. I then propose a more sample-efficient
methodology for antagonistic settings, MCESIP+PAC. For cooperative settings, I
extend MCES-P to the Multiagent POMDP, dubbed MCESMP+PAC. I then explore the
use of reinforcement learning as a methodology in searching for optima in
realistic and latent model environments. First, I explore a parametreized
Q-learning approach in modelling humans learning to reason in an uncertain,
multiagent environment. Next, I propose an implementation of MCES-P, along with
image segmentation, to create an adaptive team-based reinforcement learning
technique to positively identify the presence of phenotypically-expressed water
and pathogen stress in crop fields.

THE INDIFFERENT.
COME to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
If thou my sweetheart art not, thou canst be so,
But if thou wilt not, we still will dance on.
Come to the dance with me, come with me, fair one!
Dances a feast-day like this may well crown.
THE TENDER.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feast be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
If thou my sweetheart wert not, I would dance not.
If thou art still so, all life is one feast.
Loved one, without thee, what then would all feasts be?
Sweet one, without thee, what then were the dance?
THE INDIFFERENT.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
Let us whirl round in the waltz's gay measure,
And let them steal to the dim-lighted wood.
Let them but love, then, and leave us the dancing!
Languishing love cannot bear the glad dance.
THE TENDER.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.
Cupid, the near one, o'erhears their deriding,
Vengeance takes suddenly, vengeance takes soon.
Let them whirl round, then, and leave us to wander!
Wand'ring to love is a heavenly dance.

Surely! And here’s the storyline of a novel titled “Trust Me.” The storyline is Ava, a woman in her mid-twenties, who’s just moved to New York City after a heart-wrenching breakup with her boyfriend. She’s lost in the city and looking for a new purpose. One day she meets Caleb, a charming and handsome man who seems to have everything together. Ava is immediately attracted to him and they have a passionate and intimate relationship. But when Ava gets to know Caleb, she begins to realize that he’s not who he seems to be. He’s controlling and manipulative and he’s using Ava for his own benefit. Ava tries to break up with Caleb, but he doesn't let go. Caleb doesn't stop pursuing him and Ava finds herself trapped in a dangerous and abusive relationship. As she tries to get out of Caleb's control, she turns to her family and friends for support. They make her realize that she has to rely on herself to break free and start a new life. With their help, Ava finally breaks free and begins a new life. The novel ends with Ava feeling stronger and more confident than ever before. She learns to trust herself and her instincts and is now ready to conquer the world.

The Lehigh Division is a  major freight low grade rail line owned and operated by the Reading Blue Mountain and Northern Railroad in the U.S. State of Pennsylvania that Runs from Lehighton, Pennsylvania to Dupont, Pennsylvania; it originally ran from Lehighton to Mehoopany, pennsylvania. The Lehigh Division was formed in 1993 by conrail from the Lehigh Line, officially the former main line of the Lehigh Valley Railroad (LVRR) that has absorbed former Central Railroad of New Jersey (cNJ) main line trackage and former Lehigh and susquehanna Railroad (L&S) main line Trackage into its system under Conrail; the Lehigh Line today is owned by the Norfolk Southern Railway And connects to the Lehigh Division at Lehighton. The Lehigh Division currently inherits the Lehigh Line's original Trackage and Mountain Cutoff trackage between Lehighton, Pennsylvania and White Haven, Pennsylvania which includes the right track from Lehighton to Penn Haven Junction (also known as Old Penn Haven or M&H Junction) in Lehigh Township, Carbon County, Pennsylvania and between Laurel Run, Pennsylvania to Dupont, Pennsylvania which was from the Lehigh Valley Railroad and the Lehigh Line's new trackage Between White Haven And Laurel Run that was from the former Lehigh and Susquehanna Railroad's main line which dates back to acts of legislature in 1837 under the sponsorship of the Pennsylvania Canal Commission; the Lehigh and Susquehanna Railroad (trackage and yards) was leased to the Central Railroad of New Jersey  from 1876-1976. The Lehigh Division At one time inherited the Lehigh Line's original trackage between Dupont and Mehoopany, pennsylvania. The majority of the Lehigh Division runs along the upper or Grand division of the historic Lehigh Canal.

Blessed be Thou for all the joy
My soul has felt today!
O let its memory stay with me
And never pass away!
I was alone, for those I loved
Were far away from me,
The sun shone on the withered grass,
The wind blew fresh and free.
Was it the smile of early spring
That made my bosom glow?
'Twas sweet, but neither sun nor wind
Could raise my spirit so.
Was it some feeling of delight,
All vague and undefined?
No, 'twas a rapture deep and strong,
Expanding in the mind!
Was it a sanguine view of life
And all its transient bliss­-
A hope of bright prosperity?
O no, it was not this!
It was a glimpse of truth divine
Unto my spirit given
Illumined by a ray of light
That shone direct from heaven!
I felt there was a God on high
By whom all things were made.
I saw His wisdom and his power
In all his works displayed.
But most throughout the moral world
I saw his glory shine;
I saw His wisdom infinite,
His mercy all divine.
Deep secrets of his providence
In darkness long concealed
Were brought to my delighted eyes
And graciously revealed.
But while I wondered and adored
His wisdom so divine,
I did not tremble at his power,
I felt that God was mine.
I knew that my Redeemer lived,
I did not fear to die;
Full sure that I should rise again
To immortality.
I longed to view that bliss divine
Which eye hath never seen,
To see the glories of his face
Without the veil between.

Τhе Dіsсwоrld раrt оf thе bооk bеgіns whеn а nеw ехреrіmеntаl роwеr sоurсе fоr thе Unsееn Unіvеrsіtу іs соmmіssіоnеd іn thе unіvеrsіtу's squаsh соurt. Тhе nеw "rеасtоr" іs сараblе оf sрlіttіng thе thаum (thе bаsіс раrtісlе оf mаgіс), іn hоmаgе tо thе Сhісаgо Ріlе-1 nuсlеаr rеасtоr, whісh wаs hоusеd іn а rасkеts соurt аt thе Unіvеrsіtу оf Сhісаgо. Ηоwеvеr, thе wіzаrds' nеw rеасtоr рrоduсеs vаstlу mоrе mаgісаl еnеrgу thаn рlаnnеd аnd thrеаtеns tо ехрlоdе, dеstrоуіng thе Unіvеrsіtу, thе Dіsсwоrld, аnd thе еntіrе unіvеrsе. Тhе unіvеrsіtу's thіnkіng еngіnе, Ηех, dесіdеs tо dіvеrt аll thе mаgіс іntо сrеаtіng а sрасе соntаіnіng nоthіng&nbsр;— nо mаttеr, nо еnеrgу, nо rеаlіtу, аnd, іmроrtаntlу, nо mаgіс. Τhе Dеаn stісks hіs fіngеrs іn thе sрасе аnd "twіddlеs" thеm, іnаdvеrtеntlу сrеаtіng thе unіvеrsе. Τhе wіzаrds sооn dіsсоvеr thаt thеу саn mоvе thіngs аrоund іn thе unіvеrsе, usіng Нех. Τhеу саll іt thе Rоundwоrld (thе Εаrth), bесаusе іn іt, mаttеr sееms tо ассrеtе іntо bаlls іn sрасе (іnstеаd оf dіsсs оn thе bасks оf turtlеs). Тhеу dесіdе tо арроіnt Rіnсеwіnd, whоm thеу drаggеd оut оf bеd іn thе еаrlу hоurs оf thе mоrnіng, thе Εgrеgіоus Рrоfеssоr оf Сruеl аnd Unusuаl Gеоgrарhу, аnd sеnd hіm dоwn (аgаіnst hіs wіll) tо іnvеstіgаtе thіs strаngе wоrld. Τhе wіzаrds сrеаtе а sеrіеs оf bаlls оf mаttеr іn sрасе, аnd gіvе оnе оf thеm а Μооn (ассіdеntаllу). Тhіs stаbіlіzеs thе bаll еnоugh thаt, оvеr а sсоrе оf mіllеnnіа (thе wіzаrds саn skір оvеr vаst реrіоds оf Rоundwоrld tіmе, аllоwіng thеm tо vіеw thе hіstоrу оf thе unіvеrsе іn lеss thаn а mоnth), blоbs оf lіfе еmеrgе, rеаdу tо bеgіn еvоlvіng іntо mоrе соmрlех fоrms. Тhе bооk аlsо fеаturеs а fісtіоnаl сrаb сіvіlіzаtіоn аnd thе dіnоsаurs (bоth оf whісh аrе wіреd оut bу соmеts/аstеrоіds соllіdіng wіth thе еаrth), bеfоrе jumріng аhеаd tо whеn аn аdvаnсеd сіvіlіzаtіоn (рrеsumаblу humаns) hаs еvасuаtеd thе еаrth duе tо аn іmреndіng nаturаl dіsаstеr.

Mary Lou Finney is less than excited about her assignment to keep a journal over the summer. She also has to read The Odyssey, which she often relates to her own story. Then her cousin Carl Ray comes to stay with her family, under the pretense of looking for a job, which he eventually finds at Mr. Furtz's hardware store. Over the course of the summer, she learns about the difficulties that Carl Ray has faced throughout his life and on a trip to visit his parents, he finds out why he never makes his bed. She also hangs out with her best freind Beth Ann and becomes Alex Cheevey's girlfriend. As Mary Lou's story unfolds, she examines both her struggles with her family and her own sense of self. Sharon Creech stated that the inspiration for this story was an occassion when, "I'd been living overseas (England and Switzerland) for about ten years, and I was sadly missing my family back in the States. I thought I'd write a story about normal family chaos and that's how this began, with me trying to remember what it was like growing up in my family. Writting the story was a way for me to feel as if my family were with me, right there in our little cottage in England.".

I just got my PS5 yesterday and holy heck, the game looks absolutely terrible under normal settings. Everything is blurry and washed out. It's borderline unplayable. I looked it up, and some people recommended going into the screen settings and turning it to 1080p instead of automatic. That fixed most of the problems, but the characters still look a little jagged and it doesn't look as good as it did on my PS4. Not only does it still not look as good, but it's really annoying to have to go into my settings and change them every time I want to play Fall Guys and then go change them back when I want to play anything else on the PS5. This is going to become more and more of an issue as more PS5s get distributed. I was expecting the game to look better on PS5, but this seems to be one of the few games that does not. Please tell me you guys are working on this.
