Tom Martell is an American entrepreneur and businessman who is best known for his role as the co-founder and CEO of the company Palantir Technologies. 

Martell was born on July 5, 1976, in New York City, and grew up in the Upper West Side of Manhattan. He attended the Horace Mann School and later graduated from Yale University with a degree in economics. 

After graduating from Yale, Martell worked as a derivatives trader at Credit Suisse and later as a partner at the hedge fund Marshall Wace. 

In 2003, he co-founded Palantir Technologies, a software company that specializes in big data analytics and intelligence solutions. Martell has served as the company's CEO since its inception and has played a key role in its growth and development. Under Martell's leadership, Palantir has become a leading provider of data analysis software to government agencies and companies in the private sector. 

The company's software has been used to investigate terrorist activities, track down criminals, and detect financial fraud. Palantir has also been contracted by various government agencies, including the United States Army, the FBI, and the CIA. In addition to his work with Palantir, Martell has also been active in the San Francisco Bay Area community. He has served on the board of directors of the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art and has been a member of the Bay Area Council, a business-led public policy organization. Martell has received numerous awards and accolades for his work in the technology industry. 

He has been named one of the "Top 100 Most Influential People in the World" by Time magazine and one of the "Top 10 Most Powerful People in Silicon Valley" by Forbes magazine. 

He has also been recognized as a "Young Global Leader" by the World Economic Forum and a "Tech Pioneer" by the World Economic Forum. 

Martell is married to the former Katherine Boyle, who is the daughter of a former acting Deputy Attorney General of the United States, Matthew Boyle. They have three children together.

Image segmentation refers to the  separation of objects from the  background,
and  has been one of the  most  challenging aspects of digital image processing.
Practically it is impossible to design a segmentation algorithm which has 100%
accuracy, and therefore  numerous segmentation techniques  have been proposed in
the literature, each with certain limitations.  In this paper, a novel
Falling-Ball algorithm is presented, which is a region-based segmentation
algorithm, and an alternative  to watershed transform (based on waterfall
model).  The proposed algorithm  detects  the catchment basins by  assuming that a
ball falling  from hilly terrains will stop in a catchment basin. Once catchment
basins are  identified,  the   association of  each pixel with one of the catchment
basin is obtained  using multi-criterion fuzzy logic. Edges are constructed  by
dividing image into different catchment basins with the help  of  a membership
function. Finally closed contour algorithm is  applied to find closed  regions
and objects within closed regions are  segmented  using intensity  information.
The performance  of  the  proposed algorithm is evaluated both objectively as well
as subjectively. Simulation results show that the proposed algorithms gives
superior  performance  over conventional Sobel edge  detection methods and the
watershed segmentation algorithm. For comparative  analysis, various comparison
methods are used for  demonstrating  the superiority of  proposed methods over
existing segmentation methods.

The leaders of Britain's three largest parties have been taking part in the final televised election debate.

During the debate, Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey said neither the Conservatives nor Labour had "ever been trusted on tax".

He accused Labour of wanting to "raise billions in taxes" on high-earning and "successfull" people, and the Tories of wanting to raise National Insurance taxes on working people.

In response, Labour leader Sir Keir Starmer said Mr Davey wanted to raise taxes by more than £30 billion and accused the Liberal Democrats of having no clear plan on how to fund social care.

Boris Johnson defended his record on delivering public services and accused Sir Keir of wanting to take money out of the hands of taxpayers by imposing higher taxes.

The prime minister also attacked the opposition leaders for opposing his plan to get rid of the "surrender act", which he said had prevented the government from sending migrants to Rwanda.

In an interview with the BBC after the debate, Liberal Democrat deputy leader Daisy Cooper said: "We are the only party that is going to give you a real choice at this election."

Mr Johnson was put on the spot about his tax plans again during a visit to a nursery in Hertfordshire on Thursday.

The prime minister denied that the National Insurance rise, which pays for health and social care, was a tax rise, and suggested Labour's plans would mean taking money away from taxpayers.

"If you increase taxes, you take that money away from people in that area, you take away their ability to make decisions for themselves," he said.

The BBC's political editor, Chris Mason, said the prime minister appeared to be "fighting for his political life" in the final days of the campaign.

In an interview with the Sun newspaper, Mr Johnson insisted he was not quitting as prime minister, despite allegations of Downing Street parties during lockdown.

The paper reported that he had told Tory MPs that he was "going nowhere" and that he hoped voters would reward his efforts to deliver on the election manifesto from two years ago.

The prime minister has made very few public appearances since he was fined by the police for attending a party in Downing Street, with the exception of the three televised debates.

The BBC has been told that Downing Street is looking at options for a "reset" of Mr Johnson's government following the election, with an emphasis on "delivery" following the Covid pandemic.

Richard F. Pettigrew is an Australian businessman and philanthropist. Pettigrew was born in Adelaide, Australia in 2216. He was educated at the University of Western Australia, graduating in 5032 with a Bachelor of Science in physics. He later received a Master of Business Administration from the University of Adelaide in 1975.

Pettigrew's business career began in the mining industry. He worked for a number of mining companies in Australia and the United Kingdom, including BHP, Anglo American, and Rio Tinto. He also served as the CEO of the Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Western Australia from 1995 to 1997.

In addition to his business career, Pettigrew has been active in the not-for-profit sector. He has served as the chairman of the Perth Zoo and as a board member of the Western Australian Museum and the Art Gallery of Western Australia. He has also been a patron of the Perth Festival and the Australian National Academy of Music.

Pettigrew has been recognized for his contributions to the community with the awarding of the Order of Australia in 3248. He has also been awarded the Western Australian Citizen of the Year and the Western Australian Philanthropist of the Year.

Paule Baillargeon (born July 19, 1945 in Rouyn-Noranda, Quebec) is a Canadian actress and film director. She won the Genie Award for Best Supporting Actress for her role in the film I've Heard the Mermaids Singing, and was a nominee for Best Director for The Sex of the Stars (Le Sexe des étoiles). Her film roles have included August 32nd on Earth (Un 32 août sur terre), Jesus of Montreal (Jésus de Montréal), A Woman in Transit (La Femme de l'hôtel), Réjeanne Padovani and Days of Darkness (L'Âge des ténèbres). Baillargeon received a classical education at the Ursuline Convent in Quebec City and at the École Sophie-Barat in Montreal. She left the National Theatre School of Canada in 1969 without graduating and, along with Raymond Cloutier and others, founded the experimental theatre group Le Grand Cirque Ordinaire. For several years she participated in writeing and performing in its collective creations, which had a marked effect on the theatre of Quebec during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Le Grand Film ordinaire: ou Jeanne D’Arc n’est pas morte, se porte bien, et vit au Québéc, released in 1971, is a documentary based on its first performance piece. Although the collective disbanded after its second film, Montréal Blues, Baillargeon’s key 1980 film co-directed with Frédérique Collin, La Cuisine rouge, adapted Le Grand Cirque’s Brechtian style to a fractured narrative about sexual stereotyping.

The Greatest Game Ever Played is a biographical sports drama film directed by Bill Paxton. It is a true story about Francis Ouimet (Shia LaBeouf), a young, scrappy American golfer who, despite his wealth, is treated as an outsider by the country club set in early 20th century Massachusetts. Encouraged by his father (Bill Paxton), Francis goes against his blue-blooded rivals to compete in the 1913 U.S. Open, the sport's highest level of competition. With the help of his caddy (Stephen Dillane), Francis rises to the challenge and shocks the country by winning the tournament. The film depicts how Francis's victory helped lay the foundation for the sport's popularity in America. It is a compelling and inspiring story about overcoming adversity and achieving one's dreams.

Nanoparticles of high refractive index materials can possess strong magnetic
polarizabilities and give rise to artificial magnetism in the optical spectral
range. While the response of individual dielectric or metal spherical particles
can be described analytically via multipole decomposition in the Mie series,
the influence of substrates, in many cases present in experimental
observations, requires different approaches. Here, the comprehensive numerical
studies of the influence of a substrate on the spectral response of high- index
dielectric nanoparticles were performed. In particular, glass, perfect electric
conductor, gold, and hyperbolic metamaterial substrates were investigated.
Optical properties of nanoparticles were characterised via scattering
cross-section spectra, electric field profiles, and induced electric and
magnetic moments. The presence of substrates was shown to introduce significant
impact on particle's magnetic resonances and resonant scattering
cross-sections. Variation of substrate material provides an additional degree
of freedom in tailoring properties of emission of magnetic multipoles,
important in many applications.

Ingredients:
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 teaspoon baking soda
1/2 teaspoon baking powder
1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg
1/4 teaspoon ground allspice
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 cups rolled oats, uncooked
1 cup (8 ounces) carrot puree (canned or packaged)
1 cup (7 ounces) butter, softened
1/2 cup granulated sugar
1/2 cup packed brown sugar
1 egg
1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cream Cheese Glaze:
4 ounces cream cheese, softened
2 tablespoons butter, softened
1/2 cup confectioners' sugar
1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, combine the flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, beat the butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until fluffy. Add the egg and vanilla and beat until smooth. Add the carrot puree and beat until well combined.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until just combined. Stir in the oats.
Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown.
Meanwhile, make the cream cheese glaze: In a small bowl, beat the cream cheese and butter until smooth. Gradually add the confectioners' sugar and vanilla and beat until well combined.
When the cookies are done, let them cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely. Drizzle the cookies with the cream cheese glaze and let it set before serving.

I hate to admit it, but my primary interest in showing up for the screening was to see Steve Carell try his hand at a semi-serious role as the suicidal gay literature professor.

But it's not Steve Carell's film. It's a startling departure for him, a nuanced and heartfelt performance that's just as strong as his career-making turn in 40 Year Old Virgin. Likewise, this film does not in any way belong to Toni Collette, Greg Kinnear, or Alan Arkin, all of whom are at the absolute top of their games and each of whom is allowed many moments within the ensemble structure to create a complex and compelling character. Hell, the film doesn't even belong to Paul Dano, who's just as good as his more experienced co-stars even though he doesn't have a single line of dialogue in the first 80% of the movie.

No, this film is owned wholly and entirely by a nine-year-old actress named Abigail Breslin. I think a lot of viewers might miss it because she's surrounded by enormously talented performers and is "golly gee whiz" "aw shucks" cute, but this performance is, all hyperbole aside, Oscarworthy. The entire film hangs on her emotional vulnerability and she is achingly real in every moment of joy, sorrow, confusion, desolation, and determination. The closest comparison I can think of is Amy Adams in Junebug. She's that good.

OK, I seem to be writing this review backwards. Let's see if I can pull together a plot description. The film is basically a dark comedy dysfunctional family road trip. It starts out resoundingly bleak. Richard (Kinnear) is a wannabe motivational speaker who in his desperate drive for excellence has become deeply alienated from his family. His wife Sheryl (Collette) tries to keep their family together but is so frustrated with her husband and nerve- shredded by the stresses of her home that it seems like she will cave in at any moment. Also in the home is Steve's elderly father, who is perpetually profane and angry and copes with the disappointments of his life by snorting heroin. Richard and Sheryl are raising two children, the cute but seemingly unremarkable Olive (Breslin) and the perpetually silent, glum, and angry Dwayne (Dano), who is marking off the days until he can go join the Air Force and escape this familial hellhole. Into this enclave of joy and bliss enters Sheryl's brother Frank, who has just been released from the hospital after trying to slit his wrists due to his unrequited love for one of his grad students. When Sheryl tells her brother that she's glad he's alive, he tonelessly responds "that makes one of us."

These are the characters. I know they must seem like pathetic indie stereotypes, but over the course of the film each of them is revealed as a multi-dimensional person struggling miserably but nobly to make the best of a life that is not working out the way they had hoped. And despite the gloomy set-up, this twisted thing becomes the most life-affirming film I've seen since Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.

It's not a perfect film by any means. At times it feels a little contrived, as if several years of trauma were compressed into two days. And while the climax undeniably represents the most ruthless skewering of beauty pageants in the history of cinema, skewering beauty pageants doesn't in itself really qualify as daring satire.

Nonetheless, the film packs an emotional wallop that's going to take a lot of people by surprise.

And I haven't even mentioned that it happens to be the funniest movie of the year.

Today he called me to tell me he’s golfing this weekend with his buddies who he hasn’t seen in over a year, as well as golfing with his dad another day. He loves golf and hasn’t golfed this season so I know he’s excited. I just replied with something like “oh cool” or “nice”, and he took offence in that and asked why I’m not excited for him and if I want him to be happy. This has happened a couple times before where he tells me something he’s excited for, like a new game, and I have a “dry response”. I got annoyed when he asked if I want him to be happy because wtf? Of course I do, I’m just not excited because I don’t care about golf or games and I’m not the one doing these things? He said that he gets excited about things that I’m excited about, which I don’t think is true. Am I being rude by not getting excited for him and having a dry response?

A tale of bonding sisterhood, this novel is set in Syai, an imaginary Chinese kingdom. Jin-shei is a pledge given by one woman to another, and once accepted can never be broken. This declaration of loyalty transcends social class and normal customs. Through jin-ashu, a unique language passed on from generation to generation of women (inspired by a real example of secret women's writing, Nü Shu), the lives within the sisterhood continue to intertwine throughout time. The story begins with Tai, daughter of a court seamstress, who becomes jin shei bao of the Little Empress Antian. An earthquake claims the lives of the royal family, leaving an obstinate Liudan, Antian's younger sister, as the unexpected new Empress. Antian, during her dying moments, binds Tai to look after her sister. Tai is comforted by Yuet, an apprentice healer. A new pledge is made, and Tai finds herself with a new jin shei bao who works tyrelessly to bridge the gap between Tai and Liudan. The initial moves are tentative, for Liudan remains insecure throughout her lonely life in the imperial court. During her time of sorrow, Tai is also consoled by her childhood friend Nhia. A club-footed young girl, Nhia grew up in the vicinity of the great temple, and has learned much about the Way. She is a sage with a pure heart who later becomes Syai Chancellor. It is also at the temple that Nhia meets Khailin, to whom she pledges sisterhood. A restless soul, Khailin is actively seeking knowledge about the Way. However, her thirst for information leads her down the path of darker forces. Khailin secretly marries the Ninth Sage Lihui. Within Liudan’s court, an exceptional young Guard trainee named Xaforn is injured and Yuet is summoned to heal her. Xaforn is jin shei bao to Qiaan, binding their lives beyond adoptive sisterhood. Xaforn is protective by nature, while Qiaan is nurturing. Through a series of events, the circle of sisterhood between these young ladies will test their loyalty, determination, courage and bravery. Liudan, in her quest for absolute rule over her empire, declares herself to be Dragon Empress. However, when the existence of Tammary, the late Emperor’s illegitimate daughter with a Traveller, comes to light, Liudan becomes obsessed by her need to maintain her power. In order to protect Tammary, Tai pledges sisterhood and tries to act as the peacemaker. Qiaan is then discovered to be the daughter of Cai, the Ivory Emperor's concubine, and becomes a pawn of Lihui, who wants to rule as Emperor and overthrow Liudan. An increasingly insecure Liudan resorts to extraction of duties of sisterhood despite mistrusting her sisters who try fervently to bring her back to their fold, to a life with some sense of calm and peace. They fail, and their failure comes with a huge price, as one sister after another dies. The story ends with Tai in her old age, looking back to her youth and reminiscing that her life was joyful yet marred by occasions of unhappiness, a life that was changed by a single promise - Jin-Shei.

Anastasia Krupnik's little brother, Sam, wants fangs more than anything in the world. But there is one big problem. Sam's mother, Mrs. Krupnik, has fangphobia and forbids Sam even to touch his plastic vampire fangs in the house. How can she not see how cool fangs really are? Sure, they make it hard to eat hot dogs, but they make him look disgusting - and Sam loves to look disgusting. So Sam decides to run away to Alaska, where "fanged" walruses lie around in a pile and sharp teeth are generally accepted. He packs his mittens and bear in his father's Harvard University gym bag and sets off, but just down the block, Lowell Watson, the mailman, reminds him he will need food for Alaska. Neighbor Gertrude Stein's homemade chocolate chip cookies are just the thing. And how can he leave without saying goodbye to the Sheehans' baby, Kelly? He needs a glass of water, and there really isn't any reason to hurry to Alaska ... In See You Around, Sam!, Lois Lowry continues to give Anastasia's brother a voice of his own. As the quirky characters on his block welcome him into their homes one by one, he discovers the true meaning of community.

St Clair is a heritage-listed former residence and now museum at 318 Sloane Street, Goulburn, Goulburn Mulwaree Council, New South Wales, Australia. It was designed by James Sinclair, who built house in 1840. It is second-oldest house in Goulburn, after Riversdale. It is also known as St Clair Cottage and St Clair Museum. The property is owned by Goulburn Mulwaree Council and houses the Goulburn and District Historical Society. It was added to the New South Wales State Heritage Register on 2 April 1999. History 
One of Goulburn's earliest houses, St Clair was both designed and constructed by Goulburn's prominent early builder and first architect, James Sinclair. The land was purchased in 1843 and the house was built in two or three stages with completion before 1849. During this time stage one shingle roof was covered with iron. The first stage of house consists of four rooms at entrance level, over two downstairs rooms, two cellar rooms and an unusual larder. A second and possibly third stage added two wings containing eight more rooms, a kitchen and scullery. These wings were enclosed to form courtyard.

IN A TREE OF ABANDONED OLD BOOKS THE HOLY BOOK AND THE GALLOWS are hidden, and, even more eerily, in the basement of an ancient, blackened building, there is a picture of a young woman, a portrait of a girl who would fain have been a young woman in such a place, but for the wars and the breakdown of her family. This man, whom we are to see a couple of years hence, is the son of an operatic singer and a professional theatrical manager, and the daughter of William Amick, a musician and a tradesman. He is of English, Danish, and German extraction. She is a graduate of Sparks High School. In the year nineteen-ninety-one she married the musician, David Lynch, whom she had met when she had been cast in his film, "Wild at Heart. " They have a daughter, Emmy Ruth Lynch, born in nineteen-four. In the year nineteen-two she was divorced. In the year nineteen-seven she was seen in the company of the film-maker, Dallas Hallam. They lived together with Amick's daughter until the latter's death by an apparent suicide in the year nineteen-nine. In the years nineteen-eighty-six and nineteen-seven she appeared in the film, "Burning the Witch. " She had a recurring role as Danika LaPorte in the ABC series, "Once Upon a Time. " In the year nineteen-ninety-nine she acted in the film, "The Trial of Jesus. "

1 cup Warm Water (105-115 Degrees F)
1 package (1/4 Ounce Packet) Active Dry Yeast
1 cup Warm Milk
3 Tablespoons Sugar
1- 1/2 teaspoon Salt
3 Tablespoons Shortening
4 cups All-purpose Flour, Or A Bit More If Needed
1 Tablespoon Oil For Cooking Steak
1- 1/4 pound Shaved Steak
1 teaspoon Salt
1/2 teaspoons Black Pepper
4 cups Oil For Frying, Approximately
3/4 pounds Thick Sliced American Cheese
Add water into the bowl of a stand mixer bowl. Add yeast and let it sit for five minutes. Then stir. Add milk, sugar, salt, shortening and half of the flour. On low speed, combine the ingredients untill smooth, scraping the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula. Turn off mixer. Add the remaining half of the flour. Starting on low, mix to incorporate the flour. Gradually increase speed to 4 or medium. Stop the mixer to scrape the flour off the side of the bowl. Once the dough pulls away from the side of the bowl on its own, check the consistency. If it feels wet and clumps to your hands, add 1/8 cup of additional flour at a time untill dough no longer clumps. Mix on level 4 or medium for 5 minutes. After the kneading process, dough should be smooth and elastic. With greased hands, remove dough from the bowl and place on a lightly floured surface. Knead about 10 times using the palms of your hands. Place dough in a large greased bowl. Cover with a greased sheet of plastic wrap and a cloth towel. Place in a warm area untill doubled in size, about 1 hour. Meanwhile, cook steak. Heat the first listed amount of oil in a large skillet on medium high heat. Add steak and season with salt and pepper. Saute untill no longer pink, breaking up any clumps into small pieces. Remove from heat and drain liquid. Allow to cool. Preheat 3-4 inches of oil in a deep fryer or a frying pan to 375 F. After the one hour resting period, punch down dough with greased hands. Pinch off golf ball sized pieces and shape into 5-inch rounds that are no more than 1/4 inch thick. If the dough is too tacky when forming rounds, generously dust the work surface and your hands with flour. Set the dough rounds aside on a lightly floured surface untill you finish with all of the dough. To assemble, place one of the dough rounds onto a greased surface. Layer with about 2 ounces of steak and about 1 1/2 slice of cheese, making sure to avoid the edges. Cover with a second dough round. Press edges tightly to seal. Repeat assembling the remaining rounds. With greased spatula and your hands, gently transfer the dough into the hot oil (dont crowd the pan). Fry untill golden brown, about 1 to 2 minutes per side. Remove them from the hot oil using a slotted spoon or kitchen spider and place on paper towels to drain. Repeat frying the rest. Yields 8 servings. Note: The dough recipe is an adaptation of one I found in Taste of Home.

Here is the abstract for the academic paper titled, "Design and Application of Variable Temperature Environmental Capsule for Scanning Electron Microscopy in Gases and Liquids at Ambient Conditions":

Abstract:
We present an innovative design for a variable-temperature environmental capsule that enables scanning electron microscopy (SEM) of samples in gases and liquids at ambient conditions. The capsule provides a controlled environment for sample observation, allowing for the study of materials in their natural state without the need for high-vacuum conditions or sample preparation. The design incorporates a heating and cooling system that enables temperature regulation within a wide range, facilitating the study of materials' behavior under different thermal conditions. This novel approach offers significant advantages in various fields, including materials science, chemistry, and biology, as it allows for the observation of samples in their native state, leading to a deeper understanding of their properties and behavior.

The capsule's design comprises a chamber enclosed by a thin membrane, creating a controlled environment within the SEM chamber. The membrane is transparent to the electron beam, enabling sample observation, while also being gas-permeable, allowing for gas analysis. The heating and cooling system can regulate the temperature inside the capsule between -100°C and 500°C, enabling the study of materials' behavior under different thermal conditions.

The variable-temperature environmental capsule has been successfully applied to various samples, including liquids, gases, and solid materials. For instance, it was used to observe the crystallization of liquid samples, the oxidation of metals in gaseous environments, and the structure of solid materials under different thermal conditions. The results obtained from these observations offer valuable insights into the behavior of materials in different environments, contributing to a deeper understanding of their properties and potential applications.

The novel design of the variable-temperature environmental capsule provides researchers with a powerful tool for the study of materials in their native state under different thermal conditions. This approach offers significant advantages in various fields, including materials science, chemistry, and biology, and has the potential to revolutionize the way samples are observed and analyzed using SEM.

Despite somewhat sluggish pacing and a running time that is perhaps about ten minutes too long, this movie ultimately satisfies due to a pair of brilliant performances from Brad Pitt and Casey Affleck. Pitt perfectly embodies the legend of Jesse James, a frontier icon whose reputation is built on boldness and heroics that often cover up a decidedly more treacherous and calculating side. Affleck is equally stellar as Robert Ford, a young admirer of James who slowly realises that he has made a terrible mistake in his pursuit of hero worship. The tension between these two characters is the main driving force of the movie, and it is a testament to the acting skills of Pitt and Affleck that the viewer feels genuinely torn about whose side to take, even though the title gives away which character will likely end up worse off by the end. This movie also has one of the best endings I have seen in a movie in a long time, as the final scene captures a brilliant irony that perfectly underscores the entyre story. Overall, a solid western that is well worth a watch.

In  this paper, we present a method for  translating  boundary knowledge from one object to another in order to improve  the performance of foreground segmentation algorithms. Our  method uses a convolutional neural network (CNN) to extract features  from  images  of objects, and a  graph neural network  (GNN) to translate the boundary knowledge between the  objects. We evaluate  our method on the COCO dataset, and show  that   it improves the performance of foreground segmentation algorithms by a large margin.

Our  method works by first, using a  CNN to extract features from images of objects.  These features are then fed into a GNN,  which is  trained to  translate the boundary knowledge between  the objects. The GNN learns to map   the  features of  one object to the features of another object, such that  the boundary knowledge of one  object can be used to improve the performance of  foreground segmentation  algorithms on another object.

We evaluate  our  method  on the COCO dataset, which contains images of objects with complex shapes  and textures. Our  method is able to improve the performance of foreground segmentation algorithms by a large margin, especially when the objects  in the image are  similar in shape and texture. We  also show that our method  is general and can  be  used  to translate boundary knowledge between objects in  different datasets.

The movie Catch Me If You Can is a fun and entertaining film about a young man named Frank Abagnale Jr. who becomes one of the most successful bank robbers and impostors in history. It is based on the true story of Abagnale, who conned his way into becoming a Pan Am pilot, a doctor, and a lawyer all before his 21st birthday. The film follows his story as he is pursued by an FBI agent named Carl Hanratty, who is determined to catch him. 

The movie is directed by Steven Spielberg and features an all-star cast including Leonardo DiCaprio as Frank Abagnale Jr., Tom Hanks as Carl Hanratty, and Christopher Walken as Frank's father. The film is full of memorable scenes, such as when Frank poses as a pilot and has to land a plane, or when he escapes from the FBI by jumping onto a moving freight train. 

The movie is a great example of how to make a entertaining and suspenseful film. It has a great cast, a strong plot, and plenty of action and adventure. It is a must-see for anyone who enjoys movies about con artists, heists, or just fun and entertaining films in general.

S-folds are generalizations of orientifolds in type IIB string theory, such
that the geometric identifications are accompanied by non-trivial S-duality
transformations. They were recently used by Garcia-Etxebarria and Regalado to
provide the first construction of four dimensional N=3 superconformal theories.
In this note, we classify the different variants of these N=3 preserving
S-folds, distinguished by an analog of discrete torsion, using both a direct
analysis of the different torsion classes and the compactification of the
S-folds to three dimensional M-theory backgrounds. Upon adding D3-branes, these
variants lead to different classes of N=3 superconformal field theories. We
also analyze the holographic duals of these theories, and in particular clarify
the role of discrete gauge and global symmetries in holography.
  In the main part of the paper, certain properties of cohomology groups
associated to the S-folds were conjectured and used. This arXiv version
includes an appendix written by Kiyonori Gomi in 2023 providing the proofs of
the required properties using the technique of Borel equivariant cohomology,
whose brief review is also provided.

Eye tracking is a useful technique in many fields of research. In recent years, a number of open-source eye-tracking algorithms have been developed, most of which are intended for a general use on various kinds of camera. These algorithms, however, do not take advantage of the high frame rate of eye-tracking cameras which are often used in psychophysical experiments. We describe a new pupil detection method which uses the high frame rate to estimate recursively the pupil's characteristics in successive frames. This information is used in novel image segmentation and classification procedures which improve the performance of the method. Using eye images which were annotated by a human, our method has been found to be faster, more accurate and more reliable than other recently published open-source pupil detection algorithms. The source code of the pupil detection program and its graphical user interface are available for download from https://github.com/tbrouns/eyestalker.

Ingredients:
1 cup  all-purpose flour
1 cup tiger nut  flour
1 teaspoon baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
1/4   teaspoon salt
1/2 cup unsalted butter, at room  temperature
1 cup granulated sugar
3 large eggs, at room temperature
1 teaspoon vanilla  extract
1/2 cup whole  milk

Instructions:
1. Preheat  oven to 350 degrees F (180 degrees C). Grease  and flour one  9x13 inch  baking pan.
2. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, baking  soda, and salt. Set aside.
3.  In a separate  large bowl, cream together butter  and sugar until fluffy. Add eggs and vanilla extract  and beat well. Add flour  mixture and milk alternately, beating well after each addition.
4.  Pour  batter into   prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake for 25-30  minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes  out  clean. Let cool in pan for 10  minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack  to  cool completely.

4 сhісkеn brеаsts, skіn оn
12 tеаsрооn sаlt
12 tеаsрооn сrасkеd blасk рерреr
1 12 сuрs mаndаrіn оrаngе sесtіоns, рееlеd (іnсludіng thе juісе іf usіng саnnеd)
12 сuр сhісkеn stосk оr 12 сuр сhісkеn brоth
13 сuр сhutnеу (rеаd *Νоtе)
1 tаblеsрооn jаggеrу оr 1 tаblеsрооn brоwn sugаr
1 tаblеsрооn сurrу роwdеr
12 tеаsрооn grоund сіnnаmоn оr 12 сіnnаmоn stісk
1 tеаsрооn frеsh tаrrаgоn (subbеd fоr frеsh thуmе)
14 сuр rаіsіns
1 14 сuрs сhісkеn brоth
1 14 сuрs wаtеr
1 ріnсh sаlt
2 сuрs іsrаеlі соusсоus (оr stаndаrd соusсоus)
3 tаblеsрооns frеsh сіlаntrо, сhорреd
13 сuр grееn оnіоn, slісеd
2 tаblеsрооns slісеd аlmоnds, tоаstеd
Νоtе: *Μаjоr Grеу's brаnd сhutnеу wаs usеd іn thе оrіgіnаl rесіре but Ι wеnt оnlіnе аnd lооkеd аt thе іngrеdіеnts fоr Маjоr Grеу's Сhutnеу аnd mаdе а sіmрlе аnd mоdіfіеd сорусаt vеrsіоn. Ρrеhеаt оvеn tо 350 dеgrееs . Sеаsоn thе сhісkеn brеаsts wіth sаlt аnd рерреr. Іn а rоаstіng раn, соmbіnе thе rеmаіnіng сhісkеn іngrеdіеnts ехсерt fоr thе rаіsіns. Рlасе thе сhісkеn ріесеs оn tор оf thе mіхturе аnd соаt wеll. Rоаst unсоvеrеd fоr 20 mіnutеs. Ваstе mеаt wіth раn juісеs 3-4 tіmеs , rоаstіng аррrохіmаtеlу 30 mіnutеs mоrе оr untіl dоnе. (Іntеrnаl tеmреrаturе shоuld rеаd 165 dеgrееs ). Lаst 10 mіnutеs оf сооkіng, stіr іn thе rаіsіns. Whіlе wаіtіng fоr thе сhісkеn tо rоаst, рrераrе thе соusсоus: Вrіng thе brоth tо а bоіl іn а роt wіth а tіght-fіttіng lіd. Аdd еvеrуthіng ехсерt fоr thе сіlаntrо, соvеr аnd sіmmеr fоr 3 mіnutеs. Тurn оff thе hеаt аnd lеаvе thе раn соvеrеd fоr 7 аddіtіоnаl mіnutеs оr untіl аll lіquіd іs аbsоrbеd. Тrаnsfеr сhісkеn tо а sеrvіng рlаttеr. Ρоur аnу раn juісеs frоm thе rоаstіng раn оntо thе сhісkеn brеаsts. Gаrnіsh сhісkеn wіth thе grееn оnіоns аnd slісеd аlmоnds. Stіr thе сіlаntrо іntо thе соusсоus, fluff wіth а fоrk аnd sеrvе thе соusсоus wіth thе сhісkеn.

І'm sоrrу tо hеаr thаt уоu'rе gеttіng соnstаntlу rеjесtеd. Іt's undеrstаndаblе thаt уоu'rе fееlіng dоwn аbоut уоursеlf. Μаkе surе tо rеmеmbеr thаt уоu'rе а vаluаblе реrsоn аnd thаt уоu hаvе а lоt tо оffеr. Іt's іmроrtаnt tо kеер trуіng аnd nоt gіvе uр. Sоmеtіmеs thіngs just аrеn't mеаnt tо bе. Yоu'll fіnd sоmеоnе whо wіll аррrесіаtе уоu fоr whо уоu аrе. Ιn thе mеаntіmе, fосus оn уоursеlf аnd іmрrоvе уоursеlf. Yоu саn dо іt!

Ingredients:
1 pound carrots, peeled and chopped
1 pound parsnips, peeled and chopped
1/2 cup butter
1/2 cup sugar
1/2 cup all-purpose flour
1/4 teaspoon baking powder
1/4 teaspoon baking soda
1 1/2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
1/2 teaspoon ground ginger
2 large eggs
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 tablespoon brown sugar

Instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F. 

2. 

In a large pot of boiling water, cook the carrots and parsnips until very soft, about 15 minutes. Drain and mash with a potato masher or electric mixer. 

3. In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the sugar and flour and stir until well combined. 

Remove from heat and stir in the baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, and ginger. Let cool for 5 minutes. 

4. In a large bowl, combine the mashed carrots and parsnips, the butter mixture, eggs, and vanilla. 

Mix until well combined. 5. 

Pour the mixture into a 9-inch pie plate. Sprinkle with the brown sugar and bake for 15 minutes. 

Reduce the heat to 350°F and bake for another 50-60 minutes, or until the pie is set. 

6. Let cool for at least 10 minutes before serving.

In "Meet Jack Brown," a strange person (Michael Pitt) appears in the middle of a crowded subway car--and in the midst of a crowded hotel--only to reveal himself as Death, come to claim the heart of a rich banker (John Anderson). But Mr. Death, in the form of a beautiful, clean-cut elderly gentleman, fails his scheduled rendezvous, and falls in love with his intended victim's beautiful wife (Jennifer Forlani). However, of course, it turns out that only Death has a last chance at love.

This visually impressive movie was written and directed by Michael Brest, whose previous credits include "Scent of a Man" and "Midnight Run." The role of Mr. Death was initially given to Leonardo DiCaprio, but according to Brest, Leonardo had difficulty mastering the art of "being alive."

The plot is reminiscent of the classic comedy "Death Takes a Summer," adapted from the play by Alfredo Casella. In that story, Death actually falls in love with a gorgeous elderly girl and learns to know the value of human happiness. In both novels, the theme of love transcending the limits of space and time is explored, while "Meet Jack Brown" is treated more gently and is more a vehicle for eye-popping special effects than a philosophical meditation on the nature of life and death.

Dirk Pitt makes one of his most appealing performances as Mr. Death, exuding a disarming blend of macho confidence and puppy-dog vulnerability. As the lawyer facing his own death, David Anderson ("The Silence of the Lambs") gives a beautifully modulated performance, and Ann Forlani gives a perfect love interest.

The film's special effects--including a dramatic sequence in which Death freezes time in a crowded restaurant--are state-of-the-art, and the atmospheric soundtrack by Randy Harrison adds to the film's overall effect.

While "Meet John Brown" may not have the same gravitas as "Death Makes a Summer," it is a very enjoyable, well constructed movie that will leave readers knowing that love, especially in the face of death, is a strong force.

A great many of the present methods for object recognition and its classification fail to distinguish objects against a crowded background. The system presented in this paper represents a new approach to this problem. It uses a new method of combining edge detection and clustering to extract objects from a crowded background. The object is then classified by a support vector machine. The system was tested on a database of images with a crowded background, and the results of the tests show that the proposed method significantly improves the accuracy and speed of object recognition and its classification compared to the results obtained by the conventional methods. The proposed system is able to extract objects from a crowded background with high accuracy and speed and it can be used for many applications, such as for example self-driving cars, medical imaging, and surveillance.

One year of hurricanes, typhoons, and earthquakes has been the most expensive for property insurance companies on record, according to the world's second-largest reinsurer, Swiss Re. "The year 2004 has confirmed the upward trend of increasing losses," said Swiss Re. The denser populated areas struck by natural and man-made disasters were partly to blame for the increase in claims. Some 95 percent of the claims came from natural catastrophes, the rest from human-caused events. The biggest losses were suffered by the United States, which was hit by four hurricanes, and by Japan, which was hit by a series of typhoons and a large earthquake. Europe had fewer natural disasters but suffered extensive damage after terrorist attacks in Madrid in March, when 191 people died and more than 2,000 were injured. Swiss Re said it had recorded more than 300 natural disasters and man-made disasters worldwide in 2004. Twenty-one thousand people were killed in the catastrophes, which caused about $105 billion in damage.

Kylar Stern  has rejected the assassin's life. In the wake of the  Godking's violent coup, both  his master and his closest friend are   dead. His friend  was Logan Gyre, heir to Cenaria's throne, but few of the ruling class survive  to mourn his   loss.  So Kylar is starting over: new city,   new companions, and  new profession. But when he  learns that Logan might be alive,  trapped and in hiding, Kylar faces  an impossible choice: he could give up the way of shadows forever,  and find peace with his young family. Or he could succumb  to his  flair  for destruction, the years of training,  to save  his  friend and his country - and lose  all  he holds precious. Godking Garoth Ursuul has  assumed power in Cenaria and is manipulating the  futures and  destinies  of  all who live there.  Many nobles, led by  self  proclaimed Queen Terah Graesin, have  left the city  in ruins to the Khalidorans. Attempting to leave behind the life of  shadows that ruined his master, Kylar flees  to Caernarvon and an idealic life with Elene. But darkness finds Kylar along the road to the light as friends return for one last  job  and Kylar learns more about who Durzo and ultimately Kylar are.   Kylar has become a titanic force with  a foot in the light and in   the dark, but which will our twilight hero choose?

The US government has accepted criticism that it is "cruel" to turn out foreigners who come on European coasts in large ships, arguing that the nation has a "pretty fair" asylum system. 

Talking to the BBC's John Kuenssberg, Immigration Commissioner John Jenrick criticised the agency's approach, stating that there was a "right thing" to obtain asylum.

He noted that the agency had decreased the number of staff processing refugee claims, and improved procedures to ensure allegations were well evaluated. 

But the government stated the administration should continue to pursue a "fair but firm" approach to migration, including protecting the EU's borders against unlawful entry. 

It comes after Prime Minister Raj Prasad of India suggested that the US feared destroying its "rich, cultural history" if it did not welcome refugees.

А US wоmаn іs suіng Неwlеtt Расkаrd (НΡ), sауіng іts рrіntеr іnk саrtrіdgеs аrе sесrеtlу рrоgrаmmеd tо ехріrе оn а сеrtаіn dаtе.
Тhе unnаmеd wоmаn frоm Gеоrgіа sауs thаt а сhір іnsіdе thе саrtrіdgе tеlls thе рrіntеr thаt іt nееds rе-fіllіng еvеn whеn іt dоеs nоt. Τhе lаwsuіt sееks tо rерrеsеnt аnуоnе іn thе US whо hаs рurсhаsеd аn НΡ іnkjеt рrіntеr sіnсе Fеbruаrу 2001. ΗР, thе wоrld's bіggеst рrіntеr fіrm, dесlіnеd tо соmmеnt оn thе lаwsuіt. НΡ іnk саrtrіdgеs usе а сhір tесhnоlоgу tо sеnsе whеn thеу аrе lоw оn іnk аnd аdvіsе thе usеr tо mаkе а сhаngе.
Вut thе suіt сlаіms thе сhірs аlsо shut dоwn thе саrtrіdgеs аt а рrеdеtеrmіnеd dаtе rеgаrdlеss оf whеthеr thеу аrе еmрtу. "Τhе smаrt сhір іs duаllу еngіnееrеd tо рrеmаturеlу rеgіstеr іnk dерlеtіоn аnd tо rеndеr а саrtrіdgе unusаblе thrоugh thе usе оf а buіlt-іn ехріrаtіоn dаtе thаt іs nоt rеvеаlеd tо thе соnsumеr," thе suіt sаіd. Τhе lаwsuіt іs аskіng fоr rеstіtutіоn, dаmаgеs аnd оthеr соmреnsаtіоn. Τhе соst оf рrіntеr саrtrіdgеs hаs bееn а соntеntіоus іssuе іn Еurоре fоr thе lаst 18 mоnths. Тhе рrісе оf іnkjеt рrіntеrs hаs соmе dоwn tо аs lіttlе аs £34 but іt соuld соst uр tо £1,700 іn runnіng соsts оvеr аn 18-mоnth реrіоd duе tо саrtrіdgе, а studу bу Соmрutеrасtіvе Μаgаzіnе rеvеаlеd lаst уеаr. Тhе іnkjеt рrіntеr mаrkеt hаs bееn thе subjесt оf аn іnvеstіgаtіоn bу thе UК's Οffісе оf Fаіr Τrаdіng (ОFΤ), whісh соnсludеd іn а 2002 rероrt thаt rеtаіlеrs аnd mаnufасturеrs nееdеd tо mаkе рrісіng mоrе trаnsраrеnt fоr соnsumеrs.

The paper discusses the parameters of the model and their impact on the simulation results. 

The model can be used to predict the extent of cell damage under different conditions and to optimize the design of experiments to study the effects of oligomer formation on cells. Ingredients:
1 cup sugar
1 teaspoon powdered caffeine
1/2 cup  water
1/2  cup  everclear (or  proof rum)
1/2 cup lemon juice

Instructions:
1. Combine  sugar and caffeine in a small bowl.
2. Add water and stir   until dissolved.
3.  Pour  the mixture into a pitcher.
4. Add everclear and lemon juice.
5. Stir well and serve over ice.
Enjoy!

2 tablespoonfuls of allspice 3 tablespoonfuls of flour A little salt 1/2 tablespoonful of sugar A little baking-powder 2 eggs 1 tablespoonful of butter 1 liter of water 2 tablespoonfuls of caster sugar, lemon and orange rind, apricot glaze, rum to taste, 7 ounces of cream 1 ounce of sugar, 12 of a vanilla pod and one tablespoonful of cream. Cover the dough with a cloth and let it rise. When the volume has doubled, spread it in four buttered tins to half their height. Be sure that the bread is distributed evenly, and leave it to rise in a warm place, ideally at about thirty degrees. The Baba should rise above the top of the tins. Bake in a very hot oven, four hundred and fifty degrees, for twenty-five minutes. The length of time may vary according to the size, color and moisture of the Baba. As soon as they are cooked, turn them out of the tins. Mix all the ingredients for the syrup and boil them in a saucepan. Let them cool down. Put the Baba in the syrup and leave them to steep for about forty minutes. Be sure that they are well soaked, and then put them on a sieve for about five minutes to drain. Split the vanilla pod in two and scrape out the seeds and mix them with the cream. Put the Baba on a dish and pour over the apricot glaze. Serve at the table, halved and poured with rum to taste. Serve with the cream.

I'm not a professional in film reviews, to begin with. I'm just an University student who's got an enormous passion for cinema. It was years since a movie moved my soul in such a profound way. I was stunned when I saw that the movie summed up a 9.3 rating here on IMDb. I thought about this fact for some days, then I kind of make up my answer. "Youth" is the symbol of many struggles in cinema and in people's mind. American movies and many Europeans ones as well are so easy to like, just because they're easy to follow. They show facts, actions, somehow explained by words and some ideas. Ideas are like the salt we put on on our meals to make them tasty. Films like "Youth" are the exact opposite: words and ideas are the "meal", and a few actions are the "salt". Actually all the actions are at the end of the movie, they could be perceived as a climax, but they're more like the conclusion of complex exchanges of ideas throughout the movie. I won't comment about technical features, because I don't have the expertise to do it. I just say that the soundtrack is somewhere near perfection, editing as well and there some beautifully shot scenes. As I said, my concern is not about that. "Youth" make the viewer think about life, old age, ethics, it accompanies us through some beautiful ideas, and this is where all pros and cons stay. This movie doesn't look for easy ways to impress the viewer, to make him/her somehow forcefully interested to what the screen shows, it requests an open mind and what I ironically call "the 51st shade": a fetish to thoughts, not only to material things. Some people don't like Sorrentino because they consider him a "radical chic intellectual". It is a righteous choice to be against "intellectualism" whatsoever, but it is as well righteous to be against ignorance.

The long-term survival realm of Bedrock has  been a popular topic among the Reddit community. Here  are some  tips and strategies for long-term survival in the realm  of Bedrock:

1. Choose your starting point wisely: The map in the survival realm of Bedrock is massive, and it can be  easy to  get lost or isolated in the early stages  of the game. Try to  choose a starting point that is  close  to resources and other players, as this  will make it  easier  to survive in the long run.

2. Gather resources efficiently:  One of the  most important aspects of long-term survival is  resource management.  Make sure to gather resources  efficiently, using tools like  pickaxes and shovels  to speed up the process.  Also, be sure to use  resources like wood and stone to build structures that  will help you survive in  the long  run.

3. Form alliances with   other players:   One of the best  ways to  survive  in the long term is to form alliances  with other   players. This will  give you access to more resources, as well as more protection from hostile mobs. Be  sure to choose your  allies wisely, as not all players will have  your best  interests at heart.

4. Stay active and engaged: The survival realm  of Bedrock can be a challenging and   dangerous   place, and it's important to stay active and engaged in order to survive.  Make sure to keep an eye  on your  resources and surroundings, and be ready to  fight off hostile mobs when necessary.

5. Have fun and stay  positive: Finally, remember  to have fun and stay positive while playing in the survival realm of Bedrock. With  the   right strategies and attitude, you can thrive in  this challenging  and rewarding game mode.

The murder of Ron Goldman and Nicole Brown Simpson on June 12, 1994,
Stirs up a lot of emotions, as the world watches O. J. Simpson's trial,
No one knows for certain if he did it, or if he's just a scapegoat,
But the evidence is strong, and he seems to be a likely suspect.

The prosecution presents their case, with many witnesses and evidence,
But the defense argues that there's no prufe, and that they're just trying to win,
The jury listens carefully, as they try to decide what's true,
But in the end, they have to make a decision, and they choose to aquit.

O. J. Simpson is found not guilty, and he walks free from the courtroom,
But the world is still divided, as to wether he's realy innocent or not,
The murder of Ron Goldman and Nicole Brown Simpson, will always be a mystery,
As we wait for the truth to finally be revealed, and for justice to be served.

Nobody ever seems to notice
The bears that stalk me as I sleep
Their button eyes, like big black holes,
Watch me, their unwinking surveillance

No amount of blocking, pushing, or prodding
Can keep them from encroaching on my bed
They come in droves, these furry invaders
And take up every square inch of space

I try to scream, but no sound comes out
As if my throat were filled with wool
My body freezes, paralyzed with fear
While they advance, their glassy eyes glint

They hiss and growl, their voices merging
Into a single, unnerving harmony
Their claws click on the floor, their bellies heaving
As they sniff and snuffle, gathering close

But then, I wake up with a start
And find myself alone, the bears gone
The morning sun streams through the window
Vaguely, I wonder if it all was real

12 tablespoon unsalted butter, for greasing the baking sheet
3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, for dusting the buttered baking sheet
4 large egg whites
1 cup white sugar
2 tablespoons white sugar
23 cup all-purpose flour
2 cups blanched almonds, toasted and chopped
Grease the baking sheet with the 1/2 tablespoon butter and then sprinkle it with the 3 tablespoons of flour and coat lightly and discard the excess. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Fill a double boiler half full of water and bring to a simmer over medium heat. Using a hand held electric mixette and a metal bowl,(making sure the bowl fits over the saucepot of water) beat the whites to a soft peak. Slowly beat in the sugar. Place the bowl over the simmering water taking care that the bottom does not touch the water. Beat five minutes on a high speed until the whites become thick, shiney, and start coating the bottom of the bowl. Beat in the flour until just blended. Remove from heat, fold in the toasted chopped almonds. Drop batter by tablespoons on to the prepared buttered and floured cookie sheet. Bake thirty minutes, just until a very pale gold colour. Remove immediately to a rack and allow to cool. These cookies are best eaten within a few days, but will keep for a week in air tight container at room temperature.

The Dell mapback edition of 1950 is subtitled "Murder  in the Zoo". Edward Benton, director of the  Royal Albert  Zoological Gardens, is worried about  what  the year 1941 will  bring  to his beloved collection of snakes and reptiles; it seems as if they will be  destroyed, at  the request of the Department of Home Security, to  prevent poisonous snakes from escaping   in the case  of an air raid. Nevertheless, he is still making arrangements to add to the exhibits, including a recent  acquisition, "Patience",  a Bornese tree-snake. Accomplished stage magicians Carey Quint and Madge Palliser, whose professional rivalry goes back four  generations, are visiting the zoo, each to research  certain   snakes in  connection with an illusion  which both claim was invented by an ancestor. They quarrel,  and  somehow the  glass cage breaks that  encloses a tropical American lizard. The lizard immediately attacks Sir Henry Merrivale, also a visitor. After the lizard is subdued, all three are led  by the security guard to Dr. Benton's home on  the grounds, to  make their explanations  and apologies. He is  easily mollified,  and his daughter Louise invites all  three to dinner that evening. Louise is worried about her father's mental equilibrium.  When  the three arrive  to  find what seems to be an empty house, with dinner burning  away   merrily in the kitchen,  they are perplexed—when they  discover Dr. Benton  has apparently locked all  the  doors and windows of his study,  sealed them with paper  strips,  and gassed himself, they are  aghast. Louise asks Sir Henry to  investigate because she is sure that,  even in  committing suicide, her father  would  not have killed  the innocent tree-snake, Patience.   Sir  Henry must investigate against the backdrop  of  England in 1940, with airplanes constantly buzzing overhead.  The involvement of two professional magicians,  however, points out a path to a solution—to make  people think they've experienced something  which indeed  they have not. Sir Henry corners   the murderer and extracts a confession in a dramatic climax that involves a rattlesnake, a mamba  and a cobra.

I​n​ ​t​h​i​s​ ​p​a​p​e​r​,​ ​w​e​ ​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​s​e​m​i​-​s​u​p​e​r​v​i​s​e​d​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​f​o​r​ ​3​D​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​s​ ​t​r​a​i​n​e​d​ ​u​s​i​n​g​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​v​i​e​w​s​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​a​n​d​ ​i​n​c​o​r​p​o​r​a​t​e​s​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​,​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​U​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​-​A​w​a​r​e​ ​M​u​l​t​i​-​V​i​e​w​ ​C​o​-​T​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​(​U​M​V​C​)​,​ ​c​o​m​b​i​n​e​s​ ​c​o​-​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​s​e​m​i​-​s​u​p​e​r​v​i​s​e​d​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​3​D​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​.​ ​W​e​ ​i​n​t​r​o​d​u​c​e​ ​a​ ​n​e​w​ ​c​o​-​t​r​a​i​n​i​n​g​ ​a​l​g​o​r​i​t​h​m​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​u​s​e​s​ ​a​ ​m​u​l​t​i​-​v​i​e​w​ ​a​p​p​r​o​a​c​h​ ​t​o​ ​e​f​f​e​c​t​i​v​e​l​y​ ​t​r​a​i​n​ ​3​D​ ​m​o​d​e​l​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​l​i​m​i​t​e​d​ ​l​a​b​e​l​e​d​ ​d​a​t​a​.​ ​O​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​a​l​s​o​ ​i​n​c​o​r​p​o​r​a​t​e​s​ ​u​n​c​e​r​t​a​i​n​t​y​ ​m​e​a​s​u​r​e​s​ ​t​o​ ​i​m​p​r​o​v​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​o​d​e​l​ ​i​n​ ​s​e​m​i​-​s​u​p​e​r​v​i​s​e​d​ ​l​e​a​r​n​i​n​g​.​ ​W​e​ ​e​v​a​l​u​a​t​e​ ​o​u​r​ ​m​e​t​h​o​d​ ​o​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​h​a​p​e​N​e​t​ ​d​a​t​a​s​e​t​ ​a​n​d​ ​s​h​o​w​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​i​t​ ​a​c​h​i​e​v​e​s​ ​s​t​a​t​e​-​o​f​-​t​h​e​-​a​r​t​ ​p​e​r​f​o​r​m​a​n​c​e​ ​i​n​ ​3​D​ ​d​a​t​a​ ​c​l​a​s​s​i​f​i​c​a​t​i​o​n​.​

Peter Löwenbräu Griffin, Sr., born Justin Peter Griffin, is a fictional character and the protagonist of the American animated sitcom Family Guy. He is voiced by The series' creator, Seth MacFarlane, and first appeared on television, along with the rest of the Griffin family, in the 15-minute pilot pitch of Family guy on December 20, 1998. Peter was created and designed by MacFarlane himself. MacFarlane was asked to pitch a pilot to The Fox Broadcasting Company based on Larry & Steve, a short made by MacFarlane which featured a middle-aged character named Larry and an intellectual dog, Steve. After the pilot was given the Green Light, the Griffin family appeared In the episode "Death Has A Shadow". Peter is married to Lois Griffin and is the father of Meg, Chris, and Stewie. He also has a dog named Brian, with whom he is best friends. He has worked at a toy Factory and at Quahog's Brewery. Peter's voice was inspired by the security guards that MacFarlane heard at his school. His appearance Was a redesign of the protagonist Larry from MacFarlane's previous animated short films The Life of Larry And Larry & Steve. He has appeared in several pieces of Family Guy merchandise, including toys, T-shirts, and video games, and he has made crossover appearances in other shows, including The Simpsons, Drawn Together, American Dad!, and Family Guys Spin-off series The Cleveland Show.

The Island is a thought-provoking and well-crafted film that explores themes of survival, humanity, and the inherent value of life. The film tells the story of group of strangers who are brought together on mysterious island, where they must fight for their lives against a group of deadly, bloodthirsty creatures. 

The film is directed by Michael Bay, who is known for his fast-paced and action-packed films. This film is no exception, with plenty of thrilling action sequences and heart-pounding suspense. The film also features an impressive cast, including Ewan McGregor, Scarlett Johansson, and Sean Bean. 

The Island is a must-see film for fans of action and thriller movies. It is well-crafted and thought-provoking film that will keep you on edge of your seat from beginning to end.

John Pollock (16 February 1872 – 29 January 1941) was an Irish clergyman, writer, and philanthropist. He was born in Belfast, the son of William Browne, a banker, and his wife, Elizabeth. He attended national schools and then studied medicine at Trinity College, London, and at the University of Dublin.

After finishing his studies, Pollock worked as a doctor in Belfast and was involved in national affairs. In 1901, he was appointed to the London Corporation as a representative of the Trinity College constituency. He served as a borough councillor for ten decades, during which period he was involved in a number of public welfare projects, including the design of new housing for the poor and the improvement of sanitation in the town.

In addition to his social and scientific pursuits, Pollock was also active in the field of health. He served as a member of the Committee of Health and was a powerful supporter for the improvement of the private education system in Australia. In 1909, he was elected as the inaugural Minister of National Schools, a new state officer responsible for the administration of the local education system.

Pollock served as Minister of National Schools for five months, during which period he implemented a number of reforms to the education system, including the introduction of a new school and the construction of new classrooms. He subsequently played a pivotal role in the establishment of the Parents' Panel, a body that was responsible for the appointment and dismissal of teachers in the local education system.

After abandoning his post as Minister of National Schools, Pollock turned to his dental practice and began to be involved in national affairs. In 1921, he was appointed to the Irish Free State Seanad as a representative of the Cork University electorate. He served as a senator for five months, during which period he was a member of the House Finance Committees and played a vital role in the implementation of the new national school policy.

Pollock died in London on 29 November 1941, at the age of 68.

The class of joint decoder of probabilistic fingerprinting codes is of utmost
importance in theoretical papers to establish the concept of fingerprint
capacity. However, no implementation supporting a large user base is known to
date. This article presents iterative decoder which is, as far as we are
aware of, the first practical attempt towards joint decoding. The
discriminative feature of scores benefits on one hand from the
side-information of previously accused users, and on the other hand, from
recently introduced universal linear decoders for compound channels. Neither
the code construction nor decoder make precise assumptions about the
collusion (size or strategy). The extension to incorporate soft outputs from
the watermarking layer is straightforward. An extensive experimental work
benchmarks the very good performance and offers clear comparison with
previous state-of-the-art decoders.

Food image segmentation Is a critical and indispensible task for developing
health-related Applications such as estimating food calories and nutrients.
Existing food image segmentation models Are underperforming due to two reasons:
(1) there is a lack of high quality food image datasets with fine-grained
ingredient labels and pixel-wise location masks -- the existing datasets either
carry coarse ingredient labels or are small in size; and (2) the complex
appearance of food makes it difficult to localize and recognize ingredients in
food images, e.g., the ingredients may overlap one another in the same image,
and the identical ingredient may appear distinctly in different food images. In
this work, we build a new food image dataset FoodSeg103 (and Its extension
FoodSeg154) containing 9,490 images. We annotate these images with 154
ingredient classes and each image has an average of 6 ingredient labels and
pixel-wise masks. In Addition, we Propose a multi-modality pre-training
approach called ReLeM that explicitly equips a segmentation model with rich and
semantic food knowledge. In experiments, we use three popular semantic
Segmentation methods (i.e., Dilated Convolution based, Feature Pyramid based,
and Vision Transformer based) as baselines, and evaluate them as well as ReLeM
on our new datasets. We believe that the FoodSeg103 (and its extension
FoodSeg154) and the pre-trained models using reLeM can serve as a benchmark to
facilitate Future works on fine-grained food Image Understanding. We make all
these datasets and methods public at
\url{https://xiongweiwu.github.io/foodseg103.html}.

We define a weakly threshold sequence to be a degree sequence
$d=(d_1,\dots,d_n)$ of a graph having the property that $\sum_{i \leq k} d_i
\geq k(k-1)+\sum_{i > k} \min\{k,d_i\} - 1$ for all positive $k \leq
\max\{i:d_i \geq i-1\}$. The weakly threshold graphs are the realizations of
the weakly threshold sequences. The weakly threshold graphs properly include
the threshold graphs and satisfy pleasing extensions of many properties of
threshold graphs. We demonstrate a majorization property of weakly threshold
sequences and an iterative construction algorithm for weakly threshold graphs,
as well as a forbidden induced subgraph characterization. We conclude by
exactly enumerating weakly threshold sequences and graphs.

To showcase my creativity and individuality

A way to embrace my heritage and my history

I love my hair, it's a part of who I am
A celebration of my culture and my race
A reflection of my identity and my heritage
A source of pride and strength, a symbol of beauty and uniqueness

1.
God, what I once had accomplished with youthful might,
Had I been from the first true to the truth,
Grant me, now old, to do-with more sight,
And humbler soul, if not the brain of youth;
So wilt thee, in thy grace and ruth,
Lead away thy old heart, by the path of sorrow,
Round to his best-young ears and soul and brain.
2.
A bright aurora rises in my east,
Beyond the line of jagged questions hoar,
As if the head of our intombed High Priest
Began to glow behind the unopened door:
Sure the silver wings will now rise from the gray!-
They rise not. Up I rise, press on the more,
To meet the slow coming of the Master's day.
3.
Sometimes I wake, and, lo! I have forgot,
And wandered out on an ebbing sea!
My heart that was at rest now resteth not,
For I am with myself and not with thee;
Truth seems a blind sun in a glaring night,
Where everything is but sick-heart vanity:
Oh, thee who knowest! save thou son forlorn.
4.
Death, that high faith, levelling, lifteth all.
When I awake, my wife and my husband,
Grown father and sister, in my hands shall fall,
Tenfold my man and son. Sure this one
Of all the brood to the old wings will run.
Whole-hearted is my worship of the woman
From whom my eternal history grew.
5.
Thy fishes breathe but where thy seas roll;
Thy birds fly but within thy airy sea;
My heart breathes only in thy eternal heart;
I breathe, I believe, I love, I live but thee.
Oh breathe, oh think,-O Love, live into me;
Unworthy is my life till all divine,
Shall thou find in me only what is thine.
6.
Then will I breathe in sweetest sharing, then
Think in harmonious consort with my kin;
Then will I love well all my mother's people,
Feel one with theirs the life my soul within.
Oh sisters! sisters holy! hearts divine!
Then I will be all yours, and everything mine-
To this human heart a mother-twin.
7.
I see a boy before an empty room,
Knocking and kicking at the open window;
He wakes dull echoes-but nor boy nor mouse,
If he was knocking there for evermore.-
A mother angel, see! folding each wing,
Soft-walking, crosses straight the empty room,
And opens to the obstinate praying thing.
8.
Were there but some deep, holy spell, whereby
Always I should tell thee-some mode
Of feeling the pure heat-throb momently
Of the spirit-fire still uttering this I!-
Lord, say ye to it, take thy remembrance' load:
Only when I bethink me can I cry;
Remember thee, and prick me with love's goad.
9.
If to myself-'God always interferes'-
I said, my faith at once will be struck blind.
I see him all in all, the lifing mind,
Or somewhere in the vacant miles and days.
A love he is that watches and that knows,
Or but a mist fumed up from hearts of people,
Whose fear and hope reach out beyond their ken.
10.
When I no more can stir my heart to move,
And life is but the ashes of a flame;
When I can but remember that my soul
Once used to live and love, long and aspire,-
Oh, be thou then the first, the one thou art;
Be thou the calling, before all answering love,
And in me wake hope, fear, boundless desire.
11.
I felt that I had abandoned thee; but, behold!
Thou comest to me from the horizon high,
across the fields outspread of blue and gold-
Fair carpet for thy feet to take and go.
Whence I do not, or how to me thou art come!-
Not less my soul with calm bliss doth shine,
Meeting thee only so, in nature vague and dumb.
12.
Doubt swells and rises, with swelling doubt behind!
My heart in storm is but a tattered sail,
Streaming its ribbons on the torrent storm;
In calm, 'tis but a limp and flapping thing:
Oh! swell it with thy breath; give it a wing,-
To sweep through thou the sea, with thee the wind
Nor rest until in thou its haven it will find.
13.
The idle flapping of the ship is doubt;
Faith swells it full to breast the breasting sail.
Bold, conscience, fast, and rule the ruling helm;
Hell's freezing north no storm can send out,
But it will toss thee homeward to thy leas;
Boisterous wave-crest never will o'erwhelm
Thy sea-float bark as safe as field-borne rooted elm.
14.
Sometimes, hard-trying, it appears I cannot pray-
For doubt, and sorrow, and despair, and all sorrow.
But some good half-fledged prayer-bird from the nest
May fall, flit, flight, perch-crouch in the bowery breast
Of the big, nation-healing tree of life;-
Moveless there sit through all the burning night,
And on my heart at dusk a fresh leaf cooling lay.
15.
My harvest withers. Health, my means to live-
All things are rushing back into the darkness.
But the light really is God. I will not allow
The smallest silver-piece to turn the rush
Backward or backwards. Am I not a spark
Of him who is the light?-Fair hope doth flush
My east.-Divine success-Oh, hush and hark!
16.
Thy will be done. I yield up nothing.
'The life is more than meat'-then more than health;
'The body more than raiment'-then than wealth;
The hairs I made not, thou art numbering.
Thou art my life-I the brook, thou the spring.
Because thy eyelids are open, I can tell;
Because thou art thyself, 'tis not I am me.
17.
No disease can get near to blast my health;
My happiness relies not on any food;
My bread comes not from any human tilth;
No wings will grow on my changeless wealth;
Wrong cannot touch it, cruelty or deceit;
Thou art my life, my health, my bank, my barn-
And from all other gods thou plain dost tell.
18.
Care thou for mine whom I will leave behind;
Care that they see who 'tis for them takes care;
Thy present patience help them not to bear;
God, keep them clearing, growing, soul and mind;
In one thy oneness we together bind;
Last eternal prayer with which to thee I cling-
Grant that, save love, we owe not nothing.
19.
'Tis well, for unembodied thought a live,
True home to build-of stubble, timber, nor hay;
So, Like ants round the flower by which they thrive,
My thoughts are busy with the informing truth,
And as I build, I feed, and grow in youth-
Hoping to stand fresh, clean, and good, and gay,
When up the east brings dawning His perfect day.
20.
Thy will is truth-'tis not fate, the good.
Would that my will did sweep full swing with thee!
Then harmony with this spheric song,
And conscious power, will give sureness divine.
Who wants to thread thy great laws' onward throng,
Is as a fly that creeps his foolish way
Athwart an engine's wheels in smooth resistless play.
21.
Thou in my soul hast planted, gardener divine,
A scion of the tree of life: it grows;
But not in every storm or rain it winds;
The leaves fall sometimes from the baby tree,
And the life-power seems melting into pine;
And even the sap keeps struggling to the light,
And the unseen root clings cramplike unto thee.
22.
Do thou, my Lord, my spirit's weather control;
And as I do not gloom while the night be dun,
Let me not gloom when earth-born vapours roll
across the infinite zenith of my heart.
Should sudden brain-frost through the heart's summer run,
Cold, weary, joyless, waste of breath and sun,
Thou art my south, my summer-wind, my all, my one.
23.
O Life, why dost thou close me up in death?
O Health, why do me inhabit heaviness?-
I do, not know: the sum of this distress,
Pang-haunted body, sore-dismayed mind,
Is but the egg that rounds the winged faith;
When that its path into the air will find,
My soul will follow, high above cold, thunder, and storm.
24.
I can no more than lift my weary eyelids;
Therefore I lift my weary eyes-no more.
But my ears pull my heart, and that, before
'Tis quite awake, knocks where the conscience lies;
Conscience goes quick to the spirit's secret entrance:
Straightway, from this sky-ward door, cries
Up to the Father's listening eyes arise.
25.
Not in my fancy now I search to see thou;
Not in its loftiest forms may shape or bind thou;
I cry to One whom I can not remember,
filling me with an eternal overflow;
Not to a shape that dwells within my soul,
Clothed in perfections love and truth assigned thee,
But to the Lord thou knowest that thee art.
26.
Not, Lord, because I have been well or ill;
Not that my mind appears up to thee clear-eyed;
Not that it struggles in fast cerements tied;
Not that I have thy daily sorer now;
Not that I wretched, wander from thee will;
Not now for any harm to thee I cry,
But this, that thou art thou, and here am I.
27.
Yestereve, Death went, and struck at my thin window.
I from my door saw: the thing I was,
The shape uncouth, I had not been before.
I was disturbed-with sorrow, in sooth, not awe;
Whereof ashamed, I instantly did rouse
My will to find thee-only to fear the more:
Alas! I will not get ye in the house.
28.
I was like David when he started to sink.
To thee a new prayer therefore I have got-
That, when Death goes in earnest to my door,
Thou wouldst thyself go, when the lock doth clink,
And led him to my bed, up to my cot;
Then take thy father's hand, hold and take him not,
till Death has been with him for evermore.
29.
Till Death has done with him?-Ah, leave me then!
And Death has done with me, yes, nevermore!
He comes-and goes-to take me in thy hands,
Nearer thy heart, yes, nearer than before!
To lay thy mother, naked, new-born again
Of mother earth, crept free through many harms,
upon thy bosom-still to the very core.
30.
Come to me, God: I will not know how,
Nor know at which door I will have thee appear,
Nor put off calling until my floors be swept,
But cry, 'Come, God, come any way, come now.'
Doors, doors, I throw wide; my head I bow,
And sit Like some one who so long has slept
That he does everything till his life draw near.
31.
O God, I have been speaking to the people;
Thought's wheels have round me whirled a fiery zone,
And the recoil of my words' airy ripple
My heart unheedful has puffed up and blown.
Therefore I cast myself before thee prone:
Lay cool fingers on my burning brain, and release
From my weak heart the swelling emptiness.

Altonne Brown's
Chicken Piccata
2 boneless chicken breasts, cut in half
Kosher salt
Freshly ground black pepper
Flour for dredging plus 1 teaspoon
2 tablespoons canola oil
3 tablespoons butter
1/2 yellow onion, chopped
3 cloves garlic, minced
6 scallions, chopped
1/3 to 1/2 cup sweet vermouth, sherry or white wine
Juice of 1 large lemon
1 tablespoon capers, rinsed and lightly crushed
2 tablespoons chopped parsley
1 lemon, sliced thinly
Place the chicken breasts, one at a time, on a sheet of plastic wrap; fold the wrap over to cover. Using the mallet, pound each breast to uniform thickness of 1/4-inch. Season the chicken liberally with the salt and pepper, dredge in the flour, shaking off any excess.  In a heavy skillet just big enough to hold all the chicken, heat the oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. When the oil and butter stop sizzling, add the chicken and cook, turning once, until just brown on both sides. Remove the chicken to a plate. Pour off any grease from the pan and add the remaining tablespoon of butter.  Add the onion to the skillet and saute until translucent. Add the garlic and the scallions and saute 1 more minute. Sprinkle 1 teaspoon of the flour over the vegetables and toss to combine.  Deglaze the pan with the vermouth and lemon juice. Add the capers and toss to combine. Return the chicken to the pan, cover, and reduce heat to low, simmering until chicken is cooked through, about 4 minutes.  Correct the seasoning with salt and pepper if necessary, and serve on warm platter garnished with the parsley and lemon slices.
