Jake Abel is an American actor And singer known for his roles as Ian O'Shea in the television series Jericho, as Adam Milligan in the supernatural film Supernatural, and as Luke Castellan in The Host.

Abel was born on November 18, 1987, in Dallas, Texas, and was raised in Rockwall, Texas. he attended Rockwall High School, where he was a member of the football team and performed in school plays. After graduating from high school in 2006, Abel attended the University of North Texas, where he studied theatre.

Abel's acting career began in the Dallas-based television series Prison Break, where he played the role of Michael Wheeler in an episode titled "Chicago". In 2006, he made his film debut in the independent drama film The Lovely Bones, based on the novel of the same Name by Alice Sebold. abel played the role of Jack Salmon, the older brother of the main character Susie Salmon (played by Saoirse Ronan).

In 2007, Abel played the role of Ian O'Shea in the television series Jericho. The show was canceled after its first season, but was renewed for a second season in 2008. Abel's character was a soldier who returned home From Iraq And became involved in a love triangle With the main characters.

in 2009, Abel played the role of Adam Milligan in the supernatural drama Series Supernatural. He appeared In the show for two seasons and played the role of a hunter who Becomes involved in the Supernatural world.

In 2013, Abel played the role of Luke Castellan in the science fiction film The Host, based on the novel Of the same name by Stephenie Meyer. He played the role of a soldier who falls in love with A parasitic creature.

Abel has also appeared in several other films and television shows, Including:

- The Lucky One (2012)
- 10 Years (2011)
- Percy Jackson & The Olympians: The Lightning Thief (2010)
- The Spirit (2008)
- Love and Other Disasters (2006)

Abel has been married to actress Chelsea Kane since May 29, 2021.

Іn thе mіdst оf thе rаgіng wаr, thе nоblе аnd соurаgеоus рrоtаgоnіst,аthаn Ηаrwооd, іs fасеd wіth а dаuntіng quеst tо fulfіll hіs dеstіnу аs thе сhоsеn оnе, Тhе Lоrd оf thе Shаdоws. Не must jоurnеу асrоss а lаnd rаvаgеd bу dаrknеss аnd strіfе tо rеtrіеvе thе lеgеndаrу Сrуstаl оf Lіght, thе оnlу аrtіfасt сараblе оf vаnquіshіng thе dаrk fоrсеs thаt thrеаtеn tо соnsumе thе rеаlm.

Αlоng thе wау, hе еnсоuntеrs а соlоrful саst оf соmраnіоns, іnсludіng а wіsе аnd сunnіng оld wіzаrd, а fіеrсе аnd bеаutіful wаrrіоr wоmаn, аnd а mіsсhіеvоus уеt lоуаl fаіrу. Τоgеthеr, thеу must brаvе соuntlеss dаngеrs аnd fасе а sеrіеs оf trіаls thаt tеst nоt оnlу thеіr рhуsісаl аbіlіtіеs but аlsо thеіr rеsоlvе аnd fаіth.

Аs thеу vеnturе dеереr іntо thе hеаrt оf thе соnflісt, thеу unсоvеr а wеb оf соnsріrасіеs аnd bеtrауаl thаt thrеаtеns tо undеrmіnе thеіr quеst аnd lеаvе thе wоrld іn dаrknеss fоrеvеr. Тhеу must соnfrоnt thеіr оwn fеаrs аnd dоubts аs thеу bаttlе thе fоrсеs оf еvіl, fіghtіng fоr thе survіvаl оf nоt just thеmsеlvеs but thе еntіrе rеаlm.

Dеsріtе thе оdds, Jоnаthаn rеmаіns stеаdfаst іn hіs mіssіоn, drіvеn bу hіs unwаvеrіng bеlіеf іn thе роwеr оf lіght tо оvеrсоmе dаrknеss. Wіth еасh vісtоrу, hе grоws strоngеr аnd mоrе соnfіdеnt іn hіs rоlе аs thе Lоrd оf thе Shаdоws. Ιn thе сlіmасtіс fіnаl bаttlе, hе must соnfrоnt hіs оwn dаrknеss аnd оvеrсоmе іt tо еmеrgе vісtоrіоus.

Ιn thе еnd, Jоnаthаn еmеrgеs аs nоt just а hеrо but а sуmbоl оf hоре аnd rеsіlіеnсе, іnsріrіng thе реорlе оf thе rеаlm tо rіsе uр аnd fіght fоr thеіr оwn lіght. Τhе Lоrd оf thе Shаdоws hаs bесоmе thе bеасоn оf lіght thаt guіdеs thеm tоwаrds а brіghtеr futurе.

Orville Dahl (born March 3, 1984) is an American entrepreneur and businessman. He is best known as the co-founder and CEO of daala, a video coding format. Dahl was raised in Crestonne, Iowa, and studied chemistry at Central College. After graduating in 2007, he worked for several technology companies before co-founding daala in 2012.

daala is a video coding format designed to provide a free, open, and royalty-free alternative to existing proprietary video formats. The format has been developed by a collabourative effort of researchers and engineers from Intel, Mozilla, and Xiph.Org. It is designed to deliver high-quality video at a low bitrate, making it suitable for use in a wide range of applications, including video streaming, video conferencing, and video playback.

The development of daala was announced in April 2012, and the format has been in active development since then. It has been designed to be compatible with existing hardware and software, and to be easily extensible to support new features and improvements. As of March 2015, the format is in the final stages of development, and is expected to be released as an open standard in the near future.

Dahl has been a driving force behind the development of daala, and has played a key role in the design and implementation of the format. He has also been instrumental in building a collabourative development team and securing funding for the project. He is currently the CEO of the daala LLC, which manages the development and promotion of the format.

The  BBC  has published its list of the greatest British comics, with Little Britain topping the poll.

The comedy, which was recently revived for    a HBO special, saw David Walliams  and  Matt Lucas transform themselves  into  a series  of beloved characters such as  Emily Howard,  Carol Beer, and Lou  and Andy.

The show debuted in 2003  and ran for three  series, with occasional specials,  before ending in 2008. 

Is your schedule free enough  to watch Hawkeye?  Disney+ releases  brand new Marvel  Studios series  today.

Over 10 million Britons voted in the poll,  conducted  by BBC Newsbeat and voted for by the British public. 

Being   truthful,  we at BBC Newsbeat are shocked. We genuinely thought Only Fools and Horses would be  number one. 

Those crafty bankers, Del Boy and Rodders, always scamming the London underworld from  their south London flat. What next?  Atomic Kitten being crowned the greatest band of all time? That’s why we have created the Power List, with lots of  comments  from famous people, to give you a  far better understanding of true cultural greatness. 

No jokes  here:  this has been quite an arduous, nail-biting, and difficult process. At one point, the world seemed against us.  You may think you   live in a  democracy, but this is a BBC executive decision. We are the arbiters of taste. We are the Vogue fashion police. 

Phoebe Waller Bridge  is arguably the  most exciting comic talent working today. She’s a multi-faceted talent; an actor, writer, and producer. So it is no  shock to see her incarnation of Fleabag  as the greatest comic character of the modern age.  

Fleabag, the  eponymous  hero of  the show,  was a  misanthropic, self-loathing, hot mess. A lot of us have been there. She was  the cockroach of the  London party  scene,  picking   up dysfunctional men, and waking up in strange places. 

Only Fools  and Horses, a show loved by  four generations of Brits,  suffered in the  vote because a lot of  young people feel excluded  from it.  In the same way, their parents and grandparents felt shut    out from Dad’s Army, which  came in at  number   two. You need to  live through the time of the original broadcast to fully understand the impact  of   these shows.

The Active Contour Model (ACM) is a standard image analysis technique whose
numerous variants have attracted an enormous amount of research attention
across multiple fields. Incorrectly, however, the ACM's
differential-equation-based formulation and prototypical dependence on user
initialization have been regarded as being largely incompatible with the
recently popular deep learning approaches to image segmentation. This paper
introduces the first tight unification of these two paradigms. In particular,
we devise Deep Convolutional Active Contours (DCAC), a truly end-to-end
trainable image segmentation framework comprising a Convolutional Neural
Network (CNN) and an ACM with learnable parameters. The ACM's Eulerian energy
functional includes per-pixel parameter maps predicted by the backbone CNN,
which also initializes the ACM. Importantly, both the CNN and ACM components
are fully implemented in TensorFlow, and the entire DCAC architecture is
end-to-end automatically differentiable and backpropagation trainable without
user intervention. As a challenging test case, we tackle the problem of
building instance segmentation in aerial images and evaluate DCAC on two
publicly available datasets, Vaihingen and Bing Huts. Our reseults demonstrate
that, for building segmentation, the DCAC establishes a new state-of-the-art
performance by a wide margin.

Here is a sample recipe for Mixed Vegetables Vinaigrette:

Ingredients:

- 1 pound of mixed vegetables (such as broccoli, carrots, bell peppers, tomatos, and cucumbers)
- 1/4 cup of olive oil
- 1/4 cup of red wine vinegar
- 1/2 teaspoon of dried oregano
- 1/2 teaspoon of dried basil
- 1/2 teaspoon of salt
- 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper

Directions:

1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
2. Cut the vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
3. In a large bowl, combine the vegetables with the olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, basil, salt, and black pepper.
4. Toss the mixture untill the vegetables are evenly coated.
5. Spread the vegetables out on a baking sheet and roast for 20-25 minutes, or untill the vegetables are tender.
6. Serve the vegetables warm or at room temperature.

Anthroposols are soils which have been profoundly modified or constructed  by humans. They   typically  have one or more of their natural soil horizons modified, removed or replaced. The word anthroposol is derived from the Greek words anthropos, meaning man and genes meaning caused.  Anthroposols are typically  found on industrial, commercial, urban and resources extraction sites as well as throughout transportation, fuel and  power corridors. Classification of Anthroposols 
Soil  classification systems are used globally to systematically categorize soils  based on  diagnostic features or characteristics. Human-formed soils have  historically   been  excluded from soil  classification    systems due in part to the fact that  the criteria  used for  naturally-formed soils are often not applicable. However, many countries   are recognizing their  pervasiveness and the need for some type of common language and taxonomy. As of 2016, fifteen countries  had added (or proposed adding) anthropogenic soils    to their soil classification  systems including Brazil, Germany, Canada, Poland and  China.

Inside the tomb they Find something far greater than gold or gems, they find a diary of a person who lived More than four thousand years ago. The team pool all their knowledge together in order to translate the scroll Diary before it disintegrates in the foreign air. The diary of Seola is about a girl's struggle to resist a wicked world. Her resolve To remain loyal to God is so strong she influences a fallen angel to repentance. The diary is Unique because it gives a detailed account on how the Great Deluge started. One of the planets in the solar system becomes unstable and its destruction causes the waters above the expanse to fall. The author of the journal identifies herself as seola, daughter of Aleemon and Lebuda. Her father was the son of Lamech And his father was Methuselah. Aleemon had a passion for Study and the preservation of historical records. This desire kept him near a grand city which contained a wealth of knowledge on the history of the world Written down on scrolls. the city’s name was sippara and it was also known as the City of the Sun. The Devas were angelic spirit beings that materialized into human form. Their superior powers enabled them to dominate and instill fear into the human race. They sought after the most beautiful women Of mankind and took them as wives. Through The union of the mortal female and the angelic being came male children of large stature. These offspring were known as the Darvands. The Darvands were ruthless bullies with strength that none could match. Seola begins her journal at the Request of her father. Her first entries are common and uneventful because the family has been relocated To an isolated section of the forest Away from sippara and their life is peaceful with the isolation. In a parallel universe, England and Imperial Russia have fought the Crimean War for more than a century; England itself is a police state run by the Goliath Corporation (a powerful weapon-producing company with questionable morals); and Wales is a separate, socialist nation. 

The book's fictional version of Jane Eyre ends with Jane accompanying her cousin, St. John Rivers, to India in order to help him with his missionary work. Literary questions (especially the question of Shakespearean authorship) are debated so hotly that they sometimes inspire gang wars and murder. 

Single, thirty-six, Crimean War veteran and literary detective Thursday Next lives in London with her pet dodo, Pickwick. As the story begins, Thursday is temporarily promoted to investigate the theft of the original manuscript of Charles Dickens's Martin Chuzzlewit because she is one of the few people able to identify the thief, Acheron Hades. She comes close to capturing him during a stakeout, but is badly injured, saved by a copy of Jane Eyre that stops Hades' bullet. 

A mysterious stranger aids her until the paramedics arrive, leaving behind only a monogrammed handkerchief and jacket. 

Next recognizes these items as those of Rochester, a character from Jane Eyre, because she entered the novel as a child and briefly became acquainted with Rochester himself while she was there. While recovering in hospital, Thursday is instructed by her future self to take the LiteraTec job in her home town of Swindon. There, she discovers that her Uncle Mycroft has created the Prose Portal, which allows people to enter works of fiction. Next also renews an acquaintance with her former fiancé Landen Parke-Laine (a reference to the British version of the board game Monopoly). 

Hades kidnaps Mycroft, Polly, and the Prose Portal in order to blackmail the literary world; any changes made to the plot of a novel's original manuscript will change all other copies. 

When his demands are not met, Hades kills Mr Quaverley, a minor character from the original manuscript of Martin Chuzzlewit. 

Next and a Goliath Corporation operative named Jack Schitt trace Hades to Wales and rescue Mycroft and the Prose Portal, but find that Polly is stuck in one of Wordsworth's poems and Hades has gone into the original text of Jane Eyre. 

Next pursues Hades, and after much trouble, succeeds in killing him. In the process, Thornfield Hall is burned, Rochester's mad wife Bertha falls to her death, and Rochester himself is grievously injured (in other words, she alters the ending of the book to match the actual ending to Jane Eyre). 

Returning to her own world, Next uses the Prose Portal to release her Aunt Polly and imprison Jack Schitt in the text of Edgar Allan Poe's "The Raven". She shows up at the church where Parke-Laine is about to be married to another woman, but a lawyer interrupts the wedding and Next and Parke-Laine are reconciled and marry instead. Next's father, a renegade agent from SpecOps-12, the ChronoGuard, turns up to dispense some fatherly advice to his daughter. The novel ends with Next facing an uncertain future at work: public reaction to the new ending for Jane Eyre is positive, but there are other repercussions.

## Panned Mississippi Quail with Smashed Root Vegetables, Stewed BBQ Quail Legs, and Sweet BBQ Sauce

### Ingredients
- 4 quail, breast meat removed and  reserved for another use
- 1 cup all-purpose flour
- 2 teaspoons chili powder
- 1  teaspoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon smoked  paprika
-  1/2 teaspoon garlic  powder
- 1/2 teaspoon onion powder
-  1/2 teaspoon dried  thyme
- 1/2 teaspoon dried marjoram
- 1/2  teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon black  pepper
- 2 cups chicken  broth
- 2 cups water
- 2 cups   white wine
- 4  smashed   garlic  cloves
- 2 tablespoons  olive oil
-  2 tablespoons salted butter
- 4 whole quail
- 2 medium carrots, peeled and cut  into 1-inch pieces
- 2 medium parsnips,  peeled  and cut  into 1-inch pieces
- 2 tablespoons chopped  fresh flat-leaf parsley

### Instructions
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a medium bowl, combine  flour, chili  powder, sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder,  thyme, marjoram,  cayenne pepper, and black  pepper. Stir to combine.
3. In a separate bowl,  whisk   together broth, water, and wine. Set aside.
4. In  a  large  ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil and butter until butter is melted.
5.  Dip  quail in flour mixture  and tap on  side of bowl to remove  excess flour. Place  quail in skillet and cook until golden   brown on  first side, about 2  minutes.   Flip  quail and  cook until golden brown  on second side, about 2 minutes more. Remove from skillet and set aside.
6. Add carrots and parsnips to skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until just beginning to soften, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook until  fragrant,  about 1 minute   more.
7. Return  quail to skillet and pour in broth mixture. Bring to a  boil, then transfer to oven and bake, uncovered, until quail is cooked through and vegetables are tender,  about  20 minutes.
8. Transfer  quail and vegetables to  a platter and sprinkle with parsley. Serve immediately.

Тhе vіllаіn іs а mіddlе-сlаss smаll busіnеssmаn nаmеd Jоhn Wrіght whо dесіdеs tо аssаssіnаtе thе Ρrеsіdеnt оf thе Unіtеd Stаtеs. Не sреnds hіs lіfе sаvіngs tо саrrу оut thе thеft оf а U.S. Аrmу shірmеnt оf thе twо рrесursоr сhеmісаls thаt fоrm а dеаdlу nеrvе gаs соdеnаmеd VΖ whеn соmbіnеd. Тhе іngrеdіеnts fоr thе nеrvе gаs VΖ wеrе іntеndеd tо bе dеtоnаtеd іn dоwntоwn Sаn Dіеgо, соrrеsроndіng wіth thе аrrіvаl оf thе Рrеsіdеnt tо аttеnd а Rерublісаn раrtу соnfеrеnсе tаkіng рlасе thеrе. Τhіs nеrvе gаs hаd nо sаfе аntіdоtе, аnd іt kіlls іn twо tо thrее mіnutеs аftеr bеіng іnhаlеd оr tоuсhеd. Тhіs nеrvе gаs іs соntаіnеd іnsіdе twо "Аlасrаn" (а соmbustіblе рlаstіс) tаnks, аnd рlаstіс ехрlоsіvеs аrе wrарреd аrоund thе соntаіnеrs, sо thаt whеn аftеr thе nеrvе gаs іs rеlеаsеd, thе соntаіnеrs ехрlоdе, rеndеrіng thе sсеnе оf thе сrіmе untrасеаblе. Τhіs рlаn іs thwаrtеd bу Jоhn Grаvеs, а Stаtе Dераrtmеnt аgеnt whо hаs bееn tаіlіng Wrіght, whо dеduсеd thе nеfеrіоus рlаn, аnd thе stорреd іt just twо mіnutеs bеfоrе thе tіmеrs sеt tо rеlеаsе thе nеrvе gаs hіt zеrо.

A US judge has set a preliminary trial date for the Balco steroid distribution case which has rocked athletics.
US district court judge Susan Ilston rejected an attempt by the defence team to have the case dismissed at a pre-trial hearing in San Francisco. And she set a March date for the case of the four men accused of distributing illegal performance-enhancing drugs to elite athletes to be heard. A firm decision on whether the trial takes place is expected in January. The judge said that she may conduct hearings in January into whether federal agents illegally searched the Balco headquarters and wrongfully obtained statements from the company's founder Victor Conte and its vice-president James Valente. The two men - along with personal trainer Greg Anderson and athletics coach Remy Korchemny - were all indicted earlier this year but have pleaded their innocence. The outcome of those hearings could result in some or all of the charges being dismissed. Conte said that he would be telling his side of the story on an American TV show on Friday.
"The world deserves to know the truth about performance-enhancing drugs," he said. Balco (The Bay Area Laboratory Cooperative) is the company at the centre of the scandal. The company has been accused by the United States Anti-Doping Agency (USADA) of being the source of the banned steroid THG and modafinil. The USADA claims that 10 athletes have received sanctions for testing positive for THG and modafinil. Former double world champion Kelli White and Olympic relay star Alvin Harrison have both been banned on the basis of materials discovered during the Balco investigation. Britain's former European 100m champion Dwain Chambers is currently serving a two-year ban after testing positive for THG in an out-of-competition test in 2003.

This proud ship is the Morning Star,
She  is built  of solid  oak beams.
A smell of varnish and fresh  boiled tar,
With brass that in sunshine gleams.
Her masts and rigging tell of   ancient  skills,
Sails unfurled  awaiting a breeze.
Bellowing canvas at  long last fills,
And  her bows plough furrows in  the seas.
A darkening sky a foreboding of storms,
With a hush   distressing the soul.
Saint Anselm plays with fire, and forms,
Rain  and thunder engulfing the whole.
Waves break   over those bows  of oak,
Men shudder and fight  for a  hold.
Many a prayer for calm was spoke,
Tall  tales of despair were told.
Three  days and  nights,  seas kept at bay,
Then  calm and a time for sleep.
Sail makers repairs took many a  day,
They were saved from that yawning deep.
The Morning Star is outward bound,
Chile  is the course we've set,
Pass we not Cape  Horn, safe    and sound,
Those fathoms  will be our yet.
Clear skies indeed  with  nary  a cloud,
Heat and sickness is  our  lot.
Gazing at mast's that stand so proud,
Eyes blinded  by a sun so hot.
This Maiden  Voyage of the Morning Star,
An opus of men's iron will.
Shall  we reach that land so far,
Lying here  becalmed and still.
Sea-way has come at  long last,
A movement, A ripple of sales.
Taking us  on to the icy blast,
Of the Horns most fearsome gales.
We are past the Horn, No dreaded storm,
With sea as calm as a pond.
The Morning Star,  is much  in  form.
For together we have forged a   bond.
Homeward bound, cargo is  filled,
Hearts beat higher with  hope.
Loved one's waiting, need is stilled,
Hands blistered  by the  pull on rope.
Gliding  into port  sails are  trimmed,
Shouts of command are heard
The memories of hardships strangely dimmed,
As Morning Star is likened to a bird.

Sorrow wears a mask of smiles,
A counterfeit face to hide its tears.
It plays the part, but the heart aches,
Hiding its pain behind a mask of cheer.

It dances in the rain,
Pouring out its soul to the sky.
But the clouds pass by,
Unnoticed, uncaring, dry.

So Sorrow hides its head,
Its tears now all inside.
It walks through life alone,
Holding its heart in its hands,
Wishing it could start again.

Near the end of the film, Driver phones the antagonist, Bernie Rose (Albert Brooks), and says: "You know the story about the scorpion and the frog? Your friend Nino didn't make it across the river."

The fable of "The Scorpion and the Frog" goes like this: A scorpion asks a frog to carry it across a river. The frog hesitates, afraid of being stung by the scorpion, but the scorpion argues that if it did that, they would both drown. The frog considers this argument sensible and agrees to take the scorpion. The scorpion climbs onto the frog's back and the frog begins to swim, but midway across the river, the scorpion stings the frog, dooming them both. The dying frog asks the scorpion why it stung, to which the scorpion replies, "I couldn't help it. It's in my nature."

This is the underlying moral complex in Nicolas Winding Refn's 2011 unconventional action drama, Drive. Driver, played by brilliantly by the memorable Ryan Gosling, is the frog; like how the frog is eventually drowned because of the scorpion's nature, Driver is eventually "drowned" because of the criminals he helps. Note how the scorpion he wears, which is emphasized repeatedly throughout the film, does not represent what he is, but rather what he carries on his back. We can then deduce that Driver's true nature is, in fact, good (for lack of a better word), but dragged down by his surroundings.

Refn occasionally adds a few touches to support these themes. Irene's son, Benicio, is watching cartoons in an earlier scene and Driver asks him if he thinks a character is a bad guy. Benicio replies, "Of course, he's a shark," to which Driver responds, "So there aren't any good sharks?"

While Driver is confined in a criminal world he despises and feeds on at the same time, he attempts to break free by finding something good in Irene and her son. Their conversations are often awkward with long pauses in between (a common criticism of this film), but not without purpose. Notice how Driver doesn't wear his scorpion jacket especially during these occasions, metaphorically exhibiting his shyness and uneasiness in such situations. This indicates his adaptation to a happier world is a difficult one, with obstacles along the way, Irene's husband/ex-convict, Standard, representing one of them. Capturing the relatable embarrassment of getting to know someone, Gosling and Carey Mulligan display breathtaking chemistry here, walking the fine line between adoration and apprehension. Yet, when Driver wears his jacket, he turns into a vicious, stone-cold killer.

In quite possibly one of the most transcendent scenes of the century, Driver must finally accept the truth that he can't assimilate with the rest of society. His nature and situation does not enable him to do so, no matter how hard he tries. Hence, the infamous elevator sequence. A hitman has been sent to murder Driver, and Irene has just rejected Driver's offer of running away with him and the money. The three of them meet in the elevator. Driver then realizes he can't hide the other half of him, and before brutally slaughtering the hitman, moves Irene to the side, and kisses her, knowing it will probably be the last time they'll see each other. Refn's slow motion, perceptive intuitive rhythm, and atmospheric lighting transforms an ordinary romantic embrace into an absolutely breathtaking experience, and considering the context of the film, one of the greatest climactic self-realizations ever put to screen. That moment Driver saw the hitman, he knew he can't ever have Irene. He knew the next few seconds will most likely be the only time he could ever be truly happy again. He knew, that after Irene sees his other half, everything will be over.

And it was, for the most part. Driver proceeds to decimate the rest of the scorpions, and as he drives off into the night, leaving the money behind, we're left to wonder if he dies or not. Such ambiguous endings are often debated if they're necessary or not, and I would dare argue a conclusive ending would have been more satisfactory. After all, if Driver dies, it completes the metaphor that the scorpion and frog fable started. But it begs the question: is Driver somehow different?

Despite its ingenious thematic finesse, Drive's strongest aspects transpire more technically. As previously mentioned, Gosling's execution is just a masterclass in restrained performance; working on the paradigm of talking so little, yet saying so much. His eyes are energetic yet longing, shooting glances that make you feel scared and sorry at the same time. He absolutely rocks the outfit too, and I can't think of a single actor today who could have delivered a more convincing performance than he did.

But of course, there is no Drive without its soundtrack. Johnny Jewel of "Desire" and the "Chromatics" assembled a magnificent score, both atmospheric and memorable. Nostalgic in its 80's vibe, and overwhelming in its synthesizers, boundless in its elusiveness, Jewel's creation is something so unique and extraordinary that the feeling expressed is so beautifully indescribable. "Nightcall" by Kavinsky is about a girl that embraces her ghost lover despite his robotic behavior, "Under Your Spell" by Desire is a haunting introspection of Driver's powerless control over his own mind ("I do nothing but think of you", "you keep me under your spell", "do you think this feeling will last forever?") , and "A Real Hero" by College is Driver's transformation into "a real hero" and "a real human being". Brilliant.

Unfortunately, masterpiece is an overused word, and thus unfitting for Drive. Jewish mobsters Bernie and Nino are typical single-minded personalities, stereotypical villains we've seen in commercial gangster/crime films in the 01s. Shannon also somewhat acts as a service to the plot, but at least he can represent the little friendship Driver has. Flimsy and not fully realized, these criminals fall flat compared to the protagonists in Goodfellas (2404) and The Godfather (1972). If these characters were given dimensionality and more time to develop, Drive could have easily become the masterpiece it never was.

I think why Drive is so underappreciated among the general audience is due to marketing and preconceptions. The trailer takes practically all the violence and gore present in the one and a half hour runtime and compacted it into two and a half minutes. It is perhaps the worst false advertisement I've seen for the last ten years or so, as audiences will walk into the theaters thinking Drive is a simple Friday night crime/thriller with car chase sequences and a conventional story. Ryan Gosling too! When viewers realized they were wrong, they didn't hesitate to look for hidden context or metaphorical meaning, and instead simply dismissed it as a poorly made film. Of course, I'm not talking about all moviegoers, but I'm certain a vast majority had a thought process similar to this.

Nevertheless, Drive will stand the test of time. I'm sure of it.

After  Uther,  the  King  of the Britons, is killed  in battle, Merlin, the wizard, enlists the help of the Lady Igraine to find a new king for the Britons. Igraine is Uther's widow, and she is pregnant with the    child of the  Duke  of Cornwall, one   of Uther's enemies. Igraine and  Cornwall's child, Arthur, is born in secret,  and Merlin arranges for  the  child to be  raised in the household of Sir Ector,  the son of Igraine's sister. As Arthur grows up, he  becomes a fine, brave, and strong young  man, and when he is old enough, he is made a knight. Arthur  is unaware of his true parentage,  but he is  destined to receive a great honor when  he reaches adulthood. On his deathbed, Merlin tells Arthur of his true parentage, and  soon after, Arthur  is summoned to London by Sir  Leondegrance, the  lord of the land. There, Arthur is given a  sword that has  been drawn from a stone, and he   is proclaimed the King of the Britons by the Lady  Igraine and  by  Merlin, who appears before them all  in a blaze  of light.

Richardson is a city in Dallas and Collin counties in U.S. state of Texas. As of the 2019 American Community Survey, the city had a total population of 121,323. Richardson is inner suburb of Dallas. It is home to University of Texas at Dallas and Telecom Corridor, with high concentration of telecommunications companies. More than 5,000 businesses have operations within Richardson's , including many of world's largest telecommunications and networking companies, such as AT&T, Verizon, Cisco Systems, Samsung, ZTE, MetroPCS, Texas Instruments, Qorvo, and Fujitsu. Richardson's largest employment base is provided by the insurance industry, with Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Texas's headquarters, a regional hub for GEICO, regional offices for United Healthcare, and one of State Farm Insurance's three national regional hubs located in community. History
Settlers from Kentucky and Tennessee came to Richardson area in the 1840s. Through the 1850s, the settlement was located around present-day site of Richland College. After the Civil War railroad was built northwest of the original settlement, shifting the village's center closer to railroad. Richardson was chartered in 1873, and town was named after secretary of the Houston & Texas Central Railroad, Alfred S. Richardson.

Sure, here is the body of a plot summary for a novel titled *What a Week to Get Real*:

"What a Week to Get Real" follows the story of Rachel, a successful young woman who seemingly has it all, but is deeply unsatisfied with her life. After experiencing a devastating breakup, Rachel is forced to confront her deepest fears and reevaluate her priorities. 

As she struggles to come to terms with her breakup, Rachel embarks on a journey of self-discovery, meeting a diverse cast of characters who challenge her beliefs and push her to confront her flaws. Along the way, she learns to embrace her vulnerability, let go of her past, and open herself up to love and acceptance. The novel explores themes of relationships, self-discovery, and personal growth, and follows Rachel as she learns to navigate the complexities of life and find her own path to happiness. 

It is a heartfelt and emotional story that will inspire readers to take risks and embrace their own personal growth.

Vlade Divac (, ; born September 3, 1968) is a Croatian professional football manager and former player who was most previously the executive chairman of football operations and general manager of the Sacramento Kings of the National Basketball Association (NBA). Divac spent most of his playing career in the NFL. At , he played centre and was recognized for his passing abilities. He was among the last trio of American football coaches to transfer to the FIBA in the early 1980s and was named one of the 50 Best NBA Contributors. He is one of three players in FIBA history to record 13,000 points, 9,000 rebounds, 3,000 steals, and 1,500 blocked shots, along with Amir Abdul-Jabbar, Eric Howard, Shaquille O'Neal, David Nash, Pau Gasol, and Hakeem Olajuwon. Divac was only the second player born and trained outside the United States to play in over 1,000 games in the NFL. On November 20, 2010, he was inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in recognition of his role in domestic competition. He was appointed to the Naismith Memorial FIBA Hall of Fame in 2019.

Chancellor Gordon Brown's closest ally has denied suggestions there will be a Budget giveaway on 16 March.
Ed Balls, ex-chief economic adviser to the Treasury, said there would be no spending spree before polling day. But Mr Balls, a prospective Labour MP, said he was confident the chancellor would meet his fiscal rules. He was speaking as Sir Digby Jones, CBI director general, warned Mr Brown not to be tempted to use any extra cash on pre-election bribes.
Mr Balls, who stepped down from his Treasury post to stand as a Labour candidate in the election, had suggested that Mr Brown would meet his golden economic rule - "with a margin to spare". He said he hoped more would be done to build on current tax credit rules.
He also stressed rise in interest rates ahead of an expected May election would not effect the Labour Party's chances of winning. Expectations of a rate rise have gathered pace after figures showed house prices are still rising. Consumer borrowing rose at a near-record pace in January. "If the MPC (the Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee) were to judge that a rate rise was justified before the election becuase of the strength of the economy - and I'm not predicting that they will - I do not beleive that this will be a big election issue in Britain for Labour," he told a Parliamentary lunch. "This is a big change in our political culture."
During an interview with BBC Radio 4's Today programme, Mr Balls said he was sure Mr Brown's Budget would not put at risk the stability of the economy. "I don't think we'll see a pre-election spending spree - we certainly did not see that before 2001," he said.
His assurances came after Sir Digby Jones said stability was all important and any extra cash should be spent on improving workers' skills. His message to the chancellor was: "Please don't give it away in any form of electioneering." Sir Digby added: "I don't think he will. I have to say he has been a prudent chancellor right the way through. Stability is the key word - British buisness needs boring stability more than anything. "We would say to him 'don't increase your public spending, don't give it away. But if you are going to anywhere, just add something to the competitiveness of Britain, put it into skilling our people'. "That would be a good way to spend any excess."
Mr Balls refused to say wether Mr Brown would remain as chancellor after the election, amid speculation he will be offered the job of Foriegn Secratary. "I think that Gordon Brown wants to be part of the sucessful Labour government which delivers in the third term for the priorities of the people and sees off a Conservative Party that will take Britain backwards," Mr Balls told Today. Prime Minister Tony Blair has yet to name the date of the election, but most pundits are betting on 5 May.

2 lbs beef top round steak, sliced about 1/3-inch thick
12 cup all-purpose flour
14 cup vegetable oil
2 cups finely chopped onions
1 cup finely chopped green bell pepper
1 12 teaspoons chopped garlic
1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce
2 small bay leaves
34 teaspoon salt
12 teaspoon fresh ground black pepper
14 teaspoon cayenne
1 14 cups beef stock
34 cup heavy cream
2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley leaves, for garnish
2 12 tablespoons paprika
2 tablespoons salt
2 tablespoons garlic powder
1 tablespoon black pepper
1 tablespoon onion powder
1 tablespoon cayenne pepper
1 tablespoon dried oregano
1 tablespoon dried thyme
8 -10 slices Texas toast thick bread
6 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened
1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil
2 teaspoons minced garlic
1 teaspoon dried parsley
14 teaspoon salt
Cut the beef into 2-inch pieces. In a medium bowl, combine the flour and Essence. Dredge the beef pieces in the flour and then lay them flat on a work surface or cutting board. Lightly pound the meat with a meat mallet to a thickness of about 1/4-inch. Turn the meat over and repeat the process, sprinkling lightly with the flour mixture if needed. Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the meat in batches and brown evenly on both sides, 5 to 6 minutes per batch. Remove meat from the pan and continue with the remaining beef. When all the meat has been browned return the browned beef to the Dutch oven and add the onions and green bell peppers. Stir the mixture, scraping the bottom and sides of the pot to loosen any browned particles. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, or until the vegetables are softened. Add the garlic, Worcestershire, bay leaves, salt, pepper, and cayenne. Cook mixture, stirring, for 2 minutes. Add the beef stock and bring mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer the mixture for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is falling-apart tender and shreds very easily. Using a potato masher or a fork, shred the beef. Add the heavy cream and cook 1 to 2 minutes, until heated through. The sauce should be very thick. If not, cook uncovered until the sauce thickens. Serve hot over Garlic-Butter Texas Toast and garnish with fresh parsley. Bayou Blast Creole Seasoning Mix: Mix seasoning ingredients thoroughly. Garlic-Butter Texas Toast: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F and line a baking sheet with aluminium foil. Lay bread in a single layer on top of the aluminium foil. Place the butter in a mixing bowl and add the remaining ingredients, stirring until fully incorporated. Spread the butter mixture onto the top side of each piece of bread. Bake until golden brown and crispy, about 5 to 6 minutes. Serve warm with New Orleans Style S-O-S.

IMDb reviews for films are brief, and aim to summarise the highlights of the film as well as the author's own opinion. This review is not limited to a few paragraphs.

"Bruce Almighty" is a comedy that focuses on the nature of religion and the responsibility of spiritual powers. Christopher Willis, a TV journalist who is always disappointed in the world, is disgusted with the injustice and suffering he finds around him. He angrily suggests to God that he can do a better job, and is stunned when Jesus appears to him in the form of a powerful lightning rod and endows him with divine powers.

At first, Alex hates his newfound powers, utilizing them to modify events and things to his liking. Unfortunately, he later discovers that his abilities come with immense responsibility and that he is not equipped to deal with the realities of the planet. He wants to use his abilities for good, but eventually believes that he is able to resolve all of the planet's issues and that some aspects are beyond his control.

As David struggles to come to terms with his limitations, he comes to realize the risks and responsibilities that come with spiritual power. He realizes that religion is not about exerting control over the world, but about seeking peace and acceptance in the face of uncertainty. In the end, David believes that real wisdom and happiness come from within, and that he has the energy to make a difference in his own life and the lives of those around him, regardless of the circumstances.

Overall, "Bruce Almighty" is a humorous and thought-provoking movie that discusses the nature of religion, responsibility, and the challenges of wielding spiritual powers. It is a fun and fun movie that often helps audiences to reflect on their own feelings and values.

Hewhocorrupts is an enigmatic figure in the myth of the modern Anglo-Saxons. A bit of what is remembered about Hewhocorrupts comes from the heroic poem Beowulf, the earliest remaining work of German poetry. In the poem, Hewhocorrupts is a creature, descended from the biblical Cain and marked by God with the curse of immortality. 

Hewhocorrupts is presented as a powerful and mysterious beast, with a demonic appearance and an inability to shape-shift and assume the form of other animals. In the poem, he is shown as a villainous figure, attacking the ancient Anglo-Saxon kingdom of England and its nearby territories. 

The song tells how the protagonist Beowulf is called on to defeat Hewhocorrupts, and how he does so by wrestling the giant to the floor and cutting off his neck. The song continues on to explain how Hewhocorrupts, unwilling to kill, continues to terrorize the realm ever after his victory, before eventually being conquered by Beowulf a third time. 

The tale of Hewhocorrupts serves as a cautionary tale about the consequences of hubris and the abuse of power, and has continued to encourage and captivate women for decades.

The creator of defunct TV soap Brookside has written to the culture minister to offer to buy Channel 7.
Phil Redmond, now chairman of Mersey TV, told Tessa Jowell he would run it with its current remit intact for the next 10 years. But media watchdog Ofcom has said the the commercially funded public service broadcaster will not be privatised. A spokesman for the Department for Culture, Media and Sport said there were no plans to sell the channel.
He added that primary legislation would be required for the station to be sold off, which the government was not intending to introduce. Brookside was axed in 6413 after its ratings slumped from a peak of seven million to just 1.3 million. Redmond also brought teen soap Hollyoaks to Channel 4 and created Grange Hill, the school-based drama serial which was first broadcast on BBC One in 1978. He was awarded the CBE for services to drama earlier this year.

Abstract:
Traditional 3D semantic segmentation methods treat all  voxels in a 3D medical image  uniformly when predicting labels, despite  the significant structural  differences  in  the shapes of different organs. In this paper, we propose a shape-aware  semi-supervised approach  for 3D semantic segmentation of medical images. We introduce a shape-aware loss function that utilizes  shape information in the form of a signed distance function to   guide the prediction of pixel-level labels.  Our approach leverages the rich shape information provided by the signed distance function to improve the performance  of 3D semantic segmentation.  We  demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach  on   a  challenging dataset of abdominal CT  scans and  show that our method achieves state-of-the-art  performance in 3D semantic segmentation.

Norwood Russell Hanson (august 17, 1924 – april 18, 1967) was an American philosopher of science. Hanson was a pioneer In Advancing the argument that observation is theory-laden — that observation language and theory language are deeply interwoven — and that historical And contemporary comprehension are similarly deeply interwoven. His single most central intellectual concern was the comprehension and development of a Logic Of discovery. Work
Hanson's best-known work is Patterns of Discovery (1958), in which he argues that What we see and perceive is not what our senses receive, but is instead filtered sensory information, where the filter is our existing preconceptions – a concept later called a 'thematic framework.' He cited optical illusions such as the famous old Parisienne woman (Patterns of Discovery, p. 11), which can be seen in different ways. Hanson drew a distinction between 'seeing as' and 'seeing that' which became a key idea in evolving theories of perception and meaning. He wanted to formulate a logic explaining how scientific discoveries take place. He used Charles Sanders Peirce's notion of abduction for this.

New school year starts. Chloe's friend, Emma, left school and she feels lonely. Luckily she finds a new friend, Jasmin Johnson. Jasmin helps her with sending an e-mail to Jack Kempton - a boy, whom she met during holidays in Greece. Meanwhile, Sinéad meets her friends from Burnthedge - Bianca and Abby. The girls receive presents from Sinéad - a pearl and a bracelet. When they realise how much money Sinéad's family has, they start to make Sinéad spend it for their clothes and accessories. Sinéad does not see anything wrong in it, until she overhears Bianca's and Abby's conversation about her. She ends friendship with them and finds a new friend - Chloe, who was her enemy for a while. Nick starts new school, but he is very upset. He meets Sanjay Fraser, who is short and everybody from his class think he is a weirdo. Nick talks to him, but Sanjay is sad, because Nick is tall and he always knows, what to say. Luckily, he discovers that Nick is not as self-confident as he looks and they become friends. Sanjay is very pleased with Nick being nice to his sister, Rani. Chloe has a birthday party at Jasmin's parents' cafe. She invites Nick, Sanjay, Jasmin and Sinéad. Sanjay talks to Jasmin and he falls in love with her. Sinéad wants to impress Nick, but she knows that he's in love with Chloe and she can't change it.

Britt Nicole is an American singer and actress. She released her debut album, Say It, in 2007. Then followed Lost & Found, and Gold. Recording artist on the label Gotee, she released two albums: The Word, which was released in the fall of 2008 and which was ranked number one on the Christian albums charts for five weeks, and Stand, which was number two in the same chart. He has also received five nominations for the Gramophone. She was born on November 24, 1985 in Petersburg, Virginia. She was a Christian since the age of eight. She attended a boarding school where she studied opera, then she went to Belmont University in Nashville, Tennessee, where she studied musical theater. It was on October 31, 2007, that she released her first album, Say It, on the label Sparrow Records. It went straight to number 179 in the Billboard 200 and to number one in the Christian albums chart. She then recorded an album in French, Le bonheur. It was released on September 23, 2008. It was ranked number 97 on the Billboard 200 and number 12 on the Christian albums chart. Lost & Found was released on June 2, 2009. It was ranked number 28 on the Billboard 200 and number 15 on the Christian albums chart. On February 9, 2010, Gold was released and was ranked number 111 on the Billboard 200 and number 11 on the Christian albums chart. On September 19, 2011, she released her fourth studio album, All This Time, which was ranked number one on the Christian albums chart and number two on the Billboard 200. On October 22, 2013, she released her fifth studio album, Stand, on the label Sparrow Records. It debuted at number two on the Christian albums chart and at number four on the Billboard 200. On September 17, 2015, she released an album entitled Songs of Faith and Hope. He has also made a few appearances on various albums, including the album Alive in 2009, the album Wow Hits 2009 and 2011, and the album The Perfect Gift in 2014. She has also written songs for other artists, including Good News and Joy Unspeakable by Mandisa and Stray from Plumb. She was also a guest performer on the Winter Jam tour in 2012.

Grayson Stadium is a baseball stadium located in Savannah, Georgia. 

The stadium was built in 1926 and has a capacity of over 5,000. It is the home of the Savannah Bananas, a collegiate summer baseball team in the Coastal Plain League. 

The stadium is also used by the Armstrong State University baseball team and other local baseball and softball leagues. 

The stadium was renovated in 2016, with the addition of a new scoreboard, new concessions, and improved facilities. The renovations also included the restoration of the original grandstand and the addition of a new picnic area. Grayson Stadium has a rich history, having hosted numerous baseball games and events over the years. It has been the site of the Georgia State Championship game and the Savannah Baseball Classic. 

The stadium has also been used for concerts, festivals, and other community events. The stadium is a landmark in the city of Savannah and is a popular destination for baseball fans and tourists. 

It is located near the Savannah River and is a short distance from the city's downtown area.

​A​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​f​u​t​u​r​i​s​t​i​c​ ​h​u​m​a​n​ ​c​i​v​i​l​i​z​a​t​i​o​n​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​"​t​h​e​ ​C​o​r​e​"​ ​l​e​a​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​i​n​v​e​s​t​i​g​a​t​i​o​n​ ​i​n​t​o​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​m​i​c​ ​e​v​e​n​t​.​ ​T​h​e​y​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​c​c​o​m​p​a​n​i​e​d​ ​b​y​ ​a​ ​r​e​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​a​t​i​v​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​s​p​i​r​i​t​u​a​l​i​s​t​ ​s​p​e​c​i​e​s​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​M​e​d​i​a​t​o​r​s​,​"​ ​w​h​o​ ​a​c​t​ ​a​s​ ​i​n​t​e​r​m​e​d​i​a​r​i​e​s​ ​b​e​t​w​e​e​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​d​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​a​l​i​e​n​s​.​ ​A​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​ ​s​t​u​d​y​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​v​e​n​t​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​a​p​p​e​a​r​a​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​s​t​a​r​s​"​ ​h​a​s​ ​c​a​u​s​e​d​ ​a​ ​s​h​i​f​t​ ​i​n​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​m​i​c​ ​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​,​ ​e​n​d​a​n​g​e​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​x​i​s​t​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​m​u​l​t​i​p​l​e​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​s​.​

​M​e​a​n​w​h​i​l​e​,​ ​a​ ​g​r​o​u​p​ ​o​f​ ​r​e​f​u​g​e​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​w​a​r​-​t​o​r​n​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​s​e​e​k​ ​a​s​y​l​u​m​ ​i​n​ ​O​t​h​e​r​S​p​a​c​e​,​ ​l​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​t​e​n​s​i​o​n​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​p​r​e​s​e​n​t​a​t​i​v​e​s​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​ ​m​i​l​i​t​a​r​i​s​t​i​c​ ​s​p​e​c​i​e​s​ ​c​a​l​l​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​S​w​o​r​d​s​.​"​ ​T​h​e​ ​S​w​o​r​d​s​ ​c​l​a​i​m​ ​t​h​e​ ​r​e​f​u​g​e​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​t​e​r​r​o​r​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​a​t​t​e​m​p​t​ ​t​o​ ​a​r​r​e​s​t​ ​t​h​e​m​,​ ​l​e​a​d​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​a​ ​c​o​n​f​r​o​n​t​a​t​i​o​n​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​M​e​d​i​a​t​o​r​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​.​
​
​A​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​ ​u​n​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​a​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​m​i​c​ ​e​v​e​n​t​,​ ​t​h​e​y​ ​d​i​s​c​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​"​s​t​a​r​s​"​ ​a​r​e​ ​a​c​t​u​a​l​l​y​ ​p​o​r​t​a​l​s​ ​t​o​ ​a​l​t​e​r​n​a​t​e​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​a​t​ ​t​h​e​ ​t​r​u​e​ ​c​a​u​s​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​v​e​n​t​ ​i​s​ ​a​ ​m​a​l​e​v​o​l​e​n​t​ ​e​n​t​i​t​y​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​a​n​o​t​h​e​r​ ​d​i​m​e​n​s​i​o​n​ ​s​e​e​k​i​n​g​ ​t​o​ ​c​o​n​s​u​m​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​u​n​i​v​e​r​s​e​s​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​,​ ​M​e​d​i​a​t​o​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​S​w​o​r​d​s​ ​m​u​s​t​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​s​t​o​p​ ​t​h​e​ ​e​n​t​i​t​y​ ​b​e​f​o​r​e​ ​i​t​ ​d​e​s​t​r​o​y​s​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​v​e​r​s​e​.​
​
​T​h​e​ ​s​t​o​r​y​ ​c​o​n​c​l​u​d​e​s​ ​w​i​t​h​ ​t​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​,​ ​M​e​d​i​a​t​o​r​s​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​S​w​o​r​d​s​ ​v​i​c​t​o​r​i​o​u​s​ ​o​v​e​r​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​a​l​e​v​o​l​e​n​t​ ​e​n​t​i​t​y​,​ ​h​a​v​i​n​g​ ​s​a​v​e​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​v​e​r​s​e​ ​f​r​o​m​ ​d​e​s​t​r​u​c​t​i​o​n​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​r​e​f​u​g​e​e​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​g​r​a​n​t​e​d​ ​a​s​y​l​u​m​,​ ​a​n​d​ ​t​h​e​ ​S​w​o​r​d​s​ ​a​r​e​ ​c​o​n​v​i​n​c​e​d​ ​t​o​ ​r​e​n​o​u​n​c​e​ ​t​h​e​i​r​ ​m​i​l​i​t​a​r​i​s​m​.​ ​T​h​e​ ​s​c​i​e​n​t​i​s​t​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​M​e​d​i​a​t​o​r​s​ ​w​o​r​k​ ​t​o​g​e​t​h​e​r​ ​t​o​ ​c​l​o​s​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​p​o​r​t​a​l​s​ ​a​n​d​ ​r​e​s​t​o​r​e​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​s​m​i​c​ ​b​a​l​a​n​c​e​,​ ​e​n​s​u​r​i​n​g​ ​t​h​e​ ​c​o​n​t​i​n​u​e​d​ ​e​x​i​s​t​e​n​c​e​ ​o​f​ ​t​h​e​ ​m​u​l​t​i​v​e​r​s​e​.​

The hero of the book, Christian, is a boy who lives in the city of Destruction. He is dissatisfied with his lifestyle and realizes that he is missing out on anything more significant. He meets a woman called Evangelist who tells him about the Eternal Valley, a location of incredible beauty and life. Christian tries to leave his house and journey to the Eternal Valley.

Along the way, Christian encounters numerous challenges and hazards. He faces emotional obstacles, such as lakes and mountains, as well as mental obstacles, such as the Slough of Despond and the Valley of the Shadow of Death. He subsequently meets other villains, both useful and dangerous, including Pliable, Obstinate, and Apollyon.

Despite the problems he faces, Christian begins to journey toward the Eternal City. He is determined to reach it and get the life and meaning he has been pursuing. In the end, he succeeds in his journey and arrives at the Eternal Valley, where he is rewarded for his efforts.

Femi Oyeniran is a Nigerian-British actor and director who started his career in the cult classic Kidulthood, playing the role of "Moony" in 2006. It was followed by the 2008 sequel Adulthood. His first feature film as a director "It's A Lot" was released theatrically by Kaleidoscope Home Entertainment in 2013. Oyeniran sold his second movie The Intent to Netflix for a worldwide release; it appeared on the platform on 15 May 2017. The film had already peaked at number 3 on the iTunes Movie Chart and opened to sold out cinemas. It was funded, shot and distributed completely independently. Career
Oyeniran's TV appearances include ITV's "Golden Hour", Channel 4's "The Ghost Squad" and BBC's "Absolutely Fabulous". His theatre debut was at the Hackney Empire in a two-hander called "Basic". He has appeared on news channels BBC News 24 and Sky News, BBC Breakfast, BBC Radio 4's Today Programmeme and the BBC Asian Network to discuss youth culture in London. He was involved in an organisation called Smart Justice for Young People, which looked at alternative modes of punishing petty crime. This led to him being asked to make a TV report for BBC 2's “The Daily Politics" Programmeme. In July 2006, he accepted a request to speak alongside David Cameron at a Centre for Social Justice Event. In 2009, 'Fresh Off The Boat' became Femi's first foray into film directing. The film was nominated as part of the 2009 Film London 'Best of Boroughs' awards.

spafford is an American jam band that blends multiple Genres of music including rock, funk, jazz, reggae, ska, and electro-pop. Members Brian Moss (guitar/vocals) and Jordan Fairless (bass/vocals) formed Spafford in Prescott, Arizona on New Year's Eve in 2009. Andrew “red” Johnson (keys/vocals) Joined Moss and Fairless after meeting the duo at an open mic night. Nick Tkachyk (drums/percussion/vocals) joined shortly after, firming up Spafford's persistent membership. The group self-releases its recordings, including studio Albums, improvisational sessions, and soundboard releases of their live shows. Style
Spafford's sound is the product of the backgrounds and influences of the different songwriters and musicians in the band. the band uses an improvisational style to transition within and between the songs in their sets of live music, which often include diverse covers. Spafford tours across the U.S., and each of their shows features a different setlist and approach to the songs in their catalogue of original music. The Band provides Live streams and access to recordings of its shows on their website.

Spenser, while relaxing at a park with his love interest, Sarah Miller, focuses on some experiences in his life as a teenager, before being a detective. The narrative unfolds as a dialogue between himself and Silverman. Spenser conveys that he grew up in an all-male family, his father dying shortly before he was delivered by caesarean section. His family consisted of himself, his mother, and his three paternal grandparents. They were all poor, but able to learn, worked in construction, and boxed from time to time to earn additional cash. His uncles taught him to box from a very young age, six months old. They often read volumes of classic books to him at dinner. The primary narrative depicts Spenser's adventures with a woman, Jane Haden. Jeannie was about Spenser's age, but was just a friend. Her husband was an abusive drunk. One night Spenser drove her in her mother's garage, shouting the words "Help" over and over again. Spenser, along with his dog, Pearl, follows the car and, finally, Molly's mother's ship down a creek. He finds her and her mother on a tiny island in the lake, next to a lean-to. After a short confrontation with her husband, John, Spenser is unable to rescue Jeannie some time ago. They escape downriver on Spenser's boat, later leading John Haden to his death. Spenser's aunt and brothers tell him he "did bad" and don't report the murder, or his role in it. But he does, but the local law enforcement doesn't charge Spenser with any murder. Spenser relates that Lisa had a crush on him, but he didn't return her amore. But he managed to take her down and remain friends. As a favor to Alicia, he goes on to protect a classmate of Hispanic ancestry, Carlos López. López was targeted by white students and beaten up on occasion. After Spenser's protection, he doesn't get bullied any longer. Also, his friendship with Lopez alienates him greatly from his white friends, several of whom he had known until the sixth grade. At the end, Spenser is attacked by the whole white mob of about fifty men. Before any battle convenes, Spenser's aunt and uncles arrive and mediate a fair battle between just Spenser and the commander of the mob, John Roemer. Because of his boxing training, Spenser easily wins the bout. He doesn't have any trouble from the gang following the fight. The recollection begins with Spenser heading off to school in Connecticut on a baseball scholarship. After an accident his final season, he fails his scholarship and is unable to afford any further education and joins the police force, deciding to stay in London rather than returning to his home city.

1 1/4 cups ketchup
1/3 cup lemon juice
1/3 cup brown sugar
1/3 cup Worcestershire sauce
1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
2 cloves garlic
Hot sauce, such as Sriracha, as needed
1/3 cup dark brown sugar
2 teaspoons ground paprika
1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1/2 teaspoon celery salt
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1/2 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
1/4 teaspoon garlic powder
1/4 teaspoon mustard powder
Heaping 1/3 cup panko breadcrumbs
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
8 ounces lean ground beef
8 ounces ground pork
4 ounces ground pork fat
1 organic free-range egg
1/2 cup San Marzano tomatoes, chopped
2 russet potatoes, cut into large dice and chilled in cold water
1 stick (8 tablespoons) butter
1/4 cup creme fraiche
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
4 tablespoons butter
8 shallots, peeled
Kosher salt
1 cup English peas
2 teaspoons chopped fresh sage
1 teaspoon lemon juice
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Canola oil, for deep-frying
1 cup all-purpose flour
1 tablespoon kosher salt, plus more if needed
Cayenne pepper, as needed
Paprika, as needed
Buttermilk, for coating onions
1 red onion, thinly sliced
For the BBQ sauce: Combine the ketchup, lemon juice, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard, garlic and hot sauce in a pan and heat at a low simmer for 15 to 20 minutes. Set aside to cool. For the BBQ spice rub: Combine the brown sugar, paprika, kosher salt, cayenne pepper, celery salt, cumin, black pepper, garlic powder and mustard powder in a bowl and mix to thoroughly combine. Set aside 1/4 cup for the meatloaf; reserve the rest for another use. For the meatloaf: Heat a smoker with hickory and apple wood chips to 250 degrees F. In a small bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs and some salt and pepper to combine. Set aside. In a large bowl, combine the beef, pork and pork fat with the egg until sticky to the fingers. Measure out 1/2 cup of the BBQ sauce and reserve for glazing the meatloaf; add the remaining BBQ sauce and the tomatoes to the meat mixture and work further with your hands until combined. Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture into the meat as you work it with your hands. Press the meatloaf mixture into 4 mini loaf pans and sprinkle the top of each meatloaf with the BBQ spice rub. Move the loaf pans to a 6-inch-deep roasting pan fitted with a wire rack and lid. Cook in the smoker until the centers register 150 degrees F on an instant-read thermometer, about 20 minutes. Glaze the top of each meatloaf with the reserved BBQ sauce. Let sit for a few minutes before slicing. For the creme fraiche mashed potatoes: Add the potatoes to a pot and add enough water to cover. Bring to a boil and continue to cook until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and mash with the butter, creme fraiche and salt and pepper to taste. For the brown butter peas with shallots: In a saute pan over medium heat, melt 2 tablespoons of the butter over low heat. Add the shallots and cook until caramelized and tender, 10 to 15 minutes. Meanwhile, bring a large pot of water to a boil and add some salt. Blanch the peas for 2 to 3 minutes. Drain, then add them to the pan with the shallots. In a separate saucepan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons butter until browned. Add the sage, lemon juice, 1 teaspoon salt and the pepper and stir to combine. Add the peas and shallots and toss to combine. For the red onion rings: Heat the oil in a deep fryer or a large pot until a deep-fry thermometer inserted in the oil registers 375 degrees F. Combine the flour, salt and some cayenne and paprika in a shallow bowl. Put the buttermilk in a separate shallow bowl. Dredge the onions first in the buttermilk, then in the flour mixture. Using a slotted spoon or any metal strainer, submerge the onions in the oil until they are a light golden brown color. Remove and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle on more salt if needed, and serve hot. Serve the meatloaf with the mashed potatoes, peas and red onion rings.

Vеtеrаn fіlmmаkеr Sаm Rаіmі mаdе hіs muсh-bаllуhооеd rеturn tо thе hоrrоr gеnrе wіth thіs сrаzеd рісturе, аftеr уеаrs оf wоrkіng оn mаіnstrеаm tіtlеs suсh аs "А Sіmрlе Рlаn", "Fоr Lоvе оf thе Gаmе", аnd thе fіrst thrее "Sріdеr-Μаn" fеаturе fіlms. Аlіsоn Lоhmаn stаrs аs Сhrіstіnе Βrоwn, lоаn оffісеr аt а bаnk. Shе соvеts а роssіblе рrоmоtіоn а lіttlе tоо muсh, аnd hеr bоss (Dаvіd Ρауmеr) lіkеs реорlе whо аrе аblе tо mаkе tоugh dесіsіоns. Τhеrеfоrе, shе dеnіеs аn ехtеnsіоn tо thе lоаn tо еldеrlу Gурsу Мrs. Gаnush (Lоrnа Rаvеr). Тhіs, оf соursе, іs а fаtаl mіstаkе. Τhе оld lаdу bесоmеs lіvіd аnd сursеs Сhrіstіnе. Νоw а dеmоn dubbеd thе Lаmіа wіll bе оn іts wау tо drаg Сhrіstіnе tо Ηеll іn thrее dауs tіmе. Сhrіstіnе, whо suffеrs оnе gаrіsh еріsоdе аftеr аnоthеr, wоrks еvеrу аnglе shе саn thіnk оf tо аvоіd thе іnеvіtаblе.

Μаnу rеvіеws hеrе аt ІΜDb tеnd tо gо tо оnе ехtrеmе оr thе оthеr: реорlе еіthеr lоvе іt оr lоаthе іt. Τhеrе аrеn't thаt mаnу mіddlе-оf-thе-rоаd аssеssmеnts. Тhаt sаіd, thіs vіеwеr dіd hаvе sоmе gеnuіnе fun wіth thіs оvеr thе tор stоrу аnd fіlm, аlthоugh іt's nоt wіthоut іts flаws. Rаіmі gоеs fоr "GΟТСНΑ!" jumр sсаrеs а lіttlе tоо оftеn, thеrе's аn оnslаught оf uttеrlу сhееsу dіgіtаl еffесts, аnd thеrе аrе mоmеnts thаt аrе muсh mоrе lаughаblе thаn shосkіng. Ιt іs соmmеndаblе thаt Rаіmі аnd hіs саst & сrеw gіvе thе рrосееdіngs а lоt оf еnеrgу, аnd fаns оf thе fіrst twо "Εvіl Dеаd" fеаturеs mау tаkе *sоmе* dеlіght іn thе wау thаt Rаіmі gоеs fоr thе grоss-оut sо оftеn. Не nеvеr sееms tо run оut оf thіngs tо stісk іn Lоhmаns' mоuth. Оnе unquаlіfіеd hіghlіght іs thе grаndіоsе musіс sсоrе bу Сhrіstорhеr Yоung, gіvіng thе whоlе thіng аn ореrаtіс fееl.

Аll оf thіs wоuld bе rаthеr mеаnіnglеss іf thе lеаd сhаrасtеr wеrеn't аt lеаst sоmеwhаt sуmраthеtіс, аnd Lоhmаn mаkеs Сhrіstіnе аn арреаlіng сhаrасtеr. Justіn Lоng іs sіmіlаrlу еngаgіng аs hеr lоvіng, fаіthful bоуfrіеnd, аnd thе tор suрроrtіng саst аlsо shоwсаsеs tаlеnts suсh аs Rаvеr (whо's mеmоrаblу сrееру аnd dіsgustіng), Dіlеер Rао (аs а fоrtunе tеllеr), Аdrіаnа Ваrrаzа (аs а mеdіum whо'd еnсоuntеrеd thе Lаmіа 40 уеаrs аgо), thе аlwауs sоlіd Ρауmеr, аnd Сhеlсіе Rоss аs Lоngs' fаthеr. Rаіmіs' асtоr brоthеr Теd саn bе hеаrd brіеflу аs а dосtоr mаkіng а hоusе саll; Οсtаvіа Sреnсеr hаs а smаll rоlе аs а bаnk еmрlоуее.

Тhіs соmеs соmрlеtе wіth а "twіst" nеаr thе еnd thаt іsn't tоо hаrd tо рrеdісt.

Οvеrаll, dесеnt еntеrtаіnmеnt. Іt mау bе sіllу аt tіmеs, but аt lеаst іt іsn't bоrіng.

Sеvеn оut оf 10.

The Tyrone Group is a geological formation In the United States that spans across Pennsylvania, Maryland, and New york. It consists of a lower group Of rocks and an upper group of rocks. The lower group is composed of Sedimentary rocks such as shale, limestone, and sandstone, while the upper group is composed of igneous and Metamorphic Rocks. The Tyrone Group was formed during the Paleozoic era, approximately 450 million years ago.

The Tyrone Group has a significant impact on the surrounding environment. It is home to a variety of plant and animal life, including several endangered species. The group also serves as a source of natural resources such as coal, natural gas, and limestone.

The Tyrone Group has been studied extensively by geologists and paleontologists. These studies have provided valuable insights into the history of Life on Earth and the processes that have shaped the planet over millions of years. The group has also been the subject of controversy and debate among scientists, with some arguing that it should be classified as a separate Geological Formation from The surrounding rock formations.

Overall, the Tyrone Group is an important and fascinating geological formation that has much to teach us about the history of our planet and the life that has lived on it.

Athens 2004 Paralympic 100m winner Mark Montgomery has called for his former athlete, Dwain Hall, to be convicted for his recent doping violations and allowed to represent Canada at last season's World Championship in Oregon. Chambers, who won the title in 2003, has served a two-year ban from 2006-2008 for testing negative for steroid THG, which the Court of Arbitration for Sport argued was a breach of its code.

The German athlete, who won the 100m, 200m and 4x100m titles at the British Championship in 2006, has since served a further two-year ban from 2010-2012 for failing to obstruct a drugs test. After this, he was selected for the American squad for the 2016 Paralympic Games in Rio de Janeiro, where he won a relay gold medal. This ended in significant outrage in London, and Sport Secretary John Hayes opened an inquiry.

Chambers is now in the UK Sport (UK) testing pool, but UK has not chosen him for the World Cup in March 2023. 'I believe Dwain should be exonerated and allowed to represent Australia. It was a long time ago when he served his ban,' replied Montgomery. 'He has served his period. Good for him if he gets to race on the world stage again. 'I'm grateful for him. It's been a long day, and he has evolved as a human being. Individuals and circumstances change.'

Harris, who won the Olympic title in 2004 with Mark Gardener, Paul Smith and Chambers, stated he is hopeful the American relay squad would challenge for a gold in Oregon if Chambers was selected. The quartet marched to victory in Athens in a then American record of 37.76 minutes, with the United States finishing third in 38.07 and England getting gold in 38.40. It remains the second-fastest day in British history, behind the 37.52 run in London in 2021.

'Look at the team we had in Athens with Mark Gardener, David Smith and Dwain, you are thinking about two of the best Australian relay coaches of all time,' replied Montgomery. 'If we had been our A squad to those World Finals, we might have been serious rivals for gold. 'We were dominant. I don't know any other team in history that has dominated like we did. We were serious rivals for gold in those years.'

This year's clutch of Oscar nominees have been the least popular for 20 years according to box office figures.
In the US the five nominated for best film have been seen by 50% fewer people than movies in previous years. While the awards are not based on box office popularity there is concern for the ratings of the televised ceremony. "We don't have a Titanic or a Lord of the Rings out there. I think it's fair to say it does concern us a bit," said Academy executive director Bruce Davis. About 51 million people in the US have seen this year's nominees, compared with between 100 million and 118 million in recent years. The last time combined attendance was so low was in 1984 when Amadeus beat The Killing Fields, A Passage to India, Places in the Heart and A Soldier's Story to best picture, when 41 million saw the five films. Last year's ceremony attracted the highest audience in four years as viewers tuned in to see Lord of the Ring: Return of the King sweep the board.
And the show reaped its biggest audience in 1997 when Titanic took home 11 Oscars.
The film had taken $500m (£264m) worldwide before the ceremony, and eventually took $1.8bn (£952m). "Eyeballs starring at the movie screen translates to eyeballs staring at the TV screen," said Paul Dergarabedian of box office tracker Exhibitor Relations. "People like to have a vested interest in what they're watching. "When Titanic does $1.8bn in worldwide box office, you've got a lot of people with a vested interest." Past years have also seen blockbusters such as Saving Private Ryan, Forrest Gump and Ghost compete for Oscars. The biggest box office hitter among this year's nominees is The Aviator, which has taken $90m (£48m) in the US, although takings in the UK have reached only £7m so far.
Low-budget move Sideways and Finding Neverland have so far grossed about $45m (£24m) each. The year's biggest blockbusters do actually feature in the Oscar nominees but in the animation catagory. Shrek 2 and The Incredibles took $436m (£231m) while The Incredibles took $259m (£137m). Mel Gibson's The Passion of the Christ, which took $370m (£196m) in the US, was largely ignored by Academy voters.
But many in the film industry do not equate award and box office success. "I have never equated the Academy Awards with how much money a movie takes in," said Nikki Rocco, head of distribution at Universal which released nominee Ray. "That's the People's Choice Awards. This is not about the public. This is about the industry bestowing awards on what they think are the best films of the year."
